events.title: Origin Circle
events.description: We gather every morning to remember our shared origins on this Earth, connecting with the medicine of the Heart of the Earth (Kakaw) and opening the day for sharing our intentions, songs and prayers.

events.title: 4-Hands Massage Arnaud Mooij & Marcel Maurik
events.description:
Four hands; one experience
A unique collaboration between Marcel and Arnaud
Free, spacious, relaxed, and completely surrendered. That's how you'll feel after a massage from this professional massage duo. Working as one body, they bring your body to a state of complete relaxation, ensuring that your body feels free and open, and it seems like your heart is even fuller and more open, ready to experience the freedom at Ruigoord even more.
When two talents combine their experience and passion, something special emerges. That's what Marcel and Arnaud offer you: a fusion of our strengths as bodyworkers. From our own approach and background, we complement each other in an intuitive and healing collaboration.
You choose what you need. Do you want to feel deeply relaxed, let your energy flow, release blockages, or simply return to yourself? We offer various disciplines that you can freely combine: classic massage, energetic massage, TRAGER® Approach, intuitive bodywork, and Rebalancing techniques. And our hands know exactly where they need to be on your body to give you an amazing experience…

events.title: Bouke & Co · Free Clothes — Take Some, Bring Some
events.description:
Can you do better? Or are you just suffering from shopping urges? Come and enjoy FREE CLOTHING at Bouke & Co.

Cool clothes and accessories for free! New items every day! Unwanted styling advice included.
Feel free to bring your unwanted wardrobe too!
Everything is ️FREE️. Daily from 13:00-21:00 at Area 46.

events.title: Lach Workshop
events.description:
LAUGHING, LETTING GO & PLAYFUL CRAZINESS

Want to just let everything go? Then come and laugh with Sabine Rabbe!
Come to the laughter workshop during the Landjuweel Festival and completely lose yourself in laughter.
🤣 Laughing without reason
💃 Dancing, playing and letting loose
⚡ Recharging with festival energy
3 days of freedom, craziness and connection at Ruigoord.
A little rebellious, a little silly, and above all, lots of fun.
Leave your inhibitions behind and take your laughter with you.
Sabine Rabbe has been inspiring people for years with her contagious laughter, playfulness, and boundless energy.
As a laughter coach, performer, and connector, she shows you how liberating it is to simply let go and laugh.
With a mix of humor, spontaneity, and festival vibes, Sabine creates a space where fun, connection, and zest for life are central.
Laughter is her language, energy her trademark.
Come and laugh during the laughter workshop by Sabine Rabbe (aka "The Queen of Laughter") at Landjuweel this year!
As every year, we'll be letting loose again: a dose of humor, energy, and above all, lots of fun…

events.title: Bakfietsband
events.description:
The cargo bike band embodies joyful activism at its finest. This mobile stage brings a smile to your face and transforms the street into a dance floor. Come join us for some fun or participate with this cheerful group.

The cargo bike band plays 3 times a day, in the afternoon, evening, and night. There is also a daily guest performance, with a group from South America, AP Mayor, performing on Saturday.

events.title: Poppenkastvoorstelling
events.description: During the "Landjuweel" (a Dutch festival), puppet shows will be performed at Ruigoord 16. The start times will be announced on a sign outside. The afternoon performances are suitable for all ages.

events.title: Rasta Robert & Friends
events.description: Dreamtime Healing sound massage concerts, mantra singing, theremin, spontaneous musical improvisation, spiritual chill-out space. Every day 2:00 PM - 10:00 PM, concert sessions 2:00 PM - 3:30 PM and 8:00 PM - 9:30 PM (check-in 30 minutes prior).

events.title: Rochelle Dietz
events.description:
Rochelle Dietz' sets are a reflection of her gentle, yet dynamic spirit — capturing the essence of melodic, introspective moments while unleashing bursts of energy when the time is right. She brings a distinct perspective, blending rich melodies with unpredictable, captivating rhythms which keep the dancefloor alive.

Rochelle has collaborated with events such as Wildeburg, Into the Woods, Gardens of Babylon and Landjuweel, and is occasionally found behind the decks in bars and clubs in Berlin. She is an active member of the Utrecht creative community and her concept, Birdcage Radio, provides a platform for young and established artists to flourish.

events.title: Open Mic
events.description: Music, poetry, stories, or something completely different. The stage is yours. Your moment, your talent. Step up and surprise us. From hidden talents to spontaneous inspiration: anything goes, nothing is mandatory.

events.title: Photo Booth
events.description: Capture your favorite moment and step into our photo booth. Create a beautiful memory of the festival by taking your own picture.

events.title: SAVE THE GREEN FIELD — SIGN NOW!
events.description: The Green Field is a rare green space in the city where there is still room for wild nature, culture, and experimentation. It is run on a voluntary basis by residents and small, local businesses. But now, our free space is at risk of disappearing. Therefore, we need to act NOW, it's not too late. You can help us by signing the petition! More information at our stand.

events.title: The Floating Poet
events.description: Become a floating poet and come up to our raft to write a poem. Ask the internship manager where the secret departure point is. You can recite your creation in the garden.

events.title: The Game Cabinet
events.description:
Open a drawer, slide a box towards you, and let the fun begin. In the Games Corner, you'll find a diverse mix of card and board games waiting for new players. Challenge someone to a game of 30 seconds, go for pure chaos with a party game, or rediscover a classic you had long forgotten.

Whether you come to win, hang out, laugh, or simply chat: there's always something fun happening around the table here. Pull up a chair and join in the fun.

events.title: XXL spellen: Schaken, Jenga, Twister
events.description: Huge, bigger, wildest: With our XXL games, it's all about having fun to the fullest. Challenge your strategic mind on a chessboard as big as your ego, build towers that reach higher than your weekend plans with XXL Jenga filled with surprising questions and tasks, or get tangled up during a game of Twister that is guaranteed to lead to laughter fits.

events.title: Contribute to building the growing "Insect Village" – The Landscape.
events.description:
Donna and Mariana explore the realms of creativity, nature, and play. With a focus on what emerges in the moment, they invite children to collaborate, investigate, and unleash their imaginations.

Throughout the festival, we will collectively build a world. A playful exploration of what "home" means for each of us.

The world taking shape - Landscape
We begin with the foundation of the insect village. Using clay, branches, and natural materials, we create tunnels, hills, shelters, and other hidden spaces. A world for crawlers and ground dwellers, where a safe home can emerge among roots, passages, and hollows.
Part of a three-part workshop
events.venue: Village Square · A World in the Making

events.title: Workshop Lino Stempelen
events.description:
KIMTAM TATTOO is a tattoo artist with Javanese-Surinamese roots, based in Rotterdam. In her work, she combines craftsmanship, culture, and storytelling by creating unique designs that tell a personal story.

During this workshop, children will learn about the ancient art of printing. The artwork you see consists of lino stamps that have been hand-carved. Stamping techniques have been used around the world for centuries to print patterns and images on paper and fabric. In parts of Southeast Asia, including Malaysia, similar techniques were even used to create tattoos, where ornaments and figures were carved from wood.

This rich tradition inspired KIMTAM to design her own stamps and use them as a basis for her tattoo work.
For children aged 5-9 years:
Children playfully discover the world of stamping. They choose from various handmade lino stamps, make their own prints on paper, and then receive their favorite design as a temporary stencil on their skin.
For children aged 10+ years:
Older children get to work independently as creators…

events.title: Kloek
events.description:
KLOEK. An interactive street theatre performance. Or, to put it another way: A robust, mobile installation that only comes to life on the streets with the help of brave passersby…

Back to basics. KLOEK as pure installation theatre. Nothing hidden, nothing embellished. Robust structures, basic mechanics. Simple and transparent. The timeless power of simplicity.

Pure. Steel and wood. Nature and human strength. Strong hands of both adults and children pull on the ropes to make the sound instruments play and the puppet fly. Free as a bird.

A bold and subtle interplay between man and machine creates visual poetry with muscle power. Both intimate and exuberant, it stimulates imagination and makes fantasy a tangible reality. KLOEK.

events.title: Tender Moments
events.description:
Liesbet De Rouck is the founder of The Self Love Academy, a body-oriented facilitator, spoken word artist, and creator of transformative spaces centered around presence, connection, and emotional embodiment.

Her work weaves together movement, music, spoken word, nervous system awareness, intimacy work, and deep human connection. Through modalities such as Tender Moments, Tantric Dance, embodied relational work, meditation, and live performance, she creates spaces where people are invited to soften, feel, and reconnect with themselves beyond performance or survival.

Known for her raw honesty, warmth, and powerful stage presence, Liesbet bridges the worlds of art, healing, and collective transformation. Her performances are both grounding and emotionally evocative, inviting audiences into a deeper experience of aliveness, tenderness, and truth.

She is the founder of The Self Love Academy and facilitates workshops, intensives, and transformational programs throughout the Netherlands.

events.title: Self Wellness Practice
events.description:
Welcome to Landjuweel.
 You have just arrived and are walking around for the first time; many impressions, crowded, many people.
 How are you doing today? Are you feeling relaxed?
 How do you feel your body today?
 Can you connect with it?...


 Welcome to Landjuweel.
 You have just arrived at the festival and are exploring for the first time; many impressions, crowded, many people.
 How are you doing today? What are you feeling?
 How do you feel your body today?
 Can you connect with it?...

events.title: Embodied Dating Club
events.description:
Dating apps are overrated. Let's meet each other in real life. A fun workshop for singles who want to open the festival together. We step out of our heads, into our bodies, and create space for curiosity, attraction, and flirting. With guided exercises, you'll meet different people, feel what happens, and practice staying true to yourself while making contact. No speed dating or pressure to find someone. Instead, a warm, light, and exciting place to awaken your playfulness and bring it into the festival experience. Maybe you'll find attraction. Maybe a new festival friend. Or maybe you'll meet someone on the dance floor later who suddenly doesn't seem so strange anymore. Connect with your body, not just your bio.
 Denise Kooij
 Denise Kooij is a somatic intimacy coach and embodiment facilitator, working with men, singles, and mixed groups. Her spaces bring depth and playfulness together. Where truth is welcome, emotions are allowed, and the body guides the way. She helps people to step out of their heads and into the felt experience of life and love.…

events.title: Heart Chakra Meditation
events.description:
The Heart Chakra Meditation (as designed by Karunesh) is a one-hour active meditation designed to open, cleanse, and balance the heart energy center (Anahata). It's an active meditation that uses movement rather than sitting still, making it accessible to people who find traditional meditation difficult.
 The technique consists of specific movements, music, and breathing exercises rooted in Sufi traditions. Tension is released, and self-love and compassion are fostered, culminating in deep relaxation.
 Clear explanations will be provided before the meditation, and afterwards there will be time for questions and sharing your experiences.
 Marcel “Mizza” Hendriks lives and works at the Wajid OSHO Meditation Centre in The Hague, where he offers meditation guidance, among other things. Mizza can guide you to a state of pure consciousness in a short time using simple and understandable instructions.

events.title: Klei Jezelf Blij Workshop
events.description: "The most relaxing hour of the entire Landjuweel event," one participant reportedly told his friend during the clay workshop, "was..."

events.title: Divine Mistress of Craziness
events.description: (Born in 1939) She commutes between Cologne and the Amsterdam artist village of Ruigoord. After taking a parachute jump on her 60th birthday, she left behind her conventional life to pursue profound spiritual experiences. In her temple in Ruigoord, she celebrates encounters at a soul level and opens doors to higher levels of consciousness. More information about her unique philosophy can be found on the Ruigoord Artist Page.

events.title: Silk Fans workshop - hosted by Tara Moon
events.description: Whether you’re new to fan flow or a seasoned practicer, this workshop will have something for you! We will explore expansive foundational moves as well as a little trippy tech work, truly showcasing the range of movement and play that silk fan has to offer.

events.title: HONOUR*REVIVE*SHARE - The Workshop
events.description:
Cato Coebergh, Sana van der Linge (Kikvors Collectief)

In our current fast-paced and materialistic world, we often overlook the waste we generate on a daily basis. At our workshop, we encourage people to view their waste in a new light: We will honor its history, and furthermore, we will revive it through a creative process. Sharing our stories and creations with each other.

Cato Coebergh: People produce far too much waste on a daily basis. Cato Coebergh addresses this issue as an artist by making conscious material choices and creating mindfulness workshops to raise awareness about the topic and offer new perspectives on everyday life.

Sana van der Lingen: Sana is a visual artist who combines traditional crafts with innovative techniques to create layered art and design objects. Her work ranges from graphic design and mixed-media prints, in which she primarily focuses on working with historical heritage, to diverse objects such as accessories or household items made from recycled materials, ceramics, and textiles.

events.title: Festival Make-up & Makeover with Shotgun Cilla
I Love You, Cacao! – Opening Ceremony
events.description:
Temple of Self opens Landjuweel with "I Love You, Cacao!", a warm cacao ceremony for everyone who makes the temple possible. We come together to consciously begin the festival, set intentions, and reflect on all the people who have built, supported, and contributed to the program. Temple of Self is a special stage at Landjuweel. Currently, we are working on a crowdfunding campaign to build a permanent temple. During the opening ceremony, there will be a model of the new temple, which can be viewed in scale 1:10. Visitors are welcome to attend, drink cacao, and contribute financially to the realization of this space through donations.

The ceremony is co-created. Visitors and involved makers who want to showcase themselves, for example by singing a song, are warmly invited to participate. The goal is to open the festival from a place of connection, gratitude, and shared intention.
Limited space, cacao from Spirit of Cacao

events.title: Bon Bon Vivant chocolate tasting
events.description: Come and taste our vegan Guayusa Chocolate Bar. It gives you energy in a kind, smooth way. No jitters, no crash, just clear focus and happy vibes. Perfect to start your Landjuweel day.

events.title: Eric, the magician behind the curtain.
events.description:
Eric the Magician will appear and disappear again around the Gnome House Stage (Kabouterhuys) at Landjuweel. Hearing, seeing, smelling, tasting, feeling, and thinking are the ingredients of a magician. Eric creates his own illusions; he doesn't like to let them go and loves to involve you in them. Beyond the smoke and mirrors, there is room for attention, and before you know it, you are spellbound. You don’t need to wear a red hat to be a Gnome.


 With Landjuweel, Eric the Magician will reappear and disappear around the Kabouterhuys Stage. Hearing, seeing, smelling, tasting, feeling, and thinking are the ingredients of a magician. Eric creates his own illusions; he doesn't like to lose them and loves to make you part of them. Beyond the smoke and mirrors, there is room for attention, and before you know it, you are spellbound. You don’t need to wear a red hat to be a Gnome.

events.title: Algarabía - Bardo Teatro Físico
events.description:
The company started in 2016, with the objetive of creating a play that combines circus techniques with physical theater.
 The artistic searches of the creators lead to the same path, making the fusion between the theater and the circus, our scenic language. We are interested in moving from a performance show to a theatrical poetics and that circus resources and skills are deeply linked with a dramaturgy. In this dynamic, acrobats are modified by the text and transcend the purely physical.

events.title: Soup and sandwiches
events.description: We gather around the fire to cook together. Bring a piece of vegetables and add it to the communal soup. Small and large hands work side by side, while bread is baked over the fire. The square fills with aromas, and our hearts are warmed by being together.

events.title: Making dream catchers
events.description:
Create your own dream catcher using natural and found materials. With twigs, string, beads, and feathers collected from birds, we create a special object full of stories and imagination.

What dreams, wishes, or adventures will your dream catcher capture?

events.title: Saskia Schmink
events.description: Here, imaginations come to life. The makeup team transforms children into whatever they can dream of – from magical creatures to brave adventurers.

events.title: Story with The Troubadour Car
events.description:
Ian Merkoulov travels throughout the Low Countries as a true troubadour. With his "Troubadour Car," he collects stories for both young and old, presenting them as musical folklore that tells us about our beautiful world.

Story with The Troubadour Car
Step into a story for children and adults together with Ian Merkoulov and his friends in the "Troubadour Car."

events.title: The Puppet Circus
events.description:
Create your own circus performer for the puppet theatre. Will it be a tightrope walker, clown, acrobat, or perhaps even a lion tamer? With all sorts of materials, you'll give your puppet its own unique personality and story.

Is your artist ready for the big performance? Then step behind the puppet theatre and join in on the "Puppet Circus". Together, we will bring a colourful circus show to life!

events.title: Landing Yoga
events.description:
Need a moment to land into the weekend and into your body? Join us for some grounding Hatha yoga, an ancient practice that harmonises movement & breath through a series of longer held āsanas (poses). A gentle yet empowering homecoming to the Self. 
 Laurie King
 I'm Laurie (he/him), a queer British-Kiwi whose current home-base is Berlin. My passion is creating spaces that cultivate healthy, thriving human-to-human relationships, helping people take off their masks, feel their sensations and celebrate their aliveness on Earth. 
 Since 2019, I have organised and facilitated hundreds of Authentic Relating, Dating and Embodiment workshops in Oceania and Europe. My training includes Authentic Revolution, The Embody Lab, World Peace Yoga School, the Connection Institute, ISTA and Body Electric. I have facilitated at internationally acclaimed festivals such as Stretch and Ignite and organisations such as ART International.

events.title: Ari's Classical Radio Show: Can You Händle It?
events.description:
Arinde Jonker
 Arinde Jonker (1990) hopped to the beats of Bach’s music in her playpen before she could stand. And she never stopped. Since 2020, Arinde has been making (radio)programs in which she presents classical music in an accessible and often alternative way. She makes you listen to classical music in a way you never did before. Classical music is groovy, revolutionary and sexy. Curious? Come and listen.
 Program:
 Do you want something other than hard beats or loud guitars to start your Landjuweel weekend? 2 hours of classical music, along with good stories about Mozart, Beethoven, and other classical music stars. They are wild and juicy (and child friendly). Come and listen to great music and good stories. This will be the perfect start of the weekend.

events.title: Poi workshop by Hidde (Hidden Flow)
events.description: Step into the magical world of poi, where two simple cords transform into mesmerizing circles of rhythm, movement, and flow. Join Hidde for a playful beginner-friendly workshop and discover that anyone can learn this beautiful flow art: no experience needed, just curiosity. Come as you are, lose yourself in the dance of the circles, and leave with a little more flow than you arrived with!

events.title: Wakhan Project
events.description:
The music of Wā́khan Project is a modern ritual: a fiery fusion of ethno-tribal grooves, bouncing basslines, and a sassy energy that transforms any dancefloor into a mystical playground. His sets possess a rare power—the ability to awaken the wild, free, and joyful part that sleeps in everyone—with a smile, a twist, a spark in the air.
 Before igniting international stages, Wā́khan was already shaking the walls of Parisian squats and running club nights with his first techno collective, BPMOOD. It was there that his vision sharpened: to create music that is more organic, more tribal, more connected to something instinctive. 
 A sound that dances between raw groove, gentle spirituality, and playful mischief.
 A sound that has become his signature.

 In 2018, driven by a desire to cultivate a new scene in Paris, he founded the collective O’Tawa, now a reference point for unconventional nights and immersive experiences in the city. Under his guidance, O’Tawa has become a free, intense, and vibrant artistic space—a place where people come as much to dance as to experience a moment out of time.…

events.title: Taste of Tantra (Authentic Relating) by Tamara Groen
events.description:
Amidst the music, colors, and encounters of the festival, we invite you to slow down. To truly feel who is standing before you… and who you are in this moment. Through playful exercises, presence, and genuine attention, we will discover how connection arises when nothing needs to be proven or achieved. A gentle journey towards greater authenticity, vibrancy, and human connection. Come as you are. Leave with more connection... with yourself, and with the people around you.

Tamara Groen
Tamara Groen has spent over 20 years exploring and sharing what it truly means to be alive — in theory, through the body, breath, and raw human experience.
Her mission is to awaken the quiet power that lives inside each person — to bring people home to themselves, so that connection and peace can ripple outward into the world.
She believes that when people feel connected to themselves and each other, peace becomes natural. Her mission is to help make that happen — one breath, one person, one moment at a time.
After a fast-paced career in international business and a life that looked "successful" from the outside, Tamara hit a breaking point.…

events.title: Contribute to building the growing "Insect Village" – The Landscape.
events.description:
Donna and Mariana explore the realms of creativity, nature, and play. With a focus on what emerges in the moment, they invite children to collaborate, investigate, and unleash their imaginations.

Throughout the festival, we will collectively build a world. A playful exploration of what "home" means for each of us.

The world taking shape - Landscape
We begin with the foundation of the insect village. Using clay, branches, and natural materials, we create tunnels, hills, shelters, and other hidden spaces. A world for crawlers and ground dwellers, where a safe home can emerge among roots, passages, and hollows.
Part of a three-part workshop
events.venue: Village Square · A World in the Making

events.title: InstrumMensen Freestyle Rap Session
events.description:
Cato Fluitsma and Ian Merkoulov, with their Troubadour Car, have found each other as "Life's Small Artists" and travel together in a rhyming musical dialogue.

Can you freestyle as a "Life's Small Artist"? Everyone can! With everyone from the youngest to the oldest, we set the tone for the Landuweel festival. Pick up the Mic!

events.title: Magdalena Rose Codes
events.description:
Magdalene Rose Codes [NL]
 Are you curious about Mary Magdalene?
 Do you feel a longing for the Sacred Feminine?
 Mary Magdalene — the mysterious life partner of Yeshua (the original Hebrew name of Jesus), tantrica, apostle to the apostles, womb priestess, prostitute…?
 A historical figure or a myth?
 Who was — or who is — Mary Magdalene?
 What wisdom does SHE carry?…

The Magdalene Rose Codes offers a re-awakening and activation of the hidden feminine.
 You are warmly welcome to experience her voice, symbolism, scent, and color.
 An encounter with femininity in all its forms. Men are also very welcome.
 We advise against the use of alcohol and other intoxicating substances before and during the Magdalene Rose Codes.

Magdalene Rose Codes [EN]
 Are you curious about Mary Magdalene?
 Do you feel drawn to the Sacred Feminine?
 Mary Magdalene — the mysterious life partner of Yeshua (the original Hebrew name of Jesus), tantrica, apostle to the apostles, womb priestess, prostitute…?
 A historical figure or a myth?
 Who was — or who is — Mary Magdalene?
 What wisdom does SHE carry?…

events.title: DIY Ecstatic Bodywork —release & activation
events.description:
Robbert van Ede creates spaces where the body's natural flow can be released. As a transformative bodyworker, he helps you remember what it feels like to be completely present, free, and at peace.

With a deep trust in the wisdom of the nervous system, Robbert brings a grounded, loving, and soulful energy to the festival—ready to co-create a space where life force energy can fully express itself.

events.title: Northern Roots – body, nature & connection
events.description:
This workshop is for you if you feel a connection to nature, your body, or your roots – but also a sense of disconnection, silence, or something that was never passed on.

In many northern cultures, including Sámi traditions, the core wasn't a single ritual, but a relationship with the land, the body, and rhythm. In this workshop, we don’t try to reconstruct lost traditions, but instead create space where your own connection can begin to re-emerge.
 We work with breath, body, sound, and natural elements – listening to the nervous system and building a sense of safety. Inspiration may come from practices like Joiku, not as performance, but as a natural extension of the breath.

In the workshop, we will: arrive into the body and regulate the nervous system
 practice guided breathing (rhythm + longer exhales)
 use gentle movement and grounding (earth/contact)
 explore sound as an extension of the breath
 spend time in quiet presence

No prior experience is needed – you participate from your own boundaries.

events.title: Wild Grace
events.description:
A warm bubble of elements exists between heaven and earth.
 A place where the strength of the ground meets the freedom of the sky.
 Where bare feet touch the earth and roots grow deep into the heart.
 Wild Grace is an invitation to land fully in the present moment. To feel. To listen. To remember the voice of your soul.

 Through movement, sound, nature, and creativity, we reconnect with ourselves and with one another.
 A journey back to yourself.
 A celebration of life.
 A dance between heaven and earth.

 What Can You Expect? 
 At the heart of Wild Grace lies the powerful dialogue between live music, partnerwork, and contemporary dance theatre. The live music breathes with the performers, creating a living landscape of sound that guides every movement and emotion.
 About Ivana
 From a very young age, Ivana knew that dance was her path in life. Movement and creativity have always been her way of expressing emotions, telling stories, and connecting with the world around her.…

events.title: Organic Sound Group
events.description:
The Organic Sound Group performs an integrated sound performance with poetry for and by the butterfly garden, for the plants, for the animals, and for all the beautiful beings around us. With the special sounds of the ravast, monochord, percussion, flute, kalimbas, bells, and singing bowls, the audience and artists come closer to living nature.

A tribute to everything that grows here. A tribute to all the forest nymphs, elves, and goblins who live here. The group The Organic Sound Group consists of Jill Hesketh (UK), Gunilla Andersson (SE), Valentijn Steenhuis (NL), Frederic Petzoldt (NL) and guest artists.
 Links:
 Youtube

events.title: Flirting for people who are terrible at it
events.description: This workshop is for LGBTQ+ individuals who sometimes feel lost when it comes to flirting. It’s easy to get frustrated when you feel your flirting skills aren’t where you want them to be. But let's find ways to rediscover the joy of authentically connecting with people! With like-minded individuals, we will share positive experiences, practice together, and design our own experiment to test things out during Landjuweel.

events.title: Nevula and the Dragon's Tale
events.description:
And they lived happily ever after… This is how the story begins.

In the smoldering crater, an ancient dragon lies. Not as a prisoner, but as an ally to a princess who escaped the castle with her. They climb precariously high above the clouds, searching for something that was lost.
High above the clouds lives the daughter of the wind spirits, weaving dreams from mist. Yet, something is still missing in her life.

Can these extraordinary messengers rekindle the fire?
Fairy tales, cosmic rhythms, dance, fire, and collective ceremony.

events.title: Sulo
events.description: Under the name Sulo, Merel Hegenbart (vocals) and Job van der Linden (guitar) will take you on a journey through a melancholic landscape where intimacy and soul meet.

events.title: Music Carpet
events.description:
Magique Jean Jean, Cato Fluitsma, and Ian Merkoulov are "Life Artists" who love to give others a voice and expression.

Musical Carpet
Magique Jean Jean, Cato Fluitsma, and Ian Merkoulov weave together with friends and family a "Musical Carpet" of the day. Using sounds, words, phrases, and notes, we create a carpet that can be unrolled and enjoyed.

Would you like to join us?

events.title: Woven Voices Opening with Lex Empress
events.description:
Woven Voices originated from love. Love for bringing people together and giving everyone the space to express themselves. What remains is a beautiful work of art.

Woven Voices: Weaving Our Shared Visions Together

Woven Voices, the proud creation of Lex Empress, begins on the first day of Landjuweel. Each day, participants are invited to write intentions, dreams, and visions for our future, for the children, and for the world. These words are painted onto cotton and woven together into one collective carpet. A living artwork created from many voices becoming one. Everyone can join and become part of this shared creation. By the end of the festival, the carpet stands as a physical memory of our united prayers and possibilities.

Lex Empress
Versatile and innovative – never the same: that's Lex Empress. To understand what she does, you have to see her perform, because no performance is ever the same. She creates interactive experiences where the audience is at the center. In 7 languages, with more than 4 octaves of notes in her singing, and with a deep belief in the potential of humanity, she opens hearts and minds like no other.

events.title: Algarabía - Bardo Teatro Físico
events.description:
The company started in 2016, with the objetive of creating a play that combines circus techniques with physical theater.
 The artistic searches of the creators lead to the same path, making the fusion between the theater and the circus, our scenic language. We are interested in moving from a performance show to a theatrical poetics and that circus resources and skills are deeply linked with a dramaturgy. In this dynamic, acrobats are modified by the text and transcend the purely physical.

events.title: Heart Songs with Ukulele by Immy Angel
events.description: Following my Souls calling to share my voice and heart opening songs, guided by the sweet tune of the Ukelele. Happy to share more of my inner world with you! I'm sharing medicine and mantra songs that are about all variations of Divine Love, bringing you back to the essence of your Being. You're very welcome to come sing all together!

events.title: Somatic Healing Workshop: Raindrop Movement™
events.description:
Raindrop Movement™ is a gentle yet profoundly transformative somatic healing modality that invites us to return home to the wisdom of the body. Rooted in neuroscience, nervous system regulation, trauma-informed practice, breath, and intuitive movement, it offers a pathway beyond thinking and into direct embodied experience.
 Rather than trying to fix, force, or overcome ourselves, Raindrop Movement teaches us to slow down, listen deeply, and follow the body's innate intelligence. As we soften habitual patterns of tension, protection, and disconnection, the nervous system begins to regulate naturally, allowing greater ease, emotional resilience, vitality, and authentic expression to emerge.
 Every movement becomes an opportunity to reconnect with ourselves—not through performance, but through presence. In this space, healing is not something we achieve, but something we allow.
 Whether you are seeking stress relief, emotional release, deeper self-awareness, or a more intimate connection with your body, Raindrop Movement offers an invitation to experience the transformative power of somatic healing from the inside out.
 Our facilitators:…

events.title: Improvisation Theatre
events.description:
When was the last time you almost peed yourself laughing? If you join this workshop, tomorrow you can say: yesterday! And the best part? You don't even have to be funny yourself.
 Through accessible collaborative games you'll create, move and discover what happens when you stop overthinking. Playing without brakes, saying yes, celebrating mistakes.
 The games have their roots in improvisational theatre, but you don't have to be an actor, or ever want to become one.
 No experience needed. No script. No wrong answers.
 Laughter guaranteed.


 Wanneer heb jij voor het laatst bijna in je broek geplast van het lachen? Als je deze workshop volgt kan je morgen zeggen: gisteren! En het beste deel? Je hoeft zelf niet eens grappig te zijn.
 Via toegankelijke samenwerkingsspellen ga je samen creëren, bewegen en ontdekken wat er gebeurt als je stopt met nadenken. Spelen zonder rem, ja zeggen, fouten vieren.
 De spellen hebben hun wortels in het improvisatietheater, maar je hoeft geen acteur te zijn, of te willen worden.
 Geen ervaring nodig. Geen script. Geen foute antwoorden.
 Lachen gegarandeerd.

events.title: Live from your inner wisdom.
events.description:
Esther Simao helps people rediscover the wisdom that already exists within them. From her practice, es Imago, she works with awareness development, body-oriented work, and shifting deeply ingrained patterns in our lives. Her knowledge and experience are based on ancient wisdom traditions that help people to reconnect with themselves, nature, and their inner knowing.

In the workshop "Live Your Inner Wisdom," Esther shares insights from the Paleolithic shamanistic tradition. After a brief introduction to this ancient knowledge, participants are invited to explore it for themselves. Through practical exercises, they can have a direct encounter with their inner knowing, which goes beyond thinking. This is an experience of listening, feeling, and remembering what is already known deep within.

This workshop is an invitation to pause, connect with your own source of wisdom, and discover how it can support you in everyday life. By combining depth, simplicity, and experience, Esther creates a safe space where participants can meet their own answers.
 Live from your inner wisdom ~ Esther Simao…

events.title: Ruigoord Rebels
events.description:
Sweltering Louisiana blues, soul and funk from the 70s, controversial swing from the vibrant 30s and 40s. Ruigoord Rebels, a band firmly rooted in the renowned cultural hub, has reinvented itself with this 6-man (well, two women and four men) formation.

The band has undergone many transformations around Barbara Schroevers, the heart of the rebels. She accompanies her characteristic vocals with her dobro mandolin and takes you on a journey through life-filled songs.
Barbara is supported by Alain Eskinasi on keyboard, Simon Haneveld on guitar, Lili Bell on harmonica, Walking Walter on bass, and Hans van Brunschot on drums.

events.title: Ecstatic Dance with Dj B.ART
events.description:
Hey! I am Bart aka B.Art and I am very excited to bring you an ecstatic dance journey.
 My philosophy around Ecstatic Dance is to craft an intentional journey. Every journey tells a different story, and every journey can be embodied in the dancers own, unique, way. During this journey I invite the dancer to explore their authentic self-expression through movement, sound and to be with whatever arises in the moment. Come explore with me, together as one!
 Expect a musical adventure that carries you through highs and lows, inspired by nature, rooted in a tribal base and infused with sounds and styles that wander across the globe

events.title: DJ Witte Aap
events.description: Not a DJ, but a music selector playing wonderful tunes from all over the world!

events.title: Speeddaten
events.description: Join in, make new connections and meet interesting people. Friends, festival buddies, or maybe even more. A series of short encounters that can be anything: surprising, inspiring, awkward in a fun way, or exactly what you didn't expect. Sit down, talk, switch seats – and see what happens.

events.title: Merlijn Nash
events.description: Merlijn Nash will perform a show built from the ground up especially made for Landjuweel. Inspired by artists such as James Blake and Radiohead, he’ll perform solo with an array of electronics, beats, and vocals, drawing the listener into a space between tranquil and moodiness.

events.title: Flamenco – Everything is Colorful
events.description:
Todo es de color consists of:
Nancy Polderman; vocals, dance & flute
Jan Bianchi; guitar and
Samu Ramphal; percussion

We create Flamenco-Fusion music inspired by artists such as Paco Lucia, Vicente Amigo, and Lole & Manuel, with additional Latin influences from Alex Cuba and Silvio Rodriguez.

events.title: Juliska
events.description: In the program, "it's about now," Juliska Yonne sings and plays piano with Frans van Diemen on trumpet. The music alternates between lyrical, rhythmic, and quiet passages. The lyrics are poetic and philosophical-theatrical. They take you along on this moment, to encounters, streets, hands, and houses, together, birds, transience, love, letting go, yourself, your imagination.

events.title: Authentiek & verbindend communiceren
events.description:
My name is Yente, and I have a great fascination for human relationships and language.

How often does it happen that the words you use come across very differently to the other person than how you intended them? It's such an art to become aware of the language you use, so that it can help you in fulfilling your needs. The non-violent communication model can bring clarity and serve as a means for expressing yourself better or understanding others better. In this introductory workshop, you will learn about this model and practice using it.

events.title: Music UPLifting Handpan Experience
events.description: In this hour, we experience Handpan Sounds, Spoken Word, Breath & More as a way of reconnecting with ourselves. In this semi-interactive moment Joya is sharing little pieces and bits of how he feels most alive & what keeps him busy. He also wants to bring some interactivity & creating space for vulnerability

events.title: Shimon presents Radio Border: Sunbelt Sounds
events.description:
MUSICIAN & FRENCH PRODUCER-DJ, SHIMON, IS FROM THE CITY OF PARIS, AND HAS HAD A HUGE INFLUENCE ON THE EUROPEAN ELECTRONIC MUSIC SCENE THROUGH HIS ARTIST RESIDENCY AT KATER BLAU CLUB IN BERLIN.

WITH THRILLING MINIMAL RHYTHMS AND HYPNOTIC MELODIES THAT TAKE LISTENERS TO ANOTHER WORLD, HIS WORK CAPTURES THE PURE SPIRIT OF ELECTRONIC MUSIC. SHIMON'S EXPERTISE IS DEMONSTRATED BY HIS ABILITY TO SMOOTHLY COMBINE MANY ELECTRONIC MUSIC SUBGENRES, RESULTING IN A UNIQUE SOUND THAT IS BOTH ALLURING AND SONICALLY RICH. EACH SONG TAKES THE LISTENER ON A JOURNEY THROUGH SOUND, WITH COMPLEX SYNTHESIZER LAYERS, RUMBLING BASSLINES, AND HYPNOTIC BEATS. HIS MUSIC HAS AN IRRESISTIBLE BEAT THAT WILL GET EVERYONE ON THE DANCE FLOOR MOVING.

SHIMON'S VISION IS IN HIS ABILITY TO WRITE MUSIC THAT CUTS ACROSS DIVISIONS AND CONNECTS WITH A WIDE RANGE OF LISTENERS. HIS TUNES HAVE THE ABILITY TO INSPIRE A VARIETY OF EMOTIONS AND CREATE AN IMMERSIVE EXPERIENCE, REGARDLESS OF WHETHER YOU'RE AN EXPERIENCED HOUSE MUSIC ENTHUSIAST OR A CASUAL LISTENER.

SHIMON STANDS OUT AS AN INNOVATOR IN A WORLD OVERRUN BY ELECTRONIC MUSIC.…

events.title: Yanic
events.description:
Years of travelling the world left their mark on YANIC — in the breadth of what he carries, and the depth of how he listens.
 His sets move between worlds. Sometimes slow and juicy, sensual and grounded. Sometimes wild, ecstatic and beautifully chaotic. Sometimes all of it in the same night. For eight years he has been resident DJ and curator at De Lievelinge, a place that became his musical home. Now he's ready to be heard beyond it.
 YANIC is spreading his wings — and the dancefloor follows.

events.title: Trash Skeleton
events.description:
My name is Trash Skeleton (she/her), and I'm a trans-anarcho playwright based in Amsterdam.

I work on participatory theatre where the idea is that I have a script that is performed by audience members who have never seen the script before. The scripts are comedic, grotesque, and surreal, with the hope that the readers will break character as they try to say out loud the insane things I've written for them. I am also a character whose role is to provide stage directions.

I continue to write these plays in order to serve the communities I cherish and to decentralize theatre. It's a language spoken by all of us, and it shouldn't be restricted by "professionals". I also love providing my theatre for free or on donation. After all, it's for the people, by the people!

events.title: GUILTY-PLEASURE WOMEN…
events.description:
La Prudon & Annie Jan behind the decks, playing the cheesiest hits, guilty pleasures, and deliciously catchy sing-alongs.

The Bard's Horn
Thursday | 8:00 PM – 10:00 PM
Seriously awesome stuff. No shame.
So get moving, darling... see you on the dancefloor!

events.title: Purple Flame
events.description:
PURPLE FLAME IS A FEMINIST DJ, PRODUCER, AND MULTIMEDIA ARTIST FROM TBILISI, GEORGIA. ALONGSIDE PLAYING MUSIC, SHE IS A HUMAN RIGHTS ACTIVIST, ACTIVELY ADVOCATES FOR EQUAL RIGHTS, OFTEN INCORPORATING HER ACTIVIST MESSAGES INTO HER SETS AND USING MUSIC AS A PLATFORM FOR SOCIAL CHANGE.
 SHE ALSO EXPLORES THE HEALING POTENTIAL OF AUDIO FREQUENCIES, PRACTICING SOUND HEALING THROUGH HER COLLABORATIVE PROJECT, ‘’FORSUN’’.
 AS A MEMBER OF THE MULTICULTURAL COMMUNITY AND RECORD LABEL DOWNTEMPO RITUALS, PURPLE FLAME PERFORMS AT GIGS AND FESTIVALS THROUGHOUT GEORGIA AND ACROSS EUROPE YEAR-ROUND.

events.title: The Elements Fellowship
events.description:
The Elements Fellowship is more than just a band – it's a living expression of global sounds. A kaleidoscope of music, stories, and connection, where cultures, rhythms, and hearts come together in improvisation.

Their performances are intuitive, immersive, and unforgettable. They don't just entertain — they transform.

events.title: Rave Baby - Sandra Kaas
events.description:
Sandra Kaas is a surrealist costume and prop designer, and she has now created her first theatrical show.

"Rave Baby" is an absurdist puppet theatre performance about a pregnant woman who fears that motherhood will mean the end of her rave life.

events.title: Mantra Kirtan
events.description:
Matthieu and Marlies invite you on a chanting journey!

Come join us for the opening evening of our wonderful festival… A good start is half the battle!
Inspiration and fun will go hand-in-hand this evening in the magical tea garden... See you there!

events.title: Ctd Movement
events.description:
CTD presents a Spiritual Hip Hop showcase where music, awareness, and community come together.

From spoken word to rap, from soulful melodies to powerful beats: young artists share their stories about identity, personal growth, love, social change, and the search for meaning.
Inspired by the idea that we are all connected, this showcase invites you to listen, feel, and connect. Expect honest lyrics, fresh energy, and artists who use hip hop as a means of awareness, connection, and change.
A performance that not only makes you move, but also inspires something within you.

events.title: Kiss My Knoblauch
events.description: Naked slugs, cockroaches, ketchup, and toxic armpits presented by Fishra (she/her) and B.Ruiz (they/them) in a stew of gothic utopias and pungent criticism. Expect fast-paced words over heavy beats and catchy synth tunes with trumpet flourishes. A wake-up call to shake you awake, a lullaby to soothe you. Keep your eyes moist, keep your fists clenched.

events.title: DAF - Dutch Acid Family
events.description:
Thursday
 21:00-01:00
 Electro Magnes
 Electro Magnes is a Polish electronic duo formed by composer, producer, and multi-instrumentalist Rafał Kassaraba and vocalist-songwriter Bashka Preen. Blending live bass, electronic textures, and expressive vocals, they create atmospheric soundscapes that move between experimental electronica, ambient music, and intimate storytelling.
 Based in the foothills of the Karkonosze Mountains, the duo draws inspiration from nature, human connection, and inner transformation. Rafał's musical journey spans folk-blues, punk-rock, electro-punk, and film music projects, bringing a rich and distinctive depth to their sound.
 Bashka writes and performs in English, influenced by the years she spent living in London and its vibrant music scene. Her original songs have also been featured in film productions.
 Together, Electro Magnes invites audiences into a cinematic and immersive musical experience where electronic experimentation meets emotion, reflection, and heart.

Friday
 Sunshine Reggae
 15:00-16:30 Hobbol
 16:30-18:00 Open the gate sound
 18:00-19:30 Loddy Culture & Lyrical Benji
 19:30-21:00 Selector Depender
 21:00-22:30 Sun Collective…

events.title: The Bard’s Horn
events.description:
Thursday:
 21.00 till 01.00 DJs Archangel, Graham B, JP and Ambient Daan
 Friday:
 16.00 to 20.00 with storytellers Simone Engeln, Daan P., Belinda with musical accompaniment by Fons, musical interlude by sitar player Dougal
 20.00 to 21.00 Monty Pagan, a delightful Amsterdam folk rock band
 21.00 till 01.00 DJs Archangel, Graham B, JP and Ambient Daan
 Saturday:
 16.00 to 20.00 with storytellers Simone Engeln, Daan P., Belinda with musical accompaniment by Fons, musical interlude by sitar player Dougal
 20.00 to 21.00 Monty Pagan, a delightful Amsterdam folk rock band
 21.00 till late DJs Archangel, Graham B, JP and Ambient Daan
 Sunday:
 15.00 to 18.00 ParaNoya and friends with poetry and art
 Simone Engeln (formerly known as Simone Angel) is a versatile storyteller whose life itself is a story. After spending twenty years in the jungle of Belize, she returned to the Netherlands in 2023. Her adventurous life began at the age of 18; under the international spotlight of television…

events.title: DJ Babette
events.description:
Emerging from the queer underground - home base Vrankrijk - DJ Babette (who just entered his 50s) discovered his passion for DJing quite recently. Hellbent on squeezing every last drop out of his midlife crisis, his sets carry that extra punch of urgency of someone arriving a lil’ late to the party.
 Babette chases after bouncy, captivating grooves on the house/tech house/indie spectrum, infused with funk, latin, disco, homosexualism and elusive, quirky and euphoric twists. You’ll be going places for sure!

events.title: Get Painted
events.description: Let’s experience this first Landjuweel night in style! Two of our most experienced make up experts are waiting for you in the Rietreat to turn your face into a version of yourself that cannot wait to meet you!

events.title: Landjuweel Temple Space
events.description:
Amidst the electric chaos of the festival, a sacred portal awaits. Welcome to Temple Space: a sanctuary designed to pause the buzz and ignite your inner world.

Ready for a journey inward? Embark on a breathtaking journey through your senses and desires, anchored by deep somatic movement and connective practices. This is your playground to claim what you want, honour your boundaries, and speak your truth through authentic, heart-centred communication. Bring your light, your shadow, and everything in between—you are completely welcome here, bound only by the safety and boundaries of those around you. Check in with our Temple Gate Keepers at the threshold to receive the container guidelines before you step inside.

Temple Space is located in the largest yurt on the Natural High grounds. Temple hours: Thursday 21.00-24.00, Friday 20.00-23.00 and Sunday 20.00-23.00.
Landjuweel’s Temple Space is curated by Iskra Bela and inspired by temple arts culture, contemporary dance, ritual-performance practice and ecstatic dance. Invited co-creators this year are Lexmy van den Boogaard and Bee-bee (Bregje).

Lexmy van den Boogaard…

events.title: The Unemployed
events.description:
The Unemployed is an Amsterdam-based classic-rock band with a recognizable blend of influences from the 70s/80s, vocal harmonies, and guitar solos. Think The Faces, Herman Brood, and The Black Crowes. After their debut album, Slightly Tipped (2021), the band, in a revamped lineup, is working on new material and are ready to take the stage again.

In a world full of overproduced beats, they have a clear mission: to play as much as possible, to involve the audience in their energy, and to show that rock 'n' roll is far from dead.

events.title: HAN Here And Now
events.description:
HAN Here And Now is the musical project of singer and multi-instrumentalist Han van ’t Land. He plays world folk music, inspired by nature, (spiritual) journeys, and social themes. With his band, he creates music that moves between meditative stillness and powerful, energetic rhythms, built from acoustic and tribal sounds. Performances combine original songs with live looping and intuitive musical expression. The band consists of multi-instrumentalist Han van ’t Land, (bass) bassist Lucas Dols, and percussionist and handpan player Hashem Kabreet.

Links:
Website
Instagram

events.title: DJ Witte Aap
events.description: Not a DJ, but a music selector playing wonderful tunes from all over the world!

events.title: DJ Steve Kennedy
events.description: Wanna dance, feel happy, feel sexy, move your hips and put your hands up high? Then come and check out Steve’s groovy, sexy, uplifting, soulful DJ set! ❤️ #love #dance #techhouse #techno

events.title: Fire & Cacao Circle
events.description:
Opening Ceremony Thursday
 We gather around the fire to open this celebration of Life ~ sharing the sacred medicine of the heart (Kakaw) and becoming one same song and prayer as we share our offerings and gratitude to the Earth and the land.
 Closing Ceremony Sunday
 We gather around the fire to close this celebration of Life ~ sharing the sacred medicine of the heart (Kakaw) and becoming one same song and prayer as we share our offerings and gratitude to the Earth and the land.
 Bios of all the facilitators:
 Aliix Balam, Human. Earth Guardian. Remembering and bridging ancient practices into this time-space. His path as an ethnobiologist led to scientific work with the FAO/UN on ecosystem approach to fisheries management, which evolved into over a decade serving alongside Mayan communities on permaculture, agroecology, and food sovereignty. Today, Aliix serves as a holistic educator, facilitator, natural builder, weaver, dreamer and visionary at Winaq ~ School of Life and Ancestral Remembrance at Lake Atitlán, Guatemala
 Pim Grinter is a sustainable textile designer whose work is rooted in plant wisdom through an animistic lens.…

events.title: Victor Sams on Ngoni
events.description:
Victor Sams, a familiar name in Ruigoord, is returning for this Landjuweel edition with a solo performance. The focus will be on the enchanting sound of the ngoni, an ancient West African string instrument. Through his playing and singing, Victor brings to life the rich musical tradition of this unique instrument.

An intimate concert where music, inspiration, and craftsmanship come together, featuring warm melodies, captivating rhythms, and the magical sounds of the ngoni.

events.title: The Perculators
events.description:
Meet the Perculators, 
 This Rotterdam based 4-piece blends the soul of numerous genres into one instrumental boiling pot of funky grooves. They’ll jump from classic 60’s surf and Peruvian Cumbia to Fat funky Hiphop. Come see them play and you’ll be longing to take something home to keep your record player occupied!

events.title: Wormhol DJ
events.description: Wormhol’s (aka JAMIE NEE) first love is ITALO DISCO so you can expect nothing less. Goofy moves, happy faces, purple and green; bring it and boogie to the hysterical synthy sounds of the 80’s. However along the way we travel towards an unkown destination and we can end up in an unexpected musical dimension, just follow the WORM. Since years Jamie is involved whit Ruigoord in many corners, making art pieces, building areas, DjJ-ing at the legendary Balonnenfeest and working together with different Ruigoorders, now very delighted to play for the first time in the Boogie Bos.

events.title: DJ FeelofSofie
events.description:
FeelofSofie combines the warm, earthy sounds of organic house with the rebellious spirit of indie dance and the deep rhythms of progressive house. She carefully selects her intuitive melodies and captivating basslines. Let her take you on a journey without a destination, but one that you are guaranteed to enjoy.

Sofie has a warm, open, and curious personality. Although she is generally calm, you can feel her energy come alive when she's passionate about something. During her DJ sets, there is often a special atmosphere: an exchange of energy with the audience that you must experience yourself. Her sensitivity is one of her greatest strengths; she uses it to connect with the audience, create a sense of community, and invite them on a shared journey. For Sofie, music is one of the most beautiful ways to explore life at a deeper level. Music opens doors for her that would otherwise remain closed. 'Feel-of-Sofie' is a philosophy.

events.title: Dj Deun Legal Alien
events.description:
Part of the DJ collective Legal Aliens.
 Time for a party!

events.title: Fooi
events.description: Fooi is an Amsterdam-based rock band that plays like a great evening at the pub: unpredictable, fun, sometimes vulnerable and raw. Since June 2024, the 5 friends have been meeting in the secret studio under the cafe where they first met.

events.title: Earth Garden
events.description:
Amidst the performances, music and encounters, the Earth Garden offers a place to listen, connect to the earth, create and simply be.
 The Earth Garden Crew invites you to become more than a visitor. Spend time with the earth, create a gift from and/or for the earth with your hands, enjoy a nourishing açaí bowl and a cup of homemade blue Clitoria tea, and be carried by live music throughout the morning,...
 Thursday | 22:00 – 00:00
 *The Absinth Bar*
 Tableau Vivant & Live Performance
 Step into a surreal world where myth, ritual and imagination meet. Join us for an intimate late-night experience in the Earth Garden.
 Friday, Saturday & Sunday | 12:00 – 15:00
 Throughout the morning, accompanied by live music, you are invited to join:
 * *The Collective Clayground* – An artwork, shaped by everyone who joins. No ownership, only one piece growing in the hands of many. Connect with the earth, with each other and with your self..
 * *Gift Shop – *What Is Your Gift?** – This is not a place to buy clothes, but to exchange gifts. Find a unique festival piece and offer something in return—not money, but a talent, a kind gesture, your time or a skill.…

events.title: The Rise, Life and Death of Don Quijote
events.description:
Thijs van de Loo – Theatermaker & Hessel Pijnaker, artist; combine their talents to bring five dolls to life, revolving around the theme of metamorphosis.

“Using rattan, fabric, inner tubes and found objects, and a good dose of intuition, we create soulful objects. Both have studied Vodun religions such as Ubuntu and draw inspiration from them.”

events.title: Bliz Nochi (live)
events.description:
Bliz Nochi grew up in Moldova, born into a musician family, surrounded by the rich cultural traditions of Eastern Europe, where music was a constant part of everyday life. Now based in Hamburg, Germany, he draws on these early influences, blending them with his deep connection to nature and his curiosity about the emotional power of sound.

Through his musical style, Bliz Nochi brings together electronic beats and traditional folk sounds from around the world. His music is less about following trends and more about telling stories – stories that move both the body and the spirit, evoking the energy of a festival at sunrise or the joy of dancing with loved ones.

Whether playing live or blending genres in a hybrid DJ set, Bliz Nochi creates spaces where people can connect, reflect, and celebrate.

Over the years, Bliz Nochi has had the opportunity to perform at a wide range of venues and festivals, from Amsterdam Dance Event (NL) and Scorpios Mykonos (GR) to Fusion Festival (DE) and intimate spaces like Golden Pudel Club and Südpol (DE).

events.title: JPattersson
events.description:
Hips sway on waves, feet bounce to pumping beats in the sand, imagination dives into dubby depths for shimmering synthesizer pearls. Suddenly, the sound of a trumpet breaks through the stereo panorama like sunbeams through a dense canopy. Welcome to the world of JPATTERSSON.
 In this world, genre boundaries are not limiting but liberating: Driven by hypnotic rhythms, powerful basslines and melancholic harmonies, JPATTERSSON crafted a unique style that is both progressive and diverse. Some tracks pulse with latin heat, while others flirt with jazz. Always dope and extremely danceable. Then there’s his singing voice – raw, soulful and energetic – delivering smart lyrics over catchy melodies and electronic patterns.
 Step into JPATTERSSON’s universe and you’ll embark on a journey filled with elevating joy and emotional depth. Here, music speaks a universal truth that connects us all.
 Let go and dance!

events.title: Djembefola's
events.description:
Momo Touré

Momo Touré is the heart and soul of the drum circle at Ruigoord. For over 30 years, he has been the person who brings people together through music, using his djembe, his energy, and his gift for making everyone, from beginners to advanced players, feel part of the groove.

His roots are deeply embedded in traditional West African music. As a young man, he toured worldwide with the renowned African ballet in Guinea. He still carries this richness with him every week at Ruigoord.

Come, see, and participate!

Amier Sloot

Amier Sloot believes in the power of rhythm to bring people together. As a musician and community builder, he facilitates a drum circle every Saturday at Ruigoord alongside Momo, where everyone, regardless of experience, can immerse themselves in a shared groove and be fully present in the moment.

During Landjuweel, they (along with many other respected drummers) bring this energy: connection through music, together in the moment.

events.title: The Underbouncers
events.description: The Underbouncers tunes are rooted in exploratory drum and bass, mystical melodies and funky rhythms, and combines them with ethnic drum and spirit-channeling sounds. As the two of us have been co-involved with methods and systems of analysis of the underground as mycologists and biologist, we try to channel our own underground knowledge, taking inspiration from global sounds that do the same. Biology and mycology as professions, and drums and basses as hobby. These common grounds come together and allow for creative experimentation with waves and frequencies and bring esotery through sounds.

events.title: SUNN
events.description:
Musical hope, unity, and a rich palette of instruments.

SUNN brings a live performance that tells a story. A story about what we have experienced together, about the rising sun as a symbol of a new beginning, and about an explosion of energy. With a unique mix of acoustic and electronic elements, they create an immersive sound where passion is central. An intense musical experience that connects, touches, and inspires movement.

events.title: Gente Quente
events.description:
Gente Quente is a Dutch DJ who effortlesslly weavesrhythm, passion and style into everything she creates and shares.
 Her sets are vibrant and uplifting, blending soulful, melodic sounds with percussion-driven rhtyhms. Rooted in Afrohouse and deephouse, her sound is rich with African-inspired grooves, warm Brazilian influences and deep and flowing house and gospel elements.
 Hard to box in, yet recognizable Gente Quente, defined by her signature blend of influences.

events.title: Doris Fiore
events.description: Doris Fiore blends vintage underground (deep)house with fresh electronic sounds. Inspired and driven by her roots in drumming and love for jazz, funk and soul, her rhythm-focused music is deep, straight-forward, soulful, edgy and sensual. With her sets she takes the listener to a warm and rooted feeling. With house as her main genre, she ventures into related styles, always keeping an analogue, human touch.

events.title: Uitzendbureau
events.description: The agency is currently causing chaos in Amsterdam. More specifically, the group around frontman Valdo has even established its own venue at Paradiso. Unfiltered, extraordinary riot music: screaming about the queen, dystopia, and the established order. Sounds excellent. No need to do anything further.

events.title: Duin
events.description:
Duin is a poetic performance featuring nude models filmed up close on stage. These images are projected live and accompanied by cellist Tjalle Rens, creating a transformative and mesmerizing landscape.

Sam van Zoest is a filmmaker and writer. He draws his inspiration from the relationship between humans and nature.

events.title: Bring the Joints
events.description: Bring The Joints is a hip-hop band that combines rap and live instrumentation to create its own unique sound. With the powerful flows of rapper/guitarist Ahih Jaihi, deep bass lines from Fretless Jane, energetic drums, the colorful keys of Reezus, and warm sounds from saxophonist Robert, the band creates dynamic live experiences full of character and musical freedom.

events.title: Esta Polyesta
events.description:
Esta Polyesta is a DJ and music producer known for blending Global Beats, House, Electronic, and World Music, creating uplifting and funky sets that merge ethnic rhythms with electronic sounds.

Her style is flowing, feminine, and fun, always aiming to guide her audience to a shared peak experience. She chooses music that connects cultures and fosters collective joy through dance, reflecting her belief that "Dance is a celebration and a ritual." As a pioneer in ethnic house and Ecstatic Dance, she blends ancestral, acoustic, and world sounds with electronic beats.

Since starting her career in 2005, she has performed at major festivals and venues worldwide. She also collaborates with DJ Mashti as Polyesta, producing popular remixes like Oum's Lik, which has over 7 million plays on Spotify.
 Links:
 Soundcloud 
 Listen to Ecstatic Dance sets: [Soundcloud] 
 Video on Ecstatic Dance [Youtube] 
 Video on Ecstatic and Trance Dance [Youtube]

events.title: DJ Y∆RL b2b Kahmeya
events.description:
DJ YARL
 YARL, also known as JF.HECTOR, is an Amsterdam-based DJ operating in the space between electronic club music and ritual movement. His sets unfold slowly and with intention, blending organic percussion, global influences, and electronic grooves into long-form movement journeys that support release rather than spectacle. Drawing from a background in film music and underground club culture, YARL brings a strong sense of pacing, atmosphere, and narrative into his sets. He plays for dancers, not for attention, with a focus on flow, depth, patience, and shared moments of euphoria. YARL performs at events such as Ambrosia, Free Flow, Maankaravaan, Landjuweel and Ecstatic Dance Festival Holland. Under his alias JF.HECTOR, he explores a deeper club-oriented sound rooted in hypnotic techno and leftfield house, with appearances at Radion, Maanzin Ruigoord, Radio Radio, Sober Clubbing and The Muddshow.
 Kahmeya
 Kahmeya is a Dutch DJ, producer and vocalist with a sound that moves through indie dance, organic house, tribal grooves and live vocals.…

events.title: Dj Fred Legal Alien
events.description:
Part of the DJ collective Legal Aliens.
 Time for a party!

events.title: L.A. Sagne
events.description:
L.A. SAGNE. It’s energetic, it’s intense, it’s passionate, and when it starts playing, it makes everything move.

Are you still allowed to say in an official biography that a band is just a group of friends? No. But you don't need to either. You can hear it in every song. Friends? Yes. But the kind you wouldn’t want showing up unannounced at your mom’s birthday party.
 How they met is none of your business. Just be glad they did.
 Or not, if you don’t like punk.
 Anyway! 2025 was a good year for L.A. Sagne. Festivals like Eurosonic/Noorderslag (NL), Trans Musicales (FR) and Best Kept Secret (NL), a club tour in the Netherlands and Belgium and many other places and people all got what they deserved. Three booking agencies (Friendly Fire – NL, Live Nation – BE, and Persona Grata – FR) joined in. And as you read this, the band just finished recording their debut album 'Good Company' which will be out on March 27th 2026 accompanied by a club tour through The Netherlands and Belgium.…

events.title: Jam Session with the Improvinators
events.description: Jamsession every night after the program on the Rebel Stage until festival's closing time. The jamsessions here are legendary. We have a group of very good musicians carrying the jam. Tell musicians to come and join us. Please don't just jump on stage first contact the session leader.

events.title: Lowfire
events.description:
music that moves like wind over dunes; scratching guitars and ethereal voices that turns horizons into dreamscapes.
 Located in Amsterdam, they get their sound from the clash between city life and cold seas, dunes, and misty meadows. They live like hibrids, looking into another dimension. Lowfire, right on the intersection; the end of a big flame and the start of a new one. You feel the smolthering heat, playing through their chords and melodies.

events.title: Soul Safari
events.description:
Crafting immersive journeys through ambient, downtempo and organic electronic music, Soul Safari explores the space between sound, atmosphere and emotion.
 Based in the self-built studio ‘The Mycelium Cave’, surrounded by nature and community, Soul Safari weaves atmospheric textures, organic rhythms and cinematic depth into evolving stories. Moving through stillness, vast synthesizer landscapes and deep grooves, each set unfolds organically, inviting listeners to slow down, reconnect and become fully present in the unfolding moment.
 Ambient • Downtempo • Organic Electronica

events.title: Ippy
events.description: Ippy is trippy. If you don't know Ippy, you are not a hippie.

events.title: Abelix
events.description:
"He absorbed a magical elixir, infused with countless musical styles. When he starts to play, the elixir takes control."
 For many years, ABELIX has been active in the underground Hip Hop and rave scene, playing everywhere from squats and warehouse parties to live beat sessions and freestyle rap events. Driven by a curiosity for diverse sounds and rhythms, he has developed an eclectic style that seamlessly blends genres and cultures on the dancefloor.
 His sets move effortlessly between Afrobeat, global grooves, breakbeats, UK bass, and jungle, creating journeys that are both energetic and unpredictable.
 In recent years, ABELIX has been sharing his wide-ranging selections in cafés and intimate venues across the city. He is a resident DJ at Café Belgique, Heilig Leven, and Checkpoint Charlie, performing both solo and as one half of the duo Follow The Tracks together with Comcap.
 Expect a genre-spanning trip guided by the mysterious elixir itself.

events.title: DJ Captain Spank b2b DJ MacGyver 2.0
DJ JP
events.description:
DJ JP is a DJ from Amsterdam who has felt connected to Ruigoord since 1984. “My first major experience was intense; the contrast with city life was even greater then.
 Here at Ruigoord, you not only connect with nature and art from the numerous studio homes, but also with yourself in a spiritual way that was unheard of in the Netherlands at the time.
 This allowed me to see things differently and place them in a new perspective, which has shaped me as a person and as a musician/DJ into who I am today. In 1984, the area was very large, and the stages were far apart, which made the experience even more impressive, especially at night due to the limited lighting.
 I was surrounded by old and new friends, many of whom are still active at Ruigoord.
 And then the sun rose in all its glory, warming us with a positive energy that has never left me. That's why it is always special for me to come back here.” DJ JP as a member of the renowned EVLO/LOVE and High Quality crew…

events.title: Kayva van Gangelen & Piet Jan Blauw
events.description:
For the Landjuweel Festival 2026, Kayva van Gangelen and Piet Jan Blauw are joining forces in a unique artistic collaboration. While Blauw combines electronic soundscapes with projected images and immersive atmospheres, Van Gangelen brings her own, refined sonic world as a hobois player. Together, they present an atmospheric ambient set where organic sounds, improvisation, and stillness come together.

Their performance explores the space between music, image, and imagination. The warm tones of the hobo intertwine with atmospheric electronic layers and subtle visual projections, creating a meditative experience that invites reflection, wonder, and connection.
Kayva van Gangelen
Hobo player Kayva van Gangelen can be heard on Leonard Cohen's posthumously released album "Thanks for the Dance." As a musician, performer, and maker, she has been working at the intersection of experiment, improvisation, and contemporary music for years. She founded the Dutch ensemble Perforator, played with The Kyteman Orchestra, and is a member of the Berlin ensemble Stargaze.…

events.title: Zibabu
events.description: Zibabu is a space punk adventure through the multiverse. A bizarre extraterrestrial duo that navigates between jazz, hardcore, metal, punk, psychedelic rock, hip hop, and more. They are engaged in an endless battle against evil and decay. Two aliens crash onto the stage in the church of Ruigoord. Don't come looking for peace and serenity; instead, find an indescribable ball of energy that never stops bouncing.

events.title: Sem Kramer & Cherry
events.description:
Cherry Sour (vocals), Sem Kramer (piano) and Mark Nieuwenhuis (trumpet) take over the Salon with their musicality.
 Sem and Mark open with an improvised set, fusing classical and jazz sounds. Cherry then brings songs about life, love, and everything in between. Expect to be taken away by their musicality.

events.title: Dj Chaakra Legal Alien
events.description: Jacques Povee is a music producer, promoter, and DJ known as DJ Chaakra. More than 25 years ago, while working as an environmental activist in Groenoord, he moved to Ruigoord to protect the village from the construction of the port. Together with more than 100 activists, he successfully halted the port's development in collaboration with various residents of Ruigoord. He is also known as the founder of Trance Orient Express and Solstice Festival.

events.title: B2B Solame vs FTGRL
events.description:
FTGRL and Solame, will take you to the promissed land and over the rainbow high in the sky. Expect funny, fast and hard dance music that will put a smile on your face, but will shake your body into pieces.
 Genres: Latin-Tek, Gabber, Hardcore, Hyperpop

events.title: Kaje Row
events.description:
Through Melodies and harmonies Tales unfold, with triumph and strife.
 Through Bass and Drums, Reality holds.
 To guide the unseen. Embrace vulnerability. Self-discovery beckons.

events.title: Night Drumming
events.description:
Feel free to bring your rhythm to life with Nightdrummers. Bring your shaker, tambourine, djembe, or other instrument and join us in the groove.

We create a welcoming Drum Circle where improvisation, connection, and playfulness are central. Listen, respond, experiment, and be amazed by what emerges.
Whether you've been playing for years or are simply curious: there's space for everyone. Bring your friends along, come groove, dance, and let the night resonate with our shared heartbeat.
Everyone is welcome. Together, we create the rhythm.

events.title: yak x mari posa
events.description: yak x mari posa are on a mission to reconnect with the dance floor. live percussion riding a wild wave of progressive trance, caribbean bouyon and breaky electronics. expect moving bodies all around.

events.title: Kids' Camping
events.description:
Friday 09.00-13.00 Opening Ceremony & Crafting
 Creating mandalas and altars using natural materials
 Making insect masks
 Saturday 09.00-13.00 Opening Ceremony & Movement
 Building insect houses from natural materials
 Wayang Kulit workshop (shadow puppets)
 Sunday 09.00-13.00 Opening Ceremony with soft music
 Storytelling
 Making instruments
 Ongoing program (all days): Creating costumes from cardboard and paper for the parade

 Facilitators:
 Luis Croes
 Guides children's camps and provides creative activities, handpan and creates a pleasant atmosphere.
 Iris van Wijhe
 A versatile and musically-inclined individual with a great passion for mindfulness.
 Iris enjoys leading workshops where children can playfully discover, create, move, and relax.
 "I prefer to create spaces where imagination is encouraged, where messy hands are seen as a good thing, and where children can playfully explore and be themselves."
 We invite the children to experiment, collaborate, and simply be themselves. Because it is when there is space for freedom and wonder that the most beautiful creations arise...

events.title: Morning Yoga · The Five Elements Form
events.description:
Want do you want smooth hips, flowing energy and a smile on your face?

During this standing yoga flow, Esmee Jiskoot will guide you through the 5 Elements Form: a series of twenty fluid yoga movements that may remind you of Qigong. The movements help to release tension, deepen your breathing and bring body and mind back into balance.
The flow moves like a moving meditation: soft, powerful and surprisingly energetic all at once. Your body comes alive, your mind finds peace and you feel more present in the moment.
Whether you are an experienced yogi or taking your first yoga class, this practice invites you to slow down, feel and reconnect with yourself. A wonderful reset during the festival, that will leave you grounded, energized and smiling.
Get to know Esmee Jiskoot
Esmee Jiskoot is a yoga teacher and body-oriented coach. With warmth, enthusiasm and years of experience, she guides people towards more relaxation, body awareness and life energy.
In her classes, movement, breathing and personal development come together naturally…

events.title: Coco Coquelicot story telling
events.description:
Passionate, energetic and fearless: Coco Coquelicot's unique mix of club sounds combines warm grooves with powerful basslines. As a true music enthusiast, Coco delves deep into researching the best tracks from all musical corners. Her sets are infused with her commitment to fighting for equality. By giving marginalized voices a platform, she provides a safe haven for everyone who doesn't always have one. As co-founder of Relate Radio and organizer of Butts, the highly acclaimed queer club night in East, Coco actively promotes the progress of the Groningen music scene. Her eclectic influences come together in a rich and percussive mix that resonates through your body.
In 2023, Coco became part of Hit the North (ESNS) under the guidance of Isis van der Wel. She received a nomination for the Groninger Culture Prize and has already performed at Lowlands, Wildeburg, IsBurning, Milkshake, Eurosonic and ADE, DGTL.

events.title: Bakfietsband
events.description:
The cargo bike band embodies joyful activism at its finest. This mobile stage brings a smile to your face and transforms the street into a dance floor. Come join us for some fun or participate with this cheerful group.

The cargo bike band plays 3 times a day, in the afternoon, evening, and night. There is also a daily guest performance, with a group from South America, AP Mayor, performing on Saturday.

events.title: Earth Garden
events.description:
Amidst the performances, music and encounters, the Earth Garden offers a place to listen, connect to the earth, create and simply be.
 The Earth Garden Crew invites you to become more than a visitor. Spend time with the earth, create a gift from and/or for the earth with your hands, enjoy a nourishing açaí bowl and a cup of homemade blue Clitoria tea, and be carried by live music throughout the morning,...
