 Thursday | 22:00 – 00:00
 *The Absinth Bar*
 Tableau Vivant & Live Performance
 Step into a surreal world where myth, ritual and imagination meet. Join us for an intimate late-night experience in the Earth Garden.
 Friday, Saturday & Sunday | 12:00 – 15:00
 Throughout the morning, accompanied by live music, you are invited to join:
 * *The Collective Clayground* – An artwork, shaped by everyone who joins. No ownership, only one piece growing in the hands of many. Connect with the earth, with each other and with your self..
 * *Gift Shop – *What Is Your Gift?** – This is not a place to buy clothes, but to exchange gifts. Find a unique festival piece and offer something in return—not money, but a talent, a kind gesture, your time or a skill.…

events.title: Poppenkastvoorstelling
events.description: During the "Landjuweel" (a Dutch festival), puppet shows will be performed at Ruigoord 16. The start times will be announced on a sign outside. The afternoon performances are suitable for all ages.

events.title: Lizard Yoga
events.description:
A grounded journey from the egg to the scaly lizard basking in the Sun.
 Unmesh Joshi, Yoga Practitioner for 15+ years
 Bioplastic costumes with sustainable decorations: Lara Wijnker and Mees Muijs.

events.title: Ámika
events.description:
Ámika is an Amsterdam-based DJ, producer, and songwriter with Balkan roots, known for weaving organic house, tribal rhythms, and melodic textures into immersive musical journeys.
 Drawing inspiration from folklore, ritual, and nature, her sets move between deep percussion, hypnotic grooves, and atmospheric storytelling. Blending organic house, melodic electronica, and world influences, she creates a sound that feels both grounded and expansive.
 As a producer and vocalist, Ámika brings a unique sensitivity to the dance floor, approaching DJing not only as a performance but as a form of emotional storytelling.
 Her sets invite listeners into a space where movement, presence, and connection meet, balancing earthy rhythms with moments of reflection and release.

events.title: Contribute to building the growing "Insect Village" – The Plant Kingdom!
events.description:
Donna and Mariana explore the realms of creativity, nature, and play. With a focus on what emerges in the moment, they invite children to collaborate, investigate, and unleash their imaginations.

Throughout the festival, we will collectively build a world. A playful exploration of what "home" means for each of us.

The World in Becoming - The Plant Kingdom
From the landscape that has already emerged, let's further develop the village. We will create paths, bridges, plant structures, and green meeting spaces where runners and jumpers can move freely. The village becomes increasingly shaped, connected, and alive.
Part of a three-part workshop
events.venue: Village Square · A World in the Making

events.title: Kloek
events.description:
KLOEK. An interactive street theatre performance. Or, to put it another way: A robust, mobile installation that only comes to life on the streets with the help of brave passersby…

Back to basics. KLOEK as pure installation theatre. Nothing hidden, nothing embellished. Robust structures, basic mechanics. Simple and transparent. The timeless power of simplicity.

Pure. Steel and wood. Nature and human strength. Strong hands of both adults and children pull on the ropes to make the sound instruments play and the puppet fly. Free as a bird.

A bold and subtle interplay between man and machine creates visual poetry with muscle power. Both intimate and exuberant, it stimulates imagination and makes fantasy a tangible reality. KLOEK.

events.title: The soft landing
events.description: We start the day with a joint opening ceremony on the village square. A moment to come together, meet each other, and settle into the day calmly. With music, movement, and creativity, we discover what the daily theme has to tell us. What adventures, questions, and surprises await us today?

events.title: The soft landing
events.description: We start the day with a joint opening ceremony on the village square. A moment to come together, meet each other, and settle into the day calmly. With music, movement, and creativity, we discover what the daily theme has to tell us. What adventures, questions, and surprises await us today?

events.title: Woven Voices
events.description:
Woven Voices originated from love. Love for bringing people together and giving everyone the space to express themselves. What remains is a beautiful work of art.

Weaving Our Shared Visions Together
"Woven Voices," the proud creation of Lex Empress, begins on the first day of Landjuweel. Each day, participants are invited to write intentions, dreams, and visions for our future, for the children, and for the world. These words are painted onto cotton and woven together into one collective carpet. This is a living artwork created from many voices becoming one. Anyone can join and become part of this shared creation. By the end of the festival, the carpet stands as a physical memory of our united prayers and possibilities.

events.title: The Art Jewel on Friday
events.description:
Curators / host Q&A: Bo van der Meer & Roswitha de Boer - Zuiderveen

New at the Landjuweel Festival! A fine selection of Dutch documentaries that capture the essence of artistry, community, identity, and freedom. Step into the church, put on your headphones, open your eyes, and immerse yourself in the stories that breathe the free spirit of Ruigoord. Including Q&A with the filmmakers: from up-and-coming talent to established names. Friday, July 24 | 12:30 - 13:30

All Backwards (13 min)
Two performance artists explore the fear of the unknown by walking backwards for 600 kilometers on the Camino de Santiago, the famous pilgrimage route in northern Spain.
Afterword with directors Merette van Hijfte and Samuel van Keeken

Bewogen (22 min)
Our body is a treasure chest full of memories. Three dancers bring memories of home, freedom, and loss to life through dance, music, and words.
Q&A with director Noa Zevenbergen

First time (7 min)
We experience the new life of a 5-year-old girl. The experience is captured in a poetic way, allowing us to see the nuances in emotions that we normally miss.…

events.title: Dansjewel : Inphiknight
events.description:
Dansjewel offers a raw and intense take on ecstatic dance. A playful adventure full of contrasts: light-hearted yet profound, playful and adventurous, but also rooted in ancient traditions. Dive into the Kosmies Lek and let your dancing flow! Immerse yourself in the endless source of music, and allow the rising tones to resonate deep within you, shaping your inner self and bringing you to life! Break down the waves of sound into a prism of movement and transform yourself into a true Dansjewel.

Eccentric, eclectic, and ecstatic: DJ INPHIKNIGHT, or Thomas Meijer, weaves tapestries of music spun from his extraordinary and idiosyncratic collection of curious records from all directions. Authentic, classic, futuristic music brought together in a frenzy with deep-vibrating beats and jazzy improvisations.

Links:
Instagram
Soundcloud
Website

events.title: Origin Circle
events.description: We gather every morning to remember our shared origins on this Earth, connecting with the medicine of the Heart of the Earth (Kakaw) and opening the day for sharing our intentions, songs and prayers.

events.title: Bouke & Co · Free Clothes — Take Some, Bring Some
events.description:
Can you do better? Or are you just suffering from shopping urges? Come and enjoy FREE CLOTHING at Bouke & Co.

Cool clothes and accessories for free! New items every day! Unwanted styling advice included.
Feel free to bring your unwanted wardrobe too!
Everything is ️FREE️. Daily from 13:00-21:00 at Area 46.

events.title: Hoop Workshop by Iris Dam Hoops
events.description: You can do a lot more with a hulahoop than hoop it around your waist. In this workshop you will learn some hooptricks, and tie them together in a little sequence. Come discover how to dance and flow with a hoop, fun guaranteed! Suitable for all levels.

events.title: Shamanic Healing Journey
events.description:
Peter Moerkens developed this workshop out of his deep desire to help people reconnect with their true selves. As a healer, shaman, and coach, he creates a warm, safe space where participants can relax, feel, and open themselves to the unknown. His work combines shamanistic rituals, trance journeys, sound healing, and Reconnective Healing into a powerful inner journey. What drives him is seeing people experience more peace, space, light, and connection after the workshop – as if something ancient can be soothed and something essential within them can be awakened again. Peter has received initiations from Qúero Priest and Shaman Nicolas Pauccar Calcina II as Earth Keeper, and he is initiated into the lineage of Qúero Shamans from the high mountains of Cusco, Peru.

Shamanic Healing Journey:
“Come completely relax, sinking into your body and maximizing connection with your heart and soul through the healing light frequencies during this Shamanic Trance Journey with (sound)healing.”
During this workshop, you will be welcomed into a field of healing light frequencies and love. You achieve coherence between your heart and your brain, allowing you to relax deeply in your body…

events.title: Rasta Robert & Friends
events.description: Dreamtime Healing sound massage concerts, mantra singing, theremin, spontaneous musical improvisation, spiritual chill-out space. Every day 2:00 PM - 10:00 PM, concert sessions 2:00 PM - 3:30 PM and 8:00 PM - 9:30 PM (check-in 30 minutes prior).

events.title: 4-Hands Massage Arnaud Mooij & Marcel Maurik
events.description:
Four hands; one experience
A unique collaboration between Marcel and Arnaud
Free, spacious, relaxed, and completely surrendered. That's how you'll feel after a massage from this professional massage duo. Working as one body, they bring your body to a state of complete relaxation, ensuring that your body feels free and open, and it seems like your heart is even fuller and more open, ready to experience the freedom at Ruigoord even more.
When two talents combine their experience and passion, something special emerges. That's what Marcel and Arnaud offer you: a fusion of our strengths as bodyworkers. From our own approach and background, we complement each other in an intuitive and healing collaboration.
You choose what you need. Do you want to feel deeply relaxed, let your energy flow, release blockages, or simply return to yourself? We offer various disciplines that you can freely combine: classic massage, energetic massage, TRAGER® Approach, intuitive bodywork, and Rebalancing techniques. And our hands know exactly where they need to be on your body to give you an amazing experience…

events.title: Baba Exotica
events.description:
Baba Exotica celebrates the freedom in music, collecting and sharing the weird and wonderful worldwide. His 100% vinyl selections are an unique mix of styles coming from near and far. Get exotic with Highlife & Afro Sounds from Ghana, Gipsy music from Serbia, Psychedelic synth work from Cabo Verde, Peruvian masterpieces or Oriental Farfisa Madness & Rocking Bouzoukis from Greece.
 Tune in, get on board, be free and embrace the worldwide weird and wonderful. Love!
 Links:
 Youtube

events.title: Vinyasa yoga and breathwork
events.description:
Lindsay is a yoga teacher and facilitator. Her work weaves together yogic, somatic, tantric, and therapeutic practices, creating a space for people to slow down, reconnect with their bodies, and explore their inner world.
 In this class we will do a Vinyasa flow, where you gently flow in one posture to the next, while you connect the movements of your body with your breath.
 Alongside the yoga practice, we will explore calming breathwork and somatic exercises to release tension, awaken the body, and deepen the connection with yourself.

events.title: Contact with Movement
events.description:
Joya Aniel is on a mission to bring out the best of Humanity. By going through layers of (inherited) Shame, Loneliness, Sadness & (Social) Fear, he connects deeper with Our Essence. He uses tools such as Breathwork, Handpan, Massage & Dance to fulfill his mission.

It is Joya Aniel's wish to Create a world where everyone/everything is Welcome & Where we can Play, Explore & Expand on (this Heaven as) Earth.

In this Explorative Moment we Experience how two bodies become One, and then two again. We will sink in to our bodies & the bodies of others. With Meditative Movement & Touch we Slow Down, Feel & Connect. We welcome all emotions like Shame, Nervousness & Fear AND emotions like Harmony, Peace & Joy. Come as you Are, Enjoy this little Journey

events.title: Chakra journey
events.description:
The Chakra Journey is a transformative movement and dance experience that invites you to explore and awaken the seven energy centers of the body. Guided throughout the journey, participants are blindfolded, allowing attention to move away from the outside world and toward the wisdom of the body, intuition, and inner experience. You are invited to connect with the unique qualities and energies of each chakra in a deeply personal and authentic way.
 Through a combination of movement, dance, breathwork, sound, expression, and meditation, we travel through each of the seven chakras, awakening and exploring the unique qualities, emotions, and energies associated with each energy center. As we move through the body in this way, layers of tension, stagnation, and stored emotions can begin to soften and release, creating more space for vitality, flow, and self-expression.…

events.title: Informatief: Huiden verwerken
events.description:
Erwin navigates through life with passion, physical strength, and a connection to nature. Designer, Hunter, Bushcrafter.

Informative: Processing Hides
In this first part of the two-part series, we explore the story behind hides and their place in human history. We delve into the relationship between humans and animals throughout the ages, from hunting and survival among indigenous peoples to the careful use of what nature provides. We explore how we can meet our basic needs for life, and how respect for animals and nature plays a central role in this. This includes considering hunting as part of a broader ecological cycle, and the idea of connection with nature and "Mother Earth." It's a moment to reflect on what it means to be grounded and part of something larger. Space for questions and discussion makes this an open and insightful conversation.

This program segment is followed by a workshop on processing hides at 2 PM.

events.title: Workshop Lino Stempelen
events.description:
KIMTAM TATTOO is a tattoo artist with Javanese-Surinamese roots, based in Rotterdam. In her work, she combines craftsmanship, culture, and storytelling by creating unique designs that tell a personal story.

During this workshop, children will learn about the ancient art of printing. The artwork you see consists of lino stamps that have been hand-carved. Stamping techniques have been used around the world for centuries to print patterns and images on paper and fabric. In parts of Southeast Asia, including Malaysia, similar techniques were even used to create tattoos, where ornaments and figures were carved from wood.

This rich tradition inspired KIMTAM to design her own stamps and use them as a basis for her tattoo work.
For children aged 5-9 years:
Children playfully discover the world of stamping. They choose from various handmade lino stamps, make their own prints on paper, and then receive their favorite design as a temporary stencil on their skin.
For children aged 10+ years:
Older children get to work independently as creators…

events.title: "Poems of Fire and Earth"
events.description: Buguel Noz’s puppet poetry consists of tender and intense creations. It invites wonder and imagination. Music, dance, and puppetry come together in pure, magical moments. The performance ‘Poésie du Feu et de la Terre’ is ideally suited for festivals. This eco-poetic performance focuses on rebirth. The plant, animal, and human worlds meet around earth and fire, depicting the continuous cycle of change and renewal. Suitable for all ages, including very young children. A wordless choreography.

events.title: Saskia Schmink
events.description: Here, imaginations come to life. The makeup team transforms children into whatever they can dream of – from magical creatures to brave adventurers.

events.title: Friction - Compagnie Fô-Pá
events.description:
"A gem cannot be polished without friction, not a man perfected without trials."

Company Fô-Pá; Hans Mets and Marieke Drent (also known as La Familia Volante) began creating a new performance during a residency at Trust Collective in Portugal, where they live in the winter. Friction. The starting point of the creative process was their own relational and interpersonal dynamics. They are driven to bring raw, authentic emotions from real life onto the stage through physical theatre, clowning, acrobatics, and contact improvisation dance. This year, they have continued the process, and you will see the result in a Try-Out performance here. The performance is thematically aimed at adults and older children. As there are staged fights, a minimum age of 6 years is recommended.

events.title: Verwonderworkshop
events.description:
Wonder for both big and small people:

A small, strange object lies in the center. We hear soft harp music. Big and small people are building a world that didn't exist before. Branches become towers, boxes become houses, and what initially seemed meaningless turns out to be full of stories. Those who look closely will see it happen. Those who participate will discover more.

events.title: Awakening ceremony with Sophia Maria Kienhuis and Gilian Baracs
events.description:
On Vurige Tongen invites Sophia Maria Kienhuis and Gilian Baracs to their awakening ceremony, where you can unleash your inner firebird.

With live piano, beats, and original songs, they invite you to feel, move, and follow what wants to emerge. There is no fixed form, but a journey in which you seek the answer to the question of what it means for you to awaken.
Between stillness and radical expression, a shared experience unfolds, where the boundary between performers and audience fades away.

events.title: Kaleidoscope - hosted by Quinten
events.description: A curated musical journey where folk, hip-hop, jazz, funk, and world music come together in unique live performances and surprising musical connections.

events.title: Smooth Awakenings
events.description:
Let yourself be gently carried away by the calming piano tunes of KALEIDO’s piano virtuoso Tim Wray. 
 Tim Wray
 Tim is a pianist, composer and accompanist who believes in the power of music to shape soundscapes and create surreal, dreamlike emotional experiences. Tim's unique qualities are his use of live, musically-driven visuals and the ability to improvise based on the mood and feeling of the space around him. Tim has performed at a number of queer festivals and community events, and believes in the joy and power of music to create and shape the spaces around him.

events.title: One-Line Art workshop
events.description: Discover how a single continuous line can become a powerful tool for creativity, self-expression, and connection. Led by multidisciplinary artist Saeed Zamzam, this interactive workshop invites participants to create spontaneous one-line drawings, explore them through color, and share the stories behind their artwork in a welcoming environment. No artistic experience is needed; just curiosity and an open mind. Suitable for all ages, the workshop offers a relaxing and inspiring experience where art becomes a universal language that connects people beyond words.

events.title: Morning Qi Gong Session by Maarten Collewijn
events.description: Maarten Collewijn offers Qi Gong sessions based on an ancient practice that brings body and mind into balance through postures, movements, breathing, and mindful presence. The classes focus on enhancing vitality, health, and inner calm, allowing Qi (life energy) to flow freely through the body once again. A wonderful way to start the day!

events.title: Lach Workshop
events.description:
LAUGHING, LETTING GO & PLAYFUL CRAZINESS

Want to just let everything go? Then come and laugh with Sabine Rabbe!
Come to the laughter workshop during the Landjuweel Festival and completely lose yourself in laughter.
🤣 Laughing without reason
💃 Dancing, playing and letting loose
⚡ Recharging with festival energy
3 days of freedom, craziness and connection at Ruigoord.
A little rebellious, a little silly, and above all, lots of fun.
Leave your inhibitions behind and take your laughter with you.
Sabine Rabbe has been inspiring people for years with her contagious laughter, playfulness, and boundless energy.
As a laughter coach, performer, and connector, she shows you how liberating it is to simply let go and laugh.
With a mix of humor, spontaneity, and festival vibes, Sabine creates a space where fun, connection, and zest for life are central.
Laughter is her language, energy her trademark.
Come and laugh during the laughter workshop by Sabine Rabbe (aka "The Queen of Laughter") at Landjuweel this year!
As every year, we'll be letting loose again: a dose of humor, energy, and above all, lots of fun…

events.title: Gurdieff Kwartet
events.description:
In 2014, songwriter, singer and visual artist Ibo Bakker decides to take matters into his own hands and start performing the music that has inspired him for years. He collaborates with cellist Rani Kumar, and together they select pieces from the rich repertoire of Gurdjieff and Hartmann. These pieces are then arranged by Rani for a quartet: a string trio combined with electric and acoustic guitar, mandolin or harmonium. This results in unique interpretations of this almost 100-year-old music. While retaining the characteristic melodies, the Gurdjieff Quartet gives these magical compositions new and unheard-of soundscapes. Soon after, performances begin where the music is alternated with stories from the life and times of Gurdjieff. Meanwhile, Ibo and Rani continue to work on expanding their repertoire, paying close attention to what best translates to the quartet's instrumentation. They also seek out new stories that can be well combined with the music. In 2019, the quartet decides to record an album featuring a selection of the collected music, combined with a collection of stories and drawings by Ibo.

The Gurdjieff Quartet consists of:…

events.title: Awakening Dance
events.description: A communal experience to connect with each other and the church through dance, steps, and beats to start the day.

events.title: Transform Yourself
events.description: Yes, it’s true: a KALEIDO Make up artist is waiting for you in the Faerie Forest! Feel like transforming into something feisty, freshhh or just friggin’ sexy? Your vision is their gasoline!

events.title: The Art of Belonging
events.description:
A conversation about what community culture can teach us, featuring voices from Ruigoord, Boom Festival, and indigenous wisdom.

Community is something many of us long for, but what does it actually ask of us? Drawing on lived experience from Ruigoord, Boom Festival, and indigenous tradition, this conversation explores the values, practices, and ways of relating that help make genuine belonging possible.

Aja Waalwijk was present at the occupation of Ruigoord in 1973 and has been a participant in Ruigoord's Poets' Circus from the very beginning. He initiated the annual Saturday Landjuweel Sculpture Route, initiated and co-organized ten symposiums on Free Cultural Spaces, and organized several international ABG journeys from Ruigoord. He also co-organized the annual Paradiso performances, along with many other cultural initiatives.

Artur Mendes is co-director of Boom Festival and Being Gathering in Portugal. With a background in social and organizational psychology, culture management, and regenerative community design, he has spent decades exploring how festivals can foster lasting cultures of participation, shared values, and belonging.…

events.title: Dansworkshop: Organic movement Dance”
events.description:
Classes have the essence of a "laboratory" as we explore different tools and techniques of dance, movement, primal movement, physical theater, and dramatic body expression. We can enter into movement research with understanding of personal cycles (physical, mental, emotional) and universal cycles, through the experience of the continuous present. With a broad palette to investigate diverse movement qualities.
 This laboratory is the conjunction of methodologies such as Dramatic Body Expression (J.Walker), Physical Theater, Butoh, Noguchi Taiso, Contemporary Ballet and 21 years of study, practice and research of organic movement, in which the process of acceptance and expression of everything that emerges is a practice of presence through breathing.
 This journey of techniques and methodologies plus the essence and creativity of each being leads us to favor personal growth through movement and listening. It invites us to make personal growth a daily exercise, as vital as breathing. In this way, transitioning from body awareness to moments of inertia to recover our center, from the ancestral being to the everyday being.…

events.title: Photo Booth
events.description: Capture your favorite moment and step into our photo booth. Create a beautiful memory of the festival by taking your own picture.

events.title: SAVE THE GREEN FIELD — SIGN NOW!
events.description: The Green Field is a rare green space in the city where there is still room for wild nature, culture, and experimentation. It is run on a voluntary basis by residents and small, local businesses. But now, our free space is at risk of disappearing. Therefore, we need to act NOW, it's not too late. You can help us by signing the petition! More information at our stand.

events.title: The Floating Poet
events.description: Become a floating poet and come up to our raft to write a poem. Ask the internship manager where the secret departure point is. You can recite your creation in the garden.

events.title: The Game Cabinet
events.description:
Open a drawer, slide a box towards you, and let the fun begin. In the Games Corner, you'll find a diverse mix of card and board games waiting for new players. Challenge someone to a game of 30 seconds, go for pure chaos with a party game, or rediscover a classic you had long forgotten.

Whether you come to win, hang out, laugh, or simply chat: there's always something fun happening around the table here. Pull up a chair and join in the fun.

events.title: Waterpret: babbelbad & watergevecht
events.description:
Put on your fun water shoes, grab a super soaker, or pick up a water balloon and dive into the refreshing battle of Landjuweel.

Or just cool off from the hustle and bustle? Take a seat in the "babbelbad" (chat pool) and enjoy a good conversation. Be surprised by spontaneous encounters and enjoyable chats. Meet new people, share stories, and enjoy the conviviality.

events.title: XXL spellen: Schaken, Jenga, Twister
events.description: Huge, bigger, wildest: With our XXL games, it's all about having fun to the fullest. Challenge your strategic mind on a chessboard as big as your ego, build towers that reach higher than your weekend plans with XXL Jenga filled with surprising questions and tasks, or get tangled up during a game of Twister that is guaranteed to lead to laughter fits.

events.title: Workshop FreePlay Movement
events.description:
TEACHER
 For thirty years, former Olympic athlete Virgil Spier* built incredible explosive power. Yet, his elite fitness came with a painful paradox: he could sprint 40km/h and move massive weights, but couldn't sit through a movie or walk for an hour without aching pains. His athletic mastery had trapped him in a shrinking physical cage.
 Determined to reclaim his body, Virgil sought out world-class teachers and discovered a profound truth: true health isn't just about extreme physical capacity, but the continuous cultivation of physical options for everyday life.
 Today, as the co-founder of Movement Based Athletics, Virgil has traded grim, repetitive discipline for joyful exploration and research. His practice bridges the gap between athletic conditioning and play. By replacing traditional exercises with improvisation, communicative partnering, play fighting, and out of the box thinking, Virgil uses movement as the ultimate school of life, helping people build resilience, move with grace, and stay adaptable for the long haul.
 Practice
 Virgil’s Movement practice always revolved around creating options, autonomy and elastic capacity while never compromising on longevity.…

events.title: Morning Yoga · The Five Elements Form
events.description:
Want do you want smooth hips, flowing energy and a smile on your face?

During this standing yoga flow, Esmee Jiskoot will guide you through the 5 Elements Form: a series of twenty fluid yoga movements that may remind you of Qigong. The movements help to release tension, deepen your breathing and bring body and mind back into balance.
The flow moves like a moving meditation: soft, powerful and surprisingly energetic all at once. Your body comes alive, your mind finds peace and you feel more present in the moment.
Whether you are an experienced yogi or taking your first yoga class, this practice invites you to slow down, feel and reconnect with yourself. A wonderful reset during the festival, that will leave you grounded, energized and smiling.
Get to know Esmee Jiskoot
Esmee Jiskoot is a yoga teacher and body-oriented coach. With warmth, enthusiasm and years of experience, she guides people towards more relaxation, body awareness and life energy.
In her classes, movement, breathing and personal development come together naturally…

events.title: Reiki & Just Zen It Workshop (12+ jaar)
events.description:
Kyra Walhof is a Reiki Master, massage therapist, and founder of Zensaties and the Just Zen It method. For over fifteen years, she has been guiding people towards relaxation, mindfulness, and energy management. With her warm, practical, and humorous approach, she makes spirituality accessible and helps people connect with themselves more deeply.

Reiki & Just Zen It Workshop
Discover the power of Reiki and Just Zen It in a simple and practical way. During this workshop, you will learn how to experience more peace in your body and mind through simple meditation, breathing, and mindfulness exercises. You will gain an understanding of the basics of Reiki and discover how to better feel, manage, and recharge your energy. We will practice relaxation, presence, and increasing loving attention for yourself and those around you. No prior experience is necessary; curiosity is enough!

There are a limited number of places available. So, if you would like to participate in this workshop, you can reserve a spot at the yurt on Dorpsplein - Aardewerk.

events.title: Workshop: Huiden verwerken
events.description:
Erwin navigates through life with passion, physical strength, and a connection to nature. Designer, Hunter, Bushcrafter.

Informative: Processing Hides
In this first part of the two-part series, we explore the story behind hides and their place in human history. We delve into the relationship between humans and animals throughout the ages, from hunting and survival among indigenous peoples to the careful use of what nature provides. We explore how we can meet our basic needs for life, and how respect for animals and nature plays a central role in this. This includes considering hunting as part of a broader ecological cycle, and the idea of connection with nature and "Mother Earth." It's a moment to reflect on what it means to be grounded and part of something larger. Space for questions and discussion makes this an open and insightful conversation.

This program segment is followed by a workshop on processing hides at 2 PM.

events.title: Filosofisch kabinet
events.description:
Theme of the day:

Out of nowhere, The Wandering Company appears: three mysterious beings, a creaking caravan, and traces of a life on the road. Insects? Travelers? No one knows for sure, perhaps even themselves. One question has driven them for as long as they've been wandering: where is home? Today, they invite you to join their search. The day consists of 3 parts. First, the company appears, right in the middle of an interactive theater moment. Then, their story, "Onward to Home," comes to life through a narrative that unfolds before your eyes. And at the end of the day, everyone departs together in a festive children's parade, the ritual that transforms searching into finding home.

The Wandering Company appears:
With a creaking, rattling sound, the caravan rolls onto the square. Curious eyes look around: where have they ended up? The Wandering Company is on its way, as always, looking for an answer it hasn't yet found. This place is just one stop along the way; nothing more, nothing less. But exploring an unknown world isn't something you do alone; they need your help…

events.title: MC Katootje
events.description:
MC Katootje will take you on a journey.
Without any distractions, she'll put on a show.
With your child and with your grandparent,
She'll captivate you with her words.
She's truly the most entertaining!
Not always relaxed, but...
Eh... Come to InstrumenTaal Stage!
Then we'll all see it together
and have a great time working together :)

Weaving a musical carpet is a noble aspiration...

events.title: The "Sounds Factory" & The "Make It Up As You Go Along" Game
events.description:
Juliska Yonne writes and performs her own songs, accompanied by the piano. She also creates music and theatre performances where children are invited to play, discover, and create.

The "Geluiden-Fabriek" (Sound Factory) & The "Het Verzin-Het-Ter-Plekke-Spel" (Make It Up As You Go Game)
Step into a cheerful workshop full of movement, sound, and imagination. Together, we play with sounds, invent the craziest ideas on the spot, and discover what emerges when we look at each other and listen carefully. There is no right or wrong here, only the magic of the moment.

events.title: SanghaSang & Swierstra
events.description:
In 2025, Grietje and Vincent decided to join forces: SanghaSang & Swierstra was born. With Grietje (SanghaSang) on the Indian harmonium and Vincent (Swierstra) on the Indian sarangi, they offer a deeply grounding and relaxing mantra meditation, singing and sound bath session.
 As part of the Landjuweel Festival they will offer a mantra meditation and singing circle, followed by a sound bath. First you will be invited to hum, chant and sing along with different mantras and melodies, and the session will finish with you lying down and surrendering to the calming sounds of just the sarangi. You will leave the circle feeling deeply relaxed.
 There is no need to have any knowledge of (Indian/Sanskrit) mantras, just come with an open mind and a curiosity to explore your own voice and energy, the energy of the group, of the music, and of course the mantras.
 About SanghaSang & Swierstra
 Grietje offers mantra meditation sessions and singing circles in Amsterdam under the name SanghaSang. Sangha in Sanskrit means "spiritual community," and "sang" comes from "singing" in Frisian, her native language. With the name SanghaSang, she honours these roots.…

events.title: Move & Play: Buddy Fitness
events.description: Grab a buddy, old or new, and let’s move! KALEIDO is turning the church into our very own little queer movement playground. It’s for every body and every level, so forget the rules and let’s have fun. Come move to the beat, shake off the week, and celebrate the skin you’re in. And who knows, you might even find your new festival bestie!

events.title: Henk and the Jet Hunter: a children's disco for all ages!
events.description:
BIG tunes for KIDS of ALL ages. Because Henk and the Beam Hunter are not afraid. Nooo no.
 They swoosh passed the beaten tracks with such a rapid pace, that might make you wonder: How than?
 Make it yourself uncomfortable and dance like it’s your first time

events.title: MariGlue
events.description:
MariGlue is a Colombian DJ based in Amsterdam. She grew up in Bogotá surrounded by Latin and tropical music and she still loves dancing to it. Her sets mix nostalgic originals and new discoveries from the alternative side of Latin American and tropical sounds: cumbia, champeta, salsa, Afro-Colombian rhythms and other folkloric music from Colombia and Latin America, with Caribbean and African influences. She also has a soft spot for the growing wave of electronic music inspired by this folklore, where producers and artists draw from ancestral and traditional sounds to create something that feels both rooted and alive.
 She cares a lot about curating a vibe she herself would love to hear and dance to. Behind the decks, staying connected to the people on the floor matters to her, she wants to feel part of the dancing together with everyone else, not separate from it. Expect music to chill to in the sun, with breezy and groovy moments that slowly warm you up or suddenly make you move.

events.title: Job den Dulk
events.description:
Dark and light coming together. Stirring and feeling in a suitcase full of emotional experiences, while with every guitar note the sun rises further. Job den Dulk takes you on a journey with his soft singing and intimate guitar playing, through the flow of love, life processes, and melancholic moments.

Dark & Light is his first EP (2021), an acoustic treasure for the soul that yearns for beauty. Music that is usually small in form and arrangement, as if you find an old photo in the attic and daydream a little while remembering. A glimpse into intimate atmospheres, as if you are secretly peeking through a keyhole. Guest musicians Dick Ronteltap (flute) and Moniek de Leeuw (violin) also played on the album. He also made a live video clip of the song "Signs on the Wall" with Moniek.
In late 2022, the single "Softly Does It" was released, a cover of Ramses Shaffy. The song is enriched with piano playing by Sjaak Douma, who previously made remixes for San Holo and Nils Frahm.
On March 1, 2024, Job celebrated spring with the release of a fresh and uplifting song called "Phoenix in the Air".

events.title: DAF - Dutch Acid Family
events.description:
Thursday
 21:00-01:00
 Electro Magnes
 Electro Magnes is a Polish electronic duo formed by composer, producer, and multi-instrumentalist Rafał Kassaraba and vocalist-songwriter Bashka Preen. Blending live bass, electronic textures, and expressive vocals, they create atmospheric soundscapes that move between experimental electronica, ambient music, and intimate storytelling.
 Based in the foothills of the Karkonosze Mountains, the duo draws inspiration from nature, human connection, and inner transformation. Rafał's musical journey spans folk-blues, punk-rock, electro-punk, and film music projects, bringing a rich and distinctive depth to their sound.
 Bashka writes and performs in English, influenced by the years she spent living in London and its vibrant music scene. Her original songs have also been featured in film productions.
 Together, Electro Magnes invites audiences into a cinematic and immersive musical experience where electronic experimentation meets emotion, reflection, and heart.

Friday
 Sunshine Reggae
 15:00-16:30 Hobbol
 16:30-18:00 Open the gate sound
 18:00-19:30 Loddy Culture & Lyrical Benji
 19:30-21:00 Selector Depender
 21:00-22:30 Sun Collective…

events.title: Contact Staff Workshop hosted by The Fire Messiah
events.description: Contact staff is a beautiful tool for movement expression and connection with your own body. Join Tiago (The Fire Messiah) in this workshop where you will explore flowing with the contact staff and how this relationship can increase your awareness of your own body. We will finish the workshop with some fun games :).

events.title: Divine Mistress of Craziness
events.description: (Born in 1939) She commutes between Cologne and the Amsterdam artist village of Ruigoord. After taking a parachute jump on her 60th birthday, she left behind her conventional life to pursue profound spiritual experiences. In her temple in Ruigoord, she celebrates encounters at a soul level and opens doors to higher levels of consciousness. More information about her unique philosophy can be found on the Ruigoord Artist Page.

events.title: FreeFlow Flashmob
events.description: Somewhere, sometime, this week. Maybe tomorrow morning after breakfast, maybe when the sun sets over the church, maybe when you've just tied your shoelaces. The FreeFlow Flashmob passes by. Or rather: it comes towards you. A rave on wheels, a party that creates itself, a dance that invites you to join in. With DJs on the bike sound system and live music. No stage, no audience, just participants who don't yet know they're part of it. Dancing, contact improvisation, and homo ludens on two wheels. The party creates itself, and you participate. Afterwards, we dissolve into the wind, as if nothing had happened, except in your legs. Those who were there will know. The rest will hear the stories.

events.title: The Bard’s Horn
events.description:
Thursday:
 21.00 till 01.00 DJs Archangel, Graham B, JP and Ambient Daan
 Friday:
 16.00 to 20.00 with storytellers Simone Engeln, Daan P., Belinda with musical accompaniment by Fons, musical interlude by sitar player Dougal
 20.00 to 21.00 Monty Pagan, a delightful Amsterdam folk rock band
 21.00 till 01.00 DJs Archangel, Graham B, JP and Ambient Daan
 Saturday:
 16.00 to 20.00 with storytellers Simone Engeln, Daan P., Belinda with musical accompaniment by Fons, musical interlude by sitar player Dougal
 20.00 to 21.00 Monty Pagan, a delightful Amsterdam folk rock band
 21.00 till late DJs Archangel, Graham B, JP and Ambient Daan
 Sunday:
 15.00 to 18.00 ParaNoya and friends with poetry and art
 Simone Engeln (formerly known as Simone Angel) is a versatile storyteller whose life itself is a story. After spending twenty years in the jungle of Belize, she returned to the Netherlands in 2023. Her adventurous life began at the age of 18; under the international spotlight of television…

events.title: Marcia's Metal Masquerade
events.description:
Marcia Verwoert (Kikvors Collectief)
 Build your own mask, build on the shape of your face and create a new identity. We will be using metal wire and maybe even other materials.
 What do you want to hide behind?
 Or how do you want to be seen?
 Im Marcia, the bozeheersbeestje. I love to work with a variation of different metarials - metal wires but also fabrics. I love colours and welcome a bit of chaos in my looks. My inspiration comes a lot from nature and spirituality.

events.title: Festival Make-up & Makeover with Shotgun Cilla
Gotcha & Katherina – Folk songs from Georgia
Kalaudo
events.description:
Welcome to the world of La Lau. The biggest Non-Talent at KALEIDO? Is it time for a KaLauDo? Can this unrefined performance queen actually pull it off?

In this performance, Lau will attempt to open her show. But our chaotic "All Days, Every Day Diva" has 500 ideas for a possible opening and simply can't choose, even due to the critical eye of the new dramaturg. So she just does them all anyway. Expect unhinged characters, makeup that a toddler would do better with, Britney-worthy amateur performance, chaos, G-A-Y, lots and lots of attempts.

events.title: Queer Platonic Speeddating
events.description:
Queer Platonic Speed Dating is a fun space to meet new people without romantic pressure or awkward expectations. Through a series of short, guided encounters, you'll connect with other queer individuals who are also open to friendship, community, and unexpected connections. Come as you are! Whether you're curious, shy, chatty, or somewhere in between, you might leave with a new friend, a future collaborator, or simply a really lovely conversation. This event is for anyone who feels comfortable with or curious about their queer identity – no one is excluded!

Maud Verdam
Maud is a Ruigoord native who has experienced many different scenes and loves trying new things, even when they feel a little scary. She believes that every place, person, and experience deserves to be approached with fresh eyes. With the same curiosity and openness, she hosts Queer Platonic Speed Dating: a fun space for meeting new people without romantic pressure. Come as you are! You might leave with a new friend, an unexpected connection, or simply a lovely conversation.

events.title: Ecstatic Dance with DJ JERA
events.description:
As an Ecstatic Dance DJ, Jera aims to cultivate your natural ecstasy and love for life.

At the opening, he will provide you with tools to deepen your dance journey through breath, movement, and connection. With Psychedelic Ambient, Tribal Trance, Bass, and World Music, his set creates a journey that invites surrender, expression, and connection.
Since the age of 19, Jera has been organizing monthly Ecstatic Dances and several festivals annually at his home base, Ottermeer Estate. His passion is to bring people together, create magical experiences, stimulate personal growth, and share the beauty of life.
Driven by his love for dance, he also started DJing, to take people on a journey that can be both profound and playful. For his first time at the Temple of Self, he comes with a suitcase full of love, a generous dose of bass, and the intention to create an unforgettable celebration together.

events.title: ROOTEDANCE
events.description:
A somatic dance experience of ancestral rhythm and Earth reconnection

Rootedance is more than just a dance class – it's a ritual in motion. A space for deep, free movement where the body becomes drum, root, and prayer.
This performative practice awakens the senses, inviting the body to be explored as a living territory of memory and transformation. Inspired by Afro and Latin rhythms, Rootedance unfolds as a guided sensory journey where inner pulses are awakened, energy centers are activated, and the sacred bond with the Earth is restored.
More than just a technique, Rootedance is a safe space for self-exploration. Music and movement serve as sacred tools through which each participant is invited to inhabit their body, breathe deeply, and remember themselves as part of the living pulse of the planet. It's dance – but also release, prayer, and rebirth.
Designed for physical theatre festivals, somatic laboratories, and interdisciplinary performance spaces, Rootedance bridges tribal rhythm with inner awareness, activating a collective embodied experience that reclaims the body as home and temple.
Estimated duration: 75–90 minutes…

events.title: Florelei
events.description:
Florelei is a captivating artist who combines melancholic dark folk with dreamlike atmospheres, woven from the warm textures of voice and guitar. At the heart of it all is a voice of rare and arresting beauty, one that carries equal weight in a barely audible whisper and a soaring, gut-wrenching cry.
The music is steeped in a fascination with life, death, time, nature, and existential questions. Each song feels like a doorway into an inner world, inviting stillness and depth. Performances are more than concerts; they are moments of intimate connection, a shared journey into the depths of emotion and thought, balancing melody and chaos, light and dark.

events.title: Embroidery of a talisman
events.description: Create a personalized talisman to take with you on the go. Using thread, color, and symbols, give shape to something that is meaningful to you. A small keepsake that reminds you of the places, people, or moments where you feel at home.
events.venue: Village Square · A World in the Making

events.title: Latin percussion workshop with Jos de Haas
events.description:
Percussionist Jos de Haas has been a prominent figure in the Dutch jazz and Latin scene for years, most notably through his work with New Cool Collective. With his infectious grooves and passion for Afro-Cuban and Latin rhythms, he knows how to get everyone moving.

During the Landjuweel Festival, Jos will be leading an accessible Latin Percussion Workshop. No prior experience is required: if you have a sense of rhythm and a desire to make music together, you are warmly welcome! Expect an energetic session filled with swinging Latin rhythms, ensemble playing, and above all, lots of fun.

events.title: Soup and sandwiches
events.description: We gather around the fire to cook together. Bring a piece of vegetables and add it to the communal soup. Small and large hands work side by side, while bread is baked over the fire. The square fills with aromas, and our hearts are warmed by being together.

events.title: Inner Child Garden
events.description:
Welcome to the Inner Child Garden (Innerlijke kinderspeeltuin)
A moment to ground yourself, connect with your younger self, and let your inner desires run free. Come as you are, embrace the flow, and let’s meet the child within!

events.title: Kids' Catwalk
events.description: During the "Kids Catwalk," children of all ages can participate in the fashion show. Rietreat boasts a huge collection of clothing, and under the watchful eyes of world-renowned stylists Mannish Pixel and Hester, the children will be transformed into true supermodels. Parents are expected to provide the most enthusiastic audience imaginable.

events.title: Sound Journey with Rinco & Stéphanie from Wij Zijn Bloei (We Are Blooming)
events.description:
Treat yourself to a moment of peace and allow yourself to embark on an inner journey of sound, silence, and deep relaxation. During this special Sound Journey, Rinco & Stéphanie will guide you into the healing world of handpan, singing bowls, gong, and guided meditation.

In the peaceful energy of the Landjuweel Festival, we will create a gentle space where you can simply be. A place to land, let go, and return to yourself. As the warm sounds surround you, you can relax and feel the vibrations moving through your body, inviting every cell to rest.
 Stéphanie guides the journey with her voice, meditation, handpan, and singing bowls. Her gentle presence invites you to delve deeper into your body and create space for relaxation and connection. Rinco complements this journey with the deep, grounding resonance of the gong. Together, they weave a bed of peace, stillness, and groundedness.
 The sounds will take you to another state of being, where you can recharge, relax, and return to yourself. Afterwards, you'll return to the festival, feeling grounded, clear, and full of new energy.
 Come lie down. Breathe out. Receive.
 Sound and silence…

events.title: Somatic Playground
events.description:
We are Pau and Lois, two women who explore life through the language of the body. Pau does so through Shiatsu and Contact Improvisation, while Lois draws from Holistic Massage and Qigong.

In this workshop, we invite you to reconnect with your body. Join us on a journey of self-awareness to authentic connection through meditation, somatic practices, dance, and touch.
 By slowing down and listening to what is present in the moment, we explore movement as a natural form of expression. Step by step, we open ourselves to the space around us and to one another. Through playful exercises, elements of Contact Improvisation, and respectful touch, we explore themes such as trust, boundaries, listening, giving, and receiving.
 Open to anyone curious about body awareness, movement, and connection. No previous experience is needed.
 Bring a yoga mat or blanket to lie on if you have one (not required).

events.title: Wim Hof Method Ice Bath
events.description:
Immerse yourself in this invigorating ice bath for refreshing relaxation. Perfect for enthusiasts, but also for those with a fear of cold water. To warm the body and prepare our minds, we will perform several rounds of breathing exercises according to the Wim Hof Method. A wonderful experience, as Wim Hof himself says: "Get high on your own supply."

Afterwards, we are ready for the ultimate power boost: the ice bath. Inhale, exhale, surrender to the cold, maintain a sharp mind, and when you step out, you will feel reborn. Afterwards, you can float and dance freely across the festival grounds, feeling refreshed, clear, and full of confidence.

WHM Method Fundamentals Workshop. Duration: 2 hours.

I am Kolja van Zwoll, born and raised in Nieuw & Meer in Amsterdam, and I have been attending Landjuweel since it started :) For the past two years, I have been an instructor for the Wim Hof Method, and I have enjoyed and successfully worked on the healing field for the past two years, and I am very happy that it can do so again this year. To ensure the safety of all participants, I also bring in two trainers and an assistant for the equipment.

events.title: Drugs Zonder Drugs
events.description:
No need for any special tools or equipment. Achieve a state of flow through creativity. Through play, movement, and imagination, Vita and Thirza invite you to reconnect with your inner child and embrace that freedom. A moment away from the everyday, back to the heart. To the place where anything is possible.

Vita is a visual artist, and Thirza is a dancer and choreographer. Together, they create a unique combination that brings together energy, color, and movement in a workshop that inspires, touches, and connects.

events.title: Love Yourself' workshop
events.description:
In this workshop, we turn inward and create a safe space to open our hearts. With gentleness and without judgment, we meet ourselves exactly as we are. We connect with all aspects of ourselves, both the light and the dark, and allow what needs to be seen and felt to emerge.

Through Yin yoga, breathing exercises, meditation, and visualization, we deepen our connection with ourselves. From this inner connection, compassion grows: first for ourselves, and naturally extends outwards to the world around us. A loving journey inward, in which you are invited to fully embrace yourself.

Lieke works as a Yin yoga teacher, systemic coach, and trainer. With warmth, love, and connection, she guides individuals and groups in discovering their true potential.

events.title: Marble Workshop
events.description: Create flowing shapes with your favorite colors, let them merge on water, and transfer your unique creation onto paper. Each print is a surprise.

events.title: Roberta Petzoldt
events.description: Roberta Petzoldt works as a poet, theater maker, actress, and visual artist. In January 2019, her first poetry collection, *Vruchtwatervuurlinie*, was published by Uitgeverij Van Oorschot. This collection received the ‘Cees Buddingh-Prijs’. In autumn 2025, the collection *Zeebeving* was released. As an actress, she has played three leading roles in feature films. For ‘Meet me in Venice’, she won the ‘Best Actress Award’ at the ‘Woodstock film festival’. She graduated from the Gerrit Rietveld Academie, where she developed both her language and visual work on the 'Beeld en Taal' (Image and Language) department. “Through my indigenous Surinamese ancestors, I also developed a shamanistic awareness alongside my Western perspective. Rituals and myths are a living reality for me. Weaving these realities inspires my work as a visual artist and poet, which explains my need to combine different disciplines. This allows me to approach complex issues such as origin, our relationship with nature, and unity in diversity from multiple perspectives simultaneously.”

events.title: The Word, hosted by Yvonne van Doorn and Diana Ozon
events.description:
YVONNE VAN DOORN & DIANA OZON

Yvonne van Doorn-Mousset has been active in the Dutch avant-garde since the 1960s. She presented the radio program "Uitlaat" by Wim de Bie, was the partner and business advisor to writer and performer Johnny van Doorn, and is a board member of the performance poetry prize "De Johnny."

For over 24 years, she has organized and presented "The Word en Vurige Tongen" in Ruigoord. She did this together with poet Hans Plomp, who encouraged her to continue this work.
Since autumn 2024, Yvonne has formed a duo with punk poet Diana Ozon. Since the 1980s, Diana has been one of the most prominent female voices within Dutch performance and underground poetry. With her uncompromising performances, in which poetry, music, and activism converge, she paved the way for generations of poets and performers. Her work embodies freedom, social engagement, and artistic independence.

Yvonne (2011) and Diana (2005) have both received the Ruigoord Trophy. Together with "The Word en Vurige Tongen," they keep the traditions of poetry, performance, and free expression alive.

events.title: Eric, the magician behind the curtain.
events.description:
Eric the Magician will appear and disappear again around the Gnome House Stage (Kabouterhuys) at Landjuweel. Hearing, seeing, smelling, tasting, feeling, and thinking are the ingredients of a magician. Eric creates his own illusions; he doesn't like to let them go and loves to involve you in them. Beyond the smoke and mirrors, there is room for attention, and before you know it, you are spellbound. You don’t need to wear a red hat to be a Gnome.


 With Landjuweel, Eric the Magician will reappear and disappear around the Kabouterhuys Stage. Hearing, seeing, smelling, tasting, feeling, and thinking are the ingredients of a magician. Eric creates his own illusions; he doesn't like to lose them and loves to make you part of them. Beyond the smoke and mirrors, there is room for attention, and before you know it, you are spellbound. You don’t need to wear a red hat to be a Gnome.

events.title: The Rebels' Club: Julius Joker & Erwin Mulder
Poetry Consults
events.description:
Feeling lovesick? Despondent? Forlorn? 
 Dr. Oxytocin's poetry consults are open for business! Come get the lock on your heart fixed by inhaling their intimate poetry and 'a completely safe dose' of Oxytocin. Come check out their tent, hiding in the KALEIDO Faerie Forest!

events.title: Yanic
events.description:
Years of travelling the world left their mark on YANIC — in the breadth of what he carries, and the depth of how he listens.
 His sets move between worlds. Sometimes slow and juicy, sensual and grounded. Sometimes wild, ecstatic and beautifully chaotic. Sometimes all of it in the same night. For eight years he has been resident DJ and curator at De Lievelinge, a place that became his musical home. Now he's ready to be heard beyond it.
 YANIC is spreading his wings — and the dancefloor follows.

events.title: Eline Bierling & Mame N'Diack: The Intention
events.description:
Eline Bierling
 Eline Bierling (33) is a singer-songwriter, violinist, poet, and creator of musical experiences that resonate deeply within the listener's core. Connection – with oneself, with others, and with the greater whole – is central to her work.
 In 2019, she released her Dutch poetry album, followed in 2021 by her first Dutch singer-songwriter album.
 Through her violin, guitar, singing bowls, piano, and especially her voice, she creates a deeply meditative and emotional field. The layered vibrations of all her instruments, combined with her warm and intuitive voice, gently guide listeners inward, touching subtle and often unspoken layers within the body and nervous system. Her singer-songwriter music (in her native language: Dutch) is personal, open, and carries a healing quality that invites recognition, release, and inner calm.
 Mame N'Diack
 Mame N’Diack (47) is The Rhythm: vivid, expressive, and deeply rooted – he makes the timeless tangible.
 With his Senegalese roots in rhythm and voice, he brings an earthy, grounding presence to the performance. His musical expression adds depth and movement, supporting and expanding the emotional landscape created in the moment.…

events.title: Cronos - Bardo Teatro Físico
events.description:
The company started in 2016, with the objetive of creating a play that combines circus techniques with physical theater.
 The artistic searches of the creators lead to the same path, making the fusion between the theater and the circus, our scenic language. We are interested in moving from a performance show to a theatrical poetics and that circus resources and skills are deeply linked with a dramaturgy. In this dynamic, acrobats are modified by the text and transcend the purely physical.

events.title: Ralph de Jongh & Roos Kanis
events.description:
Roos Kanis & Ralph de Jong and magic friends - Shaman Blues

When the raw, soulful blues of Ralph de Jongh merges with the ceremonial, theatrical, and musical world of Roos Kanis, something new is created: Shaman Blues.

Ralph has been one of the most idiosyncratic blues artists in the Netherlands for years. With his deeply rooted voice, free-flowing playing, and deep roots in blues, rock, and roots music, he manages to turn every performance into a unique experience. His career has taken him to countless stages, festivals, and collaborations within the Dutch blues scene.

Roos brings another dimension: years of experience in ceremonial work, a treasure trove of songs from her traveling musical theater, and the creation of connecting experiences where music, story, and experience come together.

Together, they create not just a concert, but a musical journey. A performance where blues, groove, ceremony, and theater meet. The audience is taken on a trance of rhythm, soul, and play, where the boundary between artist and audience slowly fades away.

Shaman Blues is an invitation to meet yourself and others in a field of joy, freedom, love, and connection.…

events.title: Lino printing workshop for ages 10+
events.description:
KIMTAM TATTOO is a tattoo artist with Javanese-Surinamese roots, based in Rotterdam. She combines craftsmanship, culture, and storytelling by creating unique designs that tell a personal story. Stamping techniques have been used worldwide for centuries to print patterns and images on paper and textiles. In some parts of Southeast Asia, similar techniques were even used to create tattoos. Inspired by this rich tradition, KIMTAM designs her own stamps and uses them as the basis for her tattoo work.

• For children from 10 years old
Older children get to work themselves as creators. They learn how to design a simple image, cut it out in lino, print it on paper, and then turn it into a temporary stencil on the skin. This way, they experience the entire creative process: from idea to final result.

The workshop focuses on creativity, craftsmanship, and discovering a unique art form that people have been using for generations to make stories, symbols, and identity visible. Everyone goes home with their own prints and also a unique temporary tattoo that they have created themselves.

events.title: Lisa and the Brown Prince
events.description:
‘On a clear evening, when the sun has set and the moon looks longingly at the stars, Lisa steps out of the window of her small house. Suddenly, she sees a large palace standing on top of the mountain in the bright moonlight. She can hardly believe her eyes; she has never seen a palace before. ‘Does a prince live there?’, Lisa wonders. Are you also curious to hear how this Caribbean fairytale continues? Then come and listen and enjoy the most beautiful illustrations in Wijnand Stomp’s ‘Kamishi-bai’ magic box. The master of Caribbean stories alternates between many character types and tropical songs. Together with his puppet ‘Bird-friend’, he takes both young and old on adventures. When the snake from his story starts to coil, everyone is transported to a fairytale world.

Wijnand Stomp published this story in 1997 because he could not find any books for his own children featuring a brown prince as the main character, as an alternative to the eternal image of the (white) prince on a white horse.

events.title: Trammelant in sprookjesland
events.description:
Improvisational Theater with Daphne Zandbreg.
 Soul Song: Personal Music Mirrors For You

Trammelant in Sprookjesland is a cheerful and musically-themed performance where children and parents are invited to play together in the Ruigoord children's tent. Where do those winding paths lead? What wonderful creatures will you encounter? And... who will you be today? Together, we will bring the most exciting, beautiful, and magical stories to life!

events.title: Woven Voices: What's alive in you?
Pull & Power
events.description: In this improvisation theater workshop you are invited to discover within yourself the desire and the retraction. Based on the method you will explore how your body reacts to pursuit and caution. Through theater improvisation exercises you will come in contact with one of the main complexities of giving life to a character who (usually) experiences some kind of conflict. Dive into playing and finding out how you react to being empowered and how to embody power.

events.title: K-Faeries Training
events.description:
How can we party amidst the rise of techno-fascist-myso-genocide? If you're feeling guilty, I (tbh) love to break it to ya, but your hedonistic escape is an illusion. This is happening to you too. The revolution begins at cellular level, and we're all alive & in power.
 But fear not - the K faeries are here to help - together we'll dissociate from those barriers of perception, to embody the world that lies beyond.

events.title: Siren Rising
events.description:
Siren Rising is a small, intimate workshop for up to 25 people who are ready to explore their sensuality. Through music, movement, and play with clothes, accessories, and textures, you'll begin to understand what sensuality actually feels like in your own body, rather than according to someone else's definition of what it should or shouldn't be.

This is a space to let go of self-consciousness and embrace your own personal kind of sensuality. From there, we explore how to channel that energy into a room, into a gaze, into the space between bodies. This is a safe, supportive, and non-judgmental environment where you are actively encouraged to be more than you think you can be, and most importantly, to leave feeling incredibly sexy!

events.title: Babette
events.description:
Babette Voets is a multidisciplinary artist, performer, storyteller, and founder of Controubadour. Using harp, singing, and spoken word, she creates poetic worlds where transformation, empathy, and imagination come together. Through the practice of shapeshifting, she explores how we can create new connections with ourselves, each other, and the living world around us by temporarily adopting a different perspective.

"Pleidooi voor diversiteit" (Appeal for Diversity) is a musical exploration of all voices and worlds that exist in our lives, and how they can free us from a fixed scenario in our minds.

events.title: Laura Jayne
events.description:
DJ Laura Jayne blends her warm and spicy dance floor cocktails, mixing percussive electronic beats with synthy African vibes, Brazilian & UK funk, catchy garage, and all-time classics, with a twist of global bass and tribal dubby electronica.

Her international journey through time and space is sure to surprise you and take you on a trip through her musical selection of gems, getting you into a free flow of embodied explosion and playfulness.

Laura is known for her energizing global groove sets that she plays at festivals with DJ collectives such as the Bankra Bike Soundsystem and Moonshine Sound Collective, where live music is incorporated into the dance sets.

As a co-founder of FreeFlow, she loves hosting festival stages with musician and DJ families at venues such as Tivoli, Paradiso, Couleur Cafe (Brussels), and Zwarte Cross.

events.title: Goey & Kirkos
events.description: The duo, Goey/Kyrkos, performs a selection of medieval musical pieces using traditional instruments, all in their own unique and improvisational style, complemented by the dancing and singing of Fatima and Sabouschka.

events.title: La Prudon × Ibu Ibiza · Hats & Fashion Show
events.description:
Two designers, two crafts, one show. La Prudon (Christine Prudon) creates handmade hats as wearable art – small works of art that also happen to fit on a head. Ibu Ibiza (Su-an Dijckmeester, Ibiza) makes sustainable clothing. During Landjuweel, they will present an exclusive pop-up hat and fashion show on the Ruigoord grounds together.

Shows

Friday, July 24th, 17:00
Sunday, July 26th, 19:00

Location: De straat bij de Dingplaats, Ruigoord
Left:
Instagram La Prudon

events.title: Spin Fans workshop hosted by Tara Moon
Tattoo Station
events.description: Ideally, I prefer to do tattoos for friends and family of friends, or people who meet through friends. For you, I can (Lot) create a symbol of love or fire on your body. Come by and let me adorn you with a unique piece!

events.title: Estée & Michel
events.description:
Michel de Wit (guitar) and Estée Makampo (violin) have been playing together for many years. They create unique, atmospheric, harmonious, and colorful music in the moment. Perfect for relaxing and being inspired. Their music is connecting and is intuitively created in the present moment.

The music is unique and touches the heart and soul.
Estée - Left:
Website
Michel - Left:
Website

events.title: Jolly Mango's
events.description:
The Jolly Mangos form a unique trio with electric accordion, guitar, and percussion. They bring an energetic mix of pop music and South American rhythms that quickly gets everyone moving. Each performance feels genuine and like a collaboration between the band and the audience, creating a magical atmosphere. Whether you prefer slow or fast tempos, nostalgic or vibrant – this day will keep you entertained.

Ruigoord is also (like for many others) the birthplace of The Jolly Mangos. It's where much of their music originated, where most of their performances took place, and they are now working on setting up their own rehearsal studio in a dedicated workshop.

events.title: DJ Witte Aap
events.description: Not a DJ, but a music selector playing wonderful tunes from all over the world!

events.title: Workshop Playful Partner Yoga
events.description: In pairs (and sometimes with the whole group) we explore using our bodies in different shapes to awaken, create space, and activate in a playful way. Expect deep stretches, deep laughs and maybe making some new friends ✨✨

events.title: Your story written on your body
events.description:
Cato Fluitsma and Ian Merkoulov, with their Troubadour Car, have found each other as "Small-Scale Artists of Life" and travel together in a rhyming musical dialogue.

A story written on your body:
A journey within ourselves. What does the wind sound like to you? And the cracking earth? Let us hear it! Can we become that babbling brook where the bears have set up their tent? Pack your bags and come along!

events.title: Leadbeaters
events.description: Amsterdam-based string band that's actually way too rock 'n' roll for a folk outfit. This troupe, while not necessarily sporting beards, certainly possesses a confident and bold attitude, and has been touring the country for many years. Initially as an occasional formation for special events, they quickly transitioned to playing weekly gigs. And now, LEADBEATERS are no longer to be ignored in the local roots, folk, and Americana music scene. With a mixed grill of American traditions, old-time, rhythm & blues, bluegrass, Cajun, and some cumbia, the band offers a very danceable musical offering to the hopeless, the drummers, the poor, the troublemakers, and the outsiders. All in a rowdy fashion. The Leado's initially do this with fiddle, guitar, mandolin, double bass, and somewhat coordinated singing, but the heart and stage are large enough for passionate musical brothers and sisters.

events.title: Discover Yourself Through Art with Fleur Cecile Haak
events.description:
My name is Fleur Cecile. I am a writer, artist, and guide for authentic self-expression through creativity. I invite you into an intuitive journey of creative expression, a space to soften, connect, and remember your creative potential. Through art, words, color, and presence, we open the door to the essence of our being: the beauty and wisdom already living within us. Out of the mind and into the heart, creative expression reminds us of what words could never explain. It became my way home to myself, and now I love sharing that space with others. Let’s be colourful. Let’s play.
 Links:
 Website 
 Instagram

events.title: Join the Cult of Guru Lui Delulu
events.description: A 45 minute parody show to hold auditions for the new KALEIDO Kult for the klininally unhinged and untamed. Featuring a new 10 commandments all queered up and sex positive, conversion therapy hypnosis, lip-syncs and performances and a closing ritual.

events.title: HONOUR*REVIVE*SHARE - The Workshop
events.description:
Cato Coebergh, Sana van der Linge (Kikvors Collectief)

In our current fast-paced and materialistic world, we often overlook the waste we generate on a daily basis. At our workshop, we encourage people to view their waste in a new light: We will honor its history, and furthermore, we will revive it through a creative process. Sharing our stories and creations with each other.

Cato Coebergh: People produce far too much waste on a daily basis. Cato Coebergh addresses this issue as an artist by making conscious material choices and creating mindfulness workshops to raise awareness about the topic and offer new perspectives on everyday life.

Sana van der Lingen: Sana is a visual artist who combines traditional crafts with innovative techniques to create layered art and design objects. Her work ranges from graphic design and mixed-media prints, in which she primarily focuses on working with historical heritage, to diverse objects such as accessories or household items made from recycled materials, ceramics, and textiles.

events.title: Mantra & Cacao
events.description:
Mirthe invites you to write down a word, phrase, or intention. She will then sing and chant these texts live, accompanied by her cobalt blue harmonium.

This creates shared mantras and moments where personal words become audible in music.

Since returning from Tiru, India, Mirthe has been exploring art forms that center around voice and devotional singing. Mirthe is an artist and performer.

events.title: Vallei Orgasme
events.description:
For those who already have a ticket for the Landjuweel festival, we warmly invite you to this interactive workshop.

After an "I-power" meditation, we will discuss what "Valley" means in your life, answer questions, explain the stages of sexual development, and facilitate interaction among participants.

There may still be space for Chi Kung practice. We will conclude with a session on calming inner peace.

The tipi tent has limited capacity, so if it's full, it's full.

events.title: Heiko Dijker’s Soul Sangam - Nava Rasa
Shamballa Tribe
events.description: The Shamballa Vision Tribe is a spiritual and flow arts collective that hosts ecstatic singing, cacao ceremonies, and ancestral drumming in and around Netherlands and Belgium and in based in Ghent. They frequently organize urban singing, deep mantra singing and drum circles locally, as well as an annual "Shamballa Vision Festival".

events.title: Some Chemistry: Barbecue Space Station Set
events.description:
Some Chemistry, born into a family that traveled the world and found music to be their closest form of spirituality, is a new age nomad, and music is his first home. After leading an amateur drum ensemble in Burkina Faso, he became a guitarist for several indie-punk bands throughout The Netherlands, and eventually became captivated by the sound of Amsterdam's underground clubbing scene in the mid-2000s.

Intrigued by the infinite possibilities of a PA system and having developed a particular taste for alternative house and techno, he immersed himself into becoming a true ambassador of dance in Amsterdam by the early 2010s. If he is not in the studio or DJing, he is searching for new electronic talent and sharing his newfound discoveries by curating well-respected events in Amsterdam-based clubs such as Trouw, Studio 80, or Marktkantine, while also being invited to play at contemporary cutting-edge venues like Shelter, de Loft, Awakenings, Time Warp, Wildeburg, Welcome to the Future, Soenda, or often at Berlin's Wilde Renate.

events.title: The White Peacock Jam
events.description: A white peacock has been sighted in the Vlindertuin (Butterfly Garden). It has been heard reciting in French, denouncing the state of society with punk fury, and singing what, according to some, is the most beautiful song ever heard in the Vlindertuin. Rumours have circulated that this animal is attracted to musical instruments. So Arthur, your host for this event, decided to organize a jam session. Open to musicians of all levels, the only condition is no existing songs or covers. Improvised music only! Make those feathers and asses shake!

events.title: Kidd Nebula
events.description:
Eccentric FUNK, combined with Jazz and Hip-Hop inspired by great legends like Funkadelic/Parliament, Prince, James Brown and Jamiroquai. Kidd Nebula & The Voodoo-Funk Conspiracy transform every stage into a safe space for human connection through art, passion, and cosmic vibrations. This energetic six-piece band, fronted by Kidd Nebula, will take you on an intergalactic journey with heavy grooves and humorous themes that will sweep your feet off the dance floor!
 After releasing their new EP “BAD CHI” featuring four summer anthems, the band achieved their first international success during their seven-show EUROTOUR, playing packed venues in Prague (CZ), Budapest (HU) and Zagreb (HR). These Popronde (2026) selectees are making waves in the urban scene with their healing funky vibrations, opening for (international) names like Primaat (NL), Boukou Groove (USA), High Fade (SCH) and Prince bass player Ida Nielsen (SU).

 Excentrieke FUNK, gecombineerd met Jazz en Hip-Hop, geïnspireerd door legendes als Funkadelic/Parliament, Prince, James Brown en Jamiroquai.…

events.title: DJ Try/Me
events.description: DJ Try/Me presents: Dance Music for Birds of Paradise. Gender-blending & genre-bending.

events.title: Peacock Garden Mini Retreat Bubble
events.description:
A tiny escape with yoga, art, bubbles, music & good people. Find our reed retreat �� for a sweet way to glide into the weekend. Spoil yourself with care and connection and leave our bubble extra bubbly �� 
 All paradise birds extra super welcome. 
 18.00 – 18.45 Lazy Girl Yoga by Medooki
 19.00 – 19.45 Empathy Art by Emmy-Koos
 20.00 – 20.30 Face Massage by Kira
 20.30 – 21.00 Bubble Art Demo by Medooki 
 21.00 – 23.30 Sørenda (DJ) 
 23.30 – … Peacock b2b2b2b2b with Eli Lux, DJ Bubbles, DJooki & …

events.title: Kelsey Kluijtmans concert
events.description:
With a raw, heartfelt voice and soulful acoustic sound, she weaves music that invites deep reflection and transformation. Her songs explore the beauty of embracing our shadows and the power of opening the heart, while gently reminding us of life’s impermanence. Awakening gratitude for the present moment.
