 The more she delves into sound and resonance, the more she’s amazed by this powerful medicine. We are vibration, everything is vibration and frequency. Kelsey loves to guide people remembering their own soul frequency through the power of music and sound.
 Kelsey writes different types and styles of music, from English, Dutch, mantra to sound journeys. Music that touches the heart.
 Kelsey Kluijtmans: Singer Songwriter - Concerts - Soundhealing - Voice Liberation
 Links:
 Website

events.title: Polymer
events.description:
Polymer is Daniel Maalman, a producer, DJ and sound artist from The Netherlands, obsessed with sound in the broadest sense. From a young age Daniel was tinkering with computers and disassembling devices around the house to look how they worked. From the age of thirteen he started making electronic music with computers and tapedecks. Later, in art academy, his passion for sound evolved into building large installations, merging technology and sound, showing his sound obsession in an art context.
 Since many years Daniel moves back and forth between the world of electronic music, playing his music productions in parties and festivals as Polymer, and the world of sound art, exhibiting his creations as art installations. Performances together with visual artists and other performers has resulted in the audiovisual concept of The Polymer Experience, a liveshow where Polymers music is presented with an immersive visual show.
 In the last years he has created collaborative ‘exhibition concerts’, with his project Foundscape Orchestra, where he makes musical compositions using industrial buildings, materials and objects as a sound source.…

events.title: Oh Voyage
events.description:
Rotterdam-based duo Oh Voyage, takes listeners from the streets of Istanbul through the sunset into lightly dimmed nightclubs. Growing up in Istanbul and traveling endlessly through their music, Oh Voyage is an homage to the nomadic spirit of their ancestors as well as the drifters of our modern age.
 The duo’s unique sound blends Anatolian folk music with analog synthesizers into an emotional voyage, showcasing the duo's passion for bridging cultures through music on the dancefloor.
 Having played many shows around major cities throughout the world, Oh Voyage keeps delivering goosebumps and unforgettable live shows to loyal fans and new listeners. Their innovative approach is both fresh and rooted in their virtuosity of live instruments with electronic music productions.

events.title: Closing remarks
events.description: After a day filled with discovery, learning, making, and meeting new people, we come together to quietly conclude the day. It's a moment for reflection, to consider what has touched us and to share our experiences with each other. While enjoying a warm cup of chaga tea, we allow ourselves to be guided by the moment. Perhaps we sing together, do a simple yoga exercise, tell stories to each other, or simply enjoy being together.

events.title: Music Carpet
events.description:
Magique Jean Jean, Cato Fluitsma, and Ian Merkoulov are "Life Artists" who love to give others a voice and expression.

Musical Carpet
Magique Jean Jean, Cato Fluitsma, and Ian Merkoulov weave together with friends and family a "Musical Carpet" of the day. Using sounds, words, phrases, and notes, we create a carpet that can be unrolled and enjoyed.

Would you like to join us?

events.title: Más - La Familia Volante
events.description:
La Familia Volante, consisting of Marieke, Hans, and Yara (8), is a circus family that spends the winter in Portugal and travels during the summer, enjoying various festivals with their acrobatic clown performances and family acro workshops.

Màs!

This acrobatic family loves to play, throw, and tumble...and is always looking for playmates. Who wants to join them? They will perform their interactive, acrobatic clown show, Màs, which could even turn into an acrobatic playground for the whole family... After the performance, there's time for everyone to play together and learn acrobatic tricks from the family!

events.title: Church of the Untamed
events.description:
Leave your inhibitions at the door and enter the Church of the Untamed. Join the unholy communion of dance, mischief and collective joy. 
 Through a guided, interactive dance ritual, we’ll unleash our wild and expressive selves. Let's drop into our bodies, come alive together and let life loose through the limbs. 
 Come and celebrate every part of you, the primal and the playful, the sacred and the savage, the fabulous and the feral. 
 All creatures are welcome here.

events.title: Reed Jewel for Voice Preservation with Yente & Ocean
events.description: We are Yente and Ocean, two passionate creatives and enthusiastic conversationalists. With the Riet(j)uweel for Voice Preservation, we're setting the tone so you can enjoy your voice throughout the entire weekend. We won't reveal too much, but be sure to bring your water bottle! We look forward to welcoming you at The Temple of Self!

events.title: Cronos - Bardo Teatro Físico
events.description:
The company started in 2016, with the objetive of creating a play that combines circus techniques with physical theater.
 The artistic searches of the creators lead to the same path, making the fusion between the theater and the circus, our scenic language. We are interested in moving from a performance show to a theatrical poetics and that circus resources and skills are deeply linked with a dramaturgy. In this dynamic, acrobats are modified by the text and transcend the purely physical.

events.title: Psychedelic Tea Party
events.description: A vibrant blend of flavors, scents, and sounds, inviting you to indulge your senses and enjoy a delightful tea experience.

events.title: Sjef Rolet
events.description:
With over 20 years of experience as a DJ and producer, Sjef Rolet is renowned for his ability to move people in any setting. As a passionate dancer, he has delved into countless rhythms and musical styles – particularly those from Africa and the Americas.

Together with Jo Bissa, he forms the duo UMOJA, beloved for their dubby, tropical sound that has been traveling around the world for over ten years. Their work has received support from influential labels and platforms such as Wonderwheel, Hot Casa, Analog Africa, Multi Culti, Mawimbi, and INI Movement, the Haarlem-based label where Sjef is closely involved.

As a solo DJ, he is an tireless crate digger – someone who draws endlessly from the world's musical archives, but also stays on top of the latest music trends. Hidden gems, modern underground sounds, or idiosyncratic versions of global hits – all sounds that transcend everyday reality.

events.title: CERA
events.description: CERA has become a familiar face at KALEIDO. Last year, she almost took over the Queer Space stage, so that promises something... CERA is known for being an energetic and enthusiastic DJ, always looking for the right vibe: sometimes with recognizable tunes, sometimes high-energy, or even layered and eclectic. Expect sexy house, disco, and occasional world music influences, but above all, expect lots of fun and queer joy!

events.title: Intimate haircuts
events.description: Intimate Haircuts aim to give your junk the funky twist Landjuweel calls for. Ranadhira reshapes, bleaches, colours, paints and decorates your pubic area to create an unforgettable art piece. Find your favorite hairdresser in the KALEIDO Faerie Forest!

events.title: The Beatzers
events.description: The Beatzers! In an era of blandness, they bring more style than many can even imagine. This five-member ensemble loves to surprise the audience with songs of varying caliber. And they do it all with a cheerful attitude. They play their ska, folk, and related genres with extra energy.

events.title: Creatures on the Loose
events.description:
Ecstatic Trance Journey 
 A ritual of rhythm, rapture, and return to the essence 
 With rhythm and dance as their central medicine, the Creatures on the Loose invite you into a wild ritual of sound, sweat, and soul. Fusing tribal grooves, shamanic trance, ethereal soundscapes and deep live improvisation, their set is more than music—it’s a rite of liberation. Expect waves of pulsing energy, moments of deep surrender, and a call to awaken your inner animal. This is a primal celebration, a mythic dancefloor, a collective trance. So come—rise with your inner fire, and shake the wild creature within you loose.
 Links:
 Instagram

events.title: DJ Witte Aap
events.description: Not a DJ, but a music selector playing wonderful tunes from all over the world!

events.title: Open Jam Session
events.description: Bring your instrument or just join in! No rules, no stage pressure, just making music together. Whether you've been playing for years or are joining for the first time, everyone is welcome to jam, listen, and inspire each other musically, or simply enjoy what emerges.

events.title: Speeddaten
events.description: Join in, make new connections and meet interesting people. Friends, festival buddies, or maybe even more. A series of short encounters that can be anything: surprising, inspiring, awkward in a fun way, or exactly what you didn't expect. Sit down, talk, switch seats – and see what happens.

events.title: Gilian Baracs and band
events.description:
Gilian Baracs is a pianist, composer, and singer. He studied jazz piano at the Amsterdam Conservatory and effortlessly moves between improvisation and songcraft. With his band, he presents new material from his upcoming album: original songs that balance pop, groove, jazz, and free improvisation.

The music can be danceable, poetic, carefully composed, or completely spontaneous. In all cases, connection is central: between musicians, the audience, and the story that wants to unfold at that moment.
For this special festival show, the band will be accompanied by special guests, including singer Lex Empress.

Line-up
Gilian Baracs - keyboards, vocals
Frank Hopman - drums
Martin Zand Scholten - bass
Jur The Fresh - guitar
Robbert Scherpenisse - trumpet
João Driessen - saxophone
Special guests
Lex Empress - vocals
Vocalist(s) TBA

events.title: Dizzy Fingers
events.description:
Dizzy Fingers play Gypsy Swing, but with a completely unique style. Fans of Django Reinhardt will enjoy it. The rest of the audience will be won over by the energetic, virtuoso, and very danceable sounds that the band produces.

In short: you'll find the hottest and most swinging jazz party in Landjuweel '26 at The Willow Portal. On Friday evening at 7:00 PM, we're getting started!

events.title: DJ Yellow Rose
events.description: A techno set featuring high-energy melodic techno, tasty acid sounds, progressive and happy house bangers, indie dance music, and occasional bursts of organic, driving beats. A two-hour long, irresistible, intense, and utterly fantastic experience!

events.title: Molly Cules
events.description: As a chemistry teacher, Molly Cules likes to mix things up! He combines bouncy beats, cunty flair and some spicy meringue into one set. Shake your slutty hips, lose your voice and prepare for a PARTY.

events.title: Landjuweel Temple Space
events.description:
Amidst the electric chaos of the festival, a sacred portal awaits. Welcome to Temple Space: a sanctuary designed to pause the buzz and ignite your inner world.

Ready for a journey inward? Embark on a breathtaking journey through your senses and desires, anchored by deep somatic movement and connective practices. This is your playground to claim what you want, honour your boundaries, and speak your truth through authentic, heart-centred communication. Bring your light, your shadow, and everything in between—you are completely welcome here, bound only by the safety and boundaries of those around you. Check in with our Temple Gate Keepers at the threshold to receive the container guidelines before you step inside.

Temple Space is located in the largest yurt on the Natural High grounds. Temple hours: Thursday 21.00-24.00, Friday 20.00-23.00 and Sunday 20.00-23.00.
Landjuweel’s Temple Space is curated by Iskra Bela and inspired by temple arts culture, contemporary dance, ritual-performance practice and ecstatic dance. Invited co-creators this year are Lexmy van den Boogaard and Bee-bee (Bregje).

Lexmy van den Boogaard…

events.title: Meraki
events.description:
BORN IN BRAZIL, PEDRO SUGIURA MUSIC HAS A HUGE INFLUENCE OF HIS CONTINENT'S RICH DIVERSITY OF RHYTHMS. CURRENTLY LIVING IN AMSTERDAM, MERAKI HAS A UNIQUE IDENTITY AND PRESENTS A VERY ENERGETIC SOUND SIGNATURE. THE ARTIST HAS TRACKS RELEASED WITH NAMES LIKE DANDARA, SPANIOL, SALVADOR ARAGUAYA, GERRA G KOTOE AND OCTO SHARK
 BEGGINING HIS MUSICAL EXPERIENCE IN PSYTRANCE AS A TEENAGER LISTENER, AND LATER GETTING IN TOUCH WITH DOWNTEMPO AS A DJ, HE STARTED HIS JOURNEY WITH THE BRAZILIAN CREW SONIDO TROPICO IN 2016.
 ALWAYS SEEKING BALANCE BETWEEN THESE TWO CONTRASTING STYLES OF ELECTRONIC MUSIC, HIS MIXES ALWAYS RESULTS IN A FLOWY AND GROOVY ATMOSPHERE.
 MERAKI DID A EUROTOUR IN 2017 PLAYING IN 8 DIFFERENT COUNTRIES AS LISTED BELOW. DURING THE LAST YEARS IN BRAZIL, HE WAS CONSTANTLY PLAYING AMONG THE UNDERGROUND SCENE PARTIES AND FESTIVALS
 HIS LAST JOB WAS A RESIDENCY AT AMATA VENUE IN BRAZIL.
 Links:
 Facebook 
 Soundcloud 
 Instagram

events.title: Wasim Arslan
events.description:
Wasim Arslan is a Syrian-born artist who channels the spirit of Aleppo through the vibrant energy of Amsterdam, where he resides.

He transforms classical Arabic melodies into something that resonates – Levantine rhythm, cinematic depth, and contemporary production.

He has established himself on stage: over 150 performances, a live band, and venues that feel like one cohesive entity.

His sound is rooted in heritage and nostalgia, representing a new generation of Arab music crafted for global audiences.

events.title: Rave Baby - Sandra Kaas
events.description:
Sandra Kaas is a surrealist costume and prop designer, and she has now created her first theatrical show.

"Rave Baby" is an absurdist puppet theatre performance about a pregnant woman who fears that motherhood will mean the end of her rave life.

events.title: Josje & the Catalyst
events.description:
A unique dance set where DJ, live guitar and live vocals merge into one flowing performance.
 The Catalyst (Camillo) builds a hypnotic flow of organic, tribal beats, woven together with world fusion and mystical Arabic influences. With his live guitar playing, he adds a unique layer of depth that enriches the set while also lifting it to new heights.
 Josje’s intuitive live vocals bring a mystical and powerful layer into the set, adding an extra dimension to the music. Her voice tunes into the energy of the dance floor and translates it in the moment into sound and expression.
 Together they create a living set: deep, earthy, and immersive. It is inviting you to sink in, open up, and let your body move. From pulsing tribal rhythms to soulful sounds, flowing towards a gentle, musical landing at the end.

events.title: Kitano Band
events.description:
KITANO is a Mexican pianist, composer, and producer based in Amsterdam.
 This concert is the presentation of his new album, a journey through Jazz Fusion, Hip-Hop, Ambient, and Electronic music. Built around groove, improvisation, and rich textures, the project brings together some of the best musicians in Amsterdam, blending acoustic and electronic sounds in a way that feels both organic and contemporary.
 Rooted in groove as both a musical language and a communal experience, Kitano’s compositions invite listeners into a space where synthesizers, acoustic instruments, improvisation, and beat-driven rhythms coexist naturally. The music moves fluidly between intimate ambient moments, explosive fusion passages, and head-nodding hip-hop influences, reflecting the multicultural and genre-defying spirit of contemporary urban life.
 Inspired by experiences across Mexico, Paris, and Amsterdam, KITANO creates music that moves freely between genres while celebrating connection, spontaneity, and the joy of making music together.

events.title: Dakka Sultana
events.description: Dakka Sultana represents connection, tradition, and celebration. During performances, the group brings not only music, but also a cultural experience that celebrates the rich Moroccan heritage and brings people together through rhythm and energy.

events.title: Motorknüller: Madmotormiquel & Heimlich Knüller
events.description: Since the magical 1980s, the species known as Raver focused on creating the perfect dance move to express on this dance floor of life. Around the same time, Madmotormiquel — a young boy from the limited-travel zone of West Berlin — decided he wanted to find a sound that knew no limits to their dedication. Oh shit, another DJ is born. Armed with an expanding taste in classic punk rock and hip-hop, soon he was led into the world of powerful beats that the future-forming kids around him loved so much. He met them in the church of acid techno, big-beat, and drum and bass. In these places, he jockeyed the discs for many weekends until it was clear he needed to have a say on the beats himself. Here, under the light of a crescent moon, a new producer was born. By now, the Berlin Wall was well and truly down, and you could even buy small pieces of it from the shop. This meant his home city was filled with more space than the ravers knew how to sing and dance about. With that, they squatted its old factories and made techno rituals in the neighboring fields.…

events.title: The Seventh Colour presents: The Floating Fantasy
events.description: Violet aka ‘De Zevende Kleur’ is a performing artist. Her work reveals around body and sound, tension and space, image and imagination.

events.title: Isaie
events.description:
Isaie is a selector, curator, and long-time collector whose sets effortlessly connect house, disco, italo, Afro rhythms, techno, high-NRG, world music, and forgotten dancefloor gems. Over the years, he has released music under various aliases on respected labels including Rush Hour Music, Compost Records, XXX Record Label, Voyage Direct, Home Taping Is Killing Music and Kolour Recordings.

He is also the founder of In The Black Square, an online streaming radio station and event platform dedicated to adventurous underground music and cultural exchange.

Approaching DJing as musical storytelling rather than genre boundaries, DJ Isaie creates journeys that move organically between deep and percussive house, obscure disco, driving techno, Afro rhythms, high-NRG music, and rhythm-driven music from across the globe. Blending contemporary club music with cultural influences, rare edits, and forgotten classics, he creates dancefloors that feel both timeless and unpredictable.…

events.title: GUTEN ABEND
events.description: GUTEN ABEND is back, and once again he invites you into his pixelated virtual world, where he blasts nu-disco, synthwave, house and indie dance. There's a whole lot more KALEIDOVERSE left to explore, and GUTEN ABEND is your musical guide for this chapter of the journey. Recommended but not required: fast sunglasses and anti-gravity suits.

events.title: Flamingo hitmix
events.description:
This three-bird oscillation orchestra is far more than your average cover band, yet fits on a street corner. They perform 90s classics live, with a sugar coated beat. You might feel ecstatic, rejoiced, or even touched by long-forgotten or freshly recalled dance-upon tunes.
 At the heart of the show: vocalist Aliesje, a multi-decadal dance music chameleon. She is energetically supported by Joris and Martinus on synths and sounds. Straight from Flamingo street Amsterdam Noord, Flamingo Hitmix traverses the past and present of floor-filling club-classics.

events.title: Misfit Valley
events.description:
Lekker Limbs (Kikvors Collectief)

You are invited into a surreal, childlike universe of strange dreamscapes & playful chaos. What begins as innocent play transforms into a collective experience, a strange mission that the audience must navigate together.

Lekker Limbs is a performance duo by Inês Certo and Sara Tompkins.
They are artists working across performance, visual art, music, movement, and digital media.
Since they met, a year ago at Landjuweel where they both performed, the artists have performed together another two times, at Fuite Cosmique in France and at Time is The New Space in Rotterdam.
Inês and Sara are particularly interested in exploring their own unique style, experimenting with participation, absurdity, visual storytelling, and live interaction.

events.title: Stray Elf
events.description: Stray Elf weaves intricate tracks to create an elevated mood on the dancefloor. Taking you to all sorts of magical places with trippy beats and psychedelic atmospheres!

events.title: Flavour Coalition
events.description: Flavour Coalition is a multinational roots reggae band with six members from Amsterdam and the surrounding area. It's reggae music with a political message that fights against poverty, inequality, and corruption. The music has roots in Africa, South America, and Europe.

events.title: DJ Witte Aap
events.description: Not a DJ, but a music selector playing wonderful tunes from all over the world!

events.title: Kosmies Wilgenportaal
events.description: Kosmies: Your Intergalactic Home Away From Home.

events.title: REO MATSUMOTO- DJ Set with live Handpan
events.description:
REO MATSUMOTO's captivating DJ / LIVE Hybrid Set are rooted in a unique blend of Ethnic & World music grooves with Afro House,Deep House and Organic House, incorporating deep tribal rhythms, Street spirit and psychedelic elements.
 His signature Hybrid Live set is enhanced by live instrumentation, featuring the HandPan, beatboxing, and percussion, creating a seamless DJ/Live hybrid set. REO’s performances promise to take music enthusiasts on an uplifting, mesmerizing, otherworldly journey.

events.title: CLUBSYNTH : Tectonia
events.description:
CLUBSYNTH creates the beats that get you moving. On a stage filled with modular synthesizers, various artists perform LIVE electronic dance music. Hosted by Amsterdam-based synth builders, This is Not Rocket Science. Connecting the dancefloor.

22:00 – 23:00 Tectonia
This synth freak creates sonic waves and breaks them live into melodic pieces. Unique cinematic and melodic rave. A symphony of sound.

events.title: The Rise, Life and Death of Don Quijote
events.description:
Thijs van de Loo – Theatermaker & Hessel Pijnaker, artist; combine their talents to bring five dolls to life, revolving around the theme of metamorphosis.

“Using rattan, fabric, inner tubes and found objects, and a good dose of intuition, we create soulful objects. Both have studied Vodun religions such as Ubuntu and draw inspiration from them.”

events.title: INOYSON & ANYA [Hybrid Live DJ set]
events.description:
You are invited into a universe of lucid dreams through a Hybrid Live DJ set, offering a new perception of spiritual electronica, neoclassical trip-hop, acoustic techno, psychedelic avant-garde and alternative jazz fusion.
 INOYSON is the lucid dream universe of a film composer, multi-instrumentalist, producer, vocalist, songwriter, painter and consciousness explorer.
 INOYSON’s style is cinematic music for imaginary films — soundscapes you can see in your mind while listening to this musical voyage.
 This time at magical Kabouterhuis, experience a new program «Soul Flames Groove» together with DJ ANYA.
 Tomas Mazoes joins on percussion.
 See you there, dear souls.
 Links:
 Facebook 
 Instagram 
 Spotify 
 Youtube

events.title: Rochelle Dietz
events.description:
Rochelle Dietz' sets are a reflection of her gentle, yet dynamic spirit — capturing the essence of melodic, introspective moments while unleashing bursts of energy when the time is right. She brings a distinct perspective, blending rich melodies with unpredictable, captivating rhythms which keep the dancefloor alive.

Rochelle has collaborated with events such as Wildeburg, Into the Woods, Gardens of Babylon and Landjuweel, and is occasionally found behind the decks in bars and clubs in Berlin. She is an active member of the Utrecht creative community and her concept, Birdcage Radio, provides a platform for young and established artists to flourish.

events.title: Jimmy Moon
events.description:
Jimmy has always been interested in different genres of music. From a very young age, he was inspired by genres such as rock, classical, Latin, and electronic music. Jimmy was born in Segovia, Spain, and at only 7 years old, he began studying percussion and classical music, which led him to play in several orchestras and, later, in various bands, touring the country and performing classical and rock music. It wasn't until he was 14 that Jimmy first heard EDM and instantly fell in love with the genre. Starting to produce and play soon after, his ambition to transmit his energy and creativity in his sets, has led him to play in different international clubs, such as Club Womb (Tokyo), Singel Klub (Basel), Club Atelier (Amsterdam), and Club Hart (CDMX), and to be part of the line-up of reference festivals such as Amsterdam Dance Event in 2023 and 2024, where he performed at Q-Factory and Baut.
 Between 2024 and 2025, Jimmy relocated to Saudi Arabia, a move that would prove pivotal in his artistic journey. Immersed in the rich textures of Arabic music and sound culture, he drew deep inspiration from the region's sonic heritage, weaving new influences into his already eclectic palette.…

events.title: Cedric Vermue ft Tjalle Rens
events.description: Cedric Vermue creates neo-classical and ambient music, where piano, strings, and electronic elements blend together to create a mesmerizing soundscape. Together with cellist Tjalle Rens, they perform a live ambient set at Landjuweel in Theo's tea garden, featuring piano and cello mixed with electronics and synthesizers.

events.title: Djembefola's
events.description:
Momo Touré

Momo Touré is the heart and soul of the drum circle at Ruigoord. For over 30 years, he has been the person who brings people together through music, using his djembe, his energy, and his gift for making everyone, from beginners to advanced players, feel part of the groove.

His roots are deeply embedded in traditional West African music. As a young man, he toured worldwide with the renowned African ballet in Guinea. He still carries this richness with him every week at Ruigoord.

Come, see, and participate!

Amier Sloot

Amier Sloot believes in the power of rhythm to bring people together. As a musician and community builder, he facilitates a drum circle every Saturday at Ruigoord alongside Momo, where everyone, regardless of experience, can immerse themselves in a shared groove and be fully present in the moment.

During Landjuweel, they (along with many other respected drummers) bring this energy: connection through music, together in the moment.

events.title: Meraque
events.description:
Mèraque originates from a passion for Balkan music and curiosity about its fusion with psychedelic sounds and Middle Eastern influences. The musicians Jorick Bronius, Martin Grozdanoski, Onur Kaletas, and Talal Fayad met each other at the Rotterdam Conservatory and share a background in world music and sound design. Their collaboration results in an energetic and refined sonic universe where Eastern melodies and Mediterranean rhythms come together.

Mèraque has performed at venues and festivals such as Wildeburg (NL), Lost Gipsy Land (Bulgaria), 3000grad Festival (DE) and Fusion Festival (DE), and released music through labels like Ohxala Records, Harabe, and Krooks Records.

events.title: Quintens's Birthday Bash
events.description:
Friday night in the Salon: en Quinten celebrates his birthday with his musical friends!

The Elements Fellowship:
Quinten Rutgers
Hassie Dune
Ania Brzezinskia
Wasim Arslan
Mame N'Diack Thiam
Arda Yesiltepe
Joao Vedana
Sumanth Manjunath
Tritha Sinha
Sharrif Simmons
DJ Grazzhoppa

events.title: DJ PaGLI-Indian Dance Electronica
events.description:
PaGLi is Tritha's electronic avatar. Here, she plays her original electronic collaborations and the best tunes picked up from her travels around the world. Using the concept of Indian scales and loops, analog sounds and beats, PaGLi's intention as a DJ is to blend the best organic, pure vibes with a good selection of digital sound frequencies. "PaGLi" means crazy! And her multi-genre DJ set proves it! Her digital releases have been on Ibiza-based Sudam Music in collaboration with French techno producer Hannah Hais and Berlin-based HiFi/LoFi Records with collaborators World Wide Web & London-based Drum & Bass producers Janaka Selekta & Marzman J. She has also been featured on Chinese Man Records based in France for her highly acclaimed song "Balma". Tritha is a vocalist, composer, and music producer based out of India and Europe. She has combined her highly trained Indian classical voice with genres such as Indian dance electronica, acid house, disco, dancehall reggae, drum & bass, and Bollywood retro. Tritha was also featured by "Grimes", an American sensation, on her Instagram list of the best original tracks and artists from around the world.

Links:
Website
Youtube

events.title: Connective tissue
events.description: A collective ritual of connection guided by XOTO from KALEIDO. Expect lights, music & intensity. Perfect if you want to feel connected to a higher plane of existence. Until we let the dance floor explode with energy together!

events.title: Anxiety as a Gift
events.description:
Farah Shretah, also known as Alien Mama (Kikvors Collectief)

"Anxiety as a Gift" is a ritual in which the subconscious becomes a shared journey of moving images created by you, designed by your attention. A body stands inside the circle, carrying hidden storms that surface as vivid visual landscapes.
What was meant to remain unseen begins to unfold: fragments, memories, symbols, and inner signals made public. Through the collective gaze, anxiety is transformed from a private shadow into a shared living image.

Farah Shretah is a Syrian transmedia artist, filmmaker, and XR producer based in Amsterdam. Her work spans across places, languages, and forms: born in Syria, shaped by her life in Ukraine, and later rooted in the Netherlands, where she completed her master's degree in visual culture and artistic research.

As the founder of Alien Mama Productions, Farah creates immersive works at the intersection of emotion, technology, AI, biofeedback, performance, and storytelling. Her practice transforms inner states such as fear, anxiety, and memory into shared audiovisual experiences, inviting audiences into poetic spaces where vulnerability becomes image, movement, and collective presence.

events.title: CLUBSYNTH : noedels!
events.description:
CLUBSYNTH creates the beats that get you moving. On a stage filled with modular synthesizers, various artists perform LIVE electronic dance music. Hosted by Amsterdam-based synth builders, This is Not Rocket Science. Connecting the dancefloor.

23:00 – 01:00 hours!
Live beats, synths & vocals. These 3 artists combine Drum ‘n Bass with Funky Garage and snippets of Electro. Umami melodies with a spicy aftertaste.

events.title: Link & Herr Alt
events.description: Maya Link & Herr Alt create electronic music in Dutch, at the intersection of performance, poetry, and club music. With honest lyrics, deep basslines, and energetic beats, they take you on a life-affirming rave concert full of anger, love, and everything in between. Their live show, Galbi (Arabic for heart and soul), is a danceable journey through the heart. Let yourself be swept away.

events.title: JXSSX
events.description: Jxssx, aka Jesse, knows how to musically fire up a dancefloor. His sets are like journeys crossing different atmospheric landscapes, ranging from more deep and organic to extatic, always with elements of fun and surprise. He is a storyteller in sound, with meticulously knitted sets that have a sense of rave at the core.

events.title: Spull
events.description:
Spull is a 9-piece international ensemble from Amsterdam, known for its instrumental funk-fueled soul and uplifting afro-jazz.
 With a lineup featuring three horns, two percussionists, drums, bass, keys, and guitar, Spull brings a high-energy live show packed with hip-moving grooves, raving horn melodies, and a spicy touch of cinematic filth.
 Kicking off their journey in Amsterdam’s club circuit (Cinetol, OCCII, De Nieuwe Anita), Spull quickly gained momentum, hitting stages like the Breda Jazz Festival, the Jazz Festival Delft, the Over de Top Festival and with international dates now on the horizon.


 Spull is a band from Amsterdam with international roots, that combines styles such as Funk, Soul and Afro-jazz into a swinging groove explosion.
 The nine-piece formation, consisting of three brass instruments, two percussionists, drums, keys, bass and guitar, always ensures an overwhelming show full of tight themes and captivating improvisations. This means no show is the same, but an unforgettable party with Spull is always guaranteed!

events.title: Darkvoice
events.description:
Fear and Loathing in Ruigoord: Darkvoice Live @ Landjuweel

Since 1996, he has been working behind the scenes on his own music, but this year, Darkvoice is finally stepping into the spotlight for his very first live debut, performing for a unique audience. No DJ set with other people's records, but a 100% original story full of analog elements and live vocals.

The Trip: Hunter S. Thompson
The first part of the set will be dedicated to Fear and Loathing in Las Vegas. A rebellious, nineties-inspired rollercoaster where Darkvoice takes on the persona of Hunter S. Thompson. Expect an energetic, cinematic epic with a strong nod to the sound of Underworld, The Chemical Brothers, Prodigy, Orbital, and Massive Attack.

The Landing: Positive Buoyancy
In the second part, the journey turns inward. Darkvoice takes you on a personal, emotional journey with live versions of his album Positive Buoyancy. An introspective search for balance between fatherhood, love, deep grief, and the dark temptations of life. The result? Solid, immersive beats with a raw emotional charge…

events.title: Cuervo Cuervo
events.description:
Cuervo Cuervo is the collaboration between Argentinian musician and charango player Agustin Ganem, and American producer and multi-instrumentalist Benjamin Hill.

After a chance encounter in the streets of Bacalar in 2018, they joined forces to create a unique fusion of psychedelic electronic music blended with Latin American folk music and live improvisation. They are known for their electrifying live performances featuring virtuoso displays of musicianship, tasteful electronics, live looping, and a capacity for improvisation and collaboration. Ganem combines his mastery of South American charango and guitar with live looping, while Hill fuses North American electronic music with the folk influences of lapsteel guitar and classic Latin percussion elements such as caxixi and bongo. No two Cuervo Cuervo live performances are ever alike – each is a unique improvisational dialogue between the two that is created directly on the spot for each audience.

Links:
Website

events.title: Klam: DJSelene b2b Daktari
events.description: Night has fallen, but the coolness is nowhere to be found. It's Klam. Here, your inner animal can roam free. DJ Selene and Daktari move from darkness to light, from water to fire, from comfort to raw energy, and back again. Male strength and female divinity. Be yourself on these beats with benefits.

events.title: DJ Witte Aap
events.description: Not a DJ, but a music selector playing wonderful tunes from all over the world!

events.title: The Wet and Naughty Show
events.description:
Amy Pratt (Kikvors Collectief)
 The Wet and Naughty Show, where 1900s burlesque meets amphibians and fish. What could burlesque have looked like if the performers had taken back their power after businessmen demanded the dancers show more skin? A theatrical performance inspired by amphibians and fish.
 As a costume designer, Amy Pratt creates work centered on colorful tactility and humor. Her practice explores ways to transform heavier themes into something humorous, while highlighting the bold and expressive handwriting of her costumes. Through performances and immersive experiences she invites the viewer to step into a new and vibrant reality.

events.title: Stefnitz
events.description:
Activate the smoke machine! Let the disco balls fly! Come and see: Stefnitz jumps onto this Landjuweel booth in the cozy Boogie Bos for an extended solo DJ set! It's a special opportunity because since she brought life to this dancefloor in 2022, she has never played the wheels of steel on her own. Time for a 3-hour journey through her collection of quirky, wonky, happy, spacey, weird, punchy, edgy, bubbly, and dubby electronica!

 Get the smoke machines ready! Turn on the disco balls! Come right up: this Landjuweel Stefnitz jumps behind the cozy Boogie Bos booth for an extended solo set!
 It's a first because ever since she brought life to this dancefloor in 2022, she has never played the wheels of steel with only her own two hands. High time for a 3-hour journey through her collection of quirky, wonky, happy, spacey, weird, punchy, edgy, bubbly, and dubby electronica!

events.title: DJ Captain Spank
events.description:
Happy/melodic/up-tempo dance world-music.
 (Ska + Balkan + ElectroSwing + Tribal + Oriental + Rockabilly + Punk + Flamenco +++…) I often have musicians playing live during my sets to add an extra beat/sound (sax/percu/etc...)

events.title: Jam session with Carlos Manso
events.description: Jamsession every night after the program on the Rebel Stage until festival's closing time. The jamsessions here are legendary. We have a group of very good musicians carrying the jam. Tell musicians to come and join us. Please don't just jump on stage first contact the session leader.

events.title: Andert Tysma
events.description:
Andert Tysma is a Dutch composer and producer working at the intersection of contemporary classical music, ambient electronics and immersive sound design. His work combines piano, modular synthesis and cinematic textures into deeply atmospheric compositions that explore memory, transformation and the emotional landscapes that shape our lives.
 Following his albums Children of Trinoom (2022) and Hana (2024) on Apollo/R&S Records, Tysma returns in 2026 with Parallels, his third full-length album on R&S Records. The album reflects on the lives we live and the lives we don't, embracing the complexity of a life shaped by choice, chance and circumstance. It explores the unseen possibilities that accompany every decision and the many versions of ourselves that remain just beyond reach. Moving between darkness and light, tension and resolution, Parallels traces how identity is gradually formed through the accumulation of choices, while remaining open to the mystery of all that could have unfolded differently. 
 A seasoned musician, Tysma spent years touring internationally before focusing on his own artistic practice.…

events.title: Duin
events.description:
Duin is a poetic performance featuring nude models filmed up close on stage. These images are projected live and accompanied by cellist Tjalle Rens, creating a transformative and mesmerizing landscape.

Sam van Zoest is a filmmaker and writer. He draws his inspiration from the relationship between humans and nature.

events.title: Origins: Krien & Merante  Heroine’s Journey
events.description:
Origins: Krien & Merante create ritualistic, interdisciplinary art experiences at the intersection of performance, music, poetry, nature, and myth. Our performances are a vehicle for transformation—personal, ecological, and collective. In a world that sometimes seems to be drifting away from its roots, we build experiences where people can reconnect—with themselves, each other, and the greater whole.
 The Heroine’s Journey is an immersive, theatrical ceremony, inspired by and based on the mythic Hero’s Journey, as described by Joseph Campbell, and is performed in 7 chapters. Become a fellow traveler on a journey that takes you from your home grounds into the wide world and its temptations, through the ‘dark night of the soul’, to emerge wiser from it.
 For Landjuweel 2026 Origins: Krien & Merante will work together with very exciting collaborating artists: the mystical Gurdjieff Quartet, ritualist Myrgon, captivating sarangi player Vincent Swierstra and aroma jockey Odo7, who will accompany the heroine on her journey.

events.title: State of the Aart
events.description: At Landjuweel, State of the Aart delivers some seriously solid techno, but always with a killer groove. Armed with his Model 1.4, Aart is able to find the perfect balance. Not too loud, not too soft, but an insistent, rolling trip. No nonsense, just a really good sound that keeps you going all night long. Tested on people, and it works.

events.title: AMYIR
events.description:
Amyir (Western Australia) is the fresh collaboration between married couple Amy Cooke, playing live didgeridoo, and Amir Galili, behind the decks. They perform a hypnotic live set merging the didgeridoos organic vibration with modern electronic sounds that grounds audiences and sends them into ecstatic motion.
 Amy brings over 20 years of didgeridoo experience to the project and is returning to Landjuweel after previously performing as part of the band Loonaloop. Together, Amyir combines live instrumentation, immersive electronic soundscapes, and tribal energy into a powerful musical journey.

events.title: Beccc
events.description:
Being a seasoned performer under Shady Lady has now given Beccc the opportunity to go back to her USB and play the heavier sounds she never played before. Think hard-house grooves and 90s rave sprinkled into her classic driving energy. 
 Beccc will turn the KALEIDO takeover of the church to a whole new cosmic level.

events.title: The Altar of Flesh
events.description: Offer all your body parts to the Cult and lube yourself to pure pleasure...

events.title: Xamanoz
events.description:
Xamanoz is more than just a musical project – it's a meeting of souls, a sonic journey that travels through time, nature, and spirit. Formed by two brothers who met nearly 30 years ago, Xamanoz was born from a deep connection built through decades of friendship, shared experiences, and life paths.

From the beginning, music has been their central bond – not only as a form of expression, but also as a tool for transformation. Inspired by ancestral rhythms and the power of natural elements, they created their own language, where drums, diverse instruments, and original sounds intertwine to form unique soundscapes.

The sound of Xamanoz moves between worlds: from the pulsating energy of the forest to the mystical vibrations of Egypt, exploring dimensions beyond the physical. Each performance becomes a sensory experience, inviting the audience to dive deep within themselves while connecting to something greater.

Their music is alive, organic, and ritualistic – capable of leading into deep introspection or the ecstasy of dance, always guided by the intention to awaken, heal, and expand.

Above all, Xamanoz is a celebration of connection: between people, cultures, times, and elements…

events.title: Metik
events.description:
Metik is the Amsterdam–Berlin based duo of guitarist Bram Stadhouders and drummer Luca Marini. Long-time friends and seasoned performers, they bring over two decades of international touring experience into a refined blend of dreamy electronica and melodic IDM, music that engages the body as much as the mind.
 Educated in jazz and contemporary classical composition, they approach electronic music from a deeply instrumental perspective. Acoustic drums, percussion, guitars and a Bolivian charango intertwine in spacious yet grooving, evolving compositions. Balancing club-oriented pulse with concert-hall detail, their music is cinematic and atmospheric yet grounded in groove.
 Their debut album, out on Atomnation Records, was recorded in a home studio overlooking Lake Lugano. The surrounding landscape, its stillness, depth and shifting light, subtly shaped the atmosphere of the record, becoming an integral part of its sonic identity.

events.title: Rhei x Kahateka
events.description: Rhei and Kahateka are the driving forces behind the House of Chi Collective. In addition to functional cocktails and adaptogenic drops, these "birds of paradise" also offer mellow downtempo music for a more relaxed atmosphere.

events.title: Temple DJ's
events.description:
A slight change in our little gem: Unfortunately, DJ Shivanki will not be able to join us on Friday from 02.00 to 05.00.

However, Temple is undeterred. Sometimes the route changes when you're already on your way. Our Temple DJs step up, delve deep into the furthest corners of their USBs, and bring everything that has been waiting for exactly this moment for years.
Prepare yourself for a whirlwind night. A musical relay race without a map, carried by the people, this place, this moment, and everything that happens between 2am and sunrise.
Those who seek the path will miss the turn.

events.title: Night Drumming
events.description:
Feel free to bring your rhythm to life with Nightdrummers. Bring your shaker, tambourine, djembe, or other instrument and join us in the groove.

We create a welcoming Drum Circle where improvisation, connection, and playfulness are central. Listen, respond, experiment, and be amazed by what emerges.
Whether you've been playing for years or are simply curious: there's space for everyone. Bring your friends along, come groove, dance, and let the night resonate with our shared heartbeat.
Everyone is welcome. Together, we create the rhythm.

events.title: Mr Henky
events.description: Will dig in to his old collection of obscure minimal techno from the early 2000th. In a 2 hour set he will take you on a rare journey through the beautiful crafted sound of this rare sub genre.

events.title: Dj Chaakra Legal Alien
events.description: Jacques Povee is a music producer, promoter, and DJ known as DJ Chaakra. More than 25 years ago, while working as an environmental activist in Groenoord, he moved to Ruigoord to protect the village from the construction of the port. Together with more than 100 activists, he successfully halted the port's development in collaboration with various residents of Ruigoord. He is also known as the founder of Trance Orient Express and Solstice Festival.

events.title: Jazz Dahlia
events.description:
From hard-hitting UKG and bass-heavy club bangers to nostalgic hip-hop anthems, the Amsterdam-based Jazz Dalia has been making waves in the Dutch scene for a while now. She’s known for her high-energy sets that fuse dub, breakbeat, techno, drums and bass-driven club music. With a sound rooted in rhythm and heavy low-end, she draws inspiration from her Surinamese heritage to create dynamic and percussive journeys on the dancefloor. When on stage, her presence is driven by emotions, movement, and a deep, resonant sound that works contagiously on every audience.
 With experience ranging from steamy clubs like Garage Noord, Sissi’s & NAR to the wide fields of festivals such as Best Kept Secret & Wildeburg, Jazz has proven she knows how to get people moving - no matter the en- vironment. Beyond the conventional stages, she showcases more of her diverse sound and explores the edges of underground records at renowned radio platforms across the Netherlands, including Radio Radio, Operator & Relate.…

events.title: Julietta Vendetta
events.description:
‘ Closing in style’ 
 There's something special about closing a night at a festival like Landjuweel. The crowd, the vibe and a dancefloor that's ready for anything. I'll be taking KALEIDO out with a set woven from Goa, Tribal, Groovy Techno and Trance. Music that builds and breathes. It's going to be a proper journey from first to last beat. Come ready to move and let's close this one together in style.

events.title: Kids' Camping
events.description:
Friday 09.00-13.00 Opening Ceremony & Crafting
 Creating mandalas and altars using natural materials
 Making insect masks
 Saturday 09.00-13.00 Opening Ceremony & Movement
 Building insect houses from natural materials
 Wayang Kulit workshop (shadow puppets)
 Sunday 09.00-13.00 Opening Ceremony with soft music
 Storytelling
 Making instruments
 Ongoing program (all days): Creating costumes from cardboard and paper for the parade

 Facilitators:
 Luis Croes
 Guides children's camps and provides creative activities, handpan and creates a pleasant atmosphere.
 Iris van Wijhe
 A versatile and musically-inclined individual with a great passion for mindfulness.
 Iris enjoys leading workshops where children can playfully discover, create, move, and relax.
 "I prefer to create spaces where imagination is encouraged, where messy hands are seen as a good thing, and where children can playfully explore and be themselves."
 We invite the children to experiment, collaborate, and simply be themselves. Because it is when there is space for freedom and wonder that the most beautiful creations arise...

events.title: Morning Yoga · The Five Elements Form
events.description:
Want do you want smooth hips, flowing energy and a smile on your face?

During this standing yoga flow, Esmee Jiskoot will guide you through the 5 Elements Form: a series of twenty fluid yoga movements that may remind you of Qigong. The movements help to release tension, deepen your breathing and bring body and mind back into balance.
The flow moves like a moving meditation: soft, powerful and surprisingly energetic all at once. Your body comes alive, your mind finds peace and you feel more present in the moment.
Whether you are an experienced yogi or taking your first yoga class, this practice invites you to slow down, feel and reconnect with yourself. A wonderful reset during the festival, that will leave you grounded, energized and smiling.
Get to know Esmee Jiskoot
Esmee Jiskoot is a yoga teacher and body-oriented coach. With warmth, enthusiasm and years of experience, she guides people towards more relaxation, body awareness and life energy.
In her classes, movement, breathing and personal development come together naturally…

events.title: Misjael Monteban 'Love Songs'
events.description:
"he spoke in a voice of velvet 
 six rabbits with fluffy white ears 
 pearls where his teeth, bright diamonds his eyes 
 a million white flowers in a field in the sky 
 the children stayed children and they lived in their dreams"

events.title: DAF - Dutch Acid Family
events.description:
Thursday
 21:00-01:00
 Electro Magnes
 Electro Magnes is a Polish electronic duo formed by composer, producer, and multi-instrumentalist Rafał Kassaraba and vocalist-songwriter Bashka Preen. Blending live bass, electronic textures, and expressive vocals, they create atmospheric soundscapes that move between experimental electronica, ambient music, and intimate storytelling.
 Based in the foothills of the Karkonosze Mountains, the duo draws inspiration from nature, human connection, and inner transformation. Rafał's musical journey spans folk-blues, punk-rock, electro-punk, and film music projects, bringing a rich and distinctive depth to their sound.
 Bashka writes and performs in English, influenced by the years she spent living in London and its vibrant music scene. Her original songs have also been featured in film productions.
 Together, Electro Magnes invites audiences into a cinematic and immersive musical experience where electronic experimentation meets emotion, reflection, and heart.

Friday
 Sunshine Reggae
 15:00-16:30 Hobbol
 16:30-18:00 Open the gate sound
 18:00-19:30 Loddy Culture & Lyrical Benji
 19:30-21:00 Selector Depender
 21:00-22:30 Sun Collective…

events.title: Bakfietsband
events.description:
The cargo bike band embodies joyful activism at its finest. This mobile stage brings a smile to your face and transforms the street into a dance floor. Come join us for some fun or participate with this cheerful group.

The cargo bike band plays 3 times a day, in the afternoon, evening, and night. There is also a daily guest performance, with a group from South America, AP Mayor, performing on Saturday.

events.title: Earth Garden
events.description:
Amidst the performances, music and encounters, the Earth Garden offers a place to listen, connect to the earth, create and simply be.
 The Earth Garden Crew invites you to become more than a visitor. Spend time with the earth, create a gift from and/or for the earth with your hands, enjoy a nourishing açaí bowl and a cup of homemade blue Clitoria tea, and be carried by live music throughout the morning,...
 Thursday | 22:00 – 00:00
 *The Absinth Bar*
 Tableau Vivant & Live Performance
 Step into a surreal world where myth, ritual and imagination meet. Join us for an intimate late-night experience in the Earth Garden.
 Friday, Saturday & Sunday | 12:00 – 15:00
 Throughout the morning, accompanied by live music, you are invited to join:
 * *The Collective Clayground* – An artwork, shaped by everyone who joins. No ownership, only one piece growing in the hands of many. Connect with the earth, with each other and with your self..
 * *Gift Shop – *What Is Your Gift?** – This is not a place to buy clothes, but to exchange gifts. Find a unique festival piece and offer something in return—not money, but a talent, a kind gesture, your time or a skill.…

events.title: Poppenkastvoorstelling
events.description: During the "Landjuweel" (a Dutch festival), puppet shows will be performed at Ruigoord 16. The start times will be announced on a sign outside. The afternoon performances are suitable for all ages.

events.title: Lizard Yoga
events.description:
A grounded journey from the egg to the scaly lizard basking in the Sun.
 Unmesh Joshi, Yoga Practitioner for 15+ years
 Bioplastic costumes with sustainable decorations: Lara Wijnker and Mees Muijs.

events.title: Rodmer
events.description:
Rodmer doesn't just play music — he curates feeling. His sets move fluidly between ethereal melodic soundscapes and hypnotic downtempo grooves, weaving a sonic thread that pulls strangers into a shared moment. Whether you're losing yourself on the dancefloor or simply letting the music breathe through you, Rodmer's selections find the space between thought and movement — and live there. Rodmer loves playing in spaces where music and atmosphere become one. (Wildeburg, Into the Woods, Embassy of the Free Mind, Oosterkerk, Mandali, Italy)
 Rodmer runs Studio Rodmer en is part of The Experience Enhancers collective.

events.title: Help Build the Growing Insect Village · Air Creatures
events.description:
Donna and Mariana explore the realms of creativity, nature, and play. Paying attention to what emerges in the moment, they invite children to collaborate, investigate, and unleash their imaginations.

Throughout the festival, we will collectively build a world. A playful exploration of what "home" means for each of us.

The World in Becoming - The Air Creatures
Now that the landscape and plant life have found their place, we focus on the world above. Using light, transparent materials, we create flying creatures, floating inhabitants, and dreamy forms that dance between earth and sky.

Part of a three-part workshop
events.venue: Village Square · A World in the Making

events.title: Kloek
events.description:
KLOEK. An interactive street theatre performance. Or, to put it another way: A robust, mobile installation that only comes to life on the streets with the help of brave passersby…

Back to basics. KLOEK as pure installation theatre. Nothing hidden, nothing embellished. Robust structures, basic mechanics. Simple and transparent. The timeless power of simplicity.

Pure. Steel and wood. Nature and human strength. Strong hands of both adults and children pull on the ropes to make the sound instruments play and the puppet fly. Free as a bird.

A bold and subtle interplay between man and machine creates visual poetry with muscle power. Both intimate and exuberant, it stimulates imagination and makes fantasy a tangible reality. KLOEK.

events.title: The soft landing
events.description: We start the day with a joint opening ceremony on the village square. A moment to come together, meet each other, and settle into the day calmly. With music, movement, and creativity, we discover what the daily theme has to tell us. What adventures, questions, and surprises await us today?

events.title: The soft landing
events.description: We start the day with a joint opening ceremony on the village square. A moment to come together, meet each other, and settle into the day calmly. With music, movement, and creativity, we discover what the daily theme has to tell us. What adventures, questions, and surprises await us today?

events.title: Woven Voices
events.description:
Woven Voices originated from love. Love for bringing people together and giving everyone the space to express themselves. What remains is a beautiful work of art.

Weaving Our Shared Visions Together
"Woven Voices," the proud creation of Lex Empress, begins on the first day of Landjuweel. Each day, participants are invited to write intentions, dreams, and visions for our future, for the children, and for the world. These words are painted onto cotton and woven together into one collective carpet. This is a living artwork created from many voices becoming one. Anyone can join and become part of this shared creation. By the end of the festival, the carpet stands as a physical memory of our united prayers and possibilities.

events.title: Dansjewel : Laura Jayne
events.description:
DJ Laura Jayne blends her warm and spicy dance floor cocktails, mixing percussive electronic beats with synthy African vibes, Brazilian & UK funk, catchy garage, and all-time classics, with a twist of global bass and tribal dubby electronica.

Her international journey through time and space is sure to surprise you and take you on a trip through her musical selection of gems, getting you into a free flow of embodied explosion and playfulness.

Laura is known for her energizing global groove sets that she plays at festivals with DJ collectives such as the Bankra Bike Soundsystem and Moonshine Sound Collective, where live music is incorporated into the dance sets.

As a co-founder of FreeFlow, she loves hosting festival stages with musician and DJ families at venues such as Tivoli, Paradiso, Couleur Cafe (Brussels), and Zwarte Cross.

events.title: Origin Circle
events.description: We gather every morning to remember our shared origins on this Earth, connecting with the medicine of the Heart of the Earth (Kakaw) and opening the day for sharing our intentions, songs and prayers.

events.title: Sophia Maria Kienhuis
events.description:
There's a poem by Mark Strand in which he writes about experiencing the absence of field within a field, and striving to hold onto things. In this poetic space between presence and emptiness, Sophia Maria Kienhuis moves – at the intersection of art, radical expression, and interaction.

Her work unfolds as a living encounter, where body, voice, and material come together. This year, she weaves her performances with textile works that she brings along as carriers of story, memory, and sensory experience.
Sophia Maria has created numerous performances both indoors and outdoors at Ruigoord over the years, including contributions to art routes, Landjuweel, Balloon Shows, and various editions of Nazomer Nachtlicht. She has been affiliated with MadNes Festival for 18 years and established the ecstatic dance team in Odessa, where she also led ceremonies for several years.
In addition, she has collaborated with artists such as Spinvis, Wordbites, Primaat, Jackie and the facts, and is a singer and songwriter for Flower of Hope and the Ontwaak Ceremonie, with Gilian Baracs.…

events.title: Coffee with Jan
events.description:
Wake up in a cozy setting at the Kosmies Wilgenportaal with coffee, poetry, and music. Featuring Amber-Helena Reisig, Jan Modaal, Linde Suzanne, Laurien Schreuder, and Reyer Romkes.

Amber-Helena Reisig is a writer of prose, poetry, and opinion pieces. She is under contract with Uitgeverij Prometheus for both a novel, Het Sterfhuis (The House of Death), and a collection of poems. In addition to her work as a writer, she also works on social issues as a policy maker. In December 2025, she was elected "Officer of the Year 2025".
Jan Modaal writes punk ballads and will perform an acoustic set this morning.
Laurien, founder of Snowapple Collective and many creative projects worldwide, is always up for new adventures and is known for her eccentric ways as a composer, performer, and multi-instrumentalist.
Linde Suzanne brings poetry that is inextricably linked to her voice and body. Her work navigates between abstraction and monologue, between experience and image.
Reyer Romkes works at the Rijksmuseum (National Museum) and will present excerpts from his unpublished manuscript about living with cancer…

events.title: Bouke & Co · Free Clothes — Take Some, Bring Some
events.description:
Can you do better? Or are you just suffering from shopping urges? Come and enjoy FREE CLOTHING at Bouke & Co.

Cool clothes and accessories for free! New items every day! Unwanted styling advice included.
Feel free to bring your unwanted wardrobe too!
Everything is ️FREE️. Daily from 13:00-21:00 at Area 46.

events.title: FlowerSticks Workshop hosted by Rene FocusFire
events.description: In this Flowersticks workshop you will amaze yourself with skills that you never knew you have, and tricks anyone can learn with just 3 sticks. Discover the many possibilities and ways of playing with this versatile prop, all while you are having fun playing!

events.title: Garden of Devotion
events.description:
During Landjuweel, Garden of Devotion opens its doors as an intimate barefoot garden where nature, art, music, taste, scent and stillness come together: a haven of peace, beauty and love, a place for inspiration, reflection, connection and surrender.

Take off your shoes, enter softly and let yourself be carried away by live music, soundscapes, performance and rituals.
 Shoes off. Senses on.
 Garden Ruigoord 1 open on Saturdays and Sundays between 2:00 PM and 6:00 PM.

events.title: Till Glitter Do Us Part
events.description:
What exactly *is* a wedding? A group of people gather together. Some words are spoken. Rings may appear. Tears may be shed. A kiss is encouraged. Somehow, everyone agrees that something profound has happened. Weddings are wonderfully strange. A collective performance that we have decided to take very seriously.

In true KALEIDO fashion, "Till Glitter Do Us Part" lovingly embraces the absurdity of matrimonial ritual while celebrating the very real magic of love, devotion, friendship, desire, and chosen family. Expect a campy, queer ceremony where sincerity and nonsense walk hand in hand down the aisle.

Whether you're hopelessly romantic, deeply skeptical, gloriously single, legally married, emotionally entangled, or simply curious, you are warmly invited to witness (and perhaps participate in) this joyful exploration of commitment, chaos, and collective imagination. Come for the vows. Stay for the glitter. Leave questioning everything you thought you knew about weddings. No gifts required. Excessive enthusiasm is encouraged.

events.title: Open Your Heart' Ademreis
events.description:
Piroska & Alina guide participants through deep breathing and mindfulness experiences that emphasize gentleness, connection, and a sense of homecoming.

By combining connected breathing, music, mindfulness, and subtle shamanistic elements, they create a safe and warm space where participants can slow down, feel, and reconnect with their hearts.
During their "Open Your Heart" session, they guide participants on a gentle breathing journey that helps to release tension, give emotions space, and rediscover the natural ability of the heart to connect. Their guidance is characterized by presence, intuition, and a deep commitment to personal growth and inner well-being.
Together, they create a place where people can escape from the hustle and bustle, find stillness in their breath, and feel connected to themselves and those around them.
No prior experience with breathing exercises is necessary; everyone is welcome.

events.title: Rasta Robert & Friends
events.description: Dreamtime Healing sound massage concerts, mantra singing, theremin, spontaneous musical improvisation, spiritual chill-out space. Every day 2:00 PM - 10:00 PM, concert sessions 2:00 PM - 3:30 PM and 8:00 PM - 9:30 PM (check-in 30 minutes prior).

events.title: 4-Hands Massage Arnaud Mooij & Marcel Maurik
events.description:
Four hands; one experience
A unique collaboration between Marcel and Arnaud
Free, spacious, relaxed, and completely surrendered. That's how you'll feel after a massage from this professional massage duo. Working as one body, they bring your body to a state of complete relaxation, ensuring that your body feels free and open, and it seems like your heart is even fuller and more open, ready to experience the freedom at Ruigoord even more.
When two talents combine their experience and passion, something special emerges. That's what Marcel and Arnaud offer you: a fusion of our strengths as bodyworkers. From our own approach and background, we complement each other in an intuitive and healing collaboration.
You choose what you need. Do you want to feel deeply relaxed, let your energy flow, release blockages, or simply return to yourself? We offer various disciplines that you can freely combine: classic massage, energetic massage, TRAGER® Approach, intuitive bodywork, and Rebalancing techniques. And our hands know exactly where they need to be on your body to give you an amazing experience…

events.title: Women's Circle · The Voice of Your Female Ancestors
events.description:
For centuries, family trees were passed down through men, and women disappeared from history. In this women's circle, we reflect on our female lineage: the chain of women who made us possible.

With a short guided visualization, you connect with your ancestral female line. What do you want to see? Together, we honor their existence and their contribution to the woman you are today through a shared ritual.
A powerful and connecting session about ancestry, identity, and female strength, even if you know little about your family history.

Fien Lindelauff
Fien Lindelauff is the founder of Moederstamboom (Mother's Genealogy) and specializes in researching women's lineages. In addition, she is a tantra yoga teacher and organizes women's circles, ceremonies, and retreats.

events.title: Shake The Dust
events.description:
LilyKa (Lieneke) and Karain (Ayşegül Ivy) combine live instruments, breathwork, voice, electronics, and improvisation, inviting people on journeys of presence and transformation.

Lieneke is a musician and facilitator for breathwork and Shake the Dust.
Ayşegül Ivy is a pianist, electronic musician, and producer.

For this edition of Landjuweel, we are bringing a special Shake the Dust journey accompanied by a hybrid DJ set with live instruments.

Shake the Dust is a transformative practice that engages the body, heart, and mind. You are invited to release what no longer serves you and to reconnect with your body through music.
Guided by a poetic theme, we will move together through waves of shaking, jumping, resting, dancing, intuitive flow, plus elements of yoga, somatics, and bodyweight strength work.

events.title: Sound Medicine Journey with Tessa & Sjoerd
events.description: Sjoerd's musical background and Tessa's yoga and meditation experience brought this couple together, introducing them to the magic of healing sounds. They have immersed themselves in a Sound Medicine training on Bali and are eager to share their knowledge and experience with an audience in the Netherlands. Join us on a journey through healing sounds.

events.title: "Fire-making workshop by Robert Bleij"
events.description:
Robert Bleij, buscraft, actor and storyteller,
 Fire-making workshop
 Have you always wanted to learn how to make fire yourself using simple, natural materials?
 During this two-hour program, you will learn the basics of making primitive fire. No long theory, but mainly hands-on: trying, practicing, and discovering what is needed to turn a spark into a real flame.
 Making fire is an ancient skill that lies dormant in every human being. With the right technique, good materials, and a little patience, anyone can learn it. Experience is not required; this program is suitable for beginners.
 In a relaxed outdoor setting, you will discover:
 different old techniques for making fire;
 which natural tinder works well and where to find it;
 how to prepare the tinder yourself;
 how to turn the tinder into a flame;
 how to build a fire calmly and successfully.

 After two hours, you will leave with more knowledge, experience, and confidence to be able to make fire safely yourself.
 Links:
 Instagram

events.title: Informatief: Huiden verwerken
events.description:
Erwin navigates through life with passion, physical strength, and a connection to nature. Designer, Hunter, Bushcrafter.

Informative: Processing Hides
In this first part of the two-part series, we explore the story behind hides and their place in human history. We delve into the relationship between humans and animals throughout the ages, from hunting and survival among indigenous peoples to the careful use of what nature provides. We explore how we can meet our basic needs for life, and how respect for animals and nature plays a central role in this. This includes considering hunting as part of a broader ecological cycle, and the idea of connection with nature and "Mother Earth." It's a moment to reflect on what it means to be grounded and part of something larger. Space for questions and discussion makes this an open and insightful conversation.

This program segment is followed by a workshop on processing hides at 2 PM.

events.title: Workshop Lino Stempelen
events.description:
KIMTAM TATTOO is a tattoo artist with Javanese-Surinamese roots, based in Rotterdam. In her work, she combines craftsmanship, culture, and storytelling by creating unique designs that tell a personal story.

During this workshop, children will learn about the ancient art of printing. The artwork you see consists of lino stamps that have been hand-carved. Stamping techniques have been used around the world for centuries to print patterns and images on paper and fabric. In parts of Southeast Asia, including Malaysia, similar techniques were even used to create tattoos, where ornaments and figures were carved from wood.

This rich tradition inspired KIMTAM to design her own stamps and use them as a basis for her tattoo work.
For children aged 5-9 years:
Children playfully discover the world of stamping. They choose from various handmade lino stamps, make their own prints on paper, and then receive their favorite design as a temporary stencil on their skin.
For children aged 10+ years:
Older children get to work independently as creators…

events.title: "Poems of Fire and Earth"
events.description: Buguel Noz’s puppet poetry consists of tender and intense creations. It invites wonder and imagination. Music, dance, and puppetry come together in pure, magical moments. The performance ‘Poésie du Feu et de la Terre’ is ideally suited for festivals. This eco-poetic performance focuses on rebirth. The plant, animal, and human worlds meet around earth and fire, depicting the continuous cycle of change and renewal. Suitable for all ages, including very young children. A wordless choreography.

events.title: Saskia Schmink
events.description: Here, imaginations come to life. The makeup team transforms children into whatever they can dream of – from magical creatures to brave adventurers.

events.title: Más - La Familia Volante
events.description:
La Familia Volante, consisting of Marieke, Hans, and Yara (8), is a circus family that spends the winter in Portugal and travels during the summer, enjoying various festivals with their acrobatic clown performances and family acro workshops.

Màs!

This acrobatic family loves to play, throw, and tumble...and is always looking for playmates. Who wants to join them? They will perform their interactive, acrobatic clown show, Màs, which could even turn into an acrobatic playground for the whole family... After the performance, there's time for everyone to play together and learn acrobatic tricks from the family!

events.title: The Art Jewel on Saturday
events.description:
Curators / host Q&A: Bo van der Meer & Roswitha de Boer - Zuiderveen

New at the Landjuweel Festival! A fine selection of Dutch documentaries that capture the essence of artistry, community, identity, and freedom. Step into the church, put on your headphones, open your eyes, and immerse yourself in the stories that breathe the free spirit of Ruigoord. Including Q&A with the filmmakers: from up-and-coming talent to established names. Saturday, July 25 | 13:30 - 15:30

Cheek to Cheek (24 min)
On the dance floor of the Danspaleis, loneliness and old age seem not to exist. A film about the power and vulnerability of aging, dancing, and music.
Q&A with director Bo van der Meer

Daifonie (7 min)
In this animated documentary, three people share their experiences during the pandemic. As it turns out, they find comfort in the same way, through music.
Q&A with directors Selle Inti Sellink & Mirjam Debets

Sterre (22 min)
The 11-year-old artistic talent Sterre wants nothing more than to be accepted into the School for Young Talent. During inexplicable headaches, she finds comfort in art and drawing.
Q&A with director Catherine van Campen…

events.title: Morning Yoga · The Five Elements Form
events.description:
Want do you want smooth hips, flowing energy and a smile on your face?

During this standing yoga flow, Esmee Jiskoot will guide you through the 5 Elements Form: a series of twenty fluid yoga movements that may remind you of Qigong. The movements help to release tension, deepen your breathing and bring body and mind back into balance.
The flow moves like a moving meditation: soft, powerful and surprisingly energetic all at once. Your body comes alive, your mind finds peace and you feel more present in the moment.
Whether you are an experienced yogi or taking your first yoga class, this practice invites you to slow down, feel and reconnect with yourself. A wonderful reset during the festival, that will leave you grounded, energized and smiling.
Get to know Esmee Jiskoot
Esmee Jiskoot is a yoga teacher and body-oriented coach. With warmth, enthusiasm and years of experience, she guides people towards more relaxation, body awareness and life energy.
In her classes, movement, breathing and personal development come together naturally…

events.title: Lach Workshop
events.description:
LAUGHING, LETTING GO & PLAYFUL CRAZINESS

Want to just let everything go? Then come and laugh with Sabine Rabbe!
Come to the laughter workshop during the Landjuweel Festival and completely lose yourself in laughter.
🤣 Laughing without reason
💃 Dancing, playing and letting loose
⚡ Recharging with festival energy
3 days of freedom, craziness and connection at Ruigoord.
A little rebellious, a little silly, and above all, lots of fun.
Leave your inhibitions behind and take your laughter with you.
Sabine Rabbe has been inspiring people for years with her contagious laughter, playfulness, and boundless energy.
As a laughter coach, performer, and connector, she shows you how liberating it is to simply let go and laugh.
With a mix of humor, spontaneity, and festival vibes, Sabine creates a space where fun, connection, and zest for life are central.
Laughter is her language, energy her trademark.
Come and laugh during the laughter workshop by Sabine Rabbe (aka "The Queen of Laughter") at Landjuweel this year!
As every year, we'll be letting loose again: a dose of humor, energy, and above all, lots of fun…

events.title: Dougal Hallowes - Sitar Journeys
events.description:
The Story So Far…
 Dougal started his adventures in music as a bass player in rock bands on the Sydney scene in the early 90’s. After a move to London in ‘96 and a stint with Australian psychedelic band Om, found himself playing with Tribal Drift and spent the remainder of the decade touring around the globe.
 Tribal Drift featured Cyrung who was one of the pioneers of didgeridoo in Goa during the 80’s and was attributed to bringing the instrument into the world of trance in the early 90’s.
 In 2003 Dougal was a founding member of Australian band Loonaloop and featured as writer/performer/producer on their first 3 albums alongside many live shows.
 The journey with the sitar began with a gift from India. The sitar stayed in its box until Dougal found sitar guru Darshan Kumari in Amsterdam around 2012 and began to study.
 He has since opened the show for Dutch band My Baby with both sitar and didge on their Shamanaid tour in 2016/7 and slowly found the confidence to expand a 10 min set to a full show.
 More recently Dougal has played in 2022 and ‘23 on Ozora festival with 2 x 1.5 hour journeys from ambient to dance-floor and will again play 2 sets there in ‘24.…

events.title: Hidden Bamboo Garden
events.description:
HIDDEN BAMBOO GARDEN
 A lush, groovy, multidimensional garden hangout sprinkled with the deliciousness of our new signature cocktail, which is both sparkly and supersensory.
 VR experience at Hidden Bamboo Garden
 Nemo Vos presents Digital Darkroom
 A holistic session in VR where spirituality and exact science come together.
 Where you move between the present, the future, and the past.
 Where an inner breakthrough and shameless consumption can go through one door.
 Where the digital and analog 
 The microscopic and the enormous 
 The seed cell and the egg 
 The mundane and endlessly complex 
 Come together in embrace, so that we can all sigh with relief.
 It's finally really summer.

 Left:
 Website

events.title: Poetry Consults
events.description:
Feeling lovesick? Despondent? Forlorn? 
 Dr. Oxytocin's poetry consults are open for business! Come get the lock on your heart fixed by inhaling their intimate poetry and 'a completely safe dose' of Oxytocin. Come check out their tent, hiding in the KALEIDO Faerie Forest!

events.title: Spiritual Battleground
events.description:
What is the true path to enlightenment? Will you try to save the world? Or turn inward to save yourself? In other words: Will you start by healing the world or healing yourself? 
 In this spiritual No-Talent Show, spiritual superstars Jomanda and her nemesis Chakra Khan will lift you to a higher frequency. When it really comes down to it, what do YOU believe will set you free?

events.title: Consent Bingo
events.description: WALLY presents: Consent Bingo! In the first half, playful exercises help participants feel their own boundaries and practice verbal and non-verbal communication. Using our “toolbox” of ways to express desires and boundaries, we then play Consent Bingo! Your bingo card features different types of touch, from holding hands to kissing and more. Do you dare to ask for what you want?

events.title: Psychedelic Salon
events.description:
