The Psychedelic Salon brings together researchers, practitioners, and those interested to explore the future of psychedelics in society. Once considered fringe, psychedelics are now moving into clinics, courtrooms, and cultural conversations — raising urgent questions about how we understand, integrate, and shape these experiences responsibly. At Landjuweel, Michiel van Elk, Annabelle Abraham, and Joost Breeksema share their perspectives on psychedelic futures, each followed by an open Q&A with the audience.
 Hosted by Daan Cowan-Keiman
 14:00 — Michiel van Elk, PhD
 “A 4E Perspective on Psychedelics: Challenges, Trends and the Future”
 How can we study something as complex and self-transformative as a psychedelic experience? And what should the future of psychedelic science look like? Michiel van Elk reflects on the current challenges in psychedelic research, including methodological problems, limited evidence for efficacy and safety, and challenges with regulatory frameworks. At the same time, he observes a movement towards increased medicalisation, a rise of the underground circuit, and increased interest in psychedelic retreats.…

events.title: AfroMix
events.description:
Avalon Brown

Avalon's love for African dance began when she lived in Rio de Janeiro. She was inspired by the joy on the faces of the dancers and the freedom they experienced while dancing to the powerful percussion. To learn more and deepen her understanding, she traveled extensively throughout various African countries and within the African diaspora to study traditional dance styles.

She brings these experiences into her own AfroMix classes and performances.
It's a mix of different African dance and music styles. The movements are earthy, expansive, intense, and uplifting.
It's about letting go and releasing your energy. And finding your power! POWERRRR!!

events.title: (H)oer Hollandse spelletjes
events.description: From chair dancing and staring contests to nail-biting and jumping jacks: classic, good-natured fun with a healthy dose of competition. Join in and discover who secretly has the most intense competitive spirit.

events.title: Photo Booth
events.description: Capture your favorite moment and step into our photo booth. Create a beautiful memory of the festival by taking your own picture.

events.title: SAVE THE GREEN FIELD — SIGN NOW!
events.description: The Green Field is a rare green space in the city where there is still room for wild nature, culture, and experimentation. It is run on a voluntary basis by residents and small, local businesses. But now, our free space is at risk of disappearing. Therefore, we need to act NOW, it's not too late. You can help us by signing the petition! More information at our stand.

events.title: The Floating Poet
events.description: Become a floating poet and come up to our raft to write a poem. Ask the internship manager where the secret departure point is. You can recite your creation in the garden.

events.title: The Game Cabinet
events.description:
Open a drawer, slide a box towards you, and let the fun begin. In the Games Corner, you'll find a diverse mix of card and board games waiting for new players. Challenge someone to a game of 30 seconds, go for pure chaos with a party game, or rediscover a classic you had long forgotten.

Whether you come to win, hang out, laugh, or simply chat: there's always something fun happening around the table here. Pull up a chair and join in the fun.

events.title: XXL spellen: Schaken, Jenga, Twister
events.description: Huge, bigger, wildest: With our XXL games, it's all about having fun to the fullest. Challenge your strategic mind on a chessboard as big as your ego, build towers that reach higher than your weekend plans with XXL Jenga filled with surprising questions and tasks, or get tangled up during a game of Twister that is guaranteed to lead to laughter fits.

events.title: Workshop Playfight
events.description: Elena Zanchetta is a certified Tangi Playfight Facilitator, massage therapist, and NVC mediator based in Amsterdam. Rooted in contemporary dance, partner movement, and Non-violent Communication, she brings together strength, softness, and play to create spaces where people can connect beyond words. Together with Joris, she runs Playfight Amsterdam — a growing community of weekly circles and workshops for anyone curious about embodied, playful connection.

events.title: Yin Yoga
events.description:
Yin Yoga is a calm, meditative form of yoga where all poses are performed sitting or lying down on the floor and held for longer periods (3–5 minutes). This allows you to deeply work with your connective tissues and bring your nervous system to rest.

Let me guide you on a gentle, healing journey inward. During this session, we will combine deep, meditative Yin poses with singing or listening to mantras. This will help you relax, let go, and connect with your heart. A moment of stillness, surrender, and inner harmony.
Meet Tara

events.title: Reiki & Just Zen It Workshop (12+ jaar)
events.description:
Kyra Walhof is a Reiki Master, massage therapist, and founder of Zensaties and the Just Zen It method. For over fifteen years, she has been guiding people towards relaxation, mindfulness, and energy management. With her warm, practical, and humorous approach, she makes spirituality accessible and helps people connect with themselves more deeply.

Reiki & Just Zen It Workshop
Discover the power of Reiki and Just Zen It in a simple and practical way. During this workshop, you will learn how to experience more peace in your body and mind through simple meditation, breathing, and mindfulness exercises. You will gain an understanding of the basics of Reiki and discover how to better feel, manage, and recharge your energy. We will practice relaxation, presence, and increasing loving attention for yourself and those around you. No prior experience is necessary; curiosity is enough!

There are a limited number of places available. So, if you would like to participate in this workshop, you can reserve a spot at the yurt on Dorpsplein - Aardewerk.

events.title: Workshop: Huiden verwerken
events.description:
Erwin navigates through life with passion, physical strength, and a connection to nature. Designer, Hunter, Bushcrafter.

Informative: Processing Hides
In this first part of the two-part series, we explore the story behind hides and their place in human history. We delve into the relationship between humans and animals throughout the ages, from hunting and survival among indigenous peoples to the careful use of what nature provides. We explore how we can meet our basic needs for life, and how respect for animals and nature plays a central role in this. This includes considering hunting as part of a broader ecological cycle, and the idea of connection with nature and "Mother Earth." It's a moment to reflect on what it means to be grounded and part of something larger. Space for questions and discussion makes this an open and insightful conversation.

This program segment is followed by a workshop on processing hides at 2 PM.

events.title: Filosofisch kabinet
events.description:
Theme of the day:

Out of nowhere, The Wandering Company appears: three mysterious beings, a creaking caravan, and traces of a life on the road. Insects? Travelers? No one knows for sure, perhaps even themselves. One question has driven them for as long as they've been wandering: where is home? Today, they invite you to join their search. The day consists of 3 parts. First, the company appears, right in the middle of an interactive theater moment. Then, their story, "Onward to Home," comes to life through a narrative that unfolds before your eyes. And at the end of the day, everyone departs together in a festive children's parade, the ritual that transforms searching into finding home.

The Wandering Company appears:
With a creaking, rattling sound, the caravan rolls onto the square. Curious eyes look around: where have they ended up? The Wandering Company is on its way, as always, looking for an answer it hasn't yet found. This place is just one stop along the way; nothing more, nothing less. But exploring an unknown world isn't something you do alone; they need your help…

events.title: Music Story with The Troubadour Car
Woven Voices: What's alive in you?
DragonStaff workshop by Sem
events.description: Ready for your first steps to bend fire in your own future and guide your own light? Come play with us and find out if your flow journey start here.

events.title: Storytelling meditation by Simone Bijlard with live handpan music by Reo Matsumoto.
events.description:
Simone Bijlard studied architecture in Delft. While useful, she quickly discovered that it wasn't nearly as enjoyable as writing and drawing. She therefore soon made the (partial) switch to Kinderboekland, where she still feels perfectly at home.

Her book "Dwars door Amsterdam" takes readers on a journey through the city in a way that you would never find with a regular map: using cross-sections.

At Landjuweel, Simone reads children's poems accompanied by the musical accompaniment of musician Reo Matsumoto. In this way, they share their mutual enjoyment of language, rhythm, and imagery with an audience of all ages.

events.title: FreeFlow Flashmob
events.description: Somewhere, sometime, this week. Maybe tomorrow morning after breakfast, maybe when the sun sets over the church, maybe when you've just tied your shoelaces. The FreeFlow Flashmob passes by. Or rather: it comes towards you. A rave on wheels, a party that creates itself, a dance that invites you to join in. With DJs on the bike sound system and live music. No stage, no audience, just participants who don't yet know they're part of it. Dancing, contact improvisation, and homo ludens on two wheels. The party creates itself, and you participate. Afterwards, we dissolve into the wind, as if nothing had happened, except in your legs. Those who were there will know. The rest will hear the stories.

events.title: Nereo
events.description:
Nereo is a living, shifting sound - strings, machines, and motion colliding in real time.
 Working through an expanded vocabulary of cello and string instruments, Nereo shapes sound into movement, the cello's depth and beauty stretched into new forms, the charango's dreamy tone drifting alongside pulsing electronics and transformed fragments of everyday life. Boiling water breathes. Books rustle into rhythm.
 Playful yet precise, unpredictable yet deeply felt, Nereo unfolds as a landscape meant to be felt as much as heard. The music moves with you, around you, through you, inviting listeners, dancers, and dreamers into a shared space where sound becomes physical, and flows like a river.

events.title: Thara Bergen: Coco Blance Hip Hop Set
events.description:
Thara Bergen brings you a crisp, elevated and down-right uplifting house & techno experience.
 Growing up in Ibiza in the nineties and influenced by the house mix tapes of the era, her sound is a perfect mix of warm island vibes and the innovative city rhythms of Amsterdam - the place she now calls home.
 What does this meeting of worlds create? A clean compound of bassy sounds, snappy vocals and groovy beats.
 One of the founding curators and pioneers of the infamous concept - Project Sugar @ The Sugar Factory, Amsterdam. Thara was one of the resident DJs at this unique venue where the creativity of her and her fellow residents was projected for 5 Years.
 More recent highlights include her gracing the booth in venues such as Shelter, Thuishaven, Atelier and Sissi’s. Not to forget her debut for Isburning on Jan 1st, where she has been a familiar face in the booth since.
 In equal parts a digital and vinyl talent, with an eclectic vinyl collection and over 6 years of parties under her belt - this is one DJ to watch…
 EXTRA - inspired by…

events.title: Stoievaar x Vrolijke Vogelaar
events.description: Soon, as the morning dew evaporates from the sun-drenched trees, they descend into the Boogie Forest. She, Stoievaar, stoic and serene; he, the Cheerful Birdwatcher, who always manages to conjure a smile. Together, they explore every nook and cranny of this planet, singing its praises, collecting melodies from lush forests and majestic mountain peaks, possessed by the pure poetry of the strange bird and his accompanying song. Their precious collection is safely stored in a magical instrumentarium that captures the melodies without ever depriving the strange bird of its voice. For this unique treasure, these music-loving virtuosos will come to share their collection in the Boogie Forest, sharing with us their strangest and most joyful grooves, opening up the sky for all those who are ready to take flight and free the Paradise Bird within themselves!

events.title: Lotte Walda
events.description:
Dutch songwriter Lotte Walda writes songs to spread positive energy and help create a kinder, more connected world. Deeply inspired by nature and by the music of the sixties and seventies, Lotte feels most at home in the sounds of that era — songs full of warmth, connection and timeless storytelling. Through her music, she hopes to remind people of the light and love that still live in the world. She loves creating catchy melodies that stick with listeners. Blending folk, pop and country, her songs tell heartfelt stories about life, love and staying true to yourself. With her warm voice and honest lyrics, she aims to touch, comfort and uplift everyone who listens.
 Her musical journey began on the old Sint Servaas Bridge of Maastricht near her hometown, where she would fill the streets with her voice and guitar. From those early days, she wanted to “build a bridge that reminds people of the joy within that connects them to their inner child. This curiosity and open-heartedness soon took her on a global adventure. She’s since performed everywhere from the Bluebird Café in Nashville to festival stages across the Netherlands, and even shared her music on the streets of China.…

events.title: Divine Mistress of Craziness
events.description: (Born in 1939) She commutes between Cologne and the Amsterdam artist village of Ruigoord. After taking a parachute jump on her 60th birthday, she left behind her conventional life to pursue profound spiritual experiences. In her temple in Ruigoord, she celebrates encounters at a soul level and opens doors to higher levels of consciousness. More information about her unique philosophy can be found on the Ruigoord Artist Page.

events.title: The Bard’s Horn
events.description:
Thursday:
 21.00 till 01.00 DJs Archangel, Graham B, JP and Ambient Daan
 Friday:
 16.00 to 20.00 with storytellers Simone Engeln, Daan P., Belinda with musical accompaniment by Fons, musical interlude by sitar player Dougal
 20.00 to 21.00 Monty Pagan, a delightful Amsterdam folk rock band
 21.00 till 01.00 DJs Archangel, Graham B, JP and Ambient Daan
 Saturday:
 16.00 to 20.00 with storytellers Simone Engeln, Daan P., Belinda with musical accompaniment by Fons, musical interlude by sitar player Dougal
 20.00 to 21.00 Monty Pagan, a delightful Amsterdam folk rock band
 21.00 till late DJs Archangel, Graham B, JP and Ambient Daan
 Sunday:
 15.00 to 18.00 ParaNoya and friends with poetry and art
 Simone Engeln (formerly known as Simone Angel) is a versatile storyteller whose life itself is a story. After spending twenty years in the jungle of Belize, she returned to the Netherlands in 2023. Her adventurous life began at the age of 18; under the international spotlight of television…

events.title: Festival Make-up & Makeover with Shotgun Cilla
Voices Revelation
events.description: Soul and gospel as it should be: full of fire, groove, and soul. Voices Revelation delivers a performance that is powerful, energetic, and moving. Expect strong vocals and deep harmonies.

events.title: Prime Time Power Throuple
events.description: Embark on a bilingual journey through television. We're your favorite interactive talk/game/reality show and our "commercial breaks" aren't bad either!

events.title: JRK x Maud
events.description:
JRK.XLSX recently entered the electronic music scene and quickly found itself on festival stages. He's a big fan of his trusted CDJ-3000s & Rane MP2015, creating immersive experiences with no bigger ambition than sharing music that connects people.

Maud Sauer has been performing at Ruigoord for approximately 50 years. Since then, she has worked as an (improvising) oboist, composer, band leader, and advisor to emerging artists. More information: maudsauer.nl.

events.title: Making a kaleidoscope – The telescope of the flying creature.
events.venue: Village Square · A World in the Making

events.title: Soup and sandwiches
events.description: We gather around the fire to cook together. Bring a piece of vegetables and add it to the communal soup. Small and large hands work side by side, while bread is baked over the fire. The square fills with aromas, and our hearts are warmed by being together.

events.title: Talking drum workshop with Mame N'Diack
events.description:
Mame N’Diack Seck Thiam is a Senegalese percussionist, singer, poet, choreographer, actor, and songwriter from a renowned griot family. His musical journey began at the age of 17, as he travelled across West Africa to immerse himself in different cultures and musical traditions.

Since relocating to Uganda in 2010, Mame has developed a distinctive sound by blending West African percussion with traditional Ugandan rhythms. He is widely recognized for introducing the talking drum to new audiences and captivating listeners with its expressive, voice-like sound.

Dedicated to preserving African cultural heritage, Mame founded the annual Yella Cultural Festival, promoting cultural pride, intercultural exchange, and collaboration among young people. His mission is to keep traditional music alive while using it to bring people from different cultures together.

events.title: BINGO!!!
events.description: Grab your moment to catch your breath, and the only chance to chase some bingo balls at the Queer Music Bingo! Expect a lineup packed with queer bangerz, glittering prizes and fabulous host Brie to guide you through it all. Whether you’re in it to win it or just here for the vibes, you’re guaranteed a good time.

events.title: Le Miracle du Vivant by: Marchal
events.description: The human experience is full of uncertainties, great mysteries, and unanswered questions that may never be resolved. Even if we encounter each other, it's likely that won't change anything, but perhaps a new perspective can be found.

events.title: Introduction to Club Juggling by Nakarit and Nico
events.description: The goal is to explore your body through various positions to build muscle memory—identifying movement types—and to connect with an external object. It is open to all skill levels, allowing us to explore together how to achieve fluid juggling.

events.title: Dragtivism Workshop
events.description:
With fellow queer activists, the House of Extinction will collectively explore our views on the (history of + contemporary) drag scene and ways to - sustainably - do drag. To fuel this potential and spread like an ethically produced olive oil stain, they will host a safer learning space for queer people and allies coming together, liberating themselves in this art form and smash the capitalist cistem, one stiletto heel and make up brush at a time. 
 House of Extinction
 The House of Extinction, allegedly the first explicitly climate activist drag house in the Netherlands, unites a sustainable, conscious practice and fabulous, otherworldly art in their drag. While our species is still here, they believe we should be vehement in our demand for change and passionate about community building and empowerment. To make this vision happen, they cherish queer joy during their eclectic performances on the street and in the club next to hosting dragtivist workshops. Looking drop-dead-gorgeous to the very end, your HoE's will lipsync for all life on Earth!

events.title: Tender Tantric Dance
events.description:
Liesbet De Rouck is the founder of The Self Love Academy, a body-oriented facilitator, spoken word artist, and creator of transformative spaces centered around presence, connection, and emotional embodiment.

Her work weaves together movement, music, spoken word, nervous system awareness, intimacy work, and deep human connection. Through modalities such as Tender Moments, Tantric Dance, embodied relational work, meditation, and live performance, she creates spaces where people are invited to soften, feel, and reconnect with themselves beyond performance or survival.

Known for her raw honesty, warmth, and powerful stage presence, Liesbet bridges the worlds of art, healing, and collective transformation. Her performances are both grounding and emotionally evocative, inviting audiences into a deeper experience of aliveness, tenderness, and truth.

She is the founder of The Self Love Academy and facilitates workshops, intensives, and transformational programs throughout the Netherlands.

events.title: In the Heart
events.description:
In this workshop, you will connect with your heart and the hearts of others, within the magical unity field that we create through visualization, bodywork, and role-playing.

Whether your heart is trembling with fear of being seen, closed off, confused, or joyfully leaping towards everyone, it says nothing about whether you can receive love. The universal love from which we essentially exist and are connected embraces every state of being. And precisely where we dare to meet ourselves and each other in the place of truth, love has a chance to flow. Love for our vulnerable and our fearful hearts. This afternoon, we will bring our hearts home through gentle, careful, systemic, shamanistic, and tantric exploration.

Welcome to all the shocked, brave, and curious hearts!
Manja Gruson
About the workshop facilitator Manja Gruson: "For twelve years, I have run a healing practice in which I use my own developed methods to quickly reach the core, make connections, and carefully invite and guide my clients towards self-understanding and self-love through awareness of their protective patterns.…"

events.title: Outdoor Stage with: Sylvia van Swieten, Mohammed Cissewko, Lasana Camera and Amara Diabate
Blindfolded Self Connection with Josefine Adriana
events.description:
Eliminate outer judgement and comparison to drop into deeper connection with your true authentic self. A moment to slow down, to make space and to welcome all there is with compassion and love. Exploring your inner world, how does it feel to be 'you' in this moment?
 Josefine Adriana
 Josefine Adriana brings deep presence and awareness into her work, weaving together holistic therapy, trauma-sensitive embodiment, mindfulness and spirituality. She supports others in shedding layers and reconnecting to a deeper sense of inner freedom.
 Her own journey through dark and life changing moments led her to explore a wide range of healing modalities. It became clear that living from the heart, and trusting the innate wisdom of the body, was essential to truly feel alive.
 With a calm and grounded energy, Adriana holds transformative spaces where others can safely explore their inner landscapes. She invites students into deeper connection, while encouraging trust in their unique rhythm and the capacity to meet whatever arises.

events.title: Mahfil Duo Ali Seif
events.description: Mahfil is a transnational duo exploring the shared roots of Indian and Persian classical music through deep listening and collective memory. While their sound may echo contemporary fusion, Mahfil’s essence lies in returning to origin, where rhythm, melody, and storytelling transcend borders. The duo brings together Ali Seif (Iran/NL), a multidisciplinary artist shaped by Persian folk and cinematic soundscapes, and Dink Rupa, a tabla player of the Lucknow gharana and longtime disciple of Pandit Swapan Chaudhuri. Grounded in classical tradition yet open in form, Mahfil creates immersive performances where ancient modes and intricate rhythms meet in a living, breathing dialogue.

events.title: Klei Jezelf Blij Workshop
events.description: "The most relaxing hour of the entire Landjuweel event," one participant reportedly told his friend during the clay workshop, "was..."

events.title: Kagiso Universalis
events.description: Kagiso Universalis is a bardic troubadour. A playful and spirited life-artist and a coincidentalist, a running gig on the stage of life. As a childe he played with the English and Dutch language, and developed that into a gift of creating captivating poetry. Kagiso tried studying game design, but realised that the game of life is the most magical of all, so he decided to live fully for his art, starting to paint and write poems about all his mystical and psychedelic revelations. Kagiso honed his performance skills at open stages, and really anywhere people were willing to listen. His poetry isn’t a stage act, it’s a way of life. In 2020 he fell asleep from a treetop and lost his leg as a result, after surviving that accident, he had even more wisdom and love for life to share. To this day, Kagiso exudes that childlike playful spirit, and reminds you gently of your inner child and their long lost dreams. When Kagiso tells you one of his tales of love, adventure or mystical magic, you’ll likely be whisked away by his playful voice, and curiosity inducing cadence.…

events.title: Eric, the magician behind the curtain.
events.description:
Eric the Magician will appear and disappear again around the Gnome House Stage (Kabouterhuys) at Landjuweel. Hearing, seeing, smelling, tasting, feeling, and thinking are the ingredients of a magician. Eric creates his own illusions; he doesn't like to let them go and loves to involve you in them. Beyond the smoke and mirrors, there is room for attention, and before you know it, you are spellbound. You don’t need to wear a red hat to be a Gnome.


 With Landjuweel, Eric the Magician will reappear and disappear around the Kabouterhuys Stage. Hearing, seeing, smelling, tasting, feeling, and thinking are the ingredients of a magician. Eric creates his own illusions; he doesn't like to lose them and loves to make you part of them. Beyond the smoke and mirrors, there is room for attention, and before you know it, you are spellbound. You don’t need to wear a red hat to be a Gnome.

events.title: INOYSON : Lucid Dreams Universe
events.description:
INOYSON is the lucid dream universe of a film composer, multi-instrumentalist, producer, vocalist, songwriter, painter and consciousness explorer.
 This year, the INOYSON band will perform their Lucid Dreaming Voyage, featuring new compositions and special guests Thierry Ganz and Kidd Nebula.
 Sebastian Vega will join on bass, Thimo Gijezen on guitar, and Frank Hopman on drums.
 ANYA will also join with an intuitive movement performance.
 INOYSON’s style is cinematic music for imaginary films — soundscapes you can see in your mind while listening to the artist’s compositions.
 In this creator’s music, you can hear a fusion of symphonic/chamber orchestral waves with alternative jazz, spiritual electronica, psychedelic avant-garde, elements of experimental rock, jazzcore, soul and neoclassical trip-hop.
 Conduct your imagination with INOYSON

events.title: Pote CompLote - Las Corpa
events.description:
Pote CompLote is a cabaret format that brings together multidisciplinary women circus artists in a collective act of creation. Through their individual artistic research and performance practices, the artists affirm and share their perspectives on emancipation, visibility, and the social role of women in the contemporary circus landscape.
 Guided by the external eye of Las Corpa, this cabaret transforms the performance space into a playground for experimentation, encounter, and expression. Each act reflects a unique artistic voice while contributing to a collective narrative of strength, creativity, and expression.
 This edition features: Rocio Patricio — Fixed Trapeze
 Ana Al Bitar — Aerial Rope
 Rachele Monfort — Cyr Wheel
 Valentina Pace — Hula Hoop Juggling
 A vibrant celebration of contemporary circus, female artistry, and collective creation, Pote CompLote invites audiences into a shared space where personal stories, physical virtuosity, and poetic imagery come together in a unique experience.
 Valentina Pace
 Originally from Chile, Valentina discovered circus arts at the age of 16.…

events.title: Lino printing workshop for ages 10+
events.description:
KIMTAM TATTOO is a tattoo artist with Javanese-Surinamese roots, based in Rotterdam. She combines craftsmanship, culture, and storytelling by creating unique designs that tell a personal story. Stamping techniques have been used worldwide for centuries to print patterns and images on paper and textiles. In some parts of Southeast Asia, similar techniques were even used to create tattoos. Inspired by this rich tradition, KIMTAM designs her own stamps and uses them as the basis for her tattoo work.

• For children from 10 years old
Older children get to work themselves as creators. They learn how to design a simple image, cut it out in lino, print it on paper, and then turn it into a temporary stencil on the skin. This way, they experience the entire creative process: from idea to final result.

The workshop focuses on creativity, craftsmanship, and discovering a unique art form that people have been using for generations to make stories, symbols, and identity visible. Everyone goes home with their own prints and also a unique temporary tattoo that they have created themselves.

events.title: Making dream catchers
events.description:
Create your own dream catcher using natural and found materials. With twigs, string, beads, and feathers collected from birds, we create a special object full of stories and imagination.

What dreams, wishes, or adventures will your dream catcher capture?

events.title: MC Katootje
events.description:
MC Katootje will take you on a journey.
Without any distractions, she'll put on a show.
With your child and with your grandparent,
She'll captivate you with her words.
She's truly the most entertaining!
Not always relaxed, but...
Eh... Come to InstrumenTaal Stage!
Then we'll all see it together
and have a great time working together :)

Weaving a musical carpet is a noble aspiration...

events.title: Trammelant in sprookjesland
events.description:
Improvisational Theater with Daphne Zandbreg.
 Soul Song: Personal Music Mirrors For You

Trammelant in Sprookjesland is a cheerful and musically-themed performance where children and parents are invited to play together in the Ruigoord children's tent. Where do those winding paths lead? What wonderful creatures will you encounter? And... who will you be today? Together, we will bring the most exciting, beautiful, and magical stories to life!

events.title: Marly Niyambura
events.description:
Niyambura is a born singer, songwriter & poet from Kenya. With a deep appreciation for her African heritage she uses her voice to express matters of her heart in Kikuyu Kiswahili & english.
 Life's experiences and experiences of other people's life's journeys are her source of inspiration.

events.title: Estroe
events.description: Esther Roozendaal, known as @estroe, is an electronic music artist who explores the boundaries of sound design and atmosphere. While she once shaped dancefloors with deep and refined techno, her focus has now shifted to creating immersive soundscapes and subtle, evolving beats—created using modular synthesizers. Her music moves between texture and rhythm, blending experimental synthesis with a deep emotional core. In her approach, she embraces nuance, space, and imperfection, bringing an organic human touch to electronic sound. In 2025, Estroe is actively performing and releasing modular music, both solo and as part of a duo with Raaf Modular. She is also the driving force behind Rosedale Records, which relaunches with a fresh vision for modular-driven productions. Beyond her own artistry, she is dedicated to coaching and education, mentoring at Music Conservatory Haarlem and integrating Acceptance and Commitment Training (ACT) into her work with students and artists navigating the pressures of the music industry. She also contributes regularly as a columnist for Interface magazine, where she reflects on music, modular performances, and the artistic process.…

events.title: Ecstatic Dance & Breathflow with DJ Y∆RL & Eline Bouma
events.description:
Temple of Self invites you to Ecstatic Dance & Breathflow with DJ Y∆RL and Eline Bouma. Eline will open the space with breathwork: gentle, clear, and precise enough to ground yourself before the music takes over. Then, Y∆RL will guide the dance in a long journey from earth to sky, incorporating organic percussion, global influences, and electronic grooves.

DJ YARL
Y∆RL is an Amsterdam-based DJ who operates at the intersection of electronic club music and ritual movement. His sets unfold slowly and with intention, blending organic percussion, global influences, and electronic grooves into long journeys that support release rather than spectacle. Drawing from a background in film music and underground club culture, Y∆RL brings a strong sense of pacing, atmosphere, and narrative to his sets. He plays for dancers, not for attention, focusing on flow rather than peak-after-peak drops. The atmosphere is warm, patient, and deep, while leaving room for familiar tracks and shared moments of lightness. Y∆RL regularly performs at Ecstatic Dance events such as Ambrosia, Free Flow, Maankaravaan, and festivals including Landjuweel and Ecstatic Dance Festival Holland.…

events.title: Jungle of Wires
events.description: Dutch electronic performer Jungle of Wires (Mandy Bakker) always seeking for big sound with an ever changing small setup. There is a story in every patch with lots of rhythmic exploration and improvisations. No live performance is the same so prepare for a sonic adventure!

events.title: Roswitha B2B Carmen
events.description:
Carmen & Roswitha are a DJ duo from Amsterdam who found each other through their shared love for the early days of minimal techno. Their sound can best be described as rhythmic, deep and driven by funky basslines. On Saturday afternoon, they will cover the Boogie Bos stage with a warm blanket of clean clicks, cutting snares, mysterious vocals and bass-filled kicks.
 Roswitha has previously played at festivals such as Into the Woods, Wildeburg, and Garbicz, and also produces music with her electronic live-act Roswitha Nash. Carmen started her journey in electronic music more recently, finding her sound in playing at many local raves and festivals such as Georgie’s Wundergarten and Amuse festival. From their studio in Amsterdam Noord, they are constantly developing their individual and communal sounds.
 Together they founded ATINATI, a minimal inspired event series. Their inspiration came from a trip to Tbilisi, the capital of Georgia, where they were captivated by the city’s progressive and activist music scene. The dance floor is a space where norms are shed and a new world is created. In Georgian, ATINATI means ‘the first ray of light in the darkness’ or ‘the first knowledge’.…

events.title: Goey & Kirkos
events.description: The duo, Goey/Kyrkos, performs a selection of medieval musical pieces using traditional instruments, all in their own unique and improvisational style, complemented by the dancing and singing of Fatima and Sabouschka.

events.title: Ralph de Jongh & Roos Kanis
events.description:
Roos Kanis & Ralph de Jong and magic friends - Shaman Blues

When the raw, soulful blues of Ralph de Jongh merges with the ceremonial, theatrical, and musical world of Roos Kanis, something new is created: Shaman Blues.

Ralph has been one of the most idiosyncratic blues artists in the Netherlands for years. With his deeply rooted voice, free-flowing playing, and deep roots in blues, rock, and roots music, he manages to turn every performance into a unique experience. His career has taken him to countless stages, festivals, and collaborations within the Dutch blues scene.

Roos brings another dimension: years of experience in ceremonial work, a treasure trove of songs from her traveling musical theater, and the creation of connecting experiences where music, story, and experience come together.

Together, they create not just a concert, but a musical journey. A performance where blues, groove, ceremony, and theater meet. The audience is taken on a trance of rhythm, soul, and play, where the boundary between artist and audience slowly fades away.

Shaman Blues is an invitation to meet yourself and others in a field of joy, freedom, love, and connection.…

events.title: DJ Witte Aap
events.description: Not a DJ, but a music selector playing wonderful tunes from all over the world!

events.title: Workshop Resonance Movement Playground
events.description:
Resonance Movement is a practice designed to grow in embodying yourself fully through dance and movement. We use somatic exercises to step beyond the patterns that our bodies are familiar with, and bring intention and meaning into our dance. Together, this will form a toolbox for the situations in life to connect to our anger, sadness, joy, or just to feel grounded in the body. Expression is the most powerful tool for processing emotions.
 In the Resonance Movement Playground, we find lightness in everything that we are moving with. We work on our body awareness while connecting to others, expressing and playing. In these exercises, we will flow through limitations, embody strange characters, move through vulnerability, and most of all have fun together. We dance with lightness, presence and intention.

events.title: Sumanth Manjunath & Quinten Rutgers
events.description: Virtuoso Indian cellist Sumanth Manjunath embarks on a musical journey in Theo's Tea Garden with singer-songwriter and contemporary troubadour, Quinton Rutgers.

events.title: Lisa and the Brown Prince
events.description:
‘On a clear evening, when the sun has set and the moon looks longingly at the stars, Lisa steps out of the window of her small house. Suddenly, she sees a large palace standing on top of the mountain in the bright moonlight. She can hardly believe her eyes; she has never seen a palace before. ‘Does a prince live there?’, Lisa wonders. Are you also curious to hear how this Caribbean fairytale continues? Then come and listen and enjoy the most beautiful illustrations in Wijnand Stomp’s ‘Kamishi-bai’ magic box. The master of Caribbean stories alternates between many character types and tropical songs. Together with his puppet ‘Bird-friend’, he takes both young and old on adventures. When the snake from his story starts to coil, everyone is transported to a fairytale world.

Wijnand Stomp published this story in 1997 because he could not find any books for his own children featuring a brown prince as the main character, as an alternative to the eternal image of the (white) prince on a white horse.

events.title: Soulsong
events.description:
Lex Empress
 Versatile and innovative – never the same: that's Lex Empress. To understand what she does, you have to see her perform, as no performance is ever the same. She creates interactive experiences where the audience is at the center. With singing in 7 languages, with over 4 octaves of range, and a deep belief in the potential of humanity, she opens hearts and minds like no other.

Soul Song: Personal Music Mirrors For You
 "Soul songs" are personal songs transmitted live, reflecting the state of each individual. Together with pianist Gillian Baracs, who has now created around 9000 personal songs in seven languages worldwide, with deep musical skill and intuitive listening, we are happy to offer this again and welcome everyone who feels called to receive their song.

events.title: Shamanic Breath
events.description:
Shamanic Breathing is a powerful technique that initiates a psycho-spiritual transformation process. The Breath connects you with the pure consciousness of the element Air. It flows through you, like a touch that awakens you. Everything that moves within you comes to life, beyond everyday awareness and ego. The Breath as a portal through which you travel to remember your true self and origin, in the gentle yet powerful field of Shamanic practice.

Spaceholders:
 Gaby Moon Crow
 Lisette de Jong
 Linda Nummerdor

events.title: State of Being
events.description:
My name is Mer, and my journey from a restless and chaotic mind to one of deep observation and peace has transformed not only my life but also how I teach. Through conscious breathing, meditation, and mindful movement, I’ve come to understand the profound connection between mind and body. In my classes, I guide you to explore your own breath and body awareness, helping you remain present and grounded.
 Restless, chaotic, nervous—these were some of the adjectives that used to describe my mind and character. After years immersed in meditation, I have witnessed how these traits transformed into an observer, meticulous, and creative. The simple act of learning to observe my mind, understanding how it connects with my body, and disidentifying from both has granted me an inner peace bathed in acceptance, compassion, and LOVE for myself.
 Motivated by these experiences, I decided to training as a Mayeutik Coach (Kuestiona), Holistic Therapist specializing in conscious breathing therapy (Yogalap) and in Non-conscious mind. On the side other courses completed my tools as a therapist: Vipassana, Bodywork and Reiki.…

events.title: Tantric Shibari Workshop: how to play with Ropes
events.description:
Kim & Kyara will take you on a journey into their world of Conscious Kink and Sensual Play. We bring together two worlds that we already know well: tantra and kink. Tantra = consciously experiencing what is within you and connecting with the energy of another in a mature way. Being vulnerable and taking emotions seriously. Heart (feeling) above head (reason).

Shibari = Japanese knot art, or erotic bondage. Originating from the kink world, it has become increasingly popular... You can encounter shibari in many forms, from sadistic to cuddling knots: rope is incredibly diverse, so we introduce our favorite form:: tantric knots.
How can you play with rope in a pleasant and conscious way without it being too complicated or erotic? How do you even get started with shibari?

We will show you some demonstrations of what a shibari game looks like, and we will practice together where you can get to know the diversity of rope and play. The workshop is non-sexual/clothing remains on, and consent is sacred: if something doesn't feel good, you are welcome to look / step out of the exercise and rejoin later.

About us…

events.title: Couple Positive
events.description:
Couple Positive Uplifting from Amsterdam! It's like tasting a delicious bonbon with the flavors of wild, funky groove rhythms, global soundscapes, and poetic melodies. Alongside the West African Electro N'goni that roars and swings, the trumpet and saxophone create inspiring notes. And on top of this raw spectacle, there are moving vocals and loops that steadily get you dancing...
 Become aware of an Uplifting experience

events.title: Babette
events.description:
Babette Voets is a multidisciplinary artist, performer, storyteller, and founder of Controubadour. Using harp, singing, and spoken word, she creates poetic worlds where transformation, empathy, and imagination come together. Through the practice of shapeshifting, she explores how we can create new connections with ourselves, each other, and the living world around us by temporarily adopting a different perspective.

"Pleidooi voor diversiteit" (Appeal for Diversity) is a musical exploration of all voices and worlds that exist in our lives, and how they can free us from a fixed scenario in our minds.

events.title: Walhallala
events.description:
A self-healing journey
 There's nothing up there in the 7th heaven!
 From your head to your heart, down to your feet. Connecting with the earth. With every deep breath, you sink deeper into infinity. Your voice resonates and echoes within the AL....
 Shaking, tapping, kneading, bouncing, and sighing. A complete cleansing from top to bottom.
 Final destination: Cosmic Silence

events.title: Aron Friedman’s Psychedelic Mirror Ball
events.description:
If you’ve ever wondered what the inside of a mirror ball sounds like, you’ve come to the right place. Space disco, funk, rock, and other mind-expanding dance tunes morph into a psychedelic groove to make you move. Easy on the Kool-Aid though, it’s laced with... Oh, never mind, too late now. Buckle up and enjoy the ride!
 Links:
 Soundcloud 
 Instagram

events.title: EnkiDoeMaar
events.description:
As one of the founders of the No-Talent collective KALEIDO, Enkidoe is the main programmer of our Queer Space. And since they love trying something new, why not a DJ Debut?!
 In true No-Talent fashion, expect a DJ set that is undefinable, unexplainable and totally incomprehensible. Bring your earplugs and leave your expectations at the door, cause you’ve probably never heard such a hot mess.

events.title: Bang. Boos. Bedroefd. Blij.
events.description:
BASJE guides: Afraid. Angry. Sad. Happy.

In this workshop, we explore the full depth and breadth of human emotion. BASJE will take you through all four basic emotions. We create space for what is, without judgment or haste. Because emotions are not obstacles, but information, and they want to be seen and given space!

This workshop is for everyone, whether you are used to going deep or just starting to discover what is within you. Together, we will create a safe and open space where it is okay to be afraid, angry, sad, and happy.

Come as you are. In this workshop, you will actively explore all four emotions yourself. You will emerge renewed.

events.title: DeBodhi
events.description: Amsterdam based DJ. Expect dirty grooves, funky twists, melodic organic house with a punch of indie dance.

events.title: Trackvogels + CEF ASHANTA
events.description:
Trackvogels

Where exhilarating rhythms meet driving guitar riffs, layered with psychedelic synth textures and deep pulsating bass and drums.
They form a family-like unity on stage. Fusing traditional influences from different parts of the globe with an intergalactic touch. Making borders disappear with hypnotising, warm & gritty sounds.…

events.title: Closing remarks
events.description: After a day filled with discovery, learning, making, and meeting new people, we come together to quietly conclude the day. It's a moment for reflection, to consider what has touched us and to share our experiences with each other. While enjoying a warm cup of chaga tea, we allow ourselves to be guided by the moment. Perhaps we sing together, do a simple yoga exercise, tell stories to each other, or simply enjoy being together.

events.title: Florelei
events.description:
Florelei is a captivating artist who combines melancholic dark folk with dreamlike atmospheres, woven from the warm textures of voice and guitar. At the heart of it all is a voice of rare and arresting beauty, one that carries equal weight in a barely audible whisper and a soaring, gut-wrenching cry.
The music is steeped in a fascination with life, death, time, nature, and existential questions. Each song feels like a doorway into an inner world, inviting stillness and depth. Performances are more than concerts; they are moments of intimate connection, a shared journey into the depths of emotion and thought, balancing melody and chaos, light and dark.

events.title: Koen Boeijinga
events.description:
In his solo performance, Koen Boeijinga creates a unique one-man-band experience, inspired by shepherd tales and musical traditions from around the world. His wind instruments – saxophone, flute, and bagpipes – are central, while the mesmerizing sounds of harmonium, bass drum, Indonesian gamelan, and countless other instruments fill the space. As a modern nomad, he travels through diverse musical landscapes, where myth, ritual, and improvisation blend together.

Koen Boeijinga: soprano saxophone, wind instruments, harmonium, gamelan, percussion, fx

events.title: Pote CompLote - Las Corpa
events.description:
Pote CompLote is a cabaret format that brings together multidisciplinary women circus artists in a collective act of creation. Through their individual artistic research and performance practices, the artists affirm and share their perspectives on emancipation, visibility, and the social role of women in the contemporary circus landscape.
 Guided by the external eye of Las Corpa, this cabaret transforms the performance space into a playground for experimentation, encounter, and expression. Each act reflects a unique artistic voice while contributing to a collective narrative of strength, creativity, and expression.
 This edition features: Rocio Patricio — Fixed Trapeze
 Ana Al Bitar — Aerial Rope
 Rachele Monfort — Cyr Wheel
 Valentina Pace — Hula Hoop Juggling
 A vibrant celebration of contemporary circus, female artistry, and collective creation, Pote CompLote invites audiences into a shared space where personal stories, physical virtuosity, and poetic imagery come together in a unique experience.
 Valentina Pace
 Originally from Chile, Valentina discovered circus arts at the age of 16.…

events.title: Jolly Mango's
events.description:
The Jolly Mangos form a unique trio with electric accordion, guitar, and percussion. They bring an energetic mix of pop music and South American rhythms that quickly gets everyone moving. Each performance feels genuine and like a collaboration between the band and the audience, creating a magical atmosphere. Whether you prefer slow or fast tempos, nostalgic or vibrant – this day will keep you entertained.

Ruigoord is also (like for many others) the birthplace of The Jolly Mangos. It's where much of their music originated, where most of their performances took place, and they are now working on setting up their own rehearsal studio in a dedicated workshop.

events.title: TAJ
events.description: TAJ is a vinyl enthusiast, DJ, and producer. He enriches the nightlife of Amsterdam with an eclectic DJ style that features percussion, obscure sounds, and exotic music. TAJ constantly challenges both listeners and dancers through changing rhythms and quick transitions – expect the unexpected.

events.title: Intimate haircuts
events.description: Intimate Haircuts aim to give your junk the funky twist Landjuweel calls for. Ranadhira reshapes, bleaches, colours, paints and decorates your pubic area to create an unforgettable art piece. Find your favorite hairdresser in the KALEIDO Faerie Forest!

events.title: Heiko Dijker's Mandala Soul
events.description:
Igor Bezget: Guitar, Sarod, Oud, FX
 Simone Giaccomini: Keys, Guitar, Vocal, Modular
 Sharat Chandra Srivastava: Indian Violin
 Guro Kvernokk - Etherial Voice
 Heiko Dijker: Tabla, Konakol
 
 ALIEN ROOTS is the title of a space project that brings together 5 musicians from different parts of the Milky Way with a universal spectrum of Sound & Rhythm. Igor, Simone, Sharat and Heiko will take you through diverse sound worlds from Indian classical music to jazz and modern urban music to the influences of Roman stornellos, united by the desire for a unified sonic space, based both on the foundations of different musical traditions of the world, as well as on different approaches to otherworldly improvisation and composition, united by the futurism of electronic sounds.

events.title: Shake Your Booty with DJ Dragonslayer
events.description: With "Booty" as its overarching theme, DJ Dragonslayer will be playing music for dancing and shaking your hips. This includes Afrobeat, Favela Funk, R&B bangers, and everything in between.

events.title: DJ Witte Aap
events.description: Not a DJ, but a music selector playing wonderful tunes from all over the world!

events.title: Body paint corner
events.description:
In this workshop, we will cover the body with all sorts of neon colors so that you can confidently step out into the night in your newly designed second skin!

We work with outlines and patterns, but most importantly, let your mind be free and follow your inspiration!
From subtle lines to complete works of art – you decide how you shine.

events.title: Karaoke Bar: Valse Noten
events.description: Unleash your inner rock star, nightingale, or howling wolf! Here, we don't sing perfectly, but with full passion. Everyone can participate, whether you sound like a star or a vacuum cleaner.

events.title: Awichas DJ set
events.description:
AWICHAS is a music project initiated by Bolivian/Brazilian artist and performer Ibelisse Guardia Ferragutti. AWICHAS draws from Andean ritual from Ibelisse’s birthplace Bolivia. Where she took recordings and data from these journeys and worked with sound artist and musician Jochem van Tol to translate them into electronic music.
 During Landjuweel they will share a collection of Andean & Latinx electronic heavy DJ set. A combination of their original AWICHAS compositions and many other Latinx artists. Expect a danceable, grounding and full of joy set.
 Links:
 Website 
 Instagram

events.title: Wild Grace
events.description:
A warm bubble of elements exists between heaven and earth.
 A place where the strength of the ground meets the freedom of the sky.
 Where bare feet touch the earth and roots grow deep into the heart.
 Wild Grace is an invitation to land fully in the present moment. To feel. To listen. To remember the voice of your soul.

 Through movement, sound, nature, and creativity, we reconnect with ourselves and with one another.
 A journey back to yourself.
 A celebration of life.
 A dance between heaven and earth.

 What Can You Expect? 
 At the heart of Wild Grace lies the powerful dialogue between live music, partnerwork, and contemporary dance theatre. The live music breathes with the performers, creating a living landscape of sound that guides every movement and emotion.
 About Ivana
 From a very young age, Ivana knew that dance was her path in life. Movement and creativity have always been her way of expressing emotions, telling stories, and connecting with the world around her.…

events.title: DJ Babette
events.description:
Emerging from the queer underground - home base Vrankrijk - DJ Babette (who just entered his 50s) discovered his passion for DJing quite recently. Hellbent on squeezing every last drop out of his midlife crisis, his sets carry that extra punch of urgency of someone arriving a lil’ late to the party.
 Babette chases after bouncy, captivating grooves on the house/tech house/indie spectrum, infused with funk, latin, disco, homosexualism and elusive, quirky and euphoric twists. You’ll be going places for sure!

events.title: Pup & Cat Play: An Introduction
events.description:
Have you ever wondered what the world looks like through the eyes of your loyal four-legged friend?

Join this workshop for a playful introduction to pup/cat play. Although pet play is mostly known from the fetish and kink community, in this workshop we approach it from a non-sexual setting. BDSM therefore plays no role here. The focus is on play, imagination, and channeling your inner animal: its instincts, energy, and way of moving. Sensuality is welcome, but the emphasis is on pleasure, relaxation, and play.

Pup BoBo will share together with their kiddo pup Ozzy what puppyplay means to them, how a pup, cat, or pet persona can emerge, and how to enter the right headspace. After all, the way animals guard their boundaries and express their desires can be surprisingly instructive. More of a cat person than a puppy? This workshop is for you too. BoBo’s counterpart BeauBeau will also be there to support the kittens.

We will end the workshop with an open petplay era, where you can play, explore, or simply observe in your own way.

events.title: Cuervo Cuervo
events.description:
Cuervo Cuervo is the collaboration between Argentinian musician and charango player Agustin Ganem, and American producer and multi-instrumentalist Benjamin Hill.

After a chance encounter in the streets of Bacalar in 2018, they joined forces to create a unique fusion of psychedelic electronic music blended with Latin American folk music and live improvisation. They are known for their electrifying live performances featuring virtuoso displays of musicianship, tasteful electronics, live looping, and a capacity for improvisation and collaboration. Ganem combines his mastery of South American charango and guitar with live looping, while Hill fuses North American electronic music with the folk influences of lapsteel guitar and classic Latin percussion elements such as caxixi and bongo. No two Cuervo Cuervo live performances are ever alike – each is a unique improvisational dialogue between the two that is created directly on the spot for each audience.

Links:
Website

events.title: Divine Kirtan
events.description:
Growing up in a spiritual family in India, Kirtan has been a natural part of my life since birth. Rooted in the traditions of Bhakti Yoga, I developed a deep connection to devotional music and mantra meditation from an early age.
 In 2024, I began leading Kirtans and founded Divine Kirtan in The Hague. Since then, our community has grown from intimate gatherings in yoga studios to large-scale Kirtan festivals welcoming 150–200 participants. Through these events, we aim to share the authentic spirit and traditional roots of Bhakti Yoga, creating spaces for connection, meditation, joyful devotional singing and community
 Through Divine Kirtan, we regularly organize Kirtan gatherings across the Netherlands. Whether you are new to Kirtan or have been practicing for years, you are warmly invited to join us in chanting, connecting, and experiencing the transformative power of Bhakti.
 Come experience the joy of Kirtan with us at Ruigoord and other inspiring venues throughout the Netherlands.

events.title: Oliv Oliv
events.description:
Oliv Oliv is the project of composer, producer, and sound artist Olivier Terpstra. Combining synthesizers, instruments, and field recordings, he creates immersive soundscapes that balance electronic experimentation, dance, and storytelling.
 Inspired by cultural exchange, language, and memory, his music is a cinematic journey full of layered textures and evolving rhythms. Following his debut album Tehran Mountains (Uncloud Editions, 2025), he recently released Howl / زوزه showcasing a more expressive and experimental sound.
 For Landjuweel Festival, you can expect an hour-long set that brings an atmospheric and immersive sound, connecting electronic and acoustic elements in a spatial setting.
 Oliv Oliv has performed at events such as Modulation, Uncloud Festival, and Linecheck Festival in Milan, and was part of the Popronde 2025 tour.

events.title: Sharrif Simmons& Grazzhoppa
events.description:
Sharrif Simmons is a poet, musician, and member of the legendary The Last Poets. Together with turntablist DJ Grazzhoppa and live band, he performs spoken word full of rhythm, urgency, and the spirit of hip hop – where poetry, beats, and music come together.


Sharrif Simmons is a spoken word poet, author, and musician whose writing and performance career spans several decades. He emerged from New York City’s explosive spoken word poetry scene during the early 90’s and quickly made a name for himself performing alongside artists like Yassin Bey, Talib Kweli, and Saul Williams at popular New York venues such as Nuyorican Poets Café, Brooklyn Moon Café, CBGB's, The Blue Note, and Joe's Pub, to name a few. Sharrif is currently the newest member of the iconic poetry collective THE LAST POETS.

events.title: Ritmo Revival feat. horns from Mototroko and Zebra streetband
events.description:
Ritmo Revival injects new life into the energy of Ritmo Samba Squad, the band that performed at festivals and events both domestically and internationally between 2008 and 2016. Led by Sebastian Skeete Mc Leed, the initiator of the Yaybolo Stage, the band returns with a new generation of musicians and a fresh, contemporary sound.

With an explosive mix of Samba Reggae, Drum & Bass, and unique rhythms, Ritmo Revival delivers a show that will leave everyone captivated. After a high-energy performance at the Ballonnenfeest in Paradiso, the band presents a unique collaboration with brass from Mototroko and Zebra Streetband at Landjuweel.

Expect an immersive combination of powerful percussion, dazzling brass instruments, and pure festival energy: a rhythmic spectacle full of groove, power, and surprises.

events.title: Leon Row
events.description:
Leon Row has been shaping sound for over 14 years, a journey that started somewhere between smoky after-hours sessions, underground dance floors and a growing obsession with collecting records that simply did something to people.
 What started in the club scene at seventeen quickly grew into organising events and eventually stepping behind the decks himself. What began as a love for digging records and soundtracking afterparties soon turned into a full-blown obsession that never left.
 Recently, Leon expanded his universe with the launch of Bon Bon Vivant, a traveling circus and stage concept where music, performance, theatre, energetic chocolate and playful chaos collide. A colourful extension of the energy and imagination that already runs through his sets.
 Today, Leon moves effortlessly between styles without ever losing his own signature. His sets drift f rom eclectic grooves and slow-burning rhythms into steamy indie dance, rolling house, deep hypnotic minimal and unexpected left turns. Always layered, emotional and deeply alive.
 What ties it all together is intuition. Leon doesn’t force a set, he reads the room, taps into the moment and lets the energy unfold naturally.…

events.title: Hibotep
events.description:
“Every set is a ritual, every drop is a revelation.”
 “Hibotep doesn’t follow trends—she rewrites the map.”
 “One of the fiercest live selectors on the continent.”

Hibotep is a sonic warrior, genre-shifter, and force of nature who defies every mold. Based between Kampala and the cosmos, she channels chaos into power, building hypnotic worlds where techno, grime, gqom, hip hop, industrial, and experimental frequencies collide with East African energy and deep ancestral resonance.

Of Somali origin, DJ, producer, filmmaker, and fashion alchemist, Hibotep isn’t just multidisciplinary—she’s multidimensional. Whether performing at CTM, Dekmantel, Sonar or Nyege Nyege, her sets are wild rides through time and space: unpredictable, emotionally charged, and spiritually grounded. She doesn't just play tracks—she casts spells. In 2019 she joined the We Are Europe roster and became the face of Native Instruments traktor and became resident at the Moheim Triennale.

As part of the Nyege Nyege collective, Hibotep represents the cutting edge of underground African electronic music. Her work reclaims narrative, dismantles borders, and remixes reality itself.…

events.title: Kidd Nebula
events.description:
Eccentric FUNK, combined with Jazz and Hip-Hop inspired by great legends like Funkadelic/Parliament, Prince, James Brown and Jamiroquai. Kidd Nebula & The Voodoo-Funk Conspiracy transform every stage into a safe space for human connection through art, passion, and cosmic vibrations. This energetic six-piece band, fronted by Kidd Nebula, will take you on an intergalactic journey with heavy grooves and humorous themes that will sweep your feet off the dance floor!
 After releasing their new EP “BAD CHI” featuring four summer anthems, the band achieved their first international success during their seven-show EUROTOUR, playing packed venues in Prague (CZ), Budapest (HU) and Zagreb (HR). These Popronde (2026) selectees are making waves in the urban scene with their healing funky vibrations, opening for (international) names like Primaat (NL), Boukou Groove (USA), High Fade (SCH) and Prince bass player Ida Nielsen (SU).

 Excentrieke FUNK, gecombineerd met Jazz en Hip-Hop, geïnspireerd door legendes als Funkadelic/Parliament, Prince, James Brown en Jamiroquai.…

events.title: DJ Summer
events.description: Expect fast beats, cheeky vocals, and pure dance floor energy. DJ Summer's playful style and infectious vibe will have you bouncing from Bottom to Top.

events.title: Self-Touch & Sensual Flow
events.description: A playful and grounding space to explore self-touch, sensual movement, and self-connection. Through slow dance and guided awareness, you’ll reconnect with your body and its rhythms. We close with an optional cuddle puddle, inviting warmth, consent, and shared softness.

events.title: The Tearjerkers
events.description:
Paulisme and the "Tearjerkers" bring Dutch-language reggae/dub about love, life, and wonder. Besides his band The Amsterdelics, Paulisme has also played with De Porders (a theater/smartlaps-reggae project) and performed at Bevrijdingspop, Zwarte Cross, Paradiso, and Melkweg. His summer hit "De Zon" earned him a "Debut in a Minute" on 3FM in 2025.

PAUL VAN MUSSCHER, A.K.A. PAULISME, IS A VERSATILE SINGER/SONGWRITER, DJ, PRODUCER AND ORGANIZER WITHIN THE DUTCH DUB-, REGGAE- AND UNDERGROUND SCENE. WITH OVER THIRTY YEARS OF EXPERIENCE ON STAGES, FESTIVALS AND CLUBS, HE IS KNOWN FOR HIS ENERGIZING PERFORMANCE, SPONTANEOUS INTERACTION AND STRONG CONNECTION WITHIN THE COMMUNITY. AND NATURALLY AS THE FRONTMAN OF HIS OWN BAND, THE AMSTERDELICS. IN ADDITION TO HIS WORK AS AN ARTIST, PAUL ALSO ORGANIZES AND HOSTS EVENTS, PRODUCES HIS OWN MUSIC, AND WORKS WITH VARIOUS BANDS AND GUEST ARTISTS.

HIS PREVIOUS DUTCH-LANGUAGE PROJECT, DE PORDERS, WAS A TRIBUTE TO HIS NEPHEW JOHNNY JORDAAN. SMARTLAPS IN REGGAE AND ROCKSTEADY. IN A SOLD-OUT TREE IN CHICAGO, THEY PERFORMED THEIR THEATER SHOW ABOUT THE LIFE OF JOHNNY JORDAAN…

events.title: Trippin Jaguar (live)
events.description:
Trippin Jaguar is a Dutch organic house and downtempo artist performing both live and DJ sets operating in the space between deep listening and psychedelic club culture. Using synthesizers, live instrumentation and real-time improvisation, he creates slow-burning psychedelic journeys for immersive dance floors and sunset stages.
 Emerging from the European downtempo and psychedelic scene, he has performed worldwide for more than a decade, with highlights including Burning Man, Ozora Festival and Boom Festival, and many international club and festival shows, known for guiding audiences across a wide arc, from deep meditative passages to driving rhythmic flow without breaking the trance.

events.title: Temple of Self Art Route
events.description: Rolf Huijgen, known as Visual Hacker, brings music to life through light, color, and motion. His visuals move with the rhythm of the dance, translating sound into vivid, evolving worlds that invite people deeper into their journey. During Landjuweel, Rolf tunes into the energy of the space — blending intuition with craft to create visuals that both ground and uplift. His style balances organic flow with digital precision, weaving atmosphere, emotion, and rhythm into a unified experience. With roots in digital art and live performance, Rolf sees visuals as a form of connection — a way to turn sound into sight, and presence into shared movement

events.title: DJ Witte Aap
events.description: Not a DJ, but a music selector playing wonderful tunes from all over the world!

events.title: XOTO
events.description: XOTO brings energetic beats to the Queer Stage to make you DANCE!

events.title: Primo Disco
events.description: This "Disco dinosaur" has earned its reputation for getting crowds into a dancing state. Known for legendary pool parties and Christmas galas, Primo Disco makes no wasted minute, delivering the ultimate mix of disco, funk, and soul to satisfy your ears. Expect both familiar and new dancefloor fillers.

events.title: Anxiety as a Gift
events.description:
Farah Shretah, also known as Alien Mama (Kikvors Collectief)

"Anxiety as a Gift" is a ritual in which the subconscious becomes a shared journey of moving images created by you, designed by your attention. A body stands inside the circle, carrying hidden storms that surface as vivid visual landscapes.
What was meant to remain unseen begins to unfold: fragments, memories, symbols, and inner signals made public. Through the collective gaze, anxiety is transformed from a private shadow into a shared living image.

Farah Shretah is a Syrian transmedia artist, filmmaker, and XR producer based in Amsterdam. Her work spans across places, languages, and forms: born in Syria, shaped by her life in Ukraine, and later rooted in the Netherlands, where she completed her master's degree in visual culture and artistic research.

As the founder of Alien Mama Productions, Farah creates immersive works at the intersection of emotion, technology, AI, biofeedback, performance, and storytelling. Her practice transforms inner states such as fear, anxiety, and memory into shared audiovisual experiences, inviting audiences into poetic spaces where vulnerability becomes image, movement, and collective presence.

events.title: Yella Yellabow
events.description: Born in Amsterdam’s festival season, Yella Band fuses African roots with Western funk into 'afro-funk'. Fronted by Senegalese multi-talent Mame N’Diack Seck Thiam and Dutch bass artist Rosan Asmussen, the collective blends soulful storytelling, deep basslines, and a unique vocal language that transcends borders. Joined by Jur de Vries and Fabio Galeazzi, Yella Band creates danceable rhythms that celebrate unity through music. Music is medicine.

events.title: DIZZI GEETHA - LIVE SET
events.description:
Dizzi Geetha is a Queer POC artist from Sri Lanka, based in The Netherlands. Dizzi is an artist, creator, producer, composer, multi-instrumentalist, theatre maker, DJ and ceremonial facilitator. Their work is largely shaped by their love for nature and our planet, as well as their history of displacement and finding roots through sonic soundscapes. This gives their creations in music and theatre a truly unique and organic element that shapes the experiences of both the viewer and listener, rather than simply being consumed.

Links:
Instagram 
Spotify 
Website

events.title: Djembefola's
events.description:
Momo Touré

Momo Touré is the heart and soul of the drum circle at Ruigoord. For over 30 years, he has been the person who brings people together through music, using his djembe, his energy, and his gift for making everyone, from beginners to advanced players, feel part of the groove.

His roots are deeply embedded in traditional West African music. As a young man, he toured worldwide with the renowned African ballet in Guinea. He still carries this richness with him every week at Ruigoord.

Come, see, and participate!

Amier Sloot

Amier Sloot believes in the power of rhythm to bring people together. As a musician and community builder, he facilitates a drum circle every Saturday at Ruigoord alongside Momo, where everyone, regardless of experience, can immerse themselves in a shared groove and be fully present in the moment.

During Landjuweel, they (along with many other respected drummers) bring this energy: connection through music, together in the moment.

events.title: JUNO
events.description:
JUNO is a live act based in Amsterdam that deconstructs and rebuilds dance music using tuba, saxophone, synthesizers, and drums. What starts as dreamy electronica effortlessly transitions into pumping grooves and raw energy. Is it dreamy electronica or dreamy dancing?

On stage, the clash between live musicianship and electronics is striking. The saxophone and tuba transform into full-fledged synthesizers using pedals. Tight sequences and live music blend together: every show moves and breathes. Throughout the performance, PAUW shines: their self-developed light installation that responds to every beat and gradually takes over the room. An immersive experience in sync with the music.

This year, JUNO played a midnight set at ESNS ‘26 in Groningen. Previously, the band has performed successful shows at venues like Melkweg, TivoliVredenburg, and Doornroosje. The group released their own music with Ruben Hein, played twenty shows across the Netherlands as part of Popronde ‘25, and their live show was broadcast on NPO2.

For fans of Jungle by Night, Eefje de Visser, and Weval.


JUNO is een Amsterdamse live-act die dance uit elkaar trekt en opbouwt met tuba, sax, synths en drums.…

events.title: Velvet Tongues
events.description: Velvet Tongues is the meeting place of poetry and pulse. Andrej and Isis weave electronic and classical music with spoken word, opera and immersive ritual, creating performances that blur the line between concert, ceremony and dream. Drawing from mythology, alchemy, philosophy and the strange theatre of modern life, they create immersive experiences that seduce the senses before quietly unsettling the soul.

events.title: Boogie Movement
events.description:
Dreamy, you look up at the sky, where black and white patterns move against the blue. 60s Psychedelic movement? 70s Kinetic art? Remnants of 80s Memphis style? Or wildstyle forms from the 90s?
 The Boogie Bos moves outside of time, here dance joy is celebrated and underlined by movement in tall totems where shapes move along each other in moiré patterns, thus opening the doors to the dimensions of the eternal dancing gods and goddesses!

 Boogie Movement is a collective of DJs, musicians, builders, designers, performers, and dancers with a mission. From the Boogie Bos at the Landjuweel Festival to Boogie Ball in Ruigoord’s church, and the Boogie Crew behind it all, their events are all about pure joy, experimentation, wild fun, and unexpected musical surprises. When the Boogie Bos first debuted as a dance area at the Landjuweel Festival in 2022, it was clear that it fulfilled a need. Giving space to artists to reinvent the dancefloor together with the crowd, merging from the rebellious spirit of Ruigoord blending it with new generations towards the future.

events.title: Coen Aerts
events.description:
Coen Aerts has spent over two decades creating performances that exist in the charged space between performer and audience — encounters where neither party quite knows what happens next. Over the years he has developed around twenty alter egos, each one a different way of entering a room, making contact, and opening up the unexpected. Some are solo, some involve an ensemble; some speak, some don’t. What they share is a method: turning the audience from witness into accomplice.
 His best-known acts, Mr. Vibes and The Buttlers, have appeared at festivals, museums, galleries, corporate events and theatre stages across the Netherlands and beyond. In recent years his practice has grown to include interactive theatre for young audiences, approached with the same rigour and care as his adult work. Every performance is built fresh for its context — the theme, the space, the audience in front of him — not as an afterthought, but as the principle the whole practice runs on.

events.title: Collective in the Air
events.description: Be amazed by Collectief in the Air. During this sculpture route, these acrobats, illuminated against the dark background, present a series of spectacular magical performances. A visual fairytale high above the audience.

events.title: Dreamweavers
events.description:
DreamWeavers arise from a Time and Space unknown, arriving in the present moment to explore the ever-unfolding possibilities of the different planes. The physical, psychological, emotional, energetical and spiritual merge. What does it mean to have a body? What wants to be expressed, released? How do we see ourselves and each other? Can we anchor our Dreams in reality? What does the Collective need most? During Landjuweel's Beeldenroute you are welcome to tap into these themes with us, navigating the conscious, sub-conscious and unconscious world.
 Core Team: Thieu Smeets, Luna Rosa, Leila van Wetten, Soufyan Sahraoui. Music: Cedric Vermue.

events.title: Embodied Systems
events.description:
Nadia Schambach is a Utrecht-based maker working through experimentation and material exploration. Within her practice, she investigates how objects can develop their own presence and how they relate to their surroundings.
 For this exhibition, she presents sculptures that emerge from the idea of creating an independent being: an organism that lives its own temporary life. The works are constructed from a metal skeleton covered with a latex skin. Through a mechanical system, the sculptures slowly fill with air and release it again. This rhythmic movement gives the impression that they are breathing, creating a sense of vulnerability and physical presence.
 The works invite us to reflect on the present moment. Like living beings, they change over time and eventually transform. The materials carry the traces of their existence: the latex discolors, stretches and ages, while the metal slowly collects signs of time and use. The sculptures are therefore not created to remain unchanged, but to experience their own temporary existence.
 Through their changing nature, the works explore the relationship between presence and impermanence.…

events.title: Floor Nagler
events.description:
Floor Nagler explores the boundaries and connections between body and environment, and how human existence manifests within a larger whole. Through textiles, ritualistic costumes, and collaborations with other artists, she creates sensory spaces where the personal and the universal meet, breaking through stagnation. Her work reveals new movement and space, and moves between mysticism and matter, between body and dream, between reality and what can still be seen.

Beyond art, Floor works through touch, breath, and presence in an experiential body practice that invites self-reflection and awareness. In both her practice and her art, she encourages the discovery of new space within oneself.

events.title: Flow Arts
events.description: The Fire Messiah and the Flow Crew will give you an unforgettable light show during the Beeldenroute, we look forward to seeing you there :) !

events.title: Irene van Krimpen
events.description: Since the first time I visited Trance Oriënt (in March 2009), I was completely captivated by the atmosphere in the church, the Kabouterhuys, and around the fire. Since then, I've been a fan of Ruigoord and enjoy contributing to the atmosphere through dressing up, dancing, singing, and making music. I want to dance at the Kabouterhuys every evening as the sun sets. And on Sunday evenings, I go there to sing 3 traditional Irish songs before Krien Joseph starts playing.

events.title: Jeroen Cuypers
events.description: Jeroen Cuypers is an artist, designer, and maker. He creates light installations, objects, and sets that combine recycled materials, old technology, and simple autonomous systems. For the "Sculpture Route," he uses a series of lanterns on the Bon Bon Vivant stage. Together, they create a warm, analog light world: a collection of beacons, shadows, and small nocturnal presences. Each lantern functions both as an individual entity and as a swarm.

events.title: Magische Maskerbrigade
events.description:
Magical Mask Brigade

The Magical Mask Brigade was born at Landjuweel 2025, where the Magical Moon and the Space Person jointly crossed the dike.
