This is a continuous evening performance that is also featured on the Sculpture Route – the Magical Mask Brigade surprises, amazes, and entertains wandering psychonauts and seekers of spiritual experiences. The brigade adapts to the atmosphere of the evening and thus weaves the party-goer into the mystical realms of the mind...

events.title: Mariah Blue
events.description:
Mariah Blue (b. 1977 U.S.) is an Amsterdam-based interdisciplinary artist and instrument maker working across performance, new media, installation and sound, often in collaboration with other artists. Her practice explores technology through diverse materials, from stone-age pottery techniques to machine learning algorithms. Seeking to challenge definitions of technology that privilege only the complex, inaccessible, and expensive, Blue makes our embodied relationships with technology visible through experimentation and play. 
 She holds a bachelor's degree from the Rietveld Academie (2009) and an MFA from the Sandberg Institute (2019). Her work has been presented across the Netherlands and internationally in exhibitions, performances, and collaborative projects.

events.title: Misfit Valley
events.description:
Lekker Limbs (Kikvors Collectief)

You are invited into a surreal, childlike universe of strange dreamscapes & playful chaos. What begins as innocent play transforms into a collective experience, a strange mission that the audience must navigate together.

Lekker Limbs is a performance duo by Inês Certo and Sara Tompkins.
They are artists working across performance, visual art, music, movement, and digital media.
Since they met, a year ago at Landjuweel where they both performed, the artists have performed together another two times, at Fuite Cosmique in France and at Time is The New Space in Rotterdam.
Inês and Sara are particularly interested in exploring their own unique style, experimenting with participation, absurdity, visual storytelling, and live interaction.

events.title: Nadia Innocentia
events.description:
Nadia Innocentia is a costume and avant-garde fashion designer, performance artist, and maker whose practice investigates fashion as a living, experiential, and relational medium. Through interactive performances, embodied experiences, and sensory-driven interventions, she creates encounters that challenge perceptions of identity, beauty, value, and social conventions. 
 Her work uses the body as both material and meeting point a place where personal stories, social expectations, and collective imagination intersect. By combining costume, scent, ritual, and participation, Innocentia creates immersive situations that invite audiences to become active participants rather than passive observers. Her performances explore the tension between attraction and discomfort, intimacy and distance, individuality and belonging.
 For the Beeldenroute, Innocentia will present an interactive performance at the Bon Bon Vivant area, where chocolate becomes a medium for exploring identity, intimacy, and sensory memory. She will create personalized chocolate perfumes based on the unique body scent of each participant, transforming something deeply personal and invisible into a sensory artwork.…

events.title: Neon strings
events.description: Under blacklight, weighted strings spin in orbit, light trails, shifting shapes, spirals, loops, waves. A collective ever changing drawing made of light, gravity, and touch. Come and add your piece.

events.title: Nuki Nibu
events.description: Nuku Nibu is a ritualistic performance that takes the audience on a journey into a living universe of music, singing, and movement. In a cosmic dance, ancient forces meet in a continuous interplay between chaos and harmony, attraction and release. Mystical figures appear and disappear, while an immersive soundscape and live music create a world where everything is interconnected.

events.title: PUNXIANS COSMIC LIGHT ARKESTRA
events.description:
PUNXIANS COSMIC LIGHT ARKESTRA is a multidisciplinary ritual performance inspired by the ten teachings of Pu Xian Pusa (Samantabhadra). Each artist introduces their own sonic interpretation through their instrument and performance style, accompanied by evolving chants and layers of drone, rhythm, light, and visual transformation. The collective creates a temporary ceremonial world where the audience and performers become part of the same energetic field. PUNXIANS COSMIC LIGHT ARKESTRA explores the intersection between ancient rituals and futuristic expressions.

Participating Artists:
Matty (Spacebeest) – Guitar / cosmic effects / spatial textures
Tjebbe van der Kooij (Storm op de Maan) – Chants / electronic drones / atmospheres
Denz de Kroon – Self-made bass instruments
Jascha (Kavia) van der Hoorn – Trumpet / live effects / ritual melodies
Jimi (Elektrovolt) Hellinga – Modular drone textures
Remy Simon (Erres) – Drums / djembe / ceremonial rhythms
Bianca Runge and Franka de Jong – Make-up Deco

events.title: Sol Lysergic
events.description:
Jasper Veldkamp, aka Lysergic, creates interactive light and sound installations that use technology to pull people into the same space rather than push them apart. His practice spans projection mapping, building visual worlds, and designing reactive environments where the audience becomes the performer. With work shown from Berlin to Nairobi, his installations explore a single question: can technology bring people closer together instead of driving them apart? He is a member of kikvors, a collective of makers and performers across the Netherlands.
 Links:
 Website

events.title: Step out of your comfort zone!
events.description: "The Dragon's Egg," a performance by Luis Tromp, Rosa Schroevers, and Barbara Schroevers. Featuring music by Brainscapes (Alain Eskinasi). Makeup by Saskia Walter and Peter van Harmelen. Assistance provided by Jord Alkema.

events.title: The Mirror Field
events.description:
‘The Mirror Field’ (The Mirror Field) is an interactive installation where we can both generate and capture light. In a world that is often shrouded in darkness, ‘The Mirror Field’ asks you to take control of the light. The installation demonstrates how small actions have consequences that extend beyond our own perspective. Visitors can experience this individually or together, creating new connections, focusing light, or changing its direction.

Atelier Hyphen (-) develops spatial projects at the intersection of art, design, and community building, with a focus on participation and imagination.

events.title: The Rise, Life and Death of Don Quijote
events.description:
Thijs van de Loo – Theatermaker & Hessel Pijnaker, artist; combine their talents to bring five dolls to life, revolving around the theme of metamorphosis.

“Using rattan, fabric, inner tubes and found objects, and a good dose of intuition, we create soulful objects. Both have studied Vodun religions such as Ubuntu and draw inspiration from them.”

events.title: The Shamanic Experience
events.description:
Since 2000, Piet Jan Blauw has focused on music performances in Europe and Mexico – Sol festival (Spain), Paradiso Amsterdam, Psy Fi festival (NL), Cumbre Tajin (Mexico).

The music is a blend of Prehispanic-Electro, Down Tempo, and Trance.
Blauw's instruments produce archetypal sounds. His music can never be played exactly the same way and demands improvisation. He creates soundscapes as he plays with his hands in invisible beams. This results in a pantheon of sounds that are ministered by samplers, sequencers, and processors. He drives his own aural sculptures to their limits in every performance. Technology enables him to push the boundaries, yet he always uses it as a tool for creation.

Blauw's vision is not just the mere manipulation of technological gadgets. Their use is meant to ultimately evoke an emotional response from the audience and inspire them to discover lost domains within themselves. His objects are of sophisticated craftsmanship.

Piet Jan Blauw has highly sensitive antennae which serve him. His multi-faceted work is strongly devoted to communication in a rapidly changing society.…

events.title: The Wet and Naughty Show
events.description:
Amy Pratt (Kikvors Collectief)
 The Wet and Naughty Show, where 1900s burlesque meets amphibians and fish. What could burlesque have looked like if the performers had taken back their power after businessmen demanded the dancers show more skin? A theatrical performance inspired by amphibians and fish.
 As a costume designer, Amy Pratt creates work centered on colorful tactility and humor. Her practice explores ways to transform heavier themes into something humorous, while highlighting the bold and expressive handwriting of her costumes. Through performances and immersive experiences she invites the viewer to step into a new and vibrant reality.

events.title: Carmela Collective
events.description:
Carmela Collective is an emerging collaborative group of culture makers from Mexico, Chile, and Colombia who have migrated to The Netherlands, and whose purpose is to create meaningful encounters that celebrate and share the richness of Latin American diversity with local, migrant, and international communities in The Netherlands.

Tresdé
Tresdé is a visual artist, filmmaker, and selector. With her SadPink sessions, she creates a dreamy & melancholic sonic experience infused with her memories growing up in Cali, Colombia, to the sounds of salsa, cumbia, and chucucú. She taps into the nostalgia of yearning for echoes of the mountains, as a now migrant in the flatlands.

Ukab
Ukab is a curator and DJ part of Carmela Collective and other projects like Cumbia Noord Collective. He will bring his selections of Cumbia pirata, cumbia rebajadas, and Kumbia con K to this edition of Radio Chenin, going back-to-back with Paulah, a curator, DJ, selector, and owner of the project Nofutureberlin, a label that focuses on pushing the diaspora, especially the Latin American diaspora, into the scene.

events.title: Tritha
events.description: A Sonic Alchemist. A journey from tradition to transcendence. An Indian classical & contemporary singer, composer, DJ & Sound Yogini based out of India and Europe. Invited at Cannes festival in 2019 and Oxford this summer, she also opened for Anoushka Shankar in London in June 2025. A prolific multi-genre discography of 18 albums, touring over 24 countries in Europe, America, Africa and Asia, her first album "PaGLi" released on Sony Music India. She has also been featured on BBC, Arte and Canal+ as a music activist. Founded a socially active collective SPACE bringing awareness of women empowerment in India. Magnum Photos from France featured her as one of the 100 Women Changing India. Tritha has been twice invited to Oxford University to demonstrate how sound impacts human consciousness. Her practice bridges 30 years of Indian classical training with Naad Yoga (the Yoga of Sound). Through retreats and workshops, she facilitates a method using ancient Indian sound wisdom to promote mental, emotional, and physical healing for individual and collective consciousness.…

events.title: Johansson
events.description:
Johansson (real name Lars Johansson) has been active in the dance scene as a DJ, organizer, and producer since 1989. As the man behind well-known events such as Wooferland, Summer of Love, and a series of house classics parties, he is an established figure in the scene.

In addition to organizing events, music is generally the main driving force in his life. He began playing in a youth center at the end of the 1980s and quickly became infected with the dance virus in all its forms.

Expect an energetic mix of classics, house, techhouse, and techno during his set. With his upbeat DJ style, he knows how to keep the dance floor moving and ecstatic until the very last track.

events.title: Leon Row B2B Shimon
events.description:
What do you get when you put this Dutchman and this Frenchman behind the decks?
 A Hotel California. "You can check out any time you like, but you can never leave”.
 Yes, you read that right. If you’re into adventurous electronic music, this is your spot for Saturday night.
 Two friends, two selectors, brothers from another mother, reading each other, reading the room, and taking us somewhere nobody planned to go.
 Leon brings 14 years of underground intuition to the table. His sets are emotional, unpredictable and always rooted in the moment. Moving between eclectic grooves, rolling house, deep hypnotic minimal and unexpected turns you never saw coming, but that always feel exactly right.
 Born and raised in Paris, Shimon brings his own unique energy, shaped by his residency at Kater Blau in Berlin and a catalogue of over 30 releases on labels such as Kiosk ID and Kanto Record. Expect throbbing rhythms, hypnotic melodies and basslines that hit somewhere deep in your chest.
 This doesn’t feel like a b2b. It feels like two people who know each other deeply, connected by an effortless understanding.
 Welcome to Hotel California.…

events.title: Dolle Nina
events.description:
Nina Beem is an experience designer, poet, performer, host, and burlesque artist. At Ruigoord, she was featured in productions such as "The History of Burlesque," "Erotische Poetry Experience Vurige Tongen," and as a tour guide for "Field Trip Through the Cosmos." Not just a presenter on stage, but a creator of connections. Nina creates performative encounters where people feel seen, touched, and connected. During Landjuweel, she offers three special interactive experiences:

Grateful Lab: A playful and lighthearted experiment in gratitude. Through small encounters, questions, and rituals, visitors are invited to connect with the field of gratitude. Because where attention goes, energy grows. And perhaps a small quantum field of creation, abundance, and love opens up there.
 Fruity Refresh Station: A mobile oasis full of refreshing fruit, loving attention, and unexpected compliments. A short stop for body and soul, where visitors can recharge with fresh summer fruits and love before continuing on their way.
 Sun Queen: A ritual group performance in which spoken word, ceremony, and ritualistic burlesque come together…

events.title: Isis &  Mozes
events.description:
Isis & Mozes is a musical duo from Ruigoord, known for their performances at Landjuweel and Maanzin, among others. DJ Isis van der Wel and producer/multi-instrumentalist Mozes Meijer met each other exactly 10 years ago at Landjuweel, and they are celebrating this special occasion with you!

After the famous Beeldenroute, they will step behind the turntables together starting at midnight.…

events.title: DAF - Dutch Acid Family
events.description:
Thursday
 21:00-01:00
 Electro Magnes
 Electro Magnes is a Polish electronic duo formed by composer, producer, and multi-instrumentalist Rafał Kassaraba and vocalist-songwriter Bashka Preen. Blending live bass, electronic textures, and expressive vocals, they create atmospheric soundscapes that move between experimental electronica, ambient music, and intimate storytelling.
 Based in the foothills of the Karkonosze Mountains, the duo draws inspiration from nature, human connection, and inner transformation. Rafał's musical journey spans folk-blues, punk-rock, electro-punk, and film music projects, bringing a rich and distinctive depth to their sound.
 Bashka writes and performs in English, influenced by the years she spent living in London and its vibrant music scene. Her original songs have also been featured in film productions.
 Together, Electro Magnes invites audiences into a cinematic and immersive musical experience where electronic experimentation meets emotion, reflection, and heart.

Friday
 Sunshine Reggae
 15:00-16:30 Hobbol
 16:30-18:00 Open the gate sound
 18:00-19:30 Loddy Culture & Lyrical Benji
 19:30-21:00 Selector Depender
 21:00-22:30 Sun Collective…

events.title: DJ Captain Spank
events.description:
Happy/melodic/up-tempo dance world-music.
 (Ska + Balkan + ElectroSwing + Tribal + Oriental + Rockabilly + Punk + Flamenco +++…) I often have musicians playing live during my sets to add an extra beat/sound (sax/percu/etc...)

events.title: Wise Flower
events.description:
Amsterdam's Flower Power: Explosive groove and blossoming!

WiseFlower Band originated on the stages of Amsterdam. An international group with members from the Netherlands, Greece, Mexico, and Hungary, who play their own songs in a blend of blues and gospel, grooving into funk 'n' roll. The collected grooves travel through the vastness of space and time. It's time for Radical Change, so let's be Philosophical!

Left:
Website

events.title: Wakhan Project
events.description:
The music of Wā́khan Project is a modern ritual: a fiery fusion of ethno-tribal grooves, bouncing basslines, and a sassy energy that transforms any dancefloor into a mystical playground. His sets possess a rare power—the ability to awaken the wild, free, and joyful part that sleeps in everyone—with a smile, a twist, a spark in the air.
 Before igniting international stages, Wā́khan was already shaking the walls of Parisian squats and running club nights with his first techno collective, BPMOOD. It was there that his vision sharpened: to create music that is more organic, more tribal, more connected to something instinctive. 
 A sound that dances between raw groove, gentle spirituality, and playful mischief.
 A sound that has become his signature.

 In 2018, driven by a desire to cultivate a new scene in Paris, he founded the collective O’Tawa, now a reference point for unconventional nights and immersive experiences in the city. Under his guidance, O’Tawa has become a free, intense, and vibrant artistic space—a place where people come as much to dance as to experience a moment out of time.…

events.title: DJ Witte Aap
events.description: Not a DJ, but a music selector playing wonderful tunes from all over the world!

events.title: The Lyrebirds
events.description:
The Lyrebirds is a collective of young professional musicians who play Jazz Manouche.

Jazz Manouche, also known as Gypsy Jazz, is considered the oldest European jazz style. The music originated in the 1930s within the Romani and Sinti communities, where traditional melodies and emerging American swing jazz came together. The style was popularized by Django Reinhardt and is known as Hot Jazz: danceable, rhythmic, and full of improvisation.

The Lyrebirds play this style with their own interpretation and respect. The lineup consists of three gypsy guitars: Lars van Teeffelen, Berend Lantink, and SebaStrings, with Thimo Geijssen & Joris Das on double bass and Lucy Geelen on accordion and piano.

events.title: Julien Chaptal
events.description:
Julien Chaptal is an electronic assembly - part French electronics, part Detroit synth machine, and part Dutch minimal robot (as part of MFD, Amsterdam 661 and Le Clic).
 Purpose built with a modular brain, Julien's pertormances are switched on, live, utterly groovy, and while pulsating with machine wiring, undeniably human.
 With his modular synth and microscope, Julien explores an audiovisual cosmos of micro- organisms and sound waves. The micro organisms under Julien's magnifying glass are not just any bacilli. With every movement they make, they influence the sound of the music. So they are actually unsuspecting musicians - and Julien their conductor. What emerges is a festive fusion, a real Microbe Rave!

events.title: YEKUMI
events.description:
YEKUMI is an Electro-Ethnic duo that creates a complete live performance of electronic music production on the spot, masterfully woven together with live playing of multiple ethnic instruments – didgeridoo, oud, bouzouki, trumpet, flute, handpan, darbuka and ney. They strive to capture the essence of the moment and through melody and rhythm turn it into a dance journey between the sounds.

The live act is a ceremony, in which the creators take the listener on a journey with them, starting at a point where, amidst all the powerful forces of nature, there is something very small and intimate within each of us: a sound, a frequency, in which we move, pure energy that we recognize within ourselves. And with it, embark on a journey of connection to ourselves, into that basic power we have within and with us, and let it lead us into movement – a dance or a smile – towards the realms of mountains, streams, winds, rains, fire, solar & lunar energy, and while letting go, allow the pure energy within to freely guide us.

YEKUMI's heart beat is a deep friendship between two artists, Idan Shahar and Alon Sagi…

events.title: Battle for the Sacred Bean by: Bente Kruger & Adhem Kouta
events.description: There's a power vacuüm in the cacao kingdom. Two cacao warriors have taken arms against each other in a fight to the sweet chocolate death. Who will be crowned the new cacao king or queen?

events.title: Prince Pasensi
events.description:
Celebrated as one of the innovators of a new sound emerging from Amsterdam, Prince Pasensi’s infectious rhythms are moving dancefloors worldwide.
 Known for his unique sound that seamlessly blends traditional Surinamese music with breakbeats, bass/club, house, and techno, Prince Pasensi is carving out a distinctive place in the global electronic music scene.
 Performing at festivals like Down the Rabbit Hole and clubs such as Radio Radio and Lofi, Prince Pasensi’s sets have been described as a journey through a world of rhythms where he aims to showcase the unique creolized musical heritage of the African diaspora through music and dance.
 While producing from his home studio, his electrifying blends have resonated with partygoers worldwide and have been released on prestigious labels such as Berlin based NoFuture and GLXY Records.

events.title: Live Music Parlour with Lowfire & Liza Weald
events.description:
After the sculpture route, take a journey with Liza and Low Fire. They will guide you on an intuitive, live ambient experience. With hushed vocals, subtle soundscapes, and music that emerges in the moment, Liza & Pip Robin (Low Fire) create a soft, connected atmosphere in the Temple of Self. Come and let them carry you away.

LOWFIRE
Music that moves like wind over dunes; scratching guitars and ethereal voices that turn horizons into dreamscapes.
Located in Amsterdam, they get their sound from the clash between city life and cold seas, dunes, and misty meadows. They live like hybrids, looking into another dimension. Lowfire, right on the intersection; the end of a big flame and the start of a new one. You feel the smoothing heat, playing through their chords and melodies.
Location: Amsterdam based
Band setup: Solo, duo, trio to full band
Live setup: Guitars, drum, bass, vocals
Genre: Ambient folk
Style: Slow-burn, cinematic, immersive


Liza Weald (Lisa de Bruijn) is a musical alchemist who combines sounds from folk, pop & new age into captivating songs. Her angelic voice has a huge range and, in combination with the hopeful & catchy lyrics, she creates pure magic…

events.title: Bradu
events.description: Bradu is a music project led by Sem van der Peet. What started as a jamsession evolved in a project where a group of friends come together in different formations to explore genres like jazz, hiphop, trap, fusion and house. Come and see this and put on your dancing shoes!!

events.title: Bring the Joints
events.description: Bring The Joints is a hip-hop band that combines rap and live instrumentation to create its own unique sound. With the powerful flows of rapper/guitarist Ahih Jaihi, deep bass lines from Fretless Jane, energetic drums, the colorful keys of Reezus, and warm sounds from saxophonist Robert, the band creates dynamic live experiences full of character and musical freedom.

events.title: Jam session with Jarne X Ris
events.description: Jamsession every night after the program on the Rebel Stage until festival's closing time. The jamsessions here are legendary. We have a group of very good musicians carrying the jam. Tell musicians to come and join us. Please don't just jump on stage first contact the session leader.

events.title: Dj Fred Legal Alien
events.description:
Part of the DJ collective Legal Aliens.
 Time for a party!

events.title: Bliz Nochi & Marisa Lia
events.description:
Bliz Nochi & Marisa Lia are a dynamic DJ duo based in Hamburg/Germany who blend genres with their unique sound, which combines organic house, acid house, and indie dance, drawing strong influences from traditional and ethnic music from around the world. Their high-energy sets, filled with off-beat rhythms and intense grooves, create dancing and intellectual stimulation.

In addition to their joint performances, both Bliz Nochi and Marisa Lia perform solo DJ shows, but when they collaborate as Bliz Nochi & Marisa Lia B2B, it showcases their musical synergy and the limitless possibilities of their collaboration.

With appearances at notable festivals like Fusion Festival (DE), Amsterdam Dance Event (NL), and MS Dockville (DE), and at renowned venues including Golden Pudel Club (DE), Scorpios Mykonos, and Habitas and Nômade Tulum, Bliz Nochi & Marisa Lia have established a reputation for creating diverse, euphoric experiences that challenge and stimulate listeners, igniting the dance floor with every beat.

events.title: Temple Night with Ambient Music & Intential Tea Ceremony (Ocean, Yente & Emmy-K)
events.description:
After the sculpture route, Temple of Self transitions into a peaceful temple night. Ocean and Yente guide an intentional tea ceremony to gradually approach sunrise.

Sensual, positive, consent-first, with space for tea, cushions, cuddling, cacao, and genuine connection. The temple is Saturday night the place to reconnect with yourself and others if you can no longer see the boogie forest through the willows. The Temple of Self DJs play a set to help you unwind.

events.title: Night Drumming
events.description:
Feel free to bring your rhythm to life with Nightdrummers. Bring your shaker, tambourine, djembe, or other instrument and join us in the groove.

We create a welcoming Drum Circle where improvisation, connection, and playfulness are central. Listen, respond, experiment, and be amazed by what emerges.
Whether you've been playing for years or are simply curious: there's space for everyone. Bring your friends along, come groove, dance, and let the night resonate with our shared heartbeat.
Everyone is welcome. Together, we create the rhythm.

events.title: Dj Chaakra Legal Alien
events.description: Jacques Povee is a music producer, promoter, and DJ known as DJ Chaakra. More than 25 years ago, while working as an environmental activist in Groenoord, he moved to Ruigoord to protect the village from the construction of the port. Together with more than 100 activists, he successfully halted the port's development in collaboration with various residents of Ruigoord. He is also known as the founder of Trance Orient Express and Solstice Festival.

events.title: Edgar Ramiro
events.description:
Born and raised in Amsterdam and coming from an mixed-race family, there where always various musical influences present growing up.
 But it was around the Age of 17, when Edgar Ramiro started collecting Vinyl, Cassette tapes, CDs etc.
 After an holiday with family in Curaçao, he inherited a collection from his aunt with records and books mainly focused around the black diaspora. While cleaning up and listening to all these records, he discovered many hidden gems and got inspired by the wide variety of music within this collection..
 From this moment the urge to discover and learn more about the history of this music became bigger, and eventually lead to sharing this music with others. He started playing records at the legendary Cafe Belgique, Pacific Parc and Red light Radio and has gradually became a well known figure in Amsterdam’s night life.
 Next to his monthly residency at Cornerstore, you can find him playing at clubs and festivals such as Dekmantel Selectors, DTRH, Lentekabinet, Wildeburg, Paradiso, Garage Noord, Brilliant Corners, Unit 58, NAR to name a few.. Curating became the next step and this is when his own club night “Melting Pot” was born.…

events.title: Inphiknight
events.description: Inphiknight will take you into the depth of the night with a fine selection of mesmerizing melodies, titillating rhythms and scintillating harmonies that will weave together the pulses of the heart into a mesh of brainwaves that will dance together with the roots and the branches of the willows.

events.title: Rowel Hazelini
events.description: As dawn breaks, we welcome Rowel Hazelini. With the soul of an Italian storyteller and the voice of trombone and piano, he weaves passion, beauty, and longing into every note. Let the rising sun and his music meet as one.

events.title: Kids' Camping
events.description:
Friday 09.00-13.00 Opening Ceremony & Crafting
 Creating mandalas and altars using natural materials
 Making insect masks
 Saturday 09.00-13.00 Opening Ceremony & Movement
 Building insect houses from natural materials
 Wayang Kulit workshop (shadow puppets)
 Sunday 09.00-13.00 Opening Ceremony with soft music
 Storytelling
 Making instruments
 Ongoing program (all days): Creating costumes from cardboard and paper for the parade

 Facilitators:
 Luis Croes
 Guides children's camps and provides creative activities, handpan and creates a pleasant atmosphere.
 Iris van Wijhe
 A versatile and musically-inclined individual with a great passion for mindfulness.
 Iris enjoys leading workshops where children can playfully discover, create, move, and relax.
 "I prefer to create spaces where imagination is encouraged, where messy hands are seen as a good thing, and where children can playfully explore and be themselves."
 We invite the children to experiment, collaborate, and simply be themselves. Because it is when there is space for freedom and wonder that the most beautiful creations arise...

events.title: Morning Yoga · The Five Elements Form
events.description:
Want do you want smooth hips, flowing energy and a smile on your face?

During this standing yoga flow, Esmee Jiskoot will guide you through the 5 Elements Form: a series of twenty fluid yoga movements that may remind you of Qigong. The movements help to release tension, deepen your breathing and bring body and mind back into balance.
The flow moves like a moving meditation: soft, powerful and surprisingly energetic all at once. Your body comes alive, your mind finds peace and you feel more present in the moment.
Whether you are an experienced yogi or taking your first yoga class, this practice invites you to slow down, feel and reconnect with yourself. A wonderful reset during the festival, that will leave you grounded, energized and smiling.
Get to know Esmee Jiskoot
Esmee Jiskoot is a yoga teacher and body-oriented coach. With warmth, enthusiasm and years of experience, she guides people towards more relaxation, body awareness and life energy.
In her classes, movement, breathing and personal development come together naturally…

events.title: Le Miracle du Vivant by: Marchal
events.description: The human experience is full of uncertainties, great mysteries, and unanswered questions that may never be resolved. Even if we encounter each other, it's likely that won't change anything, but perhaps a new perspective can be found.

events.title: DAF - Dutch Acid Family
events.description:
Thursday
 21:00-01:00
 Electro Magnes
 Electro Magnes is a Polish electronic duo formed by composer, producer, and multi-instrumentalist Rafał Kassaraba and vocalist-songwriter Bashka Preen. Blending live bass, electronic textures, and expressive vocals, they create atmospheric soundscapes that move between experimental electronica, ambient music, and intimate storytelling.
 Based in the foothills of the Karkonosze Mountains, the duo draws inspiration from nature, human connection, and inner transformation. Rafał's musical journey spans folk-blues, punk-rock, electro-punk, and film music projects, bringing a rich and distinctive depth to their sound.
 Bashka writes and performs in English, influenced by the years she spent living in London and its vibrant music scene. Her original songs have also been featured in film productions.
 Together, Electro Magnes invites audiences into a cinematic and immersive musical experience where electronic experimentation meets emotion, reflection, and heart.

Friday
 Sunshine Reggae
 15:00-16:30 Hobbol
 16:30-18:00 Open the gate sound
 18:00-19:30 Loddy Culture & Lyrical Benji
 19:30-21:00 Selector Depender
 21:00-22:30 Sun Collective…

events.title: Bakfietsband
events.description:
The cargo bike band embodies joyful activism at its finest. This mobile stage brings a smile to your face and transforms the street into a dance floor. Come join us for some fun or participate with this cheerful group.

The cargo bike band plays 3 times a day, in the afternoon, evening, and night. There is also a daily guest performance, with a group from South America, AP Mayor, performing on Saturday.

events.title: Earth Garden
events.description:
Amidst the performances, music and encounters, the Earth Garden offers a place to listen, connect to the earth, create and simply be.
 The Earth Garden Crew invites you to become more than a visitor. Spend time with the earth, create a gift from and/or for the earth with your hands, enjoy a nourishing açaí bowl and a cup of homemade blue Clitoria tea, and be carried by live music throughout the morning,...
 Thursday | 22:00 – 00:00
 *The Absinth Bar*
 Tableau Vivant & Live Performance
 Step into a surreal world where myth, ritual and imagination meet. Join us for an intimate late-night experience in the Earth Garden.
 Friday, Saturday & Sunday | 12:00 – 15:00
 Throughout the morning, accompanied by live music, you are invited to join:
 * *The Collective Clayground* – An artwork, shaped by everyone who joins. No ownership, only one piece growing in the hands of many. Connect with the earth, with each other and with your self..
 * *Gift Shop – *What Is Your Gift?** – This is not a place to buy clothes, but to exchange gifts. Find a unique festival piece and offer something in return—not money, but a talent, a kind gesture, your time or a skill.…

events.title: Poppenkastvoorstelling
events.description: During the "Landjuweel" (a Dutch festival), puppet shows will be performed at Ruigoord 16. The start times will be announced on a sign outside. The afternoon performances are suitable for all ages.

events.title: Crescone
events.description: Crescone creates electronic music that explores the connection between humans and nature. His sound blends world music, downtempo, dub, ambient, house, and techno. By using field recordings, instruments, and synthesizers, he creates soundscapes that feel both earthy and dreamy.

events.title: Filosofisch kabinet
events.description:
Theme of the day:

Out of nowhere, The Wandering Company appears: three mysterious beings, a creaking caravan, and traces of a life on the road. Insects? Travelers? No one knows for sure, perhaps even themselves. One question has driven them for as long as they've been wandering: where is home? Today, they invite you to join their search. The day consists of 3 parts. First, the company appears, right in the middle of an interactive theater moment. Then, their story, "Onward to Home," comes to life through a narrative that unfolds before your eyes. And at the end of the day, everyone departs together in a festive children's parade, the ritual that transforms searching into finding home.

The Wandering Company appears:
With a creaking, rattling sound, the caravan rolls onto the square. Curious eyes look around: where have they ended up? The Wandering Company is on its way, as always, looking for an answer it hasn't yet found. This place is just one stop along the way; nothing more, nothing less. But exploring an unknown world isn't something you do alone; they need your help…

events.title: The soft landing
events.description: We start the day with a joint opening ceremony on the village square. A moment to come together, meet each other, and settle into the day calmly. With music, movement, and creativity, we discover what the daily theme has to tell us. What adventures, questions, and surprises await us today?

events.title: The soft landing
events.description: We start the day with a joint opening ceremony on the village square. A moment to come together, meet each other, and settle into the day calmly. With music, movement, and creativity, we discover what the daily theme has to tell us. What adventures, questions, and surprises await us today?

events.title: Woven Voices
events.description:
Woven Voices originated from love. Love for bringing people together and giving everyone the space to express themselves. What remains is a beautiful work of art.

Weaving Our Shared Visions Together
"Woven Voices," the proud creation of Lex Empress, begins on the first day of Landjuweel. Each day, participants are invited to write intentions, dreams, and visions for our future, for the children, and for the world. These words are painted onto cotton and woven together into one collective carpet. This is a living artwork created from many voices becoming one. Anyone can join and become part of this shared creation. By the end of the festival, the carpet stands as a physical memory of our united prayers and possibilities.

events.title: Awakening ceremony with Sophia Maria Kienhuis and Gilian Baracs
events.description:
On Vurige Tongen invites Sophia Maria Kienhuis and Gilian Baracs to their awakening ceremony, where you can unleash your inner firebird.

With live piano, beats, and original songs, they invite you to feel, move, and follow what wants to emerge. There is no fixed form, but a journey in which you seek the answer to the question of what it means for you to awaken.
Between stillness and radical expression, a shared experience unfolds, where the boundary between performers and audience fades away.

events.title: Sharing Voice / Maxi McNaaughty
events.description: Maxi McNaughty shares her thoughts and experiences in mostly rhyming form she sings and encourages you to share your voice

events.title: The Art Jewel on Sunday
events.description:
Curators / host Q&A: Bo van der Meer & Roswitha de Boer - Zuiderveen

New at the Landjuweel Festival! A fine selection of Dutch documentaries that capture the essence of artistry, community, identity, and freedom. Step into the church, put on your headphones, open your eyes, and immerse yourself in the stories that breathe the free spirit of Ruigoord. Including Q&A with the makers: from up-and-coming talent to established names. Sunday, July 26 | 12:30 - 14:00

Sneak Preview: Paradise Birds (17 min)
A street artist, a dog walker, and a sea captain fight for their survival and freedom in a city that is rapidly gentrifying and losing its edges. For Landjuweel, we're taking you into this world with a sneak preview.
Q&A with directors Debra Barraud & Luca Lange

Outside the Lines (5 min)
Short animated documentary in which three young queer people share their experiences of deviating from cis-heteronorm.
Afterword by director Stan Oversteegen

Sex Sirens (26 min)
The ballroom scene is a safe haven to find and express identity, sensuality, and sexuality.…

events.title: Origin Circle
events.description: We gather every morning to remember our shared origins on this Earth, connecting with the medicine of the Heart of the Earth (Kakaw) and opening the day for sharing our intentions, songs and prayers.

events.title: Costumes for the parade
events.description: Who do you want to be? A crawler, a jumper, a flying creature, or perhaps something that doesn't yet exist? Using fabrics, natural materials, and a healthy dose of imagination, we create costumes for the children's parade.
events.venue: Village Square · A World in the Making

events.title: Bouke & Co · Free Clothes — Take Some, Bring Some
events.description:
Can you do better? Or are you just suffering from shopping urges? Come and enjoy FREE CLOTHING at Bouke & Co.

Cool clothes and accessories for free! New items every day! Unwanted styling advice included.
Feel free to bring your unwanted wardrobe too!
Everything is ️FREE️. Daily from 13:00-21:00 at Area 46.

events.title: Bon Bon Vivant chocolate tasting
events.description: Come and taste our vegan Guayusa Chocolate Bar. It gives you energy in a kind, smooth way. No jitters, no crash, just clear focus and happy vibes. Perfect to start your Landjuweel day.

events.title: Garden of Devotion
events.description:
During Landjuweel, Garden of Devotion opens its doors as an intimate barefoot garden where nature, art, music, taste, scent and stillness come together: a haven of peace, beauty and love, a place for inspiration, reflection, connection and surrender.

Take off your shoes, enter softly and let yourself be carried away by live music, soundscapes, performance and rituals.
 Shoes off. Senses on.
 Garden Ruigoord 1 open on Saturdays and Sundays between 2:00 PM and 6:00 PM.

events.title: Poi workshop by Bartoz (Bzarr)
events.description: Step into the magical world of poi, where two simple cords transform into mesmerizing circles of rhythm, movement, and flow. Join Bartoz for a playful beginner-friendly workshop and discover that anyone can learn this beautiful flow art: no experience needed, just curiosity. Come as you are, lose yourself in the dance of the circles and leave with a little more flow than you arrived with!

events.title: Stray Elf
events.description: Stray Elf weaves intricate tracks to create an elevated mood on the dancefloor. Taking you to all sorts of magical places with trippy beats and psychedelic atmospheres!

events.title: Healing Massage Workshop
events.description:
Sanne Croese is a facilitator, trainer, and founder of Body & Soul by Sanne. She works with individuals, couples, and groups in the fields of embodiment, intimacy, healing, and personal growth. Her work combines breathwork, bodywork, elements of tantra, conscious kink, Erotic Blueprints, guided trance journeys, energy work, and bodywork. She facilitates workshops for couples, Healing Breathwork circles, bodyworker immersion days and trainings, and transformational events such as the Goddess Play event.

On Sunday, she will lead the Healing Massage Workshop, an experiential journey into the art of conscious and healing touch. This workshop is based on the belief that everyone has the ability to bring healing through their hands. Through intention, presence, awareness, energy, and touch, participants will explore how a simple massage can become a deeply nourishing and transformative experience. You will learn practical techniques while discovering the importance of attunement, listening, and working with energy. Open to all levels, this workshop invites you to reconnect with the healing power that already lives within you. You can come alone or with a partner.

events.title: Rasta Robert & Friends
events.description: Dreamtime Healing sound massage concerts, mantra singing, theremin, spontaneous musical improvisation, spiritual chill-out space. Every day 2:00 PM - 10:00 PM, concert sessions 2:00 PM - 3:30 PM and 8:00 PM - 9:30 PM (check-in 30 minutes prior).

events.title: 4-Hands Massage Arnaud Mooij & Marcel Maurik
events.description:
Four hands; one experience
A unique collaboration between Marcel and Arnaud
Free, spacious, relaxed, and completely surrendered. That's how you'll feel after a massage from this professional massage duo. Working as one body, they bring your body to a state of complete relaxation, ensuring that your body feels free and open, and it seems like your heart is even fuller and more open, ready to experience the freedom at Ruigoord even more.
When two talents combine their experience and passion, something special emerges. That's what Marcel and Arnaud offer you: a fusion of our strengths as bodyworkers. From our own approach and background, we complement each other in an intuitive and healing collaboration.
You choose what you need. Do you want to feel deeply relaxed, let your energy flow, release blockages, or simply return to yourself? We offer various disciplines that you can freely combine: classic massage, energetic massage, TRAGER® Approach, intuitive bodywork, and Rebalancing techniques. And our hands know exactly where they need to be on your body to give you an amazing experience…

events.title: Baba Exotica
events.description:
Baba Exotica celebrates the freedom in music, collecting and sharing the weird and wonderful worldwide. His 100% vinyl selections are an unique mix of styles coming from near and far. Get exotic with Highlife & Afro Sounds from Ghana, Gipsy music from Serbia, Psychedelic synth work from Cabo Verde, Peruvian masterpieces or Oriental Farfisa Madness & Rocking Bouzoukis from Greece.
 Tune in, get on board, be free and embrace the worldwide weird and wonderful. Love!
 Links:
 Youtube

events.title: Breathwork ~ Strengthening the Subtle Body
events.description:
Samir Kanji facilitates trauma-focused breathing exercises.

His work is based on the understanding that difficult life experiences can continue to shape how we relate to ourselves, others, and the world long after the events themselves have passed. These adaptations may manifest as recurring patterns, chronic stress, emotional overwhelm, disconnection, or a sense of being stuck.
Through breathing exercises, Samir supports people in working with and integrating these experiences while also strengthening internal resources such as resilience, self-trust, presence, courage, connection, playfulness, and joy. Rather than focusing only on what is difficult, his approach also cultivates the capacities that help people meet life more fully.
Born in Pakistan to an African family with Indian ancestry, raised Canadian, and now living in the Netherlands, Samir brings a cross-cultural perspective to his work. His practice is informed by a two-year, 1,000-hour training in trauma-focused breathing exercises and grounded in an anti-oppressive framework that resists colonialism, patriarchy, capitalism, and white supremacy.

events.title: Conscious Flirting & Heart-Led Desire
events.description:
Sandi Djoulfian is a facilitator, feminine embodied coach, and founder of Medicine Within. Her work weaves together embodied tantra, temple arts, feminine archetypal & elemental wisdom, shamanism, nervous system awareness, and transformational group experiences that invite people into deeper intimacy with themselves, others, and Spirit.
 Drawing from years of experience facilitating women’s circles, temple nights, retreats, kambô ceremonies, and embodiment workshops, Sandi creates spaces where authenticity, connection, and personal transformation can unfold naturally. Her approach blends ancient wisdom with grounded embodiment, supporting people to reconnect with their vitality, truth, and innate radiance.
 Based in Amsterdam and teaching internationally, she is passionate about cultivating community, conscious relationships, feminine leadership, and embodied living.

events.title: Light concert with Liza Weald & Pip Robin
events.description: An intimate acoustic concert featuring the clear vocals of Liza Weald, inviting you to slow down and find stillness. Expect warm, heartfelt songs that resonate without many words. At the end of the concert, Liza and Pip Robin (Low Fire) will perform a few collaborative pieces together.

events.title: "Fire-making workshop by Robert Bleij"
events.description:
Robert Bleij, buscraft, actor and storyteller,
 Fire-making workshop
 Have you always wanted to learn how to make fire yourself using simple, natural materials?
 During this two-hour program, you will learn the basics of making primitive fire. No long theory, but mainly hands-on: trying, practicing, and discovering what is needed to turn a spark into a real flame.
 Making fire is an ancient skill that lies dormant in every human being. With the right technique, good materials, and a little patience, anyone can learn it. Experience is not required; this program is suitable for beginners.
 In a relaxed outdoor setting, you will discover:
 different old techniques for making fire;
 which natural tinder works well and where to find it;
 how to prepare the tinder yourself;
 how to turn the tinder into a flame;
 how to build a fire calmly and successfully.

 After two hours, you will leave with more knowledge, experience, and confidence to be able to make fire safely yourself.
 Links:
 Instagram

events.title: Workshop Lino Stempelen
events.description:
KIMTAM TATTOO is a tattoo artist with Javanese-Surinamese roots, based in Rotterdam. In her work, she combines craftsmanship, culture, and storytelling by creating unique designs that tell a personal story.

During this workshop, children will learn about the ancient art of printing. The artwork you see consists of lino stamps that have been hand-carved. Stamping techniques have been used around the world for centuries to print patterns and images on paper and fabric. In parts of Southeast Asia, including Malaysia, similar techniques were even used to create tattoos, where ornaments and figures were carved from wood.

This rich tradition inspired KIMTAM to design her own stamps and use them as a basis for her tattoo work.
For children aged 5-9 years:
Children playfully discover the world of stamping. They choose from various handmade lino stamps, make their own prints on paper, and then receive their favorite design as a temporary stencil on their skin.
For children aged 10+ years:
Older children get to work independently as creators…

events.title: Making props for the parade
events.description: We create instruments together using natural and recycled materials. Shake, drum, rattle, or chime – give your instrument its own unique sound and character. Together, we'll build a cheerful parade of sounds. Bring your instrument along and join us in the children's parade at 4:30 PM!

events.title: The Troubadour Car Jam Session
events.description:
Ian Merkoulov travels throughout all of the Netherlands in his Troubadour Car. From street corners to village squares, halls to living rooms, boats to forests. A true Troubadour whose hall is the world, with poetic language and a colorful stage as its backdrop.

The Troubadour Car Jam Session
The Troubadour Car jam session for all ages and skill levels. From harmony to cacophony.

events.title: Spoken word performance with Smita James and others.
events.description:
Smita James (Nijkerk, 1983) is a Surinamese-Dutch writer, poet, and performance artist. Since 2002, she has performed her texts on renowned literary stages such as Re:definition, Cinnamon Wednesdays, Woorden worden Zinnen, SLAA Perdu, Read My World, and Poetry International. This places her among the leading figures in the spoken word scene, which was once underground but is now thriving in the Netherlands. In 2019, she contributed to the book "Hardop" (Atlas, 2019), a major work in the spoken word genre.

In addition, James has contributed to various other publications: Stichting Kaihan (Ministry of Social Affairs, 2011), DICHTER (Plint, 2018 and 2019), FREE HERI HERI (Kip Republic, 2019), Atlas van Herdenken (Wij zijn Amsterdam, 2023), Kala Pani (World Museum, 2024), Het grote Caribische Verhalen Boek (Querido, 2025).

Her work focuses on themes such as identity, metaphysics, and the Afro- and Indo-Caribbean diaspora within our social sphere. Although her writing begins on paper, her texts often extend beyond that. She enjoys working interdisciplinarily and therefore also likes to collaborate with other artists…

events.title: Making slime
events.description: Step into a magical lab where slippery dreams come true! During this fun workshop, which is easy to enter and exit, children (and adults who still want to be kids) will dive into the magical world of slime. Come play, get messy, and enjoy your own homemade slime creation!

events.title: Morning Yoga · The Five Elements Form
events.description:
Want do you want smooth hips, flowing energy and a smile on your face?

During this standing yoga flow, Esmee Jiskoot will guide you through the 5 Elements Form: a series of twenty fluid yoga movements that may remind you of Qigong. The movements help to release tension, deepen your breathing and bring body and mind back into balance.
The flow moves like a moving meditation: soft, powerful and surprisingly energetic all at once. Your body comes alive, your mind finds peace and you feel more present in the moment.
Whether you are an experienced yogi or taking your first yoga class, this practice invites you to slow down, feel and reconnect with yourself. A wonderful reset during the festival, that will leave you grounded, energized and smiling.
Get to know Esmee Jiskoot
Esmee Jiskoot is a yoga teacher and body-oriented coach. With warmth, enthusiasm and years of experience, she guides people towards more relaxation, body awareness and life energy.
In her classes, movement, breathing and personal development come together naturally…

events.title: Cedric Vermue
events.description: Cedric Vermue creates neo-classical and ambient music, where piano, strings, and electronic elements blend together to create a mesmerizing soundscape. Together with cellist Tjalle Rens, they perform a live ambient set at Landjuweel in Theo's tea garden, featuring piano and cello mixed with electronics and synthesizers.

events.title: Dougal Hallowes - Sitar Journeys
events.description:
The Story So Far…
 Dougal started his adventures in music as a bass player in rock bands on the Sydney scene in the early 90’s. After a move to London in ‘96 and a stint with Australian psychedelic band Om, found himself playing with Tribal Drift and spent the remainder of the decade touring around the globe.
 Tribal Drift featured Cyrung who was one of the pioneers of didgeridoo in Goa during the 80’s and was attributed to bringing the instrument into the world of trance in the early 90’s.
 In 2003 Dougal was a founding member of Australian band Loonaloop and featured as writer/performer/producer on their first 3 albums alongside many live shows.
 The journey with the sitar began with a gift from India. The sitar stayed in its box until Dougal found sitar guru Darshan Kumari in Amsterdam around 2012 and began to study.
 He has since opened the show for Dutch band My Baby with both sitar and didge on their Shamanaid tour in 2016/7 and slowly found the confidence to expand a 10 min set to a full show.
 More recently Dougal has played in 2022 and ‘23 on Ozora festival with 2 x 1.5 hour journeys from ambient to dance-floor and will again play 2 sets there in ‘24.…

events.title: For Sun
events.description:
A duo by Purple Flame & Ninapicks that blends everything heard and unheard. 
 ForSun is a tender act of resistance and healing, created with love. 
 For sun 
 For love 
 For peace 
 For music

events.title: Pit Hermans
events.description: Cimbalompit is a multi instrumentalist. Her main instruments are cimbalom, bass guitar, violin and cajon. In 1997 she discovered the cimbalom, a beautiful multistringed instrument from the Balkan. She performs solo (world music and impro) and in various formations: Duo Kefi (balkan/Greek/Irish/Breton), Duo Dragoste (Romanian and more), voice trio Jubel & Traan (Dutch&world) and Barrio del Mundo (rumba/flamenco/Cuban). Also she’s organising concert&jam events in Amsterdam west.

events.title: Poetry Consults
events.description:
Feeling lovesick? Despondent? Forlorn? 
 Dr. Oxytocin's poetry consults are open for business! Come get the lock on your heart fixed by inhaling their intimate poetry and 'a completely safe dose' of Oxytocin. Come check out their tent, hiding in the KALEIDO Faerie Forest!

events.title: Embrace your No-Talent: Talkshow Time
events.description: On Sunday afternoon, chaos erupts at the Queer Stage. Matthijs van Ruigkerk invites the audience to showcase their (lack of) talent and create the highly popular "The Queer World Keeps Turning" show together. Would you like to be his guest on stage? Are you willing to participate in an in-depth interview? Or will you be the musical guest? Matthijs is known for setting a high standard, so will you jump or will you limbo?

events.title: We're In It Together! · Verwonderij Foundation
Kirtan & Mantra Singing Circle
events.description: Kirtan Crew loves sharing mantras and kirtan with everyone. They have rich musical backgrounds: Bastiaan plays guitar and sings; he studied Composition & Sound Design and played worldwide as DJ/producer. Kim plays harmonium and sings; she is part of a choir and also plays ukelele and various shamanic instruments. Aravinda is a rhythm master and plays dholak and other Indian percussion in devotional ensembles. Experience an uplifting kirtan journey: soulful mantra singing, deep driving harmonium and funky Indian percussion. They weave ecstatic chants, joyful melodies and relaxing stillness into a powerful musical journey. Join their gathering to sing, dance, laugh, enjoy and open your heart.

events.title: DJ Witte Aap
events.description: Not a DJ, but a music selector playing wonderful tunes from all over the world!

events.title: Photo Booth
events.description: Capture your favorite moment and step into our photo booth. Create a beautiful memory of the festival by taking your own picture.

events.title: SAVE THE GREEN FIELD — SIGN NOW!
events.description: The Green Field is a rare green space in the city where there is still room for wild nature, culture, and experimentation. It is run on a voluntary basis by residents and small, local businesses. But now, our free space is at risk of disappearing. Therefore, we need to act NOW, it's not too late. You can help us by signing the petition! More information at our stand.

events.title: The Floating Poet
events.description: Become a floating poet and come up to our raft to write a poem. Ask the internship manager where the secret departure point is. You can recite your creation in the garden.

events.title: The Game Cabinet
events.description:
Open a drawer, slide a box towards you, and let the fun begin. In the Games Corner, you'll find a diverse mix of card and board games waiting for new players. Challenge someone to a game of 30 seconds, go for pure chaos with a party game, or rediscover a classic you had long forgotten.

Whether you come to win, hang out, laugh, or simply chat: there's always something fun happening around the table here. Pull up a chair and join in the fun.

events.title: Total Artwork · Open Workshop with Hessel Pijnaker
events.description: In a group setting, we intuitively create a work of art on one giant sheet of paper together. Come and relax! Hessel will guide you through the process, allowing you to freely express yourself with various exercises based on artistic techniques from Zen Buddhism.

events.title: XXL spellen: Schaken, Jenga, Twister
events.description: Huge, bigger, wildest: With our XXL games, it's all about having fun to the fullest. Challenge your strategic mind on a chessboard as big as your ego, build towers that reach higher than your weekend plans with XXL Jenga filled with surprising questions and tasks, or get tangled up during a game of Twister that is guaranteed to lead to laughter fits.

events.title: Workshop Playfight
events.description: Elena Zanchetta is a certified Tangi Playfight Facilitator, massage therapist, and NVC mediator based in Amsterdam. Rooted in contemporary dance, partner movement, and Non-violent Communication, she brings together strength, softness, and play to create spaces where people can connect beyond words. Together with Joris, she runs Playfight Amsterdam — a growing community of weekly circles and workshops for anyone curious about embodied, playful connection.

events.title: Hatha Yoga
events.description:
gage and relax. Breathe, connect with your body and soul. Feel what is, surrender to being.

Wonderful, relaxing, gentle yoga to recharge your energy 😇

events.title: Interactive Theatre: With the characters of the parade!
Saskia Schmink
events.description: Here, imaginations come to life. The makeup team transforms children into whatever they can dream of – from magical creatures to brave adventurers.

events.title: Woven Voices: What's alive in you?
WTF Stage
events.description: Lowest stage of the western hemisphere. Share whatever the F you want. Come and join us in/on the theatre of the crazy & openhearted. Only fans surrounding you. PA, piano & mics @ your service. So bring your own instrument if you like! Be on time otherwise you miss the grounding and opening check in..

events.title: Reve & Jessy
events.description:
Jessy (MC)
 Jessy is a meditation guide, coach, and explorer of consciousness who is passionate about creating spaces for presence, connection, and self-discovery. Through guided meditations and inner exploration, she invites people to slow down, reconnect with themselves, and remember what truly matters.
 Inspired by courage, awareness, and the wisdom that already lives within each of us, her sessions offer a gentle invitation to come home to yourself, exactly as you are.

Reve (DJ)
 Revé is a dancer, choreographer, and DJ. Drawing on her Surinamese roots and 20 years of world touring dance experience, she has developed a methodology called Energy Systema, which creates ceremonial music and movement waves to ground, uplift, and transform energy levels. This methodology is accessible through classes, dance parties, and her DJ sets. Her passion is to empower people to embrace their natural, primary energy and expression.

events.title: Velvet Tongues
events.description: Velvet Tongues is the meeting place of poetry and pulse. Andrej and Isis weave electronic and classical music with spoken word, opera and immersive ritual, creating performances that blur the line between concert, ceremony and dream. Drawing from mythology, alchemy, philosophy and the strange theatre of modern life, they create immersive experiences that seduce the senses before quietly unsettling the soul.

events.title: Mehmet Polat
events.description:
Mehmet Polat is a master ud player, composer, ensemble leader and soloist who tours internationally. From Africa to India, Persia to the Balkans, Contemporary to Jazz, he has been combining various musical genres with his Alevi Spiritual, Anatolian Folk and Ottoman classical music background. Through his improvisations, compositions and his own take on a variety of musical styles, Mehmet is constantly searching for new musical paths and inspiration. After many years of research, he invented his own technique to advance beyond the limitations of his traditional instrument. With this new left hand technique, he can play more advanced and modern pieces. He has also designed an ud with two extra bass strings to broaden the range and function of his instrument.
 Links:
 Website 
 Youtube 
 Facebook 
 Spotify 
 Bandcamp 
 Instagram

events.title: Dapo Star Workshop by The Fire Messiah
events.description: The Dapo Star has been taking over the world and dancefloors for years, and this year it makes its way to Landjuweel. Tiago (The Fire Messiah) will be teaching you how to use this wonderful tool of expression, and he is sure you will become addicted after this workshop. We will finish the workshop with some fun interactive games. More than 20 Dapo's are available for borrowing in this workshop, so plenty of space for those who don't have a Dapo!

events.title: Divine Mistress of Craziness
events.description: (Born in 1939) She commutes between Cologne and the Amsterdam artist village of Ruigoord. After taking a parachute jump on her 60th birthday, she left behind her conventional life to pursue profound spiritual experiences. In her temple in Ruigoord, she celebrates encounters at a soul level and opens doors to higher levels of consciousness. More information about her unique philosophy can be found on the Ruigoord Artist Page.

events.title: FreeFlow Flashmob
events.description: Somewhere, sometime, this week. Maybe tomorrow morning after breakfast, maybe when the sun sets over the church, maybe when you've just tied your shoelaces. The FreeFlow Flashmob passes by. Or rather: it comes towards you. A rave on wheels, a party that creates itself, a dance that invites you to join in. With DJs on the bike sound system and live music. No stage, no audience, just participants who don't yet know they're part of it. Dancing, contact improvisation, and homo ludens on two wheels. The party creates itself, and you participate. Afterwards, we dissolve into the wind, as if nothing had happened, except in your legs. Those who were there will know. The rest will hear the stories.

events.title: The Bard’s Horn
events.description:
Thursday:
 21.00 till 01.00 DJs Archangel, Graham B, JP and Ambient Daan
 Friday:
 16.00 to 20.00 with storytellers Simone Engeln, Daan P., Belinda with musical accompaniment by Fons, musical interlude by sitar player Dougal
 20.00 to 21.00 Monty Pagan, a delightful Amsterdam folk rock band
 21.00 till 01.00 DJs Archangel, Graham B, JP and Ambient Daan
 Saturday:
 16.00 to 20.00 with storytellers Simone Engeln, Daan P., Belinda with musical accompaniment by Fons, musical interlude by sitar player Dougal
 20.00 to 21.00 Monty Pagan, a delightful Amsterdam folk rock band
 21.00 till late DJs Archangel, Graham B, JP and Ambient Daan
 Sunday:
 15.00 to 18.00 ParaNoya and friends with poetry and art
 Simone Engeln (formerly known as Simone Angel) is a versatile storyteller whose life itself is a story. After spending twenty years in the jungle of Belize, she returned to the Netherlands in 2023. Her adventurous life began at the age of 18; under the international spotlight of television…

events.title: Festival Make-up & Makeover with Shotgun Cilla
Gotcha & Katherina – Folk songs from Georgia
The Great Reproduction Show
events.description:
Do you think nature is neatly divided into man, woman, and traditional romance? Then think again! Biology teacher Sven and his incredible assistant Hester will take you to the strangest, most anarchic corners of the animal kingdom.

When a stiff "Bible Belt" couple takes the stage to proclaim that all modern sexuality and gender identities are "utterly unnatural," Sven and Hester intervene. What follows is a hysterical, visual, and slightly vulgar escalation featuring flamboyant paradise birds, trans-clown fish, headless rooster, and dominant hyenas.

This absurdist theatrical performance proves that Mother Nature is the greatest gender bender on Earth. Leave your preconceptions behind, be fluid, and celebrate mating!

events.title: Avi Adir. Araam Project
events.description:
ARAM Project is a collaborative world-fusion music ensemble led by Avi Adir, a nomadic musician, vocalist, and multi-instrumentalist. Rooted in the art of spontaneous creation, the project brings together musicians from diverse backgrounds to compose music in the moment, blending improvisation with deep listening and collective expression.
 Drawing inspiration from traditions and sounds from around the world, ARAM Project creates unique musical journeys where no performance is ever repeated. Melodies, rhythms, and textures emerge organically through the interaction between the musicians, inviting audiences into a living, evolving musical experience.
 For this performance, ARAM Project features: Avi Adir – vocals, bouzouki, and flutes
 Terence Samson – percussion
 Lucas Stam – cello
 Gil Lopez – guitar

events.title: Laura Sestri x Rhei
events.description:
Since 2017, Tim and Laura have been exploring the same magical ecosystems of festivals, art, music, and free play. What began as a love/friendship has grown into a long-term creative partnership, fueled by late-night dance floors, Ruigoord adventures, and a shared passion for authentic human connection.

His sets are warm, adventurous, and connecting – a musical journey where playfulness meets depth.
Tim and Laura have previously performed together at Artlake Festival, the Tropenmuseum, and various cultural and musical venues.
For Landjuweel 2026, they bring this special chemistry to Slooase. A tribute to friendship, freedom, and the art of creating a parallel world together.

events.title: The story: with the characters of the parade.
Embrace your No-Talent by Malle Wally
events.description:
Put on your red nose, because Malle Wally is bringing clowning to the Queer Stage on Sunday! But be warned: Malle Wally is hungover.

Part of this free-spirited celebration is our famous Kids' Catwalk at 4 PM! Will you let yourself be surprised, or will you be the surprise yourself?

events.title: Maurício Lobão
events.description:
Maurício Lobão is a musician and percussionist with over 40 years dedicated to the art of sound. His journey is marked by a deep connection with global rhythms, exploring diverse instruments and musical languages that cross cultures, territories, and states of consciousness.

Throughout his path, Maurício has traveled to many countries, absorbing influences and experiences that enriched his musical expression. His work seamlessly bridges electronic and acoustic music, creating unique soundscapes that converse with both ancestral and contemporary realms.

More than just performance, his music is a lived experience. Inspired by the elements of nature—earth, water, fire, and air—Maurício crafts rhythms that evoke connection, presence, and harmony. His sounds not only entertain but also invite introspection and inner balance.

Recognized for his sensitivity and versatility, he uses percussion as a universal language, capable of uniting people and awakening states of healing and expansion. His art is an invitation to feel, listen, and reconnect with the essence of life through sound.…

events.title: Touch Me
events.description: Warmth, care and trust grow in small moments of real connection. A hand on a shoulder. A slow hug. A foot massage. These small acts of conscious touch are a superpower. In this workshop by Touch Me, we explore the topic of touch and put it into action. You are welcome as a couple, as friends, or on your own. Together, we create a world with more love and presence, one conscious touch at a time.

events.title: Wim Hof Method Ice Bath
events.description:
Immerse yourself in this invigorating ice bath for refreshing relaxation. Perfect for enthusiasts, but also for those with a fear of cold water. To warm the body and prepare our minds, we will perform several rounds of breathing exercises according to the Wim Hof Method. A wonderful experience, as Wim Hof himself says: "Get high on your own supply."

Afterwards, we are ready for the ultimate power boost: the ice bath. Inhale, exhale, surrender to the cold, maintain a sharp mind, and when you step out, you will feel reborn. Afterwards, you can float and dance freely across the festival grounds, feeling refreshed, clear, and full of confidence.

WHM Method Fundamentals Workshop. Duration: 2 hours.

I am Kolja van Zwoll, born and raised in Nieuw & Meer in Amsterdam, and I have been attending Landjuweel since it started :) For the past two years, I have been an instructor for the Wim Hof Method, and I have enjoyed and successfully worked on the healing field for the past two years, and I am very happy that it can do so again this year. To ensure the safety of all participants, I also bring in two trainers and an assistant for the equipment.

events.title: Kundalini Ceremony
events.description:
This Kundalini Ceremony is a deep and powerful experience, inviting you on a journey inward. Where you are guided to expand your consciousness, elevate your energetic frequency, and deepen the connection between earth, body, heart, and spirit.

Throughout the ceremony, participants are comfortably lying on yoga mats, allowing the body to fully relax and receive.
 The space is held with presence, care, and unconditional love, allowing you to feel safe enough to soften, let go, and surrender to your experience. In this nurturing environment, you are invited to trust the wisdom of your body and the intelligence of your own energy.

As the energy begins to move, you may experience spontaneous movements, physical sensations, emotional releases, visions, insights, feelings of bliss, or moments of profound stillness. Every expression is welcome. There is nothing to force, nothing to control, and nothing to achieve. The invitation is simply to be fully present with whatever arises, moment by moment.

Each journey is unique and deeply personal. The energy meets you exactly where you are, offering what is most aligned with your highest good in that moment.…

events.title: Yin Yoga & Reiki with DjezzeManon
events.description:
DjezzManon is a writer and facilitator, and the creator of Zachte Aarde (Soft Earth).

Through yin yoga, kundalini, Reiki, and body awareness practices, she creates sessions where slowing down, feeling, and connection are central. Her guidance is warm, accessible, and intuitive, with space for softness, playfulness, and anything that arises during the session.

Session
During this Yin & Reiki session, you are invited to slow down, feel, and deepen your connection to your body. Through gentle yin poses, you create space to release tension and experience greater presence within yourself.

During the session, DjezzManon intuitively uses Reiki: a gentle form of energetic work where life energy can flow through touch or presence. By connecting with the larger field (whatever you may call it), you create space for relaxation, softening, and inner movement.

Reiki is always applied with consent and tailored to what feels good for you.

This session invites you to step away from your thoughts, take deeper breaths, and reconnect with what you feel within yourself. A gentle, grounding experience where you don't need to solve anything, but can simply return to yourself.

events.title: Spirit Songs door Anouk Anansi
events.description:
Anouk Anansi shares gentle, meditative heart songs, guided by piano and voice. More than a performance, this is an invitation for an inner journey and to remember your wholeness.
 Especially for Landjuweel, she will be joined by Rafa Beltrão on guitar.

events.title: Simone Alyssa
events.description:
Simone Alyssa is the artist who transforms music into medicine.
She effortlessly moves between the ceremonial and the celebratory — between prayer and pure joy. Her performances are not just concerts, but immersive experiences where connection is at the heart. Come listen, sing, or dance along — everyone is welcome.
Through storytelling, poetry, and her original songs in Dutch, English, and Spanish, she brings hope, inspiration, and love to her audience. Her superpower is channeling spontaneous Heart Songs — born in the moment and touching straight into the soul.
Rooted in Aalsmeer and shaped by her creative years on the Caribbean and Balearic islands, she carries a unique blend of humanity and magic. She loves people, and she loves sharing the magic of the present moment through music.
Inspired by artists like Fia, Nessi Gomes, Sam Garrett, J.J. Cale, and Nick Mulvey, she loves sharing her music with those who are ready to discover something new.
With her book, album, and ceremonial concerts, she passionately shares the message of The Soul’s Masterplan, inspiring you to turn your own dreams into reality. Together, we can remember what paradise on Earth feels like.

events.title: Psychedelic Tea Party
events.description: A vibrant blend of flavors, scents, and sounds, inviting you to indulge your senses and enjoy a delightful tea experience.

events.title: Mayapapaya b2b Dragonslayer
events.description: Four beats. One mission. And everything that happens when you put these artists behind the decks. An electro-tropical collision of Afro, dancehall, reggaeton, cumbia, and more booty-shaking bass and beats. Hot rhythms that shake along the equator and make you move your hips.

events.title: Bram & Jara
events.description:
Bram van Langen is a songwriter based in Amsterdam who has performed over a thousand shows across Europe, from intimate venues and living rooms to renowned stages like Paradiso and Melkweg in Amsterdam. Trained as a guitarist at the Alkmaar Conservatory, his music balances technical skill with emotional depth.

Live, Bram plays on his almost hundred-year-old Swedish harp-guitar, mostly alone, but this time he will be joined by an orchestra of strings and horns. When on tour, he finds local musicians to play with him.
Inspired by artists like Nick Drake, Paul Simon, James Taylor and Paul McCartney, Bram’s music invites listeners to slow down and drift. His songs feel warm, immersive, and gently mesmerizing – a place to pause, wander, and simply listen.

Late last year, he released his first full-length album, with which he is touring all over Germany, Austria, Italy, Switzerland, Denmark, Norway and the Netherlands and Belgium.

events.title: Eric, the magician behind the curtain.
events.description:
Eric the Magician will appear and disappear again around the Gnome House Stage (Kabouterhuys) at Landjuweel. Hearing, seeing, smelling, tasting, feeling, and thinking are the ingredients of a magician. Eric creates his own illusions; he doesn't like to let them go and loves to involve you in them. Beyond the smoke and mirrors, there is room for attention, and before you know it, you are spellbound. You don’t need to wear a red hat to be a Gnome.


 With Landjuweel, Eric the Magician will reappear and disappear around the Kabouterhuys Stage. Hearing, seeing, smelling, tasting, feeling, and thinking are the ingredients of a magician. Eric creates his own illusions; he doesn't like to lose them and loves to make you part of them. Beyond the smoke and mirrors, there is room for attention, and before you know it, you are spellbound. You don’t need to wear a red hat to be a Gnome.

events.title: The Rebels' Club: Maxi McNaughty & Erwin Mulder
The Gritmoaners
events.description:
Gritmoaners featuring a performance by Walking Walter and Barbara Schroevers


The Gritmoaners is a roots and blues duo featuring Peter Lavell on guitar and vocals and Lilli Belle on harmonica. Known for their soulful performances and authentic sound, they bring a raw and heartfelt energy to the blues tradition. Their music pays homage to classic blues while infusing it with their own unique style. Whether in intimate venues or on festival stages, The Gritmoaners captivate audiences with their masterful musicianship and deep, resonant storytelling.


The Gritmoaners are a roots and blues duo featuring Peter Lavell on guitar and vocals and Lilli Belle on harmonica. Known for their soulful performances and authentic sound, they bring a raw and heartfelt energy to the blues tradition. Formed in 2018, their music pays homage to classic blues while infusing it with their own unique style. Whether in intimate venues or on festival stages, The Gritmoaners captivate audiences with their masterful musicianship and deep, resonant storytelling.

events.title: The Lyrebirds
events.description:
The Lyrebirds is a collective of young professional musicians who play Jazz Manouche.

Jazz Manouche, also known as Gypsy Jazz, is considered the oldest European jazz style. The music originated in the 1930s within the Romani and Sinti communities, where traditional melodies and emerging American swing jazz came together. The style was popularized by Django Reinhardt and is known as Hot Jazz: danceable, rhythmic, and full of improvisation.
