The Lyrebirds play this style with their own interpretation and respect. The lineup consists of three gypsy guitars: Lars van Teeffelen, Berend Lantink, and SebaStrings, with Thimo Geijssen & Joris Das on double bass and Lucy Geelen on accordion and piano.

events.title: I dreamt I had hairy teeth - Humo y Polvo
events.description:
In a strange, surreal world, two women confront their inner beast – the one they have learned to keep quiet but which still weighs on them. Between explosion and rebirth, they travel an intimate path of acceptance and rebellion. An acrobatic and visual performance constructed as a journey of thought that moves forward and backward, sharing and transforming. Driven by a shared passion for acro-dance and visual arts, Morgane Maret and Martina Calvo question in their first duo ‘I dreamt I had hairy teeth’ the representations and stereotypes of women in society.

CREDITS
Concept & performers : Morgane Maret, Martina Calvo
Sound design: Gasper Piano
Light design: Valentin Boucq
Technician: Emile Warny
Outside eye: Mayra Bosshard, Maria Clara Villa-Lobos
Design of the hairy thing: Ruben Aranda, Harold Cuellar
Supported by: la maison des cultures et de la cohésion sociale de Molenbeek, le centre culturel Wolubilis, le PALC, Passages Transfestival, la CicaS, Station Circus, le théâtre du Jura, Pianofabriek, la Maison culturelle Ath, ESAC, la Roseraie, Prohelvetia, la ville de Delémont et la compagnie SCOM.

Links:
Website
Youtube
Instagram

events.title: L’heure Classique
events.description:
Classical music is boring, isn’t it? Well think again, it’s not. And to prove this, Arinde links an hour of classical music to sex on this sexy Sunday. Be surprised by the most beautiful music, combined with dirty stories. Curious? Come and listen.
 Arinde Jonker
 Arinde Jonker (1990) hopped to the beats of Bach’s music in her playpen before she could stand. And she never stopped. Since 2020, Arinde has been making (radio)programs in which she presents classical music in an accessible and often alternative way. She makes you listen to classical music in a way you never did before. Classical music is groovy, revolutionary and sexy. Curious? Come and listen.

events.title: Rae Charmaine
events.description: Rich in soul and soothing energy: this Zimbabwean singer-songwriter makes use of melodies and composition, blending perfectly together into a lyrical potion of emotions. Her music is a combination of jazz, blues, rhythm, and reggae with African influences.

events.title: Flamenco – Everything is Colorful
events.description:
Todo es de color consists of:
Nancy Polderman; vocals, dance & flute
Jan Bianchi; guitar and
Samu Ramphal; percussion

We create Flamenco-Fusion music inspired by artists such as Paco Lucia, Vicente Amigo, and Lole & Manuel, with additional Latin influences from Alex Cuba and Silvio Rodriguez.

events.title: INOYSON : The Spiritual Journey Beyond Consciousness
events.description:
INOYSON’s style is cinematic music for imaginary films — soundscapes you can see in your mind while listening to this musical voyage.
 This time at Church, experience a new program «The Spiritual Journey Beyond Consciousness» together with DJ ANYA.
 You are invited into a universe of lucid dreams through a Hybrid Live DJ set, offering a new perception of spiritual electronica, neoclassical trip-hop, acoustic techno, psychedelic avant-garde and alternative jazz fusion.
 Tomas Mazoes joins on percussion, while Sylvia Nantier presents a cinematic dance performance.

events.title: Angel Tarot
events.description:
BASJE reads Angel Tarot

What do the angels have in store for you?
BASJE invites you to a relaxed and accessible angel card reading. With a fresh perspective, warm presence, and a generous dose of intuition, you can draw a card from the Angel Deck and then discuss it with BASJE. This is not fortune-telling, nor are there any grand promises. Instead, it's about finding a moment of quiet reflection or perhaps even intense contemplation. Perhaps this is exactly what you need.

Angel Tarot works with gentle, encouraging messages. The cards focus on growth, choices, and what lies within you. Ideal for those who are curious but not yet ready for traditional Tarot. It's a great starting point. The angels offer encouragement and never lie. Of course, experienced Tarot enthusiasts who want to explore the power of the angels are also welcome.

events.title: Children's parade – gather at 4:15 PM
events.description: The story has been told, but it's not over yet. The Wandering Company invites everyone to join in, because now it's your turn to continue the journey. Together, we will set off in a festive parade, heading towards a place where searching can transform into finding. Not because all questions have been answered, but because you can experience together how it feels to finally arrive somewhere and feel at home. From the village square to the crater, the procession moves forward, singing and dancing, with the Wandering Company, the Storyteller, and Mr. Cricket leading the way. There, in the crater, everything falls into place for a moment: the search comes to an end, the world becomes warm and festive, and "home" is no longer just a question, but a place where you can stand and celebrate together.

events.title: Red Oak Troubadours
events.description:
The international trio behind the Red Oak Troubadours formed their band in early 2023, united by their shared love for traditional folk, blues, Americana, and psychedelic rock. Their music is deeply influenced by these genres and reflects a commitment to adapting the tradition of storytelling through music for the modern world.

The Red Oak Troubadours dedicate their work to fostering community and connection. Within their repertoire, they blend their music with cultural influences, spoken word, and sound scaping to create an all-encompassing experience for their listeners. Through their own songs and stories, they explore themes of struggle, hardship, dreams, and hope, aiming to resonate with their listeners on an intimate and authentic level. The Red Oak Troubadours invite you to join them on their musical journey!

events.title: Double staff Workshop by Bartoz (Bzarr)
events.description: Two sticks. Four ends. Infinite ways to look ridiculous before you look amazing. This double staff workshop is where we stop being precious about “getting it right” and start playing. We’ll chase spins, launch throws (and drop them, proudly), untangle isolations, and flirt with contact until the staffs start feeling like extensions of your arms instead of things trying to escape them. Come find out what these things can actually do. No experience needed, just a willingness to flail with intention.

events.title: Embrace your No Talent by you?
events.description:
At this very last hour of performances at the Queer Stage, ANYTHING GOES. But there’s a disclaimer: We’ll be totally and creatively worn out, so we cannot guarantee quality on any level.
 Host? To be determined…

events.title: Bewegende Lijnen
events.description: In this workshop, we will explore drawing and movement to discover our creative side. Through surprising and fun exercises, Jana brings together children of all ages. Children up to the age of 10 will participate under the supervision of adults.

events.title: Soul Safari
events.description:
Crafting immersive journeys through ambient, downtempo and organic electronic music, Soul Safari explores the space between sound, atmosphere and emotion.
 Based in the self-built studio ‘The Mycelium Cave’, surrounded by nature and community, Soul Safari weaves atmospheric textures, organic rhythms and cinematic depth into evolving stories. Moving through stillness, vast synthesizer landscapes and deep grooves, each set unfolds organically, inviting listeners to slow down, reconnect and become fully present in the unfolding moment.
 Ambient • Downtempo • Organic Electronica

events.title: Ecstatic Dance with Richie Lee
events.description: These days, he is mostly known for the Ambrosia sound, which was partly shaped during the early years of his DJ career. He actually started out playing dancehall, reggaeton, bubbling afro, and other urban styles. For this set, he's taking a special trip back in time so we can shake everything loose in the carefree way we did as kids. Wild dancing & steamy grinding sessions. With consent, of course xx

events.title: Workshop Playful Circus Acrobatics
events.description: Climb aboard your partner like a wobbly human ladder, balance with buddy hugs, and lift with loving care for each other's bodies. Unlock wild moves you didn't know existed, build mega-trust through fizzy fun, and co-create a mini circus show that'll have everyone grinning.

events.title: Steven de Peven
events.description: Laat je verrassen!

events.title: SoulSong Activation – The Healing Power of Light Language
events.description:
In this experiential workshop, you will discover the healing power of Light Language, sound, and frequency. Through live sound transmissions, a guided meditation, and direct experience, you will learn how energetic codes and vibrations can contribute to awareness, connection, and inner transformation.

A gentle yet powerful introduction to SoulSong Activation, where voice, consciousness, and frequency come together as a path to connection, healing, and remembering your own essence. No prior experience is necessary; only an open heart and curiosity are required.

Azuni Raphael is a sound healer, voice activator, and space holder. By opening her own voice, she discovered a deeper layer of her soul's purpose: sharing healing frequencies through sound and Light Language.

With her voice and presence, she creates a supportive field in which people can experience the power of frequency, consciousness, and inner connection. Her work invites us to remember the wisdom that is already present within each of us…

events.title: Gong Meditatie
events.description: Mark Bakker, also known as "The Gong Guide of Amsterdam," offers sound journeys using gongs and singing bowls. He combines 30 years of experience as a drummer with a background in psychology. After several training sessions in the United States, Portugal, and the Netherlands, he began offering gong sessions himself two years ago. In his sessions, he takes you on a sonic journey through his musicality, where all you need to do is listen; the magical sounds of these instruments will transport you to deep relaxation. A sound massage for body and mind, in which you can completely relax, and perhaps even gain new insights.

events.title: The Art of Seduction: A tribute to being a woman
events.description:
The focus is not on seducing others. It's about coming home to yourself. About fully embracing your body, strength, beauty, and feminine energy.

In this playful, sensual, and safe workshop, we will explore where your erotic life force flows through you using bodywork, theater exercises, and mindfulness practices. Not from performance or obligation, but from pleasure, presence, and self-love.
We will unravel the myth that seduction is something you "do". Instead, you'll discover how irresistible you become when you truly allow yourself to feel, embody, and enjoy yourself.

Sexuality is just one aspect of Eros. Eros also encompasses love, beauty, vitality, and desire. Where does your natural magnetism lie? What do you yearn for more softness, freedom, or self-love?

In my opinion, a woman who can naturally enjoy herself is inherently sensual. A woman who welcomes and loves her body radiates a deep attraction. We will explore how to connect with this together during this unique workshop...

events.title: Dramm, LaBerge & Dramm
events.description:
"The 21st Century von Trapp Family," as they are often called, comprise composer and guitarist David Dramm, flutist and composer Anne La Berge, and violinist (and her daughter) Diamanda La Berge Dramm. As far back as Diamanda (Dutch Classical Talent in 2018) can remember, the La Berge Dramm family has been making music together, both on stage and at home. In 2019, they documented all their own compositions, as well as pieces by composers such as Charles Ives, along with covers of artists like The Velvet Underground, Cindy Walker, and Burt Bacharach, on a triple-LP: Modern Genetics (released by Splendor Records), featuring both harsh and tender sounds.

Expect a generous dose of harmonies and playful virtuosity.
glish blurb:
An impossible, rousing mix of punk, country, spoken word and electro. The 21st Century von Trapp family La Berge Dramm.
Line-up/Personnel:
David Dramm (guitars/vocals), Anne La Berge (flutes/electronics/vocals), Diamanda La Berge Dramm (violin/vocals/kick drum)

events.title: Healing ragas and mantras
events.description:
Tritha is a Sound Yogini, Indian classical & contemporary singer & producer and Naad yoga teacher. Naad Yoga is the discipline and knowledge of healing sound wisdom from ancient India and it's practice of singing, playing instrument or listening helps in aligning and healing the body-mind-heart-soul space. According to Hindu philosophy in Sama Veda and Naad Yoga- The Yoga of Sound, music and sound can affect the human spirit. Some ragas are used in music therapy to treat mental stress, headaches, addiction, insomnia and other health ailments. In this set, Tritha, trained in Indian classical singer for last 3 decades will be sharing a healing concert with the knowledge of mantras, chakras and Indian classical raagas or musical scales- that have certain healing benefits and therapeutic properties. Perfect as a hangover concert!!
 Links:
 Website

events.title: Coco Coquelicot story telling
events.description:
Passionate, energetic and fearless: Coco Coquelicot's unique mix of club sounds combines warm grooves with powerful basslines. As a true music enthusiast, Coco delves deep into researching the best tracks from all musical corners. Her sets are infused with her commitment to fighting for equality. By giving marginalized voices a platform, she provides a safe haven for everyone who doesn't always have one. As co-founder of Relate Radio and organizer of Butts, the highly acclaimed queer club night in East, Coco actively promotes the progress of the Groningen music scene. Her eclectic influences come together in a rich and percussive mix that resonates through your body.
In 2023, Coco became part of Hit the North (ESNS) under the guidance of Isis van der Wel. She received a nomination for the Groninger Culture Prize and has already performed at Lowlands, Wildeburg, IsBurning, Milkshake, Eurosonic and ADE, DGTL.

events.title: The Cacao Coronation by: Bente Kruger & Adhem Kouta
DJ Try/Me
events.description: DJ Try/Me presents: Dance Music for Birds of Paradise. Gender-blending & genre-bending.

events.title: Cairê & Friends - Sacred Prayers
events.description:
Born in Brazil and rooted in the ancestral wisdom of the Kamanawa people, Cairê weaves a bridge between the Amazon rainforest and the modern world. Renowned as a highlight at festivals throughout Europe, his unique concert-ceremony invites you to dive into a powerful, transformative journey. Through sacred songs and shamanic sounds, Cairê guides us into a deep remembrance of our innate connection to all living things.
 Categories: Live concert, Indigenous Music, Yawanawá, Huni Kuin.

events.title: Closing remarks
events.description: After a day filled with discovery, learning, making, and meeting new people, we come together to quietly conclude the day. It's a moment for reflection, to consider what has touched us and to share our experiences with each other. While enjoying a warm cup of chaga tea, we allow ourselves to be guided by the moment. Perhaps we sing together, do a simple yoga exercise, tell stories to each other, or simply enjoy being together.

events.title: Cézanne
events.description:
Like a sunset, Cézanne opens a portal in time filled with transformation, beauty, and mystery.
 Blending neo-soul, folk, alternative R&B, storytelling, and scent, her work exists at the intersection of ritual and live music. Her songs explore our relationship to ourselves and to the living earth. Moments of melancholy, tenderness, hurt, joy and wonder sit side by side, revealing how each is part of being human.
 Fresh from a three-month artist residency in Japan, where she immersed herself in local mythology, spiritual practices, community, and the landscape while writing her upcoming album, Cézanne brings a special performance to Landjuweel with her live band. The show weaves together songs from her debut album Sunflower Child with new, unreleased material shaped by her time in Japan.

events.title: Katie Bitchcoin
events.description:
Techno? Electroclash? Lipstickwave? Cryptopunk?
 Katie Bitchcoin is trapped in a boring, monotonous ironing-and-vacuuming office with her toxic partner and wonders if Bitchcoin can be her escape.
 Are you investing in Bitchcoin? WAGMI!


 Techno? Electroclash? Lipstickwave? Cryptopunk?
 Katie Bitchcoin is stuck in a beige nightmare of ironing, cooking, and vacuuming for her toxic partner.
 One shiny day, the Three Bitchcoins arrive! They are on a mission to save Katie and get her to invest in Bitchcoin.
 Escape the house. Join the cult. Will you invest? WAGMI!

events.title: Improv-Theater
events.description:
Step into the world of improvisational theatre and discover the joy of being present, playful, and connected. This beginner-friendly workshop invites you to break out of your "human nutshell" through fun games, creative exercises, and spontaneous storytelling. 
 No experience needed, come as you are and bring a willingness to play.

events.title: Shamballa Tribe
events.description: The Shamballa Vision Tribe is a spiritual and flow arts collective that hosts ecstatic singing, cacao ceremonies, and ancestral drumming in and around Netherlands and Belgium and in based in Ghent. They frequently organize urban singing, deep mantra singing and drum circles locally, as well as an annual "Shamballa Vision Festival".

events.title: I dreamt I had hairy teeth - Humo y Polvo
events.description:
In a strange, surreal world, two women confront their inner beast – the one they have learned to keep quiet but which still weighs on them. Between explosion and rebirth, they travel an intimate path of acceptance and rebellion. An acrobatic and visual performance constructed as a journey of thought that moves forward and backward, sharing and transforming. Driven by a shared passion for acro-dance and visual arts, Morgane Maret and Martina Calvo question in their first duo ‘I dreamt I had hairy teeth’ the representations and stereotypes of women in society.

CREDITS
Concept & performers : Morgane Maret, Martina Calvo
Sound design: Gasper Piano
Light design: Valentin Boucq
Technician: Emile Warny
Outside eye: Mayra Bosshard, Maria Clara Villa-Lobos
Design of the hairy thing: Ruben Aranda, Harold Cuellar
Supported by: la maison des cultures et de la cohésion sociale de Molenbeek, le centre culturel Wolubilis, le PALC, Passages Transfestival, la CicaS, Station Circus, le théâtre du Jura, Pianofabriek, la Maison culturelle Ath, ESAC, la Roseraie, Prohelvetia, la ville de Delémont et la compagnie SCOM.

Links:
Website
Youtube
Instagram

events.title: Solarpunk & Synths
events.description:
workshop: positive future thinking with live ambient music

Experience your own solarpunk moment and create a positive future while enjoying calming synthesizer sounds.
Solarpunk is an utopian future filled with cheerful colors. An eco-social worldview with high-tech and sustainable energy for everyone. We explore this solarpunk world and practice positive future thinking together. We will draw/paint/cut/glue our own solarpunk vision of the future.
Workshop by media psychologist Priscilla Haring and synthesizers by This is Not Rocket Science.

events.title: Steve Rachmad
events.description:
Since his earliest days in the scene, Amsterdam’s electronic music pioneer Steve Rachmad has created a legion of monikers to cover the broadness of his compositions. Black Scorpion, Tons of Tones, Rachmad Project, Scorp, Ignacio, Dreg, Parallel 9, Sterac Electronics, STERAC; they all represent a different facet of Rachmad’s music. Nowadays, Rachmad focuses on the latter three and his birth-name.
 Listing the countless releases, remixes, labels, clubs and festivals with one of Steve’s names on it, would quickly turn into a long catalogue of names, places and dates. Impressive as that list may be, let’s not go there. The real essence lies in the music itself — something best explored through the many recordings and DJ sets that are out there.
 Because as Miles had his horn and Pablo took to canvas, Steve has an unequalled gift for (almost physically) connecting with every thinkable drum machine and synthesizer out there. If the man had kids, boys would listen to Roland or Robert (Moog), and a girl would surely be named Linn, after the machine made famous by Prince and others in the 80’s.…

events.title: REO MATSUMOTO- DJ Set with live Handpan
events.description:
REO MATSUMOTO's captivating DJ / LIVE Hybrid Set are rooted in a unique blend of Ethnic & World music grooves with Afro House,Deep House and Organic House, incorporating deep tribal rhythms, Street spirit and psychedelic elements.
 His signature Hybrid Live set is enhanced by live instrumentation, featuring the HandPan, beatboxing, and percussion, creating a seamless DJ/Live hybrid set. REO’s performances promise to take music enthusiasts on an uplifting, mesmerizing, otherworldly journey.

events.title: La Prudon × Ibu Ibiza · Hats & Fashion Show
events.description:
Two designers, two crafts, one show. La Prudon (Christine Prudon) creates handmade hats as wearable art – small works of art that also happen to fit on a head. Ibu Ibiza (Su-an Dijckmeester, Ibiza) makes sustainable clothing. During Landjuweel, they will present an exclusive pop-up hat and fashion show on the Ruigoord grounds together.

Shows

Friday, July 24th, 17:00
Sunday, July 26th, 19:00

Location: De straat bij de Dingplaats, Ruigoord
Left:
Instagram La Prudon

events.title: Intimate haircuts
events.description: Intimate Haircuts aim to give your junk the funky twist Landjuweel calls for. Ranadhira reshapes, bleaches, colours, paints and decorates your pubic area to create an unforgettable art piece. Find your favorite hairdresser in the KALEIDO Faerie Forest!

events.title: Sonny & the Jets
events.description: Sonny & The Jets bring a lively mix of blues, rock, americana, soul, and roots music to the stage. Drawing from a wide range of influences, the Amsterdam-based band moves between gritty blues grooves, classic rock favourites, soulful melodies, and the occasional unexpected detour. Having cut their teeth in pubs and bars, Sonny & The Jets know how to build a set that keeps the energy up and the crowd engaged. Whether you're there to dance, sing along, or simply enjoy good live music, expect a performance full of groove, character, and a genuine love for great songs.

events.title: Nita Aartsen Kwartet
events.description:
The Aartsen Duo consists of Alexander on vocal and Nita on piano & vocal.
 Together they've been performing 
 around the world for decades, 
 bringing memories of old school 
 jazz with high artistry singing & 
 story telling, combined with fun 
tertainment.
 Join our concerts, and be part of a 
 magical musical journey.

events.title: Cuervo Cuervo
events.description:
Cuervo Cuervo is the collaboration between Argentinian musician and charango player Agustin Ganem, and American producer and multi-instrumentalist Benjamin Hill.

After a chance encounter in the streets of Bacalar in 2018, they joined forces to create a unique fusion of psychedelic electronic music blended with Latin American folk music and live improvisation. They are known for their electrifying live performances featuring virtuoso displays of musicianship, tasteful electronics, live looping, and a capacity for improvisation and collaboration. Ganem combines his mastery of South American charango and guitar with live looping, while Hill fuses North American electronic music with the folk influences of lapsteel guitar and classic Latin percussion elements such as caxixi and bongo. No two Cuervo Cuervo live performances are ever alike – each is a unique improvisational dialogue between the two that is created directly on the spot for each audience.

Links:
Website

events.title: Meraque
events.description:
Mèraque originates from a passion for Balkan music and curiosity about its fusion with psychedelic sounds and Middle Eastern influences. The musicians Jorick Bronius, Martin Grozdanoski, Onur Kaletas, and Talal Fayad met each other at the Rotterdam Conservatory and share a background in world music and sound design. Their collaboration results in an energetic and refined sonic universe where Eastern melodies and Mediterranean rhythms come together.

Mèraque has performed at venues and festivals such as Wildeburg (NL), Lost Gipsy Land (Bulgaria), 3000grad Festival (DE) and Fusion Festival (DE), and released music through labels like Ohxala Records, Harabe, and Krooks Records.

events.title: I love you too, Cacao! – Closing Ceremony with live music from Barry Zingt
events.description:
I'm Cacao from "Closing Ceremonies"
Limited spaces available, Cacao by Spirit of Cacao 🍫

events.title: DJ Witte Aap
events.description: Not a DJ, but a music selector playing wonderful tunes from all over the world!

events.title: Karaoke Bar: Valse Noten
events.description: Unleash your inner rock star, nightingale, or howling wolf! Here, we don't sing perfectly, but with full passion. Everyone can participate, whether you sound like a star or a vacuum cleaner.

events.title: MakaAno – Voice of the Soul
events.description:
Music from the heart.

Simple songs can emerge, surprisingly powerful.
With vocals, guitar, and various other instruments, rhythm, and improvisation, Wijnand, Sylvia, and Ali create a warm and intimate atmosphere where music connects people and brings them together.
Let yourself be swept away by the magic of the moment.

events.title: DIZZI GEETHA - LIVE SET
events.description:
Dizzi Geetha is a Queer POC artist from Sri Lanka, based in The Netherlands. Dizzi is an artist, creator, producer, composer, multi-instrumentalist, theatre maker, DJ and ceremonial facilitator. Their work is largely shaped by their love for nature and our planet, as well as their history of displacement and finding roots through sonic soundscapes. This gives their creations in music and theatre a truly unique and organic element that shapes the experiences of both the viewer and listener, rather than simply being consumed.

Links:
Instagram 
Spotify 
Website

events.title: Destroying the Last Horcrux
events.description: Voldemort is back again and trying to eat all the trannies. Come with us and learn some spells to fight against the Dark forces of life. You’ll go through a wonderful adventure filled with surprising encounters and surrealist creatures. We’re looking for other trashy witchxs and wizardx! We need you to start the revolution!

events.title: The Maxitaksi
events.description:
'Keep the meter running, because I'll take you places you've never been before' Like hearing your favorite songs mixed with some new beats being played while you are driven around, The Maxitaksi creates an energetic live DJ set through an eclectic combination of NYC house, disco, neo soul, electro and a sprinkle of vogue beats. 
 Join the dance floor, consensually grab a person to grind, dance and scream along with the music. You are at the destination you never knew you needed to be.

events.title: Landjuweel Temple Space
events.description:
Amidst the electric chaos of the festival, a sacred portal awaits. Welcome to Temple Space: a sanctuary designed to pause the buzz and ignite your inner world.

Ready for a journey inward? Embark on a breathtaking journey through your senses and desires, anchored by deep somatic movement and connective practices. This is your playground to claim what you want, honour your boundaries, and speak your truth through authentic, heart-centred communication. Bring your light, your shadow, and everything in between—you are completely welcome here, bound only by the safety and boundaries of those around you. Check in with our Temple Gate Keepers at the threshold to receive the container guidelines before you step inside.

Temple Space is located in the largest yurt on the Natural High grounds. Temple hours: Thursday 21.00-24.00, Friday 20.00-23.00 and Sunday 20.00-23.00.
Landjuweel’s Temple Space is curated by Iskra Bela and inspired by temple arts culture, contemporary dance, ritual-performance practice and ecstatic dance. Invited co-creators this year are Lexmy van den Boogaard and Bee-bee (Bregje).

Lexmy van den Boogaard…

events.title: MIJNHEER van Ouwenaar
events.description:
Mr. Van Ouwenaar is coming!

No one knows for sure why, but he is indeed coming!
Some claim that his Spinoza-inspired texts reveal a peculiar perspective, while others believe he only performs when the moon resembles a "psylist."
Expect unexpected beauty and songs that feel like dreams you once had on a rollercoaster to Nieuwegein, or in a hot air balloon to Ruigoord.

events.title: Tribal Organica with DJ Esthetica
events.description: Esthetica's sets feel like a musical journey in a warm bath, where you can experience a wide range of emotions. With tribal sounds, melancholic melodies, and careful arrangement, they build a story that draws you in from beginning to end. Her sound is always warm, yet often has a darker edge; both light and shadow are welcome, and can coexist. Come dance, feel, and experience the organic and progressive house beats of Esthetica at the Temple of Self.

events.title: Arp Frique & Family
events.description:
“The Colorful World of Arp Frique” is the exotic and super funky debut album of producer, musician and thinker Arp Frique. After his surprising, very well received debut release on Rush Hour, Arp Frique continues collaborating with Orlando Julius, Ed Motta, Americo Brito, Ronald Snijders and more!
 Pulsating NYC style live disco vibes, interwoven with both Caribbean and Cape Verdean sounds, are themes throughout the record with danceability always guaranteed and a healthy finishing touch of weirdness present across most tracks.
 The first single “Nos Magia” was shortlisted for Track of the Year 2017 at Gilles Peterson’s Worldwide Awards and has been given airplay by BBC Radio 6 Music, BBC Radio 1 and NTS whilst club DJs like Antal and Hunee have all supported his tracks in their sets.
 The name “Arp Frique” refers to the sound of the Arp synths/his love for analog vintage machines and is also a reference to Chic’s ‘Le Freak’, showing love to the genre of disco and deep danceable music from our entire world.…

events.title: The Long Circumferential Movement - Ointment
events.description: The Dutch indie band Zalf releases narrative lyrics in an unpredictable musical landscape. At Landjuweel, they perform with a small, acoustic ensemble and invite various guest artists for a poetic journey that consists solely of detours – along birthday parties, whispering stones, and enigmatic shadow figures. Their debut album, "De Lange Omtrekkende Beweging" (The Long Winding Movement), is therefore an encouragement to keep searching. After all, there's still plenty of time!

events.title: Mantra Kirtan
events.description:
Matthieu and Marlies invite you on a chanting journey!

Come join us for the opening evening of our wonderful festival… A good start is half the battle!
Inspiration and fun will go hand-in-hand this evening in the magical tea garden... See you there!

events.title: Woodcraft
events.description:
Woodcraft is one of the most promising string ensembles in the Netherlands. This innovative ensemble works with jazz, groove, improvisation and vocals, creating colorful and original compositions where forms and styles blend together to create adventurous musical stories.

Driven by curiosity and openness to different genres and playing techniques, Woodcraft constantly seeks ways to reimagine their instruments and thus create a fresh and distinctive sound. They are inspired by musicians such as Brad Mehldau, Herbie Hancock and the Turtle Island Quartet, and composers such as Stravinsky and Shostakovich. This leads to original compositions where forms and styles blend together to create adventurous musical stories.

Born out of a close friendship, the group gives the violin, viola and cello a renewed voice within the contemporary music landscape. Woodcraft won the "Keep an Eye" competition in 2026, and received first prize and the public award at the Dutch Jazz Competition in 2022. They have performed at venues such as North Sea Jazz Festival, Bimhuis and Orange Jazz Days.

events.title: Latin upbeat freestyle
events.description:
The Zebra Street Band presents an explosive mix of African and Caribbean influences, jazz, Latin grooves, and free improvisation. With their energetic, danceable sound and collective improvisations, no performance is ever the same.

Special for the Landjuweel Festival, the band performs with a unique lineup, alongside percussionists Jos de Haas and Sebastian Skeete McLeed. Expect a fully improvised set full of uptempo Latin grooves, infectious rhythms, and irresistible energy that will keep everyone moving.

events.title: Anna Babélva: Soft Soirrée Closing
events.description:
The sound of Anna Babèlva (Amsterdam) can best be described as a cheeky ride in a pink Cadillac. She takes you on a journey with a cocktail in hand, allowing you to transcend into a multi-coloured universe of passionate encounters.

When she is performing during one of her captivating shows, she blends musical treasures into a carefully curated selection of sound. Babèlva is one of those rare gems you discover in an underground club, a well-organized event, or at one of the great festivals of our time.

As she is always seeking new ways to express herself, you will find her working on a multitude of projects. As the founder of the female collective The Portal, she advocates for the importance of community within the creative sphere, supporting female colleagues in the music, performance, and art scene.

events.title: Meraki
events.description:
BORN IN BRAZIL, PEDRO SUGIURA MUSIC HAS A HUGE INFLUENCE OF HIS CONTINENT'S RICH DIVERSITY OF RHYTHMS. CURRENTLY LIVING IN AMSTERDAM, MERAKI HAS A UNIQUE IDENTITY AND PRESENTS A VERY ENERGETIC SOUND SIGNATURE. THE ARTIST HAS TRACKS RELEASED WITH NAMES LIKE DANDARA, SPANIOL, SALVADOR ARAGUAYA, GERRA G KOTOE AND OCTO SHARK
 BEGGINING HIS MUSICAL EXPERIENCE IN PSYTRANCE AS A TEENAGER LISTENER, AND LATER GETTING IN TOUCH WITH DOWNTEMPO AS A DJ, HE STARTED HIS JOURNEY WITH THE BRAZILIAN CREW SONIDO TROPICO IN 2016.
 ALWAYS SEEKING BALANCE BETWEEN THESE TWO CONTRASTING STYLES OF ELECTRONIC MUSIC, HIS MIXES ALWAYS RESULTS IN A FLOWY AND GROOVY ATMOSPHERE.
 MERAKI DID A EUROTOUR IN 2017 PLAYING IN 8 DIFFERENT COUNTRIES AS LISTED BELOW. DURING THE LAST YEARS IN BRAZIL, HE WAS CONSTANTLY PLAYING AMONG THE UNDERGROUND SCENE PARTIES AND FESTIVALS
 HIS LAST JOB WAS A RESIDENCY AT AMATA VENUE IN BRAZIL.
 Links:
 Facebook 
 Soundcloud 
 Instagram

events.title: WRLDBEAT
events.description:
WRLDBEAT, based in Amsterdam and surrounding areas, blends funk, Latin, hip-hop, ska, reggae & soul in a dynamic musical melting pot. This nine-piece band brings together cultures and generations to create an explosive and unique sound that leaves no one unmoved.

The powerful vocals of Larissa, Stefanie, and Daan are central to their sound. These are enhanced by Mick's raw saxophone playing, the guitar work of Nico and Luke, Jort's deep bass, Carl's dynamic drumming, and Henk's fluid percussion.

While they may not be widely known to the general public, those in the know understand that WRLDBEAT guarantees unforgettable performances.

They have played at iconic venues such as Paradiso, Patronaat, and the A'DAM Tower, as well as at festivals like Parckpop and Rollende Keukens. With over 150 live shows, the band has proven its ability to make a lasting impression.

Let yourself be swept away by the rhythm, dynamism, and passion of WRLDBEAT – we are here on this earth to dance!

events.title: Lotte Walda
events.description:
Dutch songwriter Lotte Walda writes songs to spread positive energy and help create a kinder, more connected world. Deeply inspired by nature and by the music of the sixties and seventies, Lotte feels most at home in the sounds of that era — songs full of warmth, connection and timeless storytelling. Through her music, she hopes to remind people of the light and love that still live in the world. She loves creating catchy melodies that stick with listeners. Blending folk, pop and country, her songs tell heartfelt stories about life, love and staying true to yourself. With her warm voice and honest lyrics, she aims to touch, comfort and uplift everyone who listens.
 Her musical journey began on the old Sint Servaas Bridge of Maastricht near her hometown, where she would fill the streets with her voice and guitar. From those early days, she wanted to “build a bridge that reminds people of the joy within that connects them to their inner child. This curiosity and open-heartedness soon took her on a global adventure. She’s since performed everywhere from the Bluebird Café in Nashville to festival stages across the Netherlands, and even shared her music on the streets of China.…

events.title: House of Chi Live
events.description:
At House of Chi Jam, electronic music and live improvisation flow seamlessly together. DJs provide the musical foundation, while instrumentalists weave melodies, rhythms, and textures into the music, creating a constantly evolving soundscape.

Each session unfolds organically, driven by listening, intuition, and connection. Rooted in musical traditions from around the world, but always focused on the dance floor, House of Chi Jam invites both musicians and audiences to experience an immersive event where no moment is ever the same.

events.title: DJ Witte Aap
events.description: Not a DJ, but a music selector playing wonderful tunes from all over the world!

events.title: Fire & Cacao Circle
events.description:
Opening Ceremony Thursday
 We gather around the fire to open this celebration of Life ~ sharing the sacred medicine of the heart (Kakaw) and becoming one same song and prayer as we share our offerings and gratitude to the Earth and the land.
 Closing Ceremony Sunday
 We gather around the fire to close this celebration of Life ~ sharing the sacred medicine of the heart (Kakaw) and becoming one same song and prayer as we share our offerings and gratitude to the Earth and the land.
 Bios of all the facilitators:
 Aliix Balam, Human. Earth Guardian. Remembering and bridging ancient practices into this time-space. His path as an ethnobiologist led to scientific work with the FAO/UN on ecosystem approach to fisheries management, which evolved into over a decade serving alongside Mayan communities on permaculture, agroecology, and food sovereignty. Today, Aliix serves as a holistic educator, facilitator, natural builder, weaver, dreamer and visionary at Winaq ~ School of Life and Ancestral Remembrance at Lake Atitlán, Guatemala
 Pim Grinter is a sustainable textile designer whose work is rooted in plant wisdom through an animistic lens.…

events.title: Eli Lux
events.description:
Your professional bedroom dj, ready to serve up some juice! Eli likes to brighten up rooms with a good portion of bass, infused with the right amount of light cheekiness. You can surely leave that to her, as founder of the Peacock Garden, safe haven of hedonistic paradise birds. 
 Her musical choices form a magnetic mix of housey bounce, dirty beats and all the feels, wrapped up in a delicious story into the senses. 
 Driven by the desire to bring people closer together in a world of separation, she uses her feminine superpowers to create ripples of love, moments of marvel and beauty all around. She creates an inviting atmosphere and pushes the boundaries of free expression on dance floors and beyond. Enjoy, my darlings!

events.title: Relating in the Dark
events.description:
What do you notice when you stop looking?
 By exploring intimacy without sight, we will delve into our intuitive nature, sharpening our capacity for deeper connection. We will use blindfolds to invite you into profound contact with your physical sensations and internal reality, unlocking both sensuality & vulnerability.
 Laurie King
 I'm Laurie (he/him), a queer British-Kiwi whose current home-base is Berlin. My passion is creating spaces that cultivate healthy, thriving human-to-human relationships, helping people take off their masks, feel their sensations and celebrate their aliveness on Earth. 
 Since 2019, I have organised and facilitated hundreds of Authentic Relating, Dating and Embodiment workshops in Oceania and Europe. My training includes Authentic Revolution, The Embody Lab, World Peace Yoga School, the Connection Institute, ISTA and Body Electric. I have facilitated at internationally acclaimed festivals such as Stretch and Ignite and organisations such as ART International.

events.title: Wilgendans
events.description:
Wilgendans is an open-groove ensemble featuring saxophonist Koen Boeijinga, guitarist Bram Stadhouders, and drummer Luca Marini. Hypnotic grooves and immersive soundscapes emerge from improvisation, bringing the willows to life. As custodians of the Willow Portal and trusted figures at Landjuweel, they welcome guest musicians for an ecstatic celebration of music, dance, and connection.

Koen Boeijinga: soprano saxophone, wind instruments, harmonium, percussion, fx
Bram Stadhouders: guitar, fx
Luca Marini: drums, percussion

events.title: Irene van Krimpen
events.description: Since the first time I visited Trance Oriënt (in March 2009), I was completely captivated by the atmosphere in the church, the Kabouterhuys, and around the fire. Since then, I've been a fan of Ruigoord and enjoy contributing to the atmosphere through dressing up, dancing, singing, and making music. I want to dance at the Kabouterhuys every evening as the sun sets. And on Sunday evenings, I go there to sing 3 traditional Irish songs before Krien Joseph starts playing.

events.title: DJ JamieNee
events.description: JAMIE NEE will be giving another impressive performance on Sunday at Area 46, so if you want to have a great time, then Area 46 is the place to be. Starting with some hard synth music, you can then dance like a stripper with a mix of cheeky Ghetto Tek and crazy Latin Tek – Que Locura!

events.title: Alex Covert
events.description:
Alex Covert
Alex Covert is a multidisciplinary artist based in Amsterdam. Through performance, painting, and sculpture, she explores the space where opposites meet: darkness and delight, devotion and mischief, vulnerability and power. Her work often begins with big questions and ends somewhere unexpected – less interested in answers than in play. After all, if creation is a game, perhaps God just wants to join in.
You can see more of her work on Instagram: @alexcovert.art
Anastasiya
Anastasia Prokhorova is a multidisciplinary artist, founder of Artwear Amstel Bleach, and creator of the “I see your frequency” immersive experience. Working from within systems of consumption, she treats language, materials, and the body as reused data: marked by ambition, desire, and labor.

events.title: The Rise, Life and Death of Don Quijote
events.description:
Thijs van de Loo – Theatermaker & Hessel Pijnaker, artist; combine their talents to bring five dolls to life, revolving around the theme of metamorphosis.

“Using rattan, fabric, inner tubes and found objects, and a good dose of intuition, we create soulful objects. Both have studied Vodun religions such as Ubuntu and draw inspiration from them.”

events.title: Origins: Krien Jozeph & Merante Tamar & friends
events.description: Landjuweel Sunday at the Kabouterhuis is the secret garden of exquisitely curated songs & sounds. Origins: Krien Jozeph will open his treasure chest of eclectic, deep, heartfelt and dancy rhythms & melodies, while Origins: Merante shapeshifts her way through this sea of sultry sound…

events.title: Dancing with DJ Laura Jayne
events.description:
DJ Laura Jayne blends her warm and spicy dance floor cocktails, mixing percussive electronic beats with synthy African vibes, Brazilian & UK funk, catchy garage music, and all-time classics with a twist of global bass and tribal dubby electronica. Her sets are an international journey through time and space, and she is sure to surprise you and take you on a trip through her musical selection of gems, getting you into a free flow of embodied explosion and playfulness.

Laura is known for her energizing global groove sets that she plays at festivals with DJ collectives such as the Bankra Bike Soundsystem and Moonshine Sound Collective, where live music is incorporated into the dance sets. As a co-founder of FreeFlow, she loves hosting festival stages with musician and DJ families at venues such as Tivoli, Paradiso, Couleur Cafe (Brussels), and Zwarte Cross.

events.title: Celine-Dewy, Jayati & Celestial Roots
events.description:
Celine-Dewy is a ceremonial dancer, musician, and naturopathic therapist from Amsterdam with Dutch and Indonesian roots. Guided by a deep connection to ritual, music, and nature, she creates spaces where art and healing meet. Through her project Celestial Roots, she weaves together ancestral wisdom, live performance, rhythm, and community, inviting participants into experiences of remembrance, connection, and transformation.
 On Sunday in the Krater, she will present a special Celestial Roots set with her daughter Jayati on the decks, merging rhythm, live percussion, voice, and performance into a vibrant celebration of connection and dance.

events.title: DJ Lotta Isopuro
events.description: Expect a relentless bass line holding you hostage on the dance floor, not allowing you even the slightest toilet break. Lotta Isopuro won’t cut you any slack before the collective sweat is dripping off the ceiling. She’ll pave your party path with a.o. peak time techno, indie dance and (progressive) house. And before you know what hit you, she has cleverly infiltrated her (carefully curated!) guilty pleasure bangers into the sequence. Lotta personally pre-tests all her tracks for danceability in the privacy of her living room. No groove? Disapprove! Why? Hips don’t lie.

events.title: Djembefola's
events.description:
Momo Touré

Momo Touré is the heart and soul of the drum circle at Ruigoord. For over 30 years, he has been the person who brings people together through music, using his djembe, his energy, and his gift for making everyone, from beginners to advanced players, feel part of the groove.

His roots are deeply embedded in traditional West African music. As a young man, he toured worldwide with the renowned African ballet in Guinea. He still carries this richness with him every week at Ruigoord.

Come, see, and participate!

Amier Sloot

Amier Sloot believes in the power of rhythm to bring people together. As a musician and community builder, he facilitates a drum circle every Saturday at Ruigoord alongside Momo, where everyone, regardless of experience, can immerse themselves in a shared groove and be fully present in the moment.

During Landjuweel, they (along with many other respected drummers) bring this energy: connection through music, together in the moment.

events.title: Kahmeya
events.description:
Kahmeya (DJ/Producer/Singer) – 'Let's create an energy where we can connect, share, and enjoy freedom together.'

Step onto the fiery dance floor of ‘Little firecracker’ Kahmeya. This Dutch DJ/producer & vocalist will take you on a rhythmic journey. Her DJ sets are filled with indie dance and organic/tribal house records. Connect with the driving grooves, ethnic influences, psychedelic elements, and live vocals of Kahmeya.…

events.title: Maringo Berenos
events.description: A musical journey Influenced by traditions from Africa, South-America and India - Maringo welcomes you to dive into a still point with his music from over the world. He approaches music like a teacher we can learn from; so we don’t only play music but actually learn from it. It makes us sing together, play together and stay together. Here on Landjuweel it’s always a joyful surprise where the journey takes us.

events.title: Anxiety as a Gift
events.description:
Farah Shretah, also known as Alien Mama (Kikvors Collectief)

"Anxiety as a Gift" is a ritual in which the subconscious becomes a shared journey of moving images created by you, designed by your attention. A body stands inside the circle, carrying hidden storms that surface as vivid visual landscapes.
What was meant to remain unseen begins to unfold: fragments, memories, symbols, and inner signals made public. Through the collective gaze, anxiety is transformed from a private shadow into a shared living image.

Farah Shretah is a Syrian transmedia artist, filmmaker, and XR producer based in Amsterdam. Her work spans across places, languages, and forms: born in Syria, shaped by her life in Ukraine, and later rooted in the Netherlands, where she completed her master's degree in visual culture and artistic research.

As the founder of Alien Mama Productions, Farah creates immersive works at the intersection of emotion, technology, AI, biofeedback, performance, and storytelling. Her practice transforms inner states such as fear, anxiety, and memory into shared audiovisual experiences, inviting audiences into poetic spaces where vulnerability becomes image, movement, and collective presence.

events.title: GUTEN ABEND
events.description: GUTEN ABEND is back, and once again he invites you into his pixelated virtual world, where he blasts nu-disco, synthwave, house and indie dance. There's a whole lot more KALEIDOVERSE left to explore, and GUTEN ABEND is your musical guide for this chapter of the journey. Recommended but not required: fast sunglasses and anti-gravity suits.

events.title: Los Ojos de la Rumba
events.description:
Los Ojos de la Rumba is a project based in Amsterdam, inspired by the rich traditions of Afro-Cuban music. Bringing together musicians from different backgrounds, it celebrates the rhythms, energy, and communal spirit at the heart of Cuban musical culture.

Blending rumba, son, Latin Jazz, and other Afro-Cuban influences with a fresh and contemporary approach, Los Ojos de la Rumba creates music that invites people to dance, connect, and share in the joy of live performance. Rooted in tradition while open to new sounds, the project keeps the spirit of the music alive through community, improvisation, and celebration.

events.title: AMYIR
events.description:
Amyir (Western Australia) is the fresh collaboration between married couple Amy Cooke, playing live didgeridoo, and Amir Galili, behind the decks. They perform a hypnotic live set merging the didgeridoos organic vibration with modern electronic sounds that grounds audiences and sends them into ecstatic motion.
 Amy brings over 20 years of didgeridoo experience to the project and is returning to Landjuweel after previously performing as part of the band Loonaloop. Together, Amyir combines live instrumentation, immersive electronic soundscapes, and tribal energy into a powerful musical journey.

events.title: The Grand Procession to the Chocolate Factory
Bon bon Vivant extravaganza
events.description:
We welcome you for an extravaganza closing of the Bon Bon Vivant stage.
 Step into a surreal Sunday night celebration where music, movement, cacao, the energy of guayusa and surreal storytelling come together in one immersive gathering.
 Throughout the festival the Bon Bon Vivant unfolds like a living chocolate ritual; stirring, melting, mixing, and transforming day by day. As the music fades at the stage just before midnight, a mysterious procession moves across the festival grounds. Drums, pots and pans echo, performers dance through the crowd, and we are all guided toward the church for the final chapter. Inside, the space transforms into the dreamlike Chocolate Factory: a world of rhythm, theatrical chaos and the awakening of the Cacao Goddess... An unforgettable closing ceremony where indulgence, imagination, and togetherness literally melt into one.
 Performance & music brought to you by Nadia Innocentia, Coen Aerts, Zevende Kleur , Daniel Liddiard & Leon Row

events.title: DJ Captain Spank
events.description:
Happy/melodic/up-tempo dance world-music.
 (Ska + Balkan + ElectroSwing + Tribal + Oriental + Rockabilly + Punk + Flamenco +++…) I often have musicians playing live during my sets to add an extra beat/sound (sax/percu/etc...)

events.title: Jam session with Kim Stevenson
events.description: Jamsession every night after the program on the Rebel Stage until festival's closing time. The jamsessions here are legendary. We have a group of very good musicians carrying the jam. Tell musicians to come and join us. Please don't just jump on stage first contact the session leader.

events.title: Jam olv Joao Vedana
events.description: João Vedana is a Multi-Instrumentalist and singer from Brasil. The guitar has been of great of importance in his life since the age of 9 and ever since the development never stopped. At 15 years old he started his professional career playing blues heavily inspired by Stevie Ray Vaughan , BB King and George Benson. Later on he released two original albums, João Vedana (2017) recorded in Brasil and Despertar (2022) recorded in The Netherlands via the Jazz Label Zennez Records, and has also worked as a sideman musician for many different projects. Now with 31 years old and after almost 8 years since moving to The Netherlands he lives in Rotterdam and plays concerts and festivals all over the country and also hosts Jam sessions occasionally. You can expect a strong load of Brazilian, Latin, Jazz and Funk influences on his music fueled by fiery solos, transcending grooves and spontaneous improvisation.

events.title: Purple Flame
events.description:
PURPLE FLAME IS A FEMINIST DJ, PRODUCER, AND MULTIMEDIA ARTIST FROM TBILISI, GEORGIA. ALONGSIDE PLAYING MUSIC, SHE IS A HUMAN RIGHTS ACTIVIST, ACTIVELY ADVOCATES FOR EQUAL RIGHTS, OFTEN INCORPORATING HER ACTIVIST MESSAGES INTO HER SETS AND USING MUSIC AS A PLATFORM FOR SOCIAL CHANGE.
 SHE ALSO EXPLORES THE HEALING POTENTIAL OF AUDIO FREQUENCIES, PRACTICING SOUND HEALING THROUGH HER COLLABORATIVE PROJECT, ‘’FORSUN’’.
 AS A MEMBER OF THE MULTICULTURAL COMMUNITY AND RECORD LABEL DOWNTEMPO RITUALS, PURPLE FLAME PERFORMS AT GIGS AND FESTIVALS THROUGHOUT GEORGIA AND ACROSS EUROPE YEAR-ROUND.

events.title: Foelie & Freude (Temple Closing Set)
events.description:
Folie & Freude might seem similar at first glance, but it was the music that truly brought them together. As they each pursued their own musical paths, the friends gradually discovered a shared passion for a distinctive downtempo sound. It was inevitable that these musical brothers would eventually combine their talents.

Prepare yourself for a set filled with raw, rolling basslines and infectious rhythms – somewhere between laid-back and full throttle. This is downtempo with soul, funky grooves, and an energy that will stay with you long after the last beat has faded away.

events.title: Meraki
events.description:
BORN IN BRAZIL, PEDRO SUGIURA MUSIC HAS A HUGE INFLUENCE OF HIS CONTINENT'S RICH DIVERSITY OF RHYTHMS. CURRENTLY LIVING IN AMSTERDAM, MERAKI HAS A UNIQUE IDENTITY AND PRESENTS A VERY ENERGETIC SOUND SIGNATURE. THE ARTIST HAS TRACKS RELEASED WITH NAMES LIKE DANDARA, SPANIOL, SALVADOR ARAGUAYA, GERRA G KOTOE AND OCTO SHARK
 BEGGINING HIS MUSICAL EXPERIENCE IN PSYTRANCE AS A TEENAGER LISTENER, AND LATER GETTING IN TOUCH WITH DOWNTEMPO AS A DJ, HE STARTED HIS JOURNEY WITH THE BRAZILIAN CREW SONIDO TROPICO IN 2016.
 ALWAYS SEEKING BALANCE BETWEEN THESE TWO CONTRASTING STYLES OF ELECTRONIC MUSIC, HIS MIXES ALWAYS RESULTS IN A FLOWY AND GROOVY ATMOSPHERE.
 MERAKI DID A EUROTOUR IN 2017 PLAYING IN 8 DIFFERENT COUNTRIES AS LISTED BELOW. DURING THE LAST YEARS IN BRAZIL, HE WAS CONSTANTLY PLAYING AMONG THE UNDERGROUND SCENE PARTIES AND FESTIVALS
 HIS LAST JOB WAS A RESIDENCY AT AMATA VENUE IN BRAZIL.
 Links:
 Facebook 
 Soundcloud 
 Instagram

events.title: Aggy Lou
events.description:
Do you know that feeling when you blink awake on a reckless Sunday morning? Sun kissing your skin, glitter stuck to your cheeks, sparkly souls dancing around, and nothing on the agenda but feeling the beat, sipping something cold, and vibing with the beautiful mess of it all. That’s Aggy’s rhythm. That’s the vibe she builds. With a flick of the wrist and a cheeky grin, Aggy spins sets that feel like sunshine with a side of sin. Funk-laced, disco-dipped, blues-soaked, and peppered with gospel vocals and sneaky edits, her sound doesn’t ask you to dance—it dares you not to.
 Old-school gems cozy up next to gritty urban textures and genre-blurring surprises that keep you guessing (and sweating). But be aware:
 Don’t get too comfy basking in the Sunday glow. Aggy’s not afraid to flip the switch—dragging you from sparkly daydreams into pulse-racing twilight.
 Think: tribal rhythms, deep dirty basslines, jacking house, and bold- as-hell energy that can shake the glitter right off your cheeks.…

events.title: Dj Chaakra Legal Alien
events.description: Jacques Povee is a music producer, promoter, and DJ known as DJ Chaakra. More than 25 years ago, while working as an environmental activist in Groenoord, he moved to Ruigoord to protect the village from the construction of the port. Together with more than 100 activists, he successfully halted the port's development in collaboration with various residents of Ruigoord. He is also known as the founder of Trance Orient Express and Solstice Festival.

events.title: Ed Noodle
events.description:
Hypnotizing melodies, bass and percussion blended in a deep tribal mix, that’s what gets Ed Noodle going. From downtempo sets at sunset or sunrise, to an energetic train rolling you into the night: in any case the goal will be to keep you dancing with prehistoric power.
 Ed is at his best when he takes the time to tell a story, relying on the right tracks and buildup for that exact place and moment in time. A craft he honed at gatherings like AfrikaBurn and Midburn, which fit his sound and energetic presentation like a glove. This brought him to places like The Gardens of Babylon Festival, Garbicz, GOAT in Portugal, KitKatClub and Acid Bogen at KATER in Berlin, Secret Brunch in London and Harmonizz Festival in Israel.
 Besides being a DJ, Ed also releases his own music on more and more labels and is co-founder of Wolvenroedel, a party pack that he started with YOИDI to get people dancing. And last but not least, he came up with The Noodle Lounge. It's a beautiful decorated stage at festivals where you can relax and shift back some gears, while you're sitting or lying down and enjoying some soothing downtempo music. And yes, some hot noodles to make the comfort level even bigger.

events.title: Mauricio Lobão with Jungle Tribal Dance Live Set
events.description:
Maurício Lobão is a musician and percussionist with over 40 years dedicated to the art of sound. His journey is marked by a deep connection with global rhythms, exploring diverse instruments and musical languages that cross cultures, territories, and states of consciousness.

Throughout his path, Maurício has traveled to many countries, absorbing influences and experiences that enriched his musical expression. His work seamlessly bridges electronic and acoustic music, creating unique soundscapes that converse with both ancestral and contemporary realms.

More than just performance, his music is a lived experience. Inspired by the elements of nature—earth, water, fire, and air—Maurício crafts rhythms that evoke connection, presence, and harmony. His sounds not only entertain but also invite introspection and inner balance.

Recognized for his sensitivity and versatility, he uses percussion as a universal language, capable of uniting people and awakening states of healing and expansion. His art is an invitation to feel, listen, and reconnect with the essence of life through sound.…

events.title: Night Drumming
events.description:
Feel free to bring your rhythm to life with Nightdrummers. Bring your shaker, tambourine, djembe, or other instrument and join us in the groove.

We create a welcoming Drum Circle where improvisation, connection, and playfulness are central. Listen, respond, experiment, and be amazed by what emerges.
Whether you've been playing for years or are simply curious: there's space for everyone. Bring your friends along, come groove, dance, and let the night resonate with our shared heartbeat.
Everyone is welcome. Together, we create the rhythm.

events.title: Mona Pirzad
events.description: Coming from a diverse artistic background, Mona’s music aims to explore both the profound and the lighthearted aspects within a single experience. Her musical style is clearly influenced by Berlin's deep house sounds, but also carries the influence of her Persian heritage and her experiences as a traveler.

events.title: Various DJs & Drums · Closing
events.description:
Maurício Lobão is a musician and percussionist with over 40 years dedicated to the art of sound. His journey is marked by a deep connection with global rhythms, exploring diverse instruments and musical languages that cross cultures, territories, and states of consciousness.

Throughout his path, Maurício has traveled to many countries, absorbing influences and experiences that enriched his musical expression. His work seamlessly bridges electronic and acoustic music, creating unique soundscapes that converse with both ancestral and contemporary realms.

More than just performance, his music is a lived experience. Inspired by the elements of nature—earth, water, fire, and air—Maurício crafts rhythms that evoke connection, presence, and harmony. His sounds not only entertain but also invite introspection and inner balance.

Recognized for his sensitivity and versatility, he uses percussion as a universal language, capable of uniting people and awakening states of healing and expansion. His art is an invitation to feel, listen, and reconnect with the essence of life through sound.…

profiles.name: Lach Workshop
profiles.description:
LAUGHING, LETTING GO & PLAYFUL CRAZINESS

Want to just let everything go? Then come and laugh with Sabine Rabbe!
Come to the laughter workshop during the Landjuweel Festival and completely lose yourself in laughter.
🤣 Laughing without reason
💃 Dancing, playing and letting loose
⚡ Recharging with festival energy
3 days of freedom, craziness and connection at Ruigoord.
A little rebellious, a little silly, and above all, lots of fun.
Leave your inhibitions behind and take your laughter with you.
Sabine Rabbe has been inspiring people for years with her contagious laughter, playfulness, and boundless energy.
As a laughter coach, performer, and connector, she shows you how liberating it is to simply let go and laugh.
With a mix of humor, spontaneity, and festival vibes, Sabine creates a space where fun, connection, and zest for life are central.
Laughter is her language, energy her trademark.
Come and laugh during the laughter workshop by Sabine Rabbe (aka "The Queen of Laughter") at Landjuweel this year!
As every year, we'll be letting loose again: a dose of humor, energy, and above all, lots of fun…

profiles.name: Klei Jezelf Blij Workshop
profiles.description: "The most relaxing hour of the entire Landjuweel event," one participant reportedly told his friend during the clay workshop, "was..."

profiles.name: Dj Deun Legal Alien
profiles.description:
Part of the DJ collective Legal Aliens.
 Time for a party!

profiles.name: Dj Fred Legal Alien
profiles.description:
Part of the DJ collective Legal Aliens.
 Time for a party!

profiles.name: Dj Chaakra Legal Alien
profiles.description: Jacques Povee is a music producer, promoter, and DJ known as DJ Chaakra. More than 25 years ago, while working as an environmental activist in Groenoord, he moved to Ruigoord to protect the village from the construction of the port. Together with more than 100 activists, he successfully halted the port's development in collaboration with various residents of Ruigoord. He is also known as the founder of Trance Orient Express and Solstice Festival.

profiles.name: Marble Workshop
profiles.description: Create flowing shapes with your favorite colors, let them merge on water, and transfer your unique creation onto paper. Each print is a surprise.

profiles.name: Psychedelic Tea Party
profiles.description: A vibrant blend of flavors, scents, and sounds, inviting you to indulge your senses and enjoy a delightful tea experience.

profiles.name: CLUBSYNTH : Tectonia
profiles.description:
CLUBSYNTH creates the beats that get you moving. On a stage filled with modular synthesizers, various artists perform LIVE electronic dance music. Hosted by Amsterdam-based synth builders, This is Not Rocket Science. Connecting the dancefloor.

22:00 – 23:00 Tectonia
This synth freak creates sonic waves and breaks them live into melodic pieces. Unique cinematic and melodic rave. A symphony of sound.

profiles.name: AMYIR
profiles.description:
Amyir (Western Australia) is the fresh collaboration between married couple Amy Cooke, playing live didgeridoo, and Amir Galili, behind the decks. They perform a hypnotic live set merging the didgeridoos organic vibration with modern electronic sounds that grounds audiences and sends them into ecstatic motion.
 Amy brings over 20 years of didgeridoo experience to the project and is returning to Landjuweel after previously performing as part of the band Loonaloop. Together, Amyir combines live instrumentation, immersive electronic soundscapes, and tribal energy into a powerful musical journey.

profiles.name: Bouke & Co · Free Clothes — Take Some, Bring Some
profiles.description:
Can you do better? Or are you just suffering from shopping urges? Come and enjoy FREE CLOTHING at Bouke & Co.

Cool clothes and accessories for free! New items every day! Unwanted styling advice included.
Feel free to bring your unwanted wardrobe too!
Everything is ️FREE️. Daily from 13:00-21:00 at Area 46.

profiles.name: Primo Disco
profiles.description: This "Disco dinosaur" has earned its reputation for getting crowds into a dancing state. Known for legendary pool parties and Christmas galas, Primo Disco makes no wasted minute, delivering the ultimate mix of disco, funk, and soul to satisfy your ears. Expect both familiar and new dancefloor fillers.

profiles.name: Johansson
profiles.description:
Johansson (real name Lars Johansson) has been active in the dance scene as a DJ, organizer, and producer since 1989. As the man behind well-known events such as Wooferland, Summer of Love, and a series of house classics parties, he is an established figure in the scene.

In addition to organizing events, music is generally the main driving force in his life. He began playing in a youth center at the end of the 1980s and quickly became infected with the dance virus in all its forms.

Expect an energetic mix of classics, house, techhouse, and techno during his set. With his upbeat DJ style, he knows how to keep the dance floor moving and ecstatic until the very last track.

profiles.name: Dougal Hallowes - Sitar Journeys
profiles.description:
The Story So Far…
 Dougal started his adventures in music as a bass player in rock bands on the Sydney scene in the early 90’s. After a move to London in ‘96 and a stint with Australian psychedelic band Om, found himself playing with Tribal Drift and spent the remainder of the decade touring around the globe.
 Tribal Drift featured Cyrung who was one of the pioneers of didgeridoo in Goa during the 80’s and was attributed to bringing the instrument into the world of trance in the early 90’s.
 In 2003 Dougal was a founding member of Australian band Loonaloop and featured as writer/performer/producer on their first 3 albums alongside many live shows.
 The journey with the sitar began with a gift from India. The sitar stayed in its box until Dougal found sitar guru Darshan Kumari in Amsterdam around 2012 and began to study.
 He has since opened the show for Dutch band My Baby with both sitar and didge on their Shamanaid tour in 2016/7 and slowly found the confidence to expand a 10 min set to a full show.
 More recently Dougal has played in 2022 and ‘23 on Ozora festival with 2 x 1.5 hour journeys from ambient to dance-floor and will again play 2 sets there in ‘24.…

profiles.name: Solarpunk & Synths
profiles.description:
workshop: positive future thinking with live ambient music

Experience your own solarpunk moment and create a positive future while enjoying calming synthesizer sounds.
Solarpunk is an utopian future filled with cheerful colors. An eco-social worldview with high-tech and sustainable energy for everyone. We explore this solarpunk world and practice positive future thinking together. We will draw/paint/cut/glue our own solarpunk vision of the future.
Workshop by media psychologist Priscilla Haring and synthesizers by This is Not Rocket Science.

profiles.name: DJ JamieNee
profiles.description: JAMIE NEE will be giving another impressive performance on Sunday at Area 46, so if you want to have a great time, then Area 46 is the place to be. Starting with some hard synth music, you can then dance like a stripper with a mix of cheeky Ghetto Tek and crazy Latin Tek – Que Locura!

profiles.name: CLUBSYNTH : noedels!
profiles.description:
CLUBSYNTH creates the beats that get you moving. On a stage filled with modular synthesizers, various artists perform LIVE electronic dance music. Hosted by Amsterdam-based synth builders, This is Not Rocket Science. Connecting the dancefloor.

23:00 – 01:00 hours!
Live beats, synths & vocals. These 3 artists combine Drum ‘n Bass with Funky Garage and snippets of Electro. Umami melodies with a spicy aftertaste.

profiles.name: Jolly Mango's
profiles.description:
The Jolly Mangos form a unique trio with electric accordion, guitar, and percussion. They bring an energetic mix of pop music and South American rhythms that quickly gets everyone moving. Each performance feels genuine and like a collaboration between the band and the audience, creating a magical atmosphere. Whether you prefer slow or fast tempos, nostalgic or vibrant – this day will keep you entertained.

Ruigoord is also (like for many others) the birthplace of The Jolly Mangos. It's where much of their music originated, where most of their performances took place, and they are now working on setting up their own rehearsal studio in a dedicated workshop.

profiles.name: The Wet and Naughty Show
profiles.description:
Amy Pratt (Kikvors Collectief)
 The Wet and Naughty Show, where 1900s burlesque meets amphibians and fish. What could burlesque have looked like if the performers had taken back their power after businessmen demanded the dancers show more skin? A theatrical performance inspired by amphibians and fish.
 As a costume designer, Amy Pratt creates work centered on colorful tactility and humor. Her practice explores ways to transform heavier themes into something humorous, while highlighting the bold and expressive handwriting of her costumes. Through performances and immersive experiences she invites the viewer to step into a new and vibrant reality.

profiles.name: The Rise, Life and Death of Don Quijote
profiles.description:
Thijs van de Loo – Theatermaker & Hessel Pijnaker, artist; combine their talents to bring five dolls to life, revolving around the theme of metamorphosis.

“Using rattan, fabric, inner tubes and found objects, and a good dose of intuition, we create soulful objects. Both have studied Vodun religions such as Ubuntu and draw inspiration from them.”

profiles.name: Misfit Valley
profiles.description:
Lekker Limbs (Kikvors Collectief)

You are invited into a surreal, childlike universe of strange dreamscapes & playful chaos. What begins as innocent play transforms into a collective experience, a strange mission that the audience must navigate together.

Lekker Limbs is a performance duo by Inês Certo and Sara Tompkins.
They are artists working across performance, visual art, music, movement, and digital media.
Since they met, a year ago at Landjuweel where they both performed, the artists have performed together another two times, at Fuite Cosmique in France and at Time is The New Space in Rotterdam.
Inês and Sara are particularly interested in exploring their own unique style, experimenting with participation, absurdity, visual storytelling, and live interaction.

profiles.name: Irene van Krimpen
profiles.description: Since the first time I visited Trance Oriënt (in March 2009), I was completely captivated by the atmosphere in the church, the Kabouterhuys, and around the fire. Since then, I've been a fan of Ruigoord and enjoy contributing to the atmosphere through dressing up, dancing, singing, and making music. I want to dance at the Kabouterhuys every evening as the sun sets. And on Sunday evenings, I go there to sing 3 traditional Irish songs before Krien Joseph starts playing.

profiles.name: The Shamanic Experience
profiles.description:
Since 2000, Piet Jan Blauw has focused on music performances in Europe and Mexico – Sol festival (Spain), Paradiso Amsterdam, Psy Fi festival (NL), Cumbre Tajin (Mexico).

The music is a blend of Prehispanic-Electro, Down Tempo, and Trance.
Blauw's instruments produce archetypal sounds. His music can never be played exactly the same way and demands improvisation. He creates soundscapes as he plays with his hands in invisible beams. This results in a pantheon of sounds that are ministered by samplers, sequencers, and processors. He drives his own aural sculptures to their limits in every performance. Technology enables him to push the boundaries, yet he always uses it as a tool for creation.

Blauw's vision is not just the mere manipulation of technological gadgets. Their use is meant to ultimately evoke an emotional response from the audience and inspire them to discover lost domains within themselves. His objects are of sophisticated craftsmanship.

Piet Jan Blauw has highly sensitive antennae which serve him. His multi-faceted work is strongly devoted to communication in a rapidly changing society.…

profiles.name: Embodied Systems
profiles.description:
Nadia Schambach is a Utrecht-based maker working through experimentation and material exploration. Within her practice, she investigates how objects can develop their own presence and how they relate to their surroundings.
 For this exhibition, she presents sculptures that emerge from the idea of creating an independent being: an organism that lives its own temporary life. The works are constructed from a metal skeleton covered with a latex skin. Through a mechanical system, the sculptures slowly fill with air and release it again. This rhythmic movement gives the impression that they are breathing, creating a sense of vulnerability and physical presence.
 The works invite us to reflect on the present moment. Like living beings, they change over time and eventually transform. The materials carry the traces of their existence: the latex discolors, stretches and ages, while the metal slowly collects signs of time and use. The sculptures are therefore not created to remain unchanged, but to experience their own temporary existence.
 Through their changing nature, the works explore the relationship between presence and impermanence.…

profiles.name: Sol Lysergic
profiles.description:
Jasper Veldkamp, aka Lysergic, creates interactive light and sound installations that use technology to pull people into the same space rather than push them apart. His practice spans projection mapping, building visual worlds, and designing reactive environments where the audience becomes the performer. With work shown from Berlin to Nairobi, his installations explore a single question: can technology bring people closer together instead of driving them apart? He is a member of kikvors, a collective of makers and performers across the Netherlands.
 Links:
 Website

profiles.name: Magische Maskerbrigade
profiles.description:
Magical Mask Brigade

The Magical Mask Brigade was born at Landjuweel 2025, where the Magical Moon and the Space Person jointly crossed the dike. This is a continuous evening performance that is also featured on the Sculpture Route – the Magical Mask Brigade surprises, amazes, and entertains wandering psychonauts and seekers of spiritual experiences. The brigade adapts to the atmosphere of the evening and thus weaves the party-goer into the mystical realms of the mind...

profiles.name: The Mirror Field
profiles.description:
‘The Mirror Field’ (The Mirror Field) is an interactive installation where we can both generate and capture light. In a world that is often shrouded in darkness, ‘The Mirror Field’ asks you to take control of the light. The installation demonstrates how small actions have consequences that extend beyond our own perspective. Visitors can experience this individually or together, creating new connections, focusing light, or changing its direction.

Atelier Hyphen (-) develops spatial projects at the intersection of art, design, and community building, with a focus on participation and imagination.

profiles.name: Boogie Movement
profiles.description:
Dreamy, you look up at the sky, where black and white patterns move against the blue. 60s Psychedelic movement? 70s Kinetic art? Remnants of 80s Memphis style? Or wildstyle forms from the 90s?
 The Boogie Bos moves outside of time, here dance joy is celebrated and underlined by movement in tall totems where shapes move along each other in moiré patterns, thus opening the doors to the dimensions of the eternal dancing gods and goddesses!

 Boogie Movement is a collective of DJs, musicians, builders, designers, performers, and dancers with a mission. From the Boogie Bos at the Landjuweel Festival to Boogie Ball in Ruigoord’s church, and the Boogie Crew behind it all, their events are all about pure joy, experimentation, wild fun, and unexpected musical surprises. When the Boogie Bos first debuted as a dance area at the Landjuweel Festival in 2022, it was clear that it fulfilled a need. Giving space to artists to reinvent the dancefloor together with the crowd, merging from the rebellious spirit of Ruigoord blending it with new generations towards the future.

profiles.name: Step out of your comfort zone!
profiles.description: "The Dragon's Egg," a performance by Luis Tromp, Rosa Schroevers, and Barbara Schroevers. Featuring music by Brainscapes (Alain Eskinasi). Makeup by Saskia Walter and Peter van Harmelen. Assistance provided by Jord Alkema.

profiles.name: Coen Aerts
profiles.description:
Coen Aerts has spent over two decades creating performances that exist in the charged space between performer and audience — encounters where neither party quite knows what happens next. Over the years he has developed around twenty alter egos, each one a different way of entering a room, making contact, and opening up the unexpected. Some are solo, some involve an ensemble; some speak, some don’t. What they share is a method: turning the audience from witness into accomplice.
