 His best-known acts, Mr. Vibes and The Buttlers, have appeared at festivals, museums, galleries, corporate events and theatre stages across the Netherlands and beyond. In recent years his practice has grown to include interactive theatre for young audiences, approached with the same rigour and care as his adult work. Every performance is built fresh for its context — the theme, the space, the audience in front of him — not as an afterthought, but as the principle the whole practice runs on.

profiles.name: Mariah Blue
profiles.description:
Mariah Blue (b. 1977 U.S.) is an Amsterdam-based interdisciplinary artist and instrument maker working across performance, new media, installation and sound, often in collaboration with other artists. Her practice explores technology through diverse materials, from stone-age pottery techniques to machine learning algorithms. Seeking to challenge definitions of technology that privilege only the complex, inaccessible, and expensive, Blue makes our embodied relationships with technology visible through experimentation and play. 
 She holds a bachelor's degree from the Rietveld Academie (2009) and an MFA from the Sandberg Institute (2019). Her work has been presented across the Netherlands and internationally in exhibitions, performances, and collaborative projects.

profiles.name: Flow Arts
profiles.description: The Fire Messiah and the Flow Crew will give you an unforgettable light show during the Beeldenroute, we look forward to seeing you there :) !

profiles.name: Jeroen Cuypers
profiles.description: Jeroen Cuypers is an artist, designer, and maker. He creates light installations, objects, and sets that combine recycled materials, old technology, and simple autonomous systems. For the "Sculpture Route," he uses a series of lanterns on the Bon Bon Vivant stage. Together, they create a warm, analog light world: a collection of beacons, shadows, and small nocturnal presences. Each lantern functions both as an individual entity and as a swarm.

profiles.name: Dreamweavers
profiles.description:
DreamWeavers arise from a Time and Space unknown, arriving in the present moment to explore the ever-unfolding possibilities of the different planes. The physical, psychological, emotional, energetical and spiritual merge. What does it mean to have a body? What wants to be expressed, released? How do we see ourselves and each other? Can we anchor our Dreams in reality? What does the Collective need most? During Landjuweel's Beeldenroute you are welcome to tap into these themes with us, navigating the conscious, sub-conscious and unconscious world.
 Core Team: Thieu Smeets, Luna Rosa, Leila van Wetten, Soufyan Sahraoui. Music: Cedric Vermue.

profiles.name: Floor Nagler
profiles.description:
Floor Nagler explores the boundaries and connections between body and environment, and how human existence manifests within a larger whole. Through textiles, ritualistic costumes, and collaborations with other artists, she creates sensory spaces where the personal and the universal meet, breaking through stagnation. Her work reveals new movement and space, and moves between mysticism and matter, between body and dream, between reality and what can still be seen.

Beyond art, Floor works through touch, breath, and presence in an experiential body practice that invites self-reflection and awareness. In both her practice and her art, she encourages the discovery of new space within oneself.

profiles.name: Collective in the Air
profiles.description: Be amazed by Collectief in the Air. During this sculpture route, these acrobats, illuminated against the dark background, present a series of spectacular magical performances. A visual fairytale high above the audience.

profiles.name: PUNXIANS COSMIC LIGHT ARKESTRA
profiles.description:
PUNXIANS COSMIC LIGHT ARKESTRA is a multidisciplinary ritual performance inspired by the ten teachings of Pu Xian Pusa (Samantabhadra). Each artist introduces their own sonic interpretation through their instrument and performance style, accompanied by evolving chants and layers of drone, rhythm, light, and visual transformation. The collective creates a temporary ceremonial world where the audience and performers become part of the same energetic field. PUNXIANS COSMIC LIGHT ARKESTRA explores the intersection between ancient rituals and futuristic expressions.

Participating Artists:
Matty (Spacebeest) – Guitar / cosmic effects / spatial textures
Tjebbe van der Kooij (Storm op de Maan) – Chants / electronic drones / atmospheres
Denz de Kroon – Self-made bass instruments
Jascha (Kavia) van der Hoorn – Trumpet / live effects / ritual melodies
Jimi (Elektrovolt) Hellinga – Modular drone textures
Remy Simon (Erres) – Drums / djembe / ceremonial rhythms
Bianca Runge and Franka de Jong – Make-up Deco

profiles.name: Nuki Nibu
profiles.description: Nuku Nibu is a ritualistic performance that takes the audience on a journey into a living universe of music, singing, and movement. In a cosmic dance, ancient forces meet in a continuous interplay between chaos and harmony, attraction and release. Mystical figures appear and disappear, while an immersive soundscape and live music create a world where everything is interconnected.

profiles.name: Nadia Innocentia
profiles.description:
Nadia Innocentia is a costume and avant-garde fashion designer, performance artist, and maker whose practice investigates fashion as a living, experiential, and relational medium. Through interactive performances, embodied experiences, and sensory-driven interventions, she creates encounters that challenge perceptions of identity, beauty, value, and social conventions. 
 Her work uses the body as both material and meeting point a place where personal stories, social expectations, and collective imagination intersect. By combining costume, scent, ritual, and participation, Innocentia creates immersive situations that invite audiences to become active participants rather than passive observers. Her performances explore the tension between attraction and discomfort, intimacy and distance, individuality and belonging.
 For the Beeldenroute, Innocentia will present an interactive performance at the Bon Bon Vivant area, where chocolate becomes a medium for exploring identity, intimacy, and sensory memory. She will create personalized chocolate perfumes based on the unique body scent of each participant, transforming something deeply personal and invisible into a sensory artwork.…

profiles.name: Neon strings
profiles.description: Under blacklight, weighted strings spin in orbit, light trails, shifting shapes, spirals, loops, waves. A collective ever changing drawing made of light, gravity, and touch. Come and add your piece.

profiles.name: We're In It Together! · Verwonderij Foundation
Coco Coquelicot story telling
profiles.description:
Passionate, energetic and fearless: Coco Coquelicot's unique mix of club sounds combines warm grooves with powerful basslines. As a true music enthusiast, Coco delves deep into researching the best tracks from all musical corners. Her sets are infused with her commitment to fighting for equality. By giving marginalized voices a platform, she provides a safe haven for everyone who doesn't always have one. As co-founder of Relate Radio and organizer of Butts, the highly acclaimed queer club night in East, Coco actively promotes the progress of the Groningen music scene. Her eclectic influences come together in a rich and percussive mix that resonates through your body.
In 2023, Coco became part of Hit the North (ESNS) under the guidance of Isis van der Wel. She received a nomination for the Groninger Culture Prize and has already performed at Lowlands, Wildeburg, IsBurning, Milkshake, Eurosonic and ADE, DGTL.

profiles.name: Gente Quente
profiles.description:
Gente Quente is a Dutch DJ who effortlesslly weavesrhythm, passion and style into everything she creates and shares.
 Her sets are vibrant and uplifting, blending soulful, melodic sounds with percussion-driven rhtyhms. Rooted in Afrohouse and deephouse, her sound is rich with African-inspired grooves, warm Brazilian influences and deep and flowing house and gospel elements.
 Hard to box in, yet recognizable Gente Quente, defined by her signature blend of influences.

profiles.name: Leon Row
profiles.description:
Leon Row has been shaping sound for over 14 years, a journey that started somewhere between smoky after-hours sessions, underground dance floors and a growing obsession with collecting records that simply did something to people.
 What started in the club scene at seventeen quickly grew into organising events and eventually stepping behind the decks himself. What began as a love for digging records and soundtracking afterparties soon turned into a full-blown obsession that never left.
 Recently, Leon expanded his universe with the launch of Bon Bon Vivant, a traveling circus and stage concept where music, performance, theatre, energetic chocolate and playful chaos collide. A colourful extension of the energy and imagination that already runs through his sets.
 Today, Leon moves effortlessly between styles without ever losing his own signature. His sets drift f rom eclectic grooves and slow-burning rhythms into steamy indie dance, rolling house, deep hypnotic minimal and unexpected left turns. Always layered, emotional and deeply alive.
 What ties it all together is intuition. Leon doesn’t force a set, he reads the room, taps into the moment and lets the energy unfold naturally.…

profiles.name: Leon Row B2B Shimon
profiles.description:
What do you get when you put this Dutchman and this Frenchman behind the decks?
 A Hotel California. "You can check out any time you like, but you can never leave”.
 Yes, you read that right. If you’re into adventurous electronic music, this is your spot for Saturday night.
 Two friends, two selectors, brothers from another mother, reading each other, reading the room, and taking us somewhere nobody planned to go.
 Leon brings 14 years of underground intuition to the table. His sets are emotional, unpredictable and always rooted in the moment. Moving between eclectic grooves, rolling house, deep hypnotic minimal and unexpected turns you never saw coming, but that always feel exactly right.
 Born and raised in Paris, Shimon brings his own unique energy, shaped by his residency at Kater Blau in Berlin and a catalogue of over 30 releases on labels such as Kiosk ID and Kanto Record. Expect throbbing rhythms, hypnotic melodies and basslines that hit somewhere deep in your chest.
 This doesn’t feel like a b2b. It feels like two people who know each other deeply, connected by an effortless understanding.
 Welcome to Hotel California.…

profiles.name: Misjael Monteban 'Love Songs'
profiles.description:
"he spoke in a voice of velvet 
 six rabbits with fluffy white ears 
 pearls where his teeth, bright diamonds his eyes 
 a million white flowers in a field in the sky 
 the children stayed children and they lived in their dreams"

profiles.name: Henk and the Jet Hunter: a children's disco for all ages!
profiles.description:
BIG tunes for KIDS of ALL ages. Because Henk and the Beam Hunter are not afraid. Nooo no.
 They swoosh passed the beaten tracks with such a rapid pace, that might make you wonder: How than?
 Make it yourself uncomfortable and dance like it’s your first time

profiles.name: Motorknüller: Madmotormiquel & Heimlich Knüller
profiles.description: Since the magical 1980s, the species known as Raver focused on creating the perfect dance move to express on this dance floor of life. Around the same time, Madmotormiquel — a young boy from the limited-travel zone of West Berlin — decided he wanted to find a sound that knew no limits to their dedication. Oh shit, another DJ is born. Armed with an expanding taste in classic punk rock and hip-hop, soon he was led into the world of powerful beats that the future-forming kids around him loved so much. He met them in the church of acid techno, big-beat, and drum and bass. In these places, he jockeyed the discs for many weekends until it was clear he needed to have a say on the beats himself. Here, under the light of a crescent moon, a new producer was born. By now, the Berlin Wall was well and truly down, and you could even buy small pieces of it from the shop. This meant his home city was filled with more space than the ravers knew how to sing and dance about. With that, they squatted its old factories and made techno rituals in the neighboring fields.…

profiles.name: Thara Bergen: Coco Blance Hip Hop Set
profiles.description:
Thara Bergen brings you a crisp, elevated and down-right uplifting house & techno experience.
 Growing up in Ibiza in the nineties and influenced by the house mix tapes of the era, her sound is a perfect mix of warm island vibes and the innovative city rhythms of Amsterdam - the place she now calls home.
 What does this meeting of worlds create? A clean compound of bassy sounds, snappy vocals and groovy beats.
 One of the founding curators and pioneers of the infamous concept - Project Sugar @ The Sugar Factory, Amsterdam. Thara was one of the resident DJs at this unique venue where the creativity of her and her fellow residents was projected for 5 Years.
 More recent highlights include her gracing the booth in venues such as Shelter, Thuishaven, Atelier and Sissi’s. Not to forget her debut for Isburning on Jan 1st, where she has been a familiar face in the booth since.
 In equal parts a digital and vinyl talent, with an eclectic vinyl collection and over 6 years of parties under her belt - this is one DJ to watch…
 EXTRA - inspired by…

profiles.name: Some Chemistry: Barbecue Space Station Set
profiles.description:
Some Chemistry, born into a family that traveled the world and found music to be their closest form of spirituality, is a new age nomad, and music is his first home. After leading an amateur drum ensemble in Burkina Faso, he became a guitarist for several indie-punk bands throughout The Netherlands, and eventually became captivated by the sound of Amsterdam's underground clubbing scene in the mid-2000s.

Intrigued by the infinite possibilities of a PA system and having developed a particular taste for alternative house and techno, he immersed himself into becoming a true ambassador of dance in Amsterdam by the early 2010s. If he is not in the studio or DJing, he is searching for new electronic talent and sharing his newfound discoveries by curating well-respected events in Amsterdam-based clubs such as Trouw, Studio 80, or Marktkantine, while also being invited to play at contemporary cutting-edge venues like Shelter, de Loft, Awakenings, Time Warp, Wildeburg, Welcome to the Future, Soenda, or often at Berlin's Wilde Renate.

profiles.name: Ari's Classical Radio Show: Can You Händle It?
profiles.description:
Arinde Jonker
 Arinde Jonker (1990) hopped to the beats of Bach’s music in her playpen before she could stand. And she never stopped. Since 2020, Arinde has been making (radio)programs in which she presents classical music in an accessible and often alternative way. She makes you listen to classical music in a way you never did before. Classical music is groovy, revolutionary and sexy. Curious? Come and listen.
 Program:
 Do you want something other than hard beats or loud guitars to start your Landjuweel weekend? 2 hours of classical music, along with good stories about Mozart, Beethoven, and other classical music stars. They are wild and juicy (and child friendly). Come and listen to great music and good stories. This will be the perfect start of the weekend.

profiles.name: Shimon presents Radio Border: Sunbelt Sounds
profiles.description:
MUSICIAN & FRENCH PRODUCER-DJ, SHIMON, IS FROM THE CITY OF PARIS, AND HAS HAD A HUGE INFLUENCE ON THE EUROPEAN ELECTRONIC MUSIC SCENE THROUGH HIS ARTIST RESIDENCY AT KATER BLAU CLUB IN BERLIN.

WITH THRILLING MINIMAL RHYTHMS AND HYPNOTIC MELODIES THAT TAKE LISTENERS TO ANOTHER WORLD, HIS WORK CAPTURES THE PURE SPIRIT OF ELECTRONIC MUSIC. SHIMON'S EXPERTISE IS DEMONSTRATED BY HIS ABILITY TO SMOOTHLY COMBINE MANY ELECTRONIC MUSIC SUBGENRES, RESULTING IN A UNIQUE SOUND THAT IS BOTH ALLURING AND SONICALLY RICH. EACH SONG TAKES THE LISTENER ON A JOURNEY THROUGH SOUND, WITH COMPLEX SYNTHESIZER LAYERS, RUMBLING BASSLINES, AND HYPNOTIC BEATS. HIS MUSIC HAS AN IRRESISTIBLE BEAT THAT WILL GET EVERYONE ON THE DANCE FLOOR MOVING.

SHIMON'S VISION IS IN HIS ABILITY TO WRITE MUSIC THAT CUTS ACROSS DIVISIONS AND CONNECTS WITH A WIDE RANGE OF LISTENERS. HIS TUNES HAVE THE ABILITY TO INSPIRE A VARIETY OF EMOTIONS AND CREATE AN IMMERSIVE EXPERIENCE, REGARDLESS OF WHETHER YOU'RE AN EXPERIENCED HOUSE MUSIC ENTHUSIAST OR A CASUAL LISTENER.

SHIMON STANDS OUT AS AN INNOVATOR IN A WORLD OVERRUN BY ELECTRONIC MUSIC.…

profiles.name: Anna Babélva: Soft Soirrée Closing
profiles.description:
The sound of Anna Babèlva (Amsterdam) can best be described as a cheeky ride in a pink Cadillac. She takes you on a journey with a cocktail in hand, allowing you to transcend into a multi-coloured universe of passionate encounters.

When she is performing during one of her captivating shows, she blends musical treasures into a carefully curated selection of sound. Babèlva is one of those rare gems you discover in an underground club, a well-organized event, or at one of the great festivals of our time.

As she is always seeking new ways to express herself, you will find her working on a multitude of projects. As the founder of the female collective The Portal, she advocates for the importance of community within the creative sphere, supporting female colleagues in the music, performance, and art scene.

profiles.name: The Grand Procession to the Chocolate Factory
Bon Bon Vivant chocolate tasting
profiles.description: Come and taste our vegan Guayusa Chocolate Bar. It gives you energy in a kind, smooth way. No jitters, no crash, just clear focus and happy vibes. Perfect to start your Landjuweel day.

profiles.name: Aron Friedman’s Psychedelic Mirror Ball
profiles.description:
If you’ve ever wondered what the inside of a mirror ball sounds like, you’ve come to the right place. Space disco, funk, rock, and other mind-expanding dance tunes morph into a psychedelic groove to make you move. Easy on the Kool-Aid though, it’s laced with... Oh, never mind, too late now. Buckle up and enjoy the ride!
 Links:
 Soundcloud 
 Instagram

profiles.name: Velvet Tongues
profiles.description: Velvet Tongues is the meeting place of poetry and pulse. Andrej and Isis weave electronic and classical music with spoken word, opera and immersive ritual, creating performances that blur the line between concert, ceremony and dream. Drawing from mythology, alchemy, philosophy and the strange theatre of modern life, they create immersive experiences that seduce the senses before quietly unsettling the soul.

profiles.name: Battle for the Sacred Bean by: Bente Kruger & Adhem Kouta
profiles.description: There's a power vacuüm in the cacao kingdom. Two cacao warriors have taken arms against each other in a fight to the sweet chocolate death. Who will be crowned the new cacao king or queen?

profiles.name: The Cacao Coronation by: Bente Kruger & Adhem Kouta
Le Miracle du Vivant by: Marchal
profiles.description: The human experience is full of uncertainties, great mysteries, and unanswered questions that may never be resolved. Even if we encounter each other, it's likely that won't change anything, but perhaps a new perspective can be found.

profiles.name: The Seventh Colour presents: The Floating Fantasy
profiles.description: Violet aka ‘De Zevende Kleur’ is a performing artist. Her work reveals around body and sound, tension and space, image and imagination.

profiles.name: yak x mari posa
profiles.description: yak x mari posa are on a mission to reconnect with the dance floor. live percussion riding a wild wave of progressive trance, caribbean bouyon and breaky electronics. expect moving bodies all around.

profiles.name: Hibotep
profiles.description:
“Every set is a ritual, every drop is a revelation.”
 “Hibotep doesn’t follow trends—she rewrites the map.”
 “One of the fiercest live selectors on the continent.”

Hibotep is a sonic warrior, genre-shifter, and force of nature who defies every mold. Based between Kampala and the cosmos, she channels chaos into power, building hypnotic worlds where techno, grime, gqom, hip hop, industrial, and experimental frequencies collide with East African energy and deep ancestral resonance.

Of Somali origin, DJ, producer, filmmaker, and fashion alchemist, Hibotep isn’t just multidisciplinary—she’s multidimensional. Whether performing at CTM, Dekmantel, Sonar or Nyege Nyege, her sets are wild rides through time and space: unpredictable, emotionally charged, and spiritually grounded. She doesn't just play tracks—she casts spells. In 2019 she joined the We Are Europe roster and became the face of Native Instruments traktor and became resident at the Moheim Triennale.

As part of the Nyege Nyege collective, Hibotep represents the cutting edge of underground African electronic music. Her work reclaims narrative, dismantles borders, and remixes reality itself.…

profiles.name: Carmela Collective
profiles.description:
Carmela Collective is an emerging collaborative group of culture makers from Mexico, Chile, and Colombia who have migrated to The Netherlands, and whose purpose is to create meaningful encounters that celebrate and share the richness of Latin American diversity with local, migrant, and international communities in The Netherlands.

Tresdé
Tresdé is a visual artist, filmmaker, and selector. With her SadPink sessions, she creates a dreamy & melancholic sonic experience infused with her memories growing up in Cali, Colombia, to the sounds of salsa, cumbia, and chucucú. She taps into the nostalgia of yearning for echoes of the mountains, as a now migrant in the flatlands.

Ukab
Ukab is a curator and DJ part of Carmela Collective and other projects like Cumbia Noord Collective. He will bring his selections of Cumbia pirata, cumbia rebajadas, and Kumbia con K to this edition of Radio Chenin, going back-to-back with Paulah, a curator, DJ, selector, and owner of the project Nofutureberlin, a label that focuses on pushing the diaspora, especially the Latin American diaspora, into the scene.

profiles.name: Yanic
profiles.description:
Years of travelling the world left their mark on YANIC — in the breadth of what he carries, and the depth of how he listens.
 His sets move between worlds. Sometimes slow and juicy, sensual and grounded. Sometimes wild, ecstatic and beautifully chaotic. Sometimes all of it in the same night. For eight years he has been resident DJ and curator at De Lievelinge, a place that became his musical home. Now he's ready to be heard beyond it.
 YANIC is spreading his wings — and the dancefloor follows.

profiles.name: Wormhol DJ
profiles.description: Wormhol’s (aka JAMIE NEE) first love is ITALO DISCO so you can expect nothing less. Goofy moves, happy faces, purple and green; bring it and boogie to the hysterical synthy sounds of the 80’s. However along the way we travel towards an unkown destination and we can end up in an unexpected musical dimension, just follow the WORM. Since years Jamie is involved whit Ruigoord in many corners, making art pieces, building areas, DjJ-ing at the legendary Balonnenfeest and working together with different Ruigoorders, now very delighted to play for the first time in the Boogie Bos.

profiles.name: Doris Fiore
profiles.description: Doris Fiore blends vintage underground (deep)house with fresh electronic sounds. Inspired and driven by her roots in drumming and love for jazz, funk and soul, her rhythm-focused music is deep, straight-forward, soulful, edgy and sensual. With her sets she takes the listener to a warm and rooted feeling. With house as her main genre, she ventures into related styles, always keeping an analogue, human touch.

profiles.name: Abelix
profiles.description:
"He absorbed a magical elixir, infused with countless musical styles. When he starts to play, the elixir takes control."
 For many years, ABELIX has been active in the underground Hip Hop and rave scene, playing everywhere from squats and warehouse parties to live beat sessions and freestyle rap events. Driven by a curiosity for diverse sounds and rhythms, he has developed an eclectic style that seamlessly blends genres and cultures on the dancefloor.
 His sets move effortlessly between Afrobeat, global grooves, breakbeats, UK bass, and jungle, creating journeys that are both energetic and unpredictable.
 In recent years, ABELIX has been sharing his wide-ranging selections in cafés and intimate venues across the city. He is a resident DJ at Café Belgique, Heilig Leven, and Checkpoint Charlie, performing both solo and as one half of the duo Follow The Tracks together with Comcap.
 Expect a genre-spanning trip guided by the mysterious elixir itself.

profiles.name: MariGlue
profiles.description:
MariGlue is a Colombian DJ based in Amsterdam. She grew up in Bogotá surrounded by Latin and tropical music and she still loves dancing to it. Her sets mix nostalgic originals and new discoveries from the alternative side of Latin American and tropical sounds: cumbia, champeta, salsa, Afro-Colombian rhythms and other folkloric music from Colombia and Latin America, with Caribbean and African influences. She also has a soft spot for the growing wave of electronic music inspired by this folklore, where producers and artists draw from ancestral and traditional sounds to create something that feels both rooted and alive.
 She cares a lot about curating a vibe she herself would love to hear and dance to. Behind the decks, staying connected to the people on the floor matters to her, she wants to feel part of the dancing together with everyone else, not separate from it. Expect music to chill to in the sun, with breezy and groovy moments that slowly warm you up or suddenly make you move.

profiles.name: Laura Jayne
profiles.description:
DJ Laura Jayne blends her warm and spicy dance floor cocktails, mixing percussive electronic beats with synthy African vibes, Brazilian & UK funk, catchy garage, and all-time classics, with a twist of global bass and tribal dubby electronica.

Her international journey through time and space is sure to surprise you and take you on a trip through her musical selection of gems, getting you into a free flow of embodied explosion and playfulness.

Laura is known for her energizing global groove sets that she plays at festivals with DJ collectives such as the Bankra Bike Soundsystem and Moonshine Sound Collective, where live music is incorporated into the dance sets.

As a co-founder of FreeFlow, she loves hosting festival stages with musician and DJ families at venues such as Tivoli, Paradiso, Couleur Cafe (Brussels), and Zwarte Cross.

profiles.name: Sjef Rolet
profiles.description:
With over 20 years of experience as a DJ and producer, Sjef Rolet is renowned for his ability to move people in any setting. As a passionate dancer, he has delved into countless rhythms and musical styles – particularly those from Africa and the Americas.

Together with Jo Bissa, he forms the duo UMOJA, beloved for their dubby, tropical sound that has been traveling around the world for over ten years. Their work has received support from influential labels and platforms such as Wonderwheel, Hot Casa, Analog Africa, Multi Culti, Mawimbi, and INI Movement, the Haarlem-based label where Sjef is closely involved.

As a solo DJ, he is an tireless crate digger – someone who draws endlessly from the world's musical archives, but also stays on top of the latest music trends. Hidden gems, modern underground sounds, or idiosyncratic versions of global hits – all sounds that transcend everyday reality.

profiles.name: Isaie
profiles.description:
Isaie is a selector, curator, and long-time collector whose sets effortlessly connect house, disco, italo, Afro rhythms, techno, high-NRG, world music, and forgotten dancefloor gems. Over the years, he has released music under various aliases on respected labels including Rush Hour Music, Compost Records, XXX Record Label, Voyage Direct, Home Taping Is Killing Music and Kolour Recordings.

He is also the founder of In The Black Square, an online streaming radio station and event platform dedicated to adventurous underground music and cultural exchange.

Approaching DJing as musical storytelling rather than genre boundaries, DJ Isaie creates journeys that move organically between deep and percussive house, obscure disco, driving techno, Afro rhythms, high-NRG music, and rhythm-driven music from across the globe. Blending contemporary club music with cultural influences, rare edits, and forgotten classics, he creates dancefloors that feel both timeless and unpredictable.…

profiles.name: Link & Herr Alt
profiles.description: Maya Link & Herr Alt create electronic music in Dutch, at the intersection of performance, poetry, and club music. With honest lyrics, deep basslines, and energetic beats, they take you on a life-affirming rave concert full of anger, love, and everything in between. Their live show, Galbi (Arabic for heart and soul), is a danceable journey through the heart. Let yourself be swept away.

profiles.name: Stefnitz
profiles.description:
Activate the smoke machine! Let the disco balls fly! Come and see: Stefnitz jumps onto this Landjuweel booth in the cozy Boogie Bos for an extended solo DJ set! It's a special opportunity because since she brought life to this dancefloor in 2022, she has never played the wheels of steel on her own. Time for a 3-hour journey through her collection of quirky, wonky, happy, spacey, weird, punchy, edgy, bubbly, and dubby electronica!

 Get the smoke machines ready! Turn on the disco balls! Come right up: this Landjuweel Stefnitz jumps behind the cozy Boogie Bos booth for an extended solo set!
 It's a first because ever since she brought life to this dancefloor in 2022, she has never played the wheels of steel with only her own two hands. High time for a 3-hour journey through her collection of quirky, wonky, happy, spacey, weird, punchy, edgy, bubbly, and dubby electronica!

profiles.name: Jazz Dahlia
profiles.description:
From hard-hitting UKG and bass-heavy club bangers to nostalgic hip-hop anthems, the Amsterdam-based Jazz Dalia has been making waves in the Dutch scene for a while now. She’s known for her high-energy sets that fuse dub, breakbeat, techno, drums and bass-driven club music. With a sound rooted in rhythm and heavy low-end, she draws inspiration from her Surinamese heritage to create dynamic and percussive journeys on the dancefloor. When on stage, her presence is driven by emotions, movement, and a deep, resonant sound that works contagiously on every audience.
 With experience ranging from steamy clubs like Garage Noord, Sissi’s & NAR to the wide fields of festivals such as Best Kept Secret & Wildeburg, Jazz has proven she knows how to get people moving - no matter the en- vironment. Beyond the conventional stages, she showcases more of her diverse sound and explores the edges of underground records at renowned radio platforms across the Netherlands, including Radio Radio, Operator & Relate.…

profiles.name: Stoievaar x Vrolijke Vogelaar
profiles.description: Soon, as the morning dew evaporates from the sun-drenched trees, they descend into the Boogie Forest. She, Stoievaar, stoic and serene; he, the Cheerful Birdwatcher, who always manages to conjure a smile. Together, they explore every nook and cranny of this planet, singing its praises, collecting melodies from lush forests and majestic mountain peaks, possessed by the pure poetry of the strange bird and his accompanying song. Their precious collection is safely stored in a magical instrumentarium that captures the melodies without ever depriving the strange bird of its voice. For this unique treasure, these music-loving virtuosos will come to share their collection in the Boogie Forest, sharing with us their strangest and most joyful grooves, opening up the sky for all those who are ready to take flight and free the Paradise Bird within themselves!

profiles.name: Roswitha B2B Carmen
profiles.description:
Carmen & Roswitha are a DJ duo from Amsterdam who found each other through their shared love for the early days of minimal techno. Their sound can best be described as rhythmic, deep and driven by funky basslines. On Saturday afternoon, they will cover the Boogie Bos stage with a warm blanket of clean clicks, cutting snares, mysterious vocals and bass-filled kicks.
 Roswitha has previously played at festivals such as Into the Woods, Wildeburg, and Garbicz, and also produces music with her electronic live-act Roswitha Nash. Carmen started her journey in electronic music more recently, finding her sound in playing at many local raves and festivals such as Georgie’s Wundergarten and Amuse festival. From their studio in Amsterdam Noord, they are constantly developing their individual and communal sounds.
 Together they founded ATINATI, a minimal inspired event series. Their inspiration came from a trip to Tbilisi, the capital of Georgia, where they were captivated by the city’s progressive and activist music scene. The dance floor is a space where norms are shed and a new world is created. In Georgian, ATINATI means ‘the first ray of light in the darkness’ or ‘the first knowledge’.…

profiles.name: TAJ
profiles.description: TAJ is a vinyl enthusiast, DJ, and producer. He enriches the nightlife of Amsterdam with an eclectic DJ style that features percussion, obscure sounds, and exotic music. TAJ constantly challenges both listeners and dancers through changing rhythms and quick transitions – expect the unexpected.

profiles.name: Prince Pasensi
profiles.description:
Celebrated as one of the innovators of a new sound emerging from Amsterdam, Prince Pasensi’s infectious rhythms are moving dancefloors worldwide.
 Known for his unique sound that seamlessly blends traditional Surinamese music with breakbeats, bass/club, house, and techno, Prince Pasensi is carving out a distinctive place in the global electronic music scene.
 Performing at festivals like Down the Rabbit Hole and clubs such as Radio Radio and Lofi, Prince Pasensi’s sets have been described as a journey through a world of rhythms where he aims to showcase the unique creolized musical heritage of the African diaspora through music and dance.
 While producing from his home studio, his electrifying blends have resonated with partygoers worldwide and have been released on prestigious labels such as Berlin based NoFuture and GLXY Records.

profiles.name: Edgar Ramiro
profiles.description:
Born and raised in Amsterdam and coming from an mixed-race family, there where always various musical influences present growing up.
 But it was around the Age of 17, when Edgar Ramiro started collecting Vinyl, Cassette tapes, CDs etc.
 After an holiday with family in Curaçao, he inherited a collection from his aunt with records and books mainly focused around the black diaspora. While cleaning up and listening to all these records, he discovered many hidden gems and got inspired by the wide variety of music within this collection..
 From this moment the urge to discover and learn more about the history of this music became bigger, and eventually lead to sharing this music with others. He started playing records at the legendary Cafe Belgique, Pacific Parc and Red light Radio and has gradually became a well known figure in Amsterdam’s night life.
 Next to his monthly residency at Cornerstore, you can find him playing at clubs and festivals such as Dekmantel Selectors, DTRH, Lentekabinet, Wildeburg, Paradiso, Garage Noord, Brilliant Corners, Unit 58, NAR to name a few.. Curating became the next step and this is when his own club night “Melting Pot” was born.…

profiles.name: For Sun
profiles.description:
A duo by Purple Flame & Ninapicks that blends everything heard and unheard. 
 ForSun is a tender act of resistance and healing, created with love. 
 For sun 
 For love 
 For peace 
 For music

profiles.name: Mayapapaya b2b Dragonslayer
profiles.description: Four beats. One mission. And everything that happens when you put these artists behind the decks. An electro-tropical collision of Afro, dancehall, reggaeton, cumbia, and more booty-shaking bass and beats. Hot rhythms that shake along the equator and make you move your hips.

profiles.name: Katie Bitchcoin
profiles.description:
Techno? Electroclash? Lipstickwave? Cryptopunk?
 Katie Bitchcoin is trapped in a boring, monotonous ironing-and-vacuuming office with her toxic partner and wonders if Bitchcoin can be her escape.
 Are you investing in Bitchcoin? WAGMI!


 Techno? Electroclash? Lipstickwave? Cryptopunk?
 Katie Bitchcoin is stuck in a beige nightmare of ironing, cooking, and vacuuming for her toxic partner.
 One shiny day, the Three Bitchcoins arrive! They are on a mission to save Katie and get her to invest in Bitchcoin.
 Escape the house. Join the cult. Will you invest? WAGMI!

profiles.name: Steve Rachmad
profiles.description:
Since his earliest days in the scene, Amsterdam’s electronic music pioneer Steve Rachmad has created a legion of monikers to cover the broadness of his compositions. Black Scorpion, Tons of Tones, Rachmad Project, Scorp, Ignacio, Dreg, Parallel 9, Sterac Electronics, STERAC; they all represent a different facet of Rachmad’s music. Nowadays, Rachmad focuses on the latter three and his birth-name.
 Listing the countless releases, remixes, labels, clubs and festivals with one of Steve’s names on it, would quickly turn into a long catalogue of names, places and dates. Impressive as that list may be, let’s not go there. The real essence lies in the music itself — something best explored through the many recordings and DJ sets that are out there.
 Because as Miles had his horn and Pablo took to canvas, Steve has an unequalled gift for (almost physically) connecting with every thinkable drum machine and synthesizer out there. If the man had kids, boys would listen to Roland or Robert (Moog), and a girl would surely be named Linn, after the machine made famous by Prince and others in the 80’s.…

profiles.name: Kids' Camping
profiles.description:
Friday 09.00-13.00 Opening Ceremony & Crafting
 Creating mandalas and altars using natural materials
 Making insect masks
 Saturday 09.00-13.00 Opening Ceremony & Movement
 Building insect houses from natural materials
 Wayang Kulit workshop (shadow puppets)
 Sunday 09.00-13.00 Opening Ceremony with soft music
 Storytelling
 Making instruments
 Ongoing program (all days): Creating costumes from cardboard and paper for the parade

 Facilitators:
 Luis Croes
 Guides children's camps and provides creative activities, handpan and creates a pleasant atmosphere.
 Iris van Wijhe
 A versatile and musically-inclined individual with a great passion for mindfulness.
 Iris enjoys leading workshops where children can playfully discover, create, move, and relax.
 "I prefer to create spaces where imagination is encouraged, where messy hands are seen as a good thing, and where children can playfully explore and be themselves."
 We invite the children to experiment, collaborate, and simply be themselves. Because it is when there is space for freedom and wonder that the most beautiful creations arise...

profiles.name: Morning Yoga · The Five Elements Form
profiles.description:
Want do you want smooth hips, flowing energy and a smile on your face?

During this standing yoga flow, Esmee Jiskoot will guide you through the 5 Elements Form: a series of twenty fluid yoga movements that may remind you of Qigong. The movements help to release tension, deepen your breathing and bring body and mind back into balance.
The flow moves like a moving meditation: soft, powerful and surprisingly energetic all at once. Your body comes alive, your mind finds peace and you feel more present in the moment.
Whether you are an experienced yogi or taking your first yoga class, this practice invites you to slow down, feel and reconnect with yourself. A wonderful reset during the festival, that will leave you grounded, energized and smiling.
Get to know Esmee Jiskoot
Esmee Jiskoot is a yoga teacher and body-oriented coach. With warmth, enthusiasm and years of experience, she guides people towards more relaxation, body awareness and life energy.
In her classes, movement, breathing and personal development come together naturally…

profiles.name: DAF - Dutch Acid Family
profiles.description:
Thursday
 21:00-01:00
 Electro Magnes
 Electro Magnes is a Polish electronic duo formed by composer, producer, and multi-instrumentalist Rafał Kassaraba and vocalist-songwriter Bashka Preen. Blending live bass, electronic textures, and expressive vocals, they create atmospheric soundscapes that move between experimental electronica, ambient music, and intimate storytelling.
 Based in the foothills of the Karkonosze Mountains, the duo draws inspiration from nature, human connection, and inner transformation. Rafał's musical journey spans folk-blues, punk-rock, electro-punk, and film music projects, bringing a rich and distinctive depth to their sound.
 Bashka writes and performs in English, influenced by the years she spent living in London and its vibrant music scene. Her original songs have also been featured in film productions.
 Together, Electro Magnes invites audiences into a cinematic and immersive musical experience where electronic experimentation meets emotion, reflection, and heart.

Friday
 Sunshine Reggae
 15:00-16:30 Hobbol
 16:30-18:00 Open the gate sound
 18:00-19:30 Loddy Culture & Lyrical Benji
 19:30-21:00 Selector Depender
 21:00-22:30 Sun Collective…

profiles.name: The Game Cabinet
profiles.description:
Open a drawer, slide a box towards you, and let the fun begin. In the Games Corner, you'll find a diverse mix of card and board games waiting for new players. Challenge someone to a game of 30 seconds, go for pure chaos with a party game, or rediscover a classic you had long forgotten.

Whether you come to win, hang out, laugh, or simply chat: there's always something fun happening around the table here. Pull up a chair and join in the fun.

profiles.name: DJ Lotta Isopuro
profiles.description: Expect a relentless bass line holding you hostage on the dance floor, not allowing you even the slightest toilet break. Lotta Isopuro won’t cut you any slack before the collective sweat is dripping off the ceiling. She’ll pave your party path with a.o. peak time techno, indie dance and (progressive) house. And before you know what hit you, she has cleverly infiltrated her (carefully curated!) guilty pleasure bangers into the sequence. Lotta personally pre-tests all her tracks for danceability in the privacy of her living room. No groove? Disapprove! Why? Hips don’t lie.

profiles.name: DJ Steve Kennedy
profiles.description: Wanna dance, feel happy, feel sexy, move your hips and put your hands up high? Then come and check out Steve’s groovy, sexy, uplifting, soulful DJ set! ❤️ #love #dance #techhouse #techno

profiles.name: XXL spellen: Schaken, Jenga, Twister
profiles.description: Huge, bigger, wildest: With our XXL games, it's all about having fun to the fullest. Challenge your strategic mind on a chessboard as big as your ego, build towers that reach higher than your weekend plans with XXL Jenga filled with surprising questions and tasks, or get tangled up during a game of Twister that is guaranteed to lead to laughter fits.

profiles.name: The Floating Poet
profiles.description: Become a floating poet and come up to our raft to write a poem. Ask the internship manager where the secret departure point is. You can recite your creation in the garden.

profiles.name: Photo Booth
profiles.description: Capture your favorite moment and step into our photo booth. Create a beautiful memory of the festival by taking your own picture.

profiles.name: Open Mic
profiles.description: Music, poetry, stories, or something completely different. The stage is yours. Your moment, your talent. Step up and surprise us. From hidden talents to spontaneous inspiration: anything goes, nothing is mandatory.

profiles.name: Speeddaten
profiles.description: Join in, make new connections and meet interesting people. Friends, festival buddies, or maybe even more. A series of short encounters that can be anything: surprising, inspiring, awkward in a fun way, or exactly what you didn't expect. Sit down, talk, switch seats – and see what happens.

profiles.name: Waterpret: babbelbad & watergevecht
profiles.description:
Put on your fun water shoes, grab a super soaker, or pick up a water balloon and dive into the refreshing battle of Landjuweel.

Or just cool off from the hustle and bustle? Take a seat in the "babbelbad" (chat pool) and enjoy a good conversation. Be surprised by spontaneous encounters and enjoyable chats. Meet new people, share stories, and enjoy the conviviality.

profiles.name: Open Jam Session
profiles.description: Bring your instrument or just join in! No rules, no stage pressure, just making music together. Whether you've been playing for years or are joining for the first time, everyone is welcome to jam, listen, and inspire each other musically, or simply enjoy what emerges.

profiles.name: Darkvoice
profiles.description:
Fear and Loathing in Ruigoord: Darkvoice Live @ Landjuweel

Since 1996, he has been working behind the scenes on his own music, but this year, Darkvoice is finally stepping into the spotlight for his very first live debut, performing for a unique audience. No DJ set with other people's records, but a 100% original story full of analog elements and live vocals.

The Trip: Hunter S. Thompson
The first part of the set will be dedicated to Fear and Loathing in Las Vegas. A rebellious, nineties-inspired rollercoaster where Darkvoice takes on the persona of Hunter S. Thompson. Expect an energetic, cinematic epic with a strong nod to the sound of Underworld, The Chemical Brothers, Prodigy, Orbital, and Massive Attack.

The Landing: Positive Buoyancy
In the second part, the journey turns inward. Darkvoice takes you on a personal, emotional journey with live versions of his album Positive Buoyancy. An introspective search for balance between fatherhood, love, deep grief, and the dark temptations of life. The result? Solid, immersive beats with a raw emotional charge…

profiles.name: State of the Aart
profiles.description: At Landjuweel, State of the Aart delivers some seriously solid techno, but always with a killer groove. Armed with his Model 1.4, Aart is able to find the perfect balance. Not too loud, not too soft, but an insistent, rolling trip. No nonsense, just a really good sound that keeps you going all night long. Tested on people, and it works.

profiles.name: (H)oer Hollandse spelletjes
profiles.description: From chair dancing and staring contests to nail-biting and jumping jacks: classic, good-natured fun with a healthy dose of competition. Join in and discover who secretly has the most intense competitive spirit.

profiles.name: Karaoke Bar: Valse Noten
profiles.description: Unleash your inner rock star, nightingale, or howling wolf! Here, we don't sing perfectly, but with full passion. Everyone can participate, whether you sound like a star or a vacuum cleaner.

profiles.name: Body paint corner
profiles.description:
In this workshop, we will cover the body with all sorts of neon colors so that you can confidently step out into the night in your newly designed second skin!

We work with outlines and patterns, but most importantly, let your mind be free and follow your inspiration!
From subtle lines to complete works of art – you decide how you shine.

profiles.name: Total Artwork · Open Workshop with Hessel Pijnaker
profiles.description: In a group setting, we intuitively create a work of art on one giant sheet of paper together. Come and relax! Hessel will guide you through the process, allowing you to freely express yourself with various exercises based on artistic techniques from Zen Buddhism.

profiles.name: SAVE THE GREEN FIELD — SIGN NOW!
profiles.description: The Green Field is a rare green space in the city where there is still room for wild nature, culture, and experimentation. It is run on a voluntary basis by residents and small, local businesses. But now, our free space is at risk of disappearing. Therefore, we need to act NOW, it's not too late. You can help us by signing the petition! More information at our stand.

profiles.name: DJ JP
profiles.description:
DJ JP is a DJ from Amsterdam who has felt connected to Ruigoord since 1984. “My first major experience was intense; the contrast with city life was even greater then.
 Here at Ruigoord, you not only connect with nature and art from the numerous studio homes, but also with yourself in a spiritual way that was unheard of in the Netherlands at the time.
 This allowed me to see things differently and place them in a new perspective, which has shaped me as a person and as a musician/DJ into who I am today. In 1984, the area was very large, and the stages were far apart, which made the experience even more impressive, especially at night due to the limited lighting.
 I was surrounded by old and new friends, many of whom are still active at Ruigoord.
 And then the sun rose in all its glory, warming us with a positive energy that has never left me. That's why it is always special for me to come back here.” DJ JP as a member of the renowned EVLO/LOVE and High Quality crew…

profiles.name: Informatief: Huiden verwerken
profiles.description:
Erwin navigates through life with passion, physical strength, and a connection to nature. Designer, Hunter, Bushcrafter.

Informative: Processing Hides
In this first part of the two-part series, we explore the story behind hides and their place in human history. We delve into the relationship between humans and animals throughout the ages, from hunting and survival among indigenous peoples to the careful use of what nature provides. We explore how we can meet our basic needs for life, and how respect for animals and nature plays a central role in this. This includes considering hunting as part of a broader ecological cycle, and the idea of connection with nature and "Mother Earth." It's a moment to reflect on what it means to be grounded and part of something larger. Space for questions and discussion makes this an open and insightful conversation.

This program segment is followed by a workshop on processing hides at 2 PM.

profiles.name: Lino printing workshop for ages 10+
profiles.description:
KIMTAM TATTOO is a tattoo artist with Javanese-Surinamese roots, based in Rotterdam. She combines craftsmanship, culture, and storytelling by creating unique designs that tell a personal story. Stamping techniques have been used worldwide for centuries to print patterns and images on paper and textiles. In some parts of Southeast Asia, similar techniques were even used to create tattoos. Inspired by this rich tradition, KIMTAM designs her own stamps and uses them as the basis for her tattoo work.

• For children from 10 years old
Older children get to work themselves as creators. They learn how to design a simple image, cut it out in lino, print it on paper, and then turn it into a temporary stencil on the skin. This way, they experience the entire creative process: from idea to final result.

The workshop focuses on creativity, craftsmanship, and discovering a unique art form that people have been using for generations to make stories, symbols, and identity visible. Everyone goes home with their own prints and also a unique temporary tattoo that they have created themselves.

profiles.name: Closing remarks
profiles.description: After a day filled with discovery, learning, making, and meeting new people, we come together to quietly conclude the day. It's a moment for reflection, to consider what has touched us and to share our experiences with each other. While enjoying a warm cup of chaga tea, we allow ourselves to be guided by the moment. Perhaps we sing together, do a simple yoga exercise, tell stories to each other, or simply enjoy being together.

profiles.name: Making dream catchers
profiles.description:
Create your own dream catcher using natural and found materials. With twigs, string, beads, and feathers collected from birds, we create a special object full of stories and imagination.

What dreams, wishes, or adventures will your dream catcher capture?

profiles.name: Workshop Lino Stempelen
profiles.description:
KIMTAM TATTOO is a tattoo artist with Javanese-Surinamese roots, based in Rotterdam. In her work, she combines craftsmanship, culture, and storytelling by creating unique designs that tell a personal story.

During this workshop, children will learn about the ancient art of printing. The artwork you see consists of lino stamps that have been hand-carved. Stamping techniques have been used around the world for centuries to print patterns and images on paper and fabric. In parts of Southeast Asia, including Malaysia, similar techniques were even used to create tattoos, where ornaments and figures were carved from wood.

This rich tradition inspired KIMTAM to design her own stamps and use them as a basis for her tattoo work.
For children aged 5-9 years:
Children playfully discover the world of stamping. They choose from various handmade lino stamps, make their own prints on paper, and then receive their favorite design as a temporary stencil on their skin.
For children aged 10+ years:
Older children get to work independently as creators…

profiles.name: "Fire-making workshop by Robert Bleij"
profiles.description:
Robert Bleij, buscraft, actor and storyteller,
 Fire-making workshop
 Have you always wanted to learn how to make fire yourself using simple, natural materials?
 During this two-hour program, you will learn the basics of making primitive fire. No long theory, but mainly hands-on: trying, practicing, and discovering what is needed to turn a spark into a real flame.
 Making fire is an ancient skill that lies dormant in every human being. With the right technique, good materials, and a little patience, anyone can learn it. Experience is not required; this program is suitable for beginners.
 In a relaxed outdoor setting, you will discover:
 different old techniques for making fire;
 which natural tinder works well and where to find it;
 how to prepare the tinder yourself;
 how to turn the tinder into a flame;
 how to build a fire calmly and successfully.

 After two hours, you will leave with more knowledge, experience, and confidence to be able to make fire safely yourself.
 Links:
 Instagram

profiles.name: Workshop: Huiden verwerken
profiles.description:
Erwin navigates through life with passion, physical strength, and a connection to nature. Designer, Hunter, Bushcrafter.

Informative: Processing Hides
In this first part of the two-part series, we explore the story behind hides and their place in human history. We delve into the relationship between humans and animals throughout the ages, from hunting and survival among indigenous peoples to the careful use of what nature provides. We explore how we can meet our basic needs for life, and how respect for animals and nature plays a central role in this. This includes considering hunting as part of a broader ecological cycle, and the idea of connection with nature and "Mother Earth." It's a moment to reflect on what it means to be grounded and part of something larger. Space for questions and discussion makes this an open and insightful conversation.

This program segment is followed by a workshop on processing hides at 2 PM.

profiles.name: Reiki & Just Zen It Workshop (12+ jaar)
profiles.description:
Kyra Walhof is a Reiki Master, massage therapist, and founder of Zensaties and the Just Zen It method. For over fifteen years, she has been guiding people towards relaxation, mindfulness, and energy management. With her warm, practical, and humorous approach, she makes spirituality accessible and helps people connect with themselves more deeply.

Reiki & Just Zen It Workshop
Discover the power of Reiki and Just Zen It in a simple and practical way. During this workshop, you will learn how to experience more peace in your body and mind through simple meditation, breathing, and mindfulness exercises. You will gain an understanding of the basics of Reiki and discover how to better feel, manage, and recharge your energy. We will practice relaxation, presence, and increasing loving attention for yourself and those around you. No prior experience is necessary; curiosity is enough!

There are a limited number of places available. So, if you would like to participate in this workshop, you can reserve a spot at the yurt on Dorpsplein - Aardewerk.

profiles.name: Making a kaleidoscope – The telescope of the flying creature.
Embroidery of a talisman
profiles.description: Create a personalized talisman to take with you on the go. Using thread, color, and symbols, give shape to something that is meaningful to you. A small keepsake that reminds you of the places, people, or moments where you feel at home.

profiles.name: Costumes for the parade
profiles.description: Who do you want to be? A crawler, a jumper, a flying creature, or perhaps something that doesn't yet exist? Using fabrics, natural materials, and a healthy dose of imagination, we create costumes for the children's parade.

profiles.name: Contribute to building the growing "Insect Village" – The Landscape.
profiles.description:
Donna and Mariana explore the realms of creativity, nature, and play. With a focus on what emerges in the moment, they invite children to collaborate, investigate, and unleash their imaginations.

Throughout the festival, we will collectively build a world. A playful exploration of what "home" means for each of us.

The world taking shape - Landscape
We begin with the foundation of the insect village. Using clay, branches, and natural materials, we create tunnels, hills, shelters, and other hidden spaces. A world for crawlers and ground dwellers, where a safe home can emerge among roots, passages, and hollows.
Part of a three-part workshop

profiles.name: Contribute to building the growing "Insect Village" – The Plant Kingdom!
profiles.description:
Donna and Mariana explore the realms of creativity, nature, and play. With a focus on what emerges in the moment, they invite children to collaborate, investigate, and unleash their imaginations.

Throughout the festival, we will collectively build a world. A playful exploration of what "home" means for each of us.

The World in Becoming - The Plant Kingdom
From the landscape that has already emerged, let's further develop the village. We will create paths, bridges, plant structures, and green meeting spaces where runners and jumpers can move freely. The village becomes increasingly shaped, connected, and alive.
Part of a three-part workshop

profiles.name: Help Build the Growing Insect Village · Air Creatures
profiles.description:
Donna and Mariana explore the realms of creativity, nature, and play. Paying attention to what emerges in the moment, they invite children to collaborate, investigate, and unleash their imaginations.

Throughout the festival, we will collectively build a world. A playful exploration of what "home" means for each of us.

The World in Becoming - The Air Creatures
Now that the landscape and plant life have found their place, we focus on the world above. Using light, transparent materials, we create flying creatures, floating inhabitants, and dreamy forms that dance between earth and sky.

Part of a three-part workshop

profiles.name: InstrumMensen Freestyle Rap Session
profiles.description:
Cato Fluitsma and Ian Merkoulov, with their Troubadour Car, have found each other as "Life's Small Artists" and travel together in a rhyming musical dialogue.

Can you freestyle as a "Life's Small Artist"? Everyone can! With everyone from the youngest to the oldest, we set the tone for the Landuweel festival. Pick up the Mic!

profiles.name: The soft landing
profiles.description: We start the day with a joint opening ceremony on the village square. A moment to come together, meet each other, and settle into the day calmly. With music, movement, and creativity, we discover what the daily theme has to tell us. What adventures, questions, and surprises await us today?

profiles.name: Story with The Troubadour Car
profiles.description:
Ian Merkoulov travels throughout the Low Countries as a true troubadour. With his "Troubadour Car," he collects stories for both young and old, presenting them as musical folklore that tells us about our beautiful world.

Story with The Troubadour Car
Step into a story for children and adults together with Ian Merkoulov and his friends in the "Troubadour Car."

profiles.name: Music Carpet
profiles.description:
Magique Jean Jean, Cato Fluitsma, and Ian Merkoulov are "Life Artists" who love to give others a voice and expression.

Musical Carpet
Magique Jean Jean, Cato Fluitsma, and Ian Merkoulov weave together with friends and family a "Musical Carpet" of the day. Using sounds, words, phrases, and notes, we create a carpet that can be unrolled and enjoyed.

Would you like to join us?

profiles.name: MC Katootje
profiles.description:
MC Katootje will take you on a journey.
Without any distractions, she'll put on a show.
With your child and with your grandparent,
She'll captivate you with her words.
She's truly the most entertaining!
Not always relaxed, but...
Eh... Come to InstrumenTaal Stage!
Then we'll all see it together
and have a great time working together :)

Weaving a musical carpet is a noble aspiration...

profiles.name: Lisa and the Brown Prince
profiles.description:
‘On a clear evening, when the sun has set and the moon looks longingly at the stars, Lisa steps out of the window of her small house. Suddenly, she sees a large palace standing on top of the mountain in the bright moonlight. She can hardly believe her eyes; she has never seen a palace before. ‘Does a prince live there?’, Lisa wonders. Are you also curious to hear how this Caribbean fairytale continues? Then come and listen and enjoy the most beautiful illustrations in Wijnand Stomp’s ‘Kamishi-bai’ magic box. The master of Caribbean stories alternates between many character types and tropical songs. Together with his puppet ‘Bird-friend’, he takes both young and old on adventures. When the snake from his story starts to coil, everyone is transported to a fairytale world.

Wijnand Stomp published this story in 1997 because he could not find any books for his own children featuring a brown prince as the main character, as an alternative to the eternal image of the (white) prince on a white horse.

profiles.name: Babette
profiles.description:
Babette Voets is a multidisciplinary artist, performer, storyteller, and founder of Controubadour. Using harp, singing, and spoken word, she creates poetic worlds where transformation, empathy, and imagination come together. Through the practice of shapeshifting, she explores how we can create new connections with ourselves, each other, and the living world around us by temporarily adopting a different perspective.

"Pleidooi voor diversiteit" (Appeal for Diversity) is a musical exploration of all voices and worlds that exist in our lives, and how they can free us from a fixed scenario in our minds.

profiles.name: Your story written on your body
profiles.description:
Cato Fluitsma and Ian Merkoulov, with their Troubadour Car, have found each other as "Small-Scale Artists of Life" and travel together in a rhyming musical dialogue.

A story written on your body:
A journey within ourselves. What does the wind sound like to you? And the cracking earth? Let us hear it! Can we become that babbling brook where the bears have set up their tent? Pack your bags and come along!

profiles.name: Music Story with The Troubadour Car
The Troubadour Car Jam Session
profiles.description:
Ian Merkoulov travels throughout all of the Netherlands in his Troubadour Car. From street corners to village squares, halls to living rooms, boats to forests. A true Troubadour whose hall is the world, with poetic language and a colorful stage as its backdrop.

The Troubadour Car Jam Session
The Troubadour Car jam session for all ages and skill levels. From harmony to cacophony.

profiles.name: Making props for the parade
profiles.description: We create instruments together using natural and recycled materials. Shake, drum, rattle, or chime – give your instrument its own unique sound and character. Together, we'll build a cheerful parade of sounds. Bring your instrument along and join us in the children's parade at 4:30 PM!

profiles.name: Woven Voices
profiles.description:
Woven Voices originated from love. Love for bringing people together and giving everyone the space to express themselves. What remains is a beautiful work of art.

Weaving Our Shared Visions Together
"Woven Voices," the proud creation of Lex Empress, begins on the first day of Landjuweel. Each day, participants are invited to write intentions, dreams, and visions for our future, for the children, and for the world. These words are painted onto cotton and woven together into one collective carpet. This is a living artwork created from many voices becoming one. Anyone can join and become part of this shared creation. By the end of the festival, the carpet stands as a physical memory of our united prayers and possibilities.

profiles.name: Gilian Baracs and band
profiles.description:
Gilian Baracs is a pianist, composer, and singer. He studied jazz piano at the Amsterdam Conservatory and effortlessly moves between improvisation and songcraft. With his band, he presents new material from his upcoming album: original songs that balance pop, groove, jazz, and free improvisation.

The music can be danceable, poetic, carefully composed, or completely spontaneous. In all cases, connection is central: between musicians, the audience, and the story that wants to unfold at that moment.
For this special festival show, the band will be accompanied by special guests, including singer Lex Empress.

Line-up
Gilian Baracs - keyboards, vocals
Frank Hopman - drums
Martin Zand Scholten - bass
Jur The Fresh - guitar
Robbert Scherpenisse - trumpet
João Driessen - saxophone
Special guests
Lex Empress - vocals
Vocalist(s) TBA

profiles.name: Awakening ceremony with Sophia Maria Kienhuis and Gilian Baracs
profiles.description:
On Vurige Tongen invites Sophia Maria Kienhuis and Gilian Baracs to their awakening ceremony, where you can unleash your inner firebird.

With live piano, beats, and original songs, they invite you to feel, move, and follow what wants to emerge. There is no fixed form, but a journey in which you seek the answer to the question of what it means for you to awaken.
Between stillness and radical expression, a shared experience unfolds, where the boundary between performers and audience fades away.

profiles.name: Sophia Maria Kienhuis
profiles.description:
There's a poem by Mark Strand in which he writes about experiencing the absence of field within a field, and striving to hold onto things. In this poetic space between presence and emptiness, Sophia Maria Kienhuis moves – at the intersection of art, radical expression, and interaction.

Her work unfolds as a living encounter, where body, voice, and material come together. This year, she weaves her performances with textile works that she brings along as carriers of story, memory, and sensory experience.
Sophia Maria has created numerous performances both indoors and outdoors at Ruigoord over the years, including contributions to art routes, Landjuweel, Balloon Shows, and various editions of Nazomer Nachtlicht. She has been affiliated with MadNes Festival for 18 years and established the ecstatic dance team in Odessa, where she also led ceremonies for several years.
In addition, she has collaborated with artists such as Spinvis, Wordbites, Primaat, Jackie and the facts, and is a singer and songwriter for Flower of Hope and the Ontwaak Ceremonie, with Gilian Baracs.…

profiles.name: Woven Voices Opening with Lex Empress
profiles.description:
Woven Voices originated from love. Love for bringing people together and giving everyone the space to express themselves. What remains is a beautiful work of art.

Woven Voices: Weaving Our Shared Visions Together

Woven Voices, the proud creation of Lex Empress, begins on the first day of Landjuweel. Each day, participants are invited to write intentions, dreams, and visions for our future, for the children, and for the world. These words are painted onto cotton and woven together into one collective carpet. A living artwork created from many voices becoming one. Everyone can join and become part of this shared creation. By the end of the festival, the carpet stands as a physical memory of our united prayers and possibilities.

Lex Empress
Versatile and innovative – never the same: that's Lex Empress. To understand what she does, you have to see her perform, because no performance is ever the same. She creates interactive experiences where the audience is at the center. In 7 languages, with more than 4 octaves of notes in her singing, and with a deep belief in the potential of humanity, she opens hearts and minds like no other.

profiles.name: Ctd Movement
profiles.description:
CTD presents a Spiritual Hip Hop showcase where music, awareness, and community come together.

From spoken word to rap, from soulful melodies to powerful beats: young artists share their stories about identity, personal growth, love, social change, and the search for meaning.
Inspired by the idea that we are all connected, this showcase invites you to listen, feel, and connect. Expect honest lyrics, fresh energy, and artists who use hip hop as a means of awareness, connection, and change.
A performance that not only makes you move, but also inspires something within you.

profiles.name: Marly Niyambura
profiles.description:
Niyambura is a born singer, songwriter & poet from Kenya. With a deep appreciation for her African heritage she uses her voice to express matters of her heart in Kikuyu Kiswahili & english.
 Life's experiences and experiences of other people's life's journeys are her source of inspiration.

profiles.name: Spoken word performance with Smita James and others.
profiles.description:
Smita James (Nijkerk, 1983) is a Surinamese-Dutch writer, poet, and performance artist. Since 2002, she has performed her texts on renowned literary stages such as Re:definition, Cinnamon Wednesdays, Woorden worden Zinnen, SLAA Perdu, Read My World, and Poetry International. This places her among the leading figures in the spoken word scene, which was once underground but is now thriving in the Netherlands. In 2019, she contributed to the book "Hardop" (Atlas, 2019), a major work in the spoken word genre.

In addition, James has contributed to various other publications: Stichting Kaihan (Ministry of Social Affairs, 2011), DICHTER (Plint, 2018 and 2019), FREE HERI HERI (Kip Republic, 2019), Atlas van Herdenken (Wij zijn Amsterdam, 2023), Kala Pani (World Museum, 2024), Het grote Caribische Verhalen Boek (Querido, 2025).

Her work focuses on themes such as identity, metaphysics, and the Afro- and Indo-Caribbean diaspora within our social sphere. Although her writing begins on paper, her texts often extend beyond that. She enjoys working interdisciplinarily and therefore also likes to collaborate with other artists…

profiles.name: Awichas DJ set
profiles.description:
AWICHAS is a music project initiated by Bolivian/Brazilian artist and performer Ibelisse Guardia Ferragutti. AWICHAS draws from Andean ritual from Ibelisse’s birthplace Bolivia. Where she took recordings and data from these journeys and worked with sound artist and musician Jochem van Tol to translate them into electronic music.
 During Landjuweel they will share a collection of Andean & Latinx electronic heavy DJ set. A combination of their original AWICHAS compositions and many other Latinx artists. Expect a danceable, grounding and full of joy set.
 Links:
 Website 
 Instagram

profiles.name: Sharing Voice / Maxi McNaaughty
profiles.description: Maxi McNaughty shares her thoughts and experiences in mostly rhyming form she sings and encourages you to share your voice

profiles.name: Woven Voices: What's alive in you?
Night Drumming
profiles.description:
Feel free to bring your rhythm to life with Nightdrummers. Bring your shaker, tambourine, djembe, or other instrument and join us in the groove.

We create a welcoming Drum Circle where improvisation, connection, and playfulness are central. Listen, respond, experiment, and be amazed by what emerges.
Whether you've been playing for years or are simply curious: there's space for everyone. Bring your friends along, come groove, dance, and let the night resonate with our shared heartbeat.
Everyone is welcome. Together, we create the rhythm.

profiles.name: Djembefola's
profiles.description:
Momo Touré

Momo Touré is the heart and soul of the drum circle at Ruigoord. For over 30 years, he has been the person who brings people together through music, using his djembe, his energy, and his gift for making everyone, from beginners to advanced players, feel part of the groove.

His roots are deeply embedded in traditional West African music. As a young man, he toured worldwide with the renowned African ballet in Guinea. He still carries this richness with him every week at Ruigoord.

Come, see, and participate!

Amier Sloot

Amier Sloot believes in the power of rhythm to bring people together. As a musician and community builder, he facilitates a drum circle every Saturday at Ruigoord alongside Momo, where everyone, regardless of experience, can immerse themselves in a shared groove and be fully present in the moment.

During Landjuweel, they (along with many other respected drummers) bring this energy: connection through music, together in the moment.

profiles.name: Victor Sams on Ngoni
profiles.description:
Victor Sams, a familiar name in Ruigoord, is returning for this Landjuweel edition with a solo performance. The focus will be on the enchanting sound of the ngoni, an ancient West African string instrument. Through his playing and singing, Victor brings to life the rich musical tradition of this unique instrument.

An intimate concert where music, inspiration, and craftsmanship come together, featuring warm melodies, captivating rhythms, and the magical sounds of the ngoni.

profiles.name: Ritmo Revival feat. horns from Mototroko and Zebra streetband
profiles.description:
Ritmo Revival injects new life into the energy of Ritmo Samba Squad, the band that performed at festivals and events both domestically and internationally between 2008 and 2016. Led by Sebastian Skeete Mc Leed, the initiator of the Yaybolo Stage, the band returns with a new generation of musicians and a fresh, contemporary sound.

With an explosive mix of Samba Reggae, Drum & Bass, and unique rhythms, Ritmo Revival delivers a show that will leave everyone captivated. After a high-energy performance at the Ballonnenfeest in Paradiso, the band presents a unique collaboration with brass from Mototroko and Zebra Streetband at Landjuweel.

Expect an immersive combination of powerful percussion, dazzling brass instruments, and pure festival energy: a rhythmic spectacle full of groove, power, and surprises.

profiles.name: Soup and sandwiches
profiles.description: We gather around the fire to cook together. Bring a piece of vegetables and add it to the communal soup. Small and large hands work side by side, while bread is baked over the fire. The square fills with aromas, and our hearts are warmed by being together.

profiles.name: Dakka Sultana
profiles.description: Dakka Sultana represents connection, tradition, and celebration. During performances, the group brings not only music, but also a cultural experience that celebrates the rich Moroccan heritage and brings people together through rhythm and energy.

profiles.name: Latin upbeat freestyle
profiles.description:
The Zebra Street Band presents an explosive mix of African and Caribbean influences, jazz, Latin grooves, and free improvisation. With their energetic, danceable sound and collective improvisations, no performance is ever the same.

Special for the Landjuweel Festival, the band performs with a unique lineup, alongside percussionists Jos de Haas and Sebastian Skeete McLeed. Expect a fully improvised set full of uptempo Latin grooves, infectious rhythms, and irresistible energy that will keep everyone moving.

profiles.name: Talking drum workshop with Mame N'Diack
profiles.description:
Mame N’Diack Seck Thiam is a Senegalese percussionist, singer, poet, choreographer, actor, and songwriter from a renowned griot family. His musical journey began at the age of 17, as he travelled across West Africa to immerse himself in different cultures and musical traditions.

Since relocating to Uganda in 2010, Mame has developed a distinctive sound by blending West African percussion with traditional Ugandan rhythms. He is widely recognized for introducing the talking drum to new audiences and captivating listeners with its expressive, voice-like sound.

Dedicated to preserving African cultural heritage, Mame founded the annual Yella Cultural Festival, promoting cultural pride, intercultural exchange, and collaboration among young people. His mission is to keep traditional music alive while using it to bring people from different cultures together.

profiles.name: Latin percussion workshop with Jos de Haas
profiles.description:
Percussionist Jos de Haas has been a prominent figure in the Dutch jazz and Latin scene for years, most notably through his work with New Cool Collective. With his infectious grooves and passion for Afro-Cuban and Latin rhythms, he knows how to get everyone moving.

During the Landjuweel Festival, Jos will be leading an accessible Latin Percussion Workshop. No prior experience is required: if you have a sense of rhythm and a desire to make music together, you are warmly welcome! Expect an energetic session filled with swinging Latin rhythms, ensemble playing, and above all, lots of fun.

profiles.name: Kloek
profiles.description:
KLOEK. An interactive street theatre performance. Or, to put it another way: A robust, mobile installation that only comes to life on the streets with the help of brave passersby…

Back to basics. KLOEK as pure installation theatre. Nothing hidden, nothing embellished. Robust structures, basic mechanics. Simple and transparent. The timeless power of simplicity.

Pure. Steel and wood. Nature and human strength. Strong hands of both adults and children pull on the ropes to make the sound instruments play and the puppet fly. Free as a bird.

A bold and subtle interplay between man and machine creates visual poetry with muscle power. Both intimate and exuberant, it stimulates imagination and makes fantasy a tangible reality. KLOEK.

profiles.name: "Poems of Fire and Earth"
profiles.description: Buguel Noz’s puppet poetry consists of tender and intense creations. It invites wonder and imagination. Music, dance, and puppetry come together in pure, magical moments. The performance ‘Poésie du Feu et de la Terre’ is ideally suited for festivals. This eco-poetic performance focuses on rebirth.
