Together with cellist Tjalle Rens, they perform a live ambient set at Landjuweel in Theo's tea garden, featuring piano and cello mixed with electronics and synthesizers.

profiles.name: Avi Adir. Araam Project
profiles.description:
ARAM Project is a collaborative world-fusion music ensemble led by Avi Adir, a nomadic musician, vocalist, and multi-instrumentalist. Rooted in the art of spontaneous creation, the project brings together musicians from diverse backgrounds to compose music in the moment, blending improvisation with deep listening and collective expression.
 Drawing inspiration from traditions and sounds from around the world, ARAM Project creates unique musical journeys where no performance is ever repeated. Melodies, rhythms, and textures emerge organically through the interaction between the musicians, inviting audiences into a living, evolving musical experience.
 For this performance, ARAM Project features: Avi Adir – vocals, bouzouki, and flutes
 Terence Samson – percussion
 Lucas Stam – cello
 Gil Lopez – guitar

profiles.name: Rae Charmaine
profiles.description: Rich in soul and soothing energy: this Zimbabwean singer-songwriter makes use of melodies and composition, blending perfectly together into a lyrical potion of emotions. Her music is a combination of jazz, blues, rhythm, and reggae with African influences.

profiles.name: Dramm, LaBerge & Dramm
profiles.description:
"The 21st Century von Trapp Family," as they are often called, comprise composer and guitarist David Dramm, flutist and composer Anne La Berge, and violinist (and her daughter) Diamanda La Berge Dramm. As far back as Diamanda (Dutch Classical Talent in 2018) can remember, the La Berge Dramm family has been making music together, both on stage and at home. In 2019, they documented all their own compositions, as well as pieces by composers such as Charles Ives, along with covers of artists like The Velvet Underground, Cindy Walker, and Burt Bacharach, on a triple-LP: Modern Genetics (released by Splendor Records), featuring both harsh and tender sounds.

Expect a generous dose of harmonies and playful virtuosity.
glish blurb:
An impossible, rousing mix of punk, country, spoken word and electro. The 21st Century von Trapp family La Berge Dramm.
Line-up/Personnel:
David Dramm (guitars/vocals), Anne La Berge (flutes/electronics/vocals), Diamanda La Berge Dramm (violin/vocals/kick drum)

profiles.name: Nita Aartsen Kwartet
profiles.description:
The Aartsen Duo consists of Alexander on vocal and Nita on piano & vocal.
 Together they've been performing 
 around the world for decades, 
 bringing memories of old school 
 jazz with high artistry singing & 
 story telling, combined with fun 
tertainment.
 Join our concerts, and be part of a 
 magical musical journey.

profiles.name: MIJNHEER van Ouwenaar
profiles.description:
Mr. Van Ouwenaar is coming!

No one knows for sure why, but he is indeed coming!
Some claim that his Spinoza-inspired texts reveal a peculiar perspective, while others believe he only performs when the moon resembles a "psylist."
Expect unexpected beauty and songs that feel like dreams you once had on a rollercoaster to Nieuwegein, or in a hot air balloon to Ruigoord.

profiles.name: Lotte Walda
profiles.description:
Dutch songwriter Lotte Walda writes songs to spread positive energy and help create a kinder, more connected world. Deeply inspired by nature and by the music of the sixties and seventies, Lotte feels most at home in the sounds of that era — songs full of warmth, connection and timeless storytelling. Through her music, she hopes to remind people of the light and love that still live in the world. She loves creating catchy melodies that stick with listeners. Blending folk, pop and country, her songs tell heartfelt stories about life, love and staying true to yourself. With her warm voice and honest lyrics, she aims to touch, comfort and uplift everyone who listens.
 Her musical journey began on the old Sint Servaas Bridge of Maastricht near her hometown, where she would fill the streets with her voice and guitar. From those early days, she wanted to “build a bridge that reminds people of the joy within that connects them to their inner child. This curiosity and open-heartedness soon took her on a global adventure. She’s since performed everywhere from the Bluebird Café in Nashville to festival stages across the Netherlands, and even shared her music on the streets of China.…

profiles.name: Maringo Berenos
profiles.description: A musical journey Influenced by traditions from Africa, South-America and India - Maringo welcomes you to dive into a still point with his music from over the world. He approaches music like a teacher we can learn from; so we don’t only play music but actually learn from it. It makes us sing together, play together and stay together. Here on Landjuweel it’s always a joyful surprise where the journey takes us.

profiles.name: Jam olv Joao Vedana
profiles.description: João Vedana is a Multi-Instrumentalist and singer from Brasil. The guitar has been of great of importance in his life since the age of 9 and ever since the development never stopped. At 15 years old he started his professional career playing blues heavily inspired by Stevie Ray Vaughan , BB King and George Benson. Later on he released two original albums, João Vedana (2017) recorded in Brasil and Despertar (2022) recorded in The Netherlands via the Jazz Label Zennez Records, and has also worked as a sideman musician for many different projects. Now with 31 years old and after almost 8 years since moving to The Netherlands he lives in Rotterdam and plays concerts and festivals all over the country and also hosts Jam sessions occasionally. You can expect a strong load of Brazilian, Latin, Jazz and Funk influences on his music fueled by fiery solos, transcending grooves and spontaneous improvisation.

profiles.name: The Art of Belonging
profiles.description:
A conversation about what community culture can teach us, featuring voices from Ruigoord, Boom Festival, and indigenous wisdom.

Community is something many of us long for, but what does it actually ask of us? Drawing on lived experience from Ruigoord, Boom Festival, and indigenous tradition, this conversation explores the values, practices, and ways of relating that help make genuine belonging possible.

Aja Waalwijk was present at the occupation of Ruigoord in 1973 and has been a participant in Ruigoord's Poets' Circus from the very beginning. He initiated the annual Saturday Landjuweel Sculpture Route, initiated and co-organized ten symposiums on Free Cultural Spaces, and organized several international ABG journeys from Ruigoord. He also co-organized the annual Paradiso performances, along with many other cultural initiatives.

Artur Mendes is co-director of Boom Festival and Being Gathering in Portugal. With a background in social and organizational psychology, culture management, and regenerative community design, he has spent decades exploring how festivals can foster lasting cultures of participation, shared values, and belonging.…

profiles.name: Heiko Dijker’s Soul Sangam - Nava Rasa
Wakhan Project
profiles.description:
The music of Wā́khan Project is a modern ritual: a fiery fusion of ethno-tribal grooves, bouncing basslines, and a sassy energy that transforms any dancefloor into a mystical playground. His sets possess a rare power—the ability to awaken the wild, free, and joyful part that sleeps in everyone—with a smile, a twist, a spark in the air.
 Before igniting international stages, Wā́khan was already shaking the walls of Parisian squats and running club nights with his first techno collective, BPMOOD. It was there that his vision sharpened: to create music that is more organic, more tribal, more connected to something instinctive. 
 A sound that dances between raw groove, gentle spirituality, and playful mischief.
 A sound that has become his signature.

 In 2018, driven by a desire to cultivate a new scene in Paris, he founded the collective O’Tawa, now a reference point for unconventional nights and immersive experiences in the city. Under his guidance, O’Tawa has become a free, intense, and vibrant artistic space—a place where people come as much to dance as to experience a moment out of time.…

profiles.name: Purple Flame
profiles.description:
PURPLE FLAME IS A FEMINIST DJ, PRODUCER, AND MULTIMEDIA ARTIST FROM TBILISI, GEORGIA. ALONGSIDE PLAYING MUSIC, SHE IS A HUMAN RIGHTS ACTIVIST, ACTIVELY ADVOCATES FOR EQUAL RIGHTS, OFTEN INCORPORATING HER ACTIVIST MESSAGES INTO HER SETS AND USING MUSIC AS A PLATFORM FOR SOCIAL CHANGE.
 SHE ALSO EXPLORES THE HEALING POTENTIAL OF AUDIO FREQUENCIES, PRACTICING SOUND HEALING THROUGH HER COLLABORATIVE PROJECT, ‘’FORSUN’’.
 AS A MEMBER OF THE MULTICULTURAL COMMUNITY AND RECORD LABEL DOWNTEMPO RITUALS, PURPLE FLAME PERFORMS AT GIGS AND FESTIVALS THROUGHOUT GEORGIA AND ACROSS EUROPE YEAR-ROUND.

profiles.name: Bliz Nochi (live)
profiles.description:
Bliz Nochi grew up in Moldova, born into a musician family, surrounded by the rich cultural traditions of Eastern Europe, where music was a constant part of everyday life. Now based in Hamburg, Germany, he draws on these early influences, blending them with his deep connection to nature and his curiosity about the emotional power of sound.

Through his musical style, Bliz Nochi brings together electronic beats and traditional folk sounds from around the world. His music is less about following trends and more about telling stories – stories that move both the body and the spirit, evoking the energy of a festival at sunrise or the joy of dancing with loved ones.

Whether playing live or blending genres in a hybrid DJ set, Bliz Nochi creates spaces where people can connect, reflect, and celebrate.

Over the years, Bliz Nochi has had the opportunity to perform at a wide range of venues and festivals, from Amsterdam Dance Event (NL) and Scorpios Mykonos (GR) to Fusion Festival (DE) and intimate spaces like Golden Pudel Club and Südpol (DE).

profiles.name: Soul Safari
profiles.description:
Crafting immersive journeys through ambient, downtempo and organic electronic music, Soul Safari explores the space between sound, atmosphere and emotion.
 Based in the self-built studio ‘The Mycelium Cave’, surrounded by nature and community, Soul Safari weaves atmospheric textures, organic rhythms and cinematic depth into evolving stories. Moving through stillness, vast synthesizer landscapes and deep grooves, each set unfolds organically, inviting listeners to slow down, reconnect and become fully present in the unfolding moment.
 Ambient • Downtempo • Organic Electronica

profiles.name: Kaje Row
profiles.description:
Through Melodies and harmonies Tales unfold, with triumph and strife.
 Through Bass and Drums, Reality holds.
 To guide the unseen. Embrace vulnerability. Self-discovery beckons.

profiles.name: Ámika
profiles.description:
Ámika is an Amsterdam-based DJ, producer, and songwriter with Balkan roots, known for weaving organic house, tribal rhythms, and melodic textures into immersive musical journeys.
 Drawing inspiration from folklore, ritual, and nature, her sets move between deep percussion, hypnotic grooves, and atmospheric storytelling. Blending organic house, melodic electronica, and world influences, she creates a sound that feels both grounded and expansive.
 As a producer and vocalist, Ámika brings a unique sensitivity to the dance floor, approaching DJing not only as a performance but as a form of emotional storytelling.
 Her sets invite listeners into a space where movement, presence, and connection meet, balancing earthy rhythms with moments of reflection and release.

profiles.name: SanghaSang & Swierstra
profiles.description:
In 2025, Grietje and Vincent decided to join forces: SanghaSang & Swierstra was born. With Grietje (SanghaSang) on the Indian harmonium and Vincent (Swierstra) on the Indian sarangi, they offer a deeply grounding and relaxing mantra meditation, singing and sound bath session.
 As part of the Landjuweel Festival they will offer a mantra meditation and singing circle, followed by a sound bath. First you will be invited to hum, chant and sing along with different mantras and melodies, and the session will finish with you lying down and surrendering to the calming sounds of just the sarangi. You will leave the circle feeling deeply relaxed.
 There is no need to have any knowledge of (Indian/Sanskrit) mantras, just come with an open mind and a curiosity to explore your own voice and energy, the energy of the group, of the music, and of course the mantras.
 About SanghaSang & Swierstra
 Grietje offers mantra meditation sessions and singing circles in Amsterdam under the name SanghaSang. Sangha in Sanskrit means "spiritual community," and "sang" comes from "singing" in Frisian, her native language. With the name SanghaSang, she honours these roots.…

profiles.name: Polymer
profiles.description:
Polymer is Daniel Maalman, a producer, DJ and sound artist from The Netherlands, obsessed with sound in the broadest sense. From a young age Daniel was tinkering with computers and disassembling devices around the house to look how they worked. From the age of thirteen he started making electronic music with computers and tapedecks. Later, in art academy, his passion for sound evolved into building large installations, merging technology and sound, showing his sound obsession in an art context.
 Since many years Daniel moves back and forth between the world of electronic music, playing his music productions in parties and festivals as Polymer, and the world of sound art, exhibiting his creations as art installations. Performances together with visual artists and other performers has resulted in the audiovisual concept of The Polymer Experience, a liveshow where Polymers music is presented with an immersive visual show.
 In the last years he has created collaborative ‘exhibition concerts’, with his project Foundscape Orchestra, where he makes musical compositions using industrial buildings, materials and objects as a sound source.…

profiles.name: Andert Tysma
profiles.description:
Andert Tysma is a Dutch composer and producer working at the intersection of contemporary classical music, ambient electronics and immersive sound design. His work combines piano, modular synthesis and cinematic textures into deeply atmospheric compositions that explore memory, transformation and the emotional landscapes that shape our lives.
 Following his albums Children of Trinoom (2022) and Hana (2024) on Apollo/R&S Records, Tysma returns in 2026 with Parallels, his third full-length album on R&S Records. The album reflects on the lives we live and the lives we don't, embracing the complexity of a life shaped by choice, chance and circumstance. It explores the unseen possibilities that accompany every decision and the many versions of ourselves that remain just beyond reach. Moving between darkness and light, tension and resolution, Parallels traces how identity is gradually formed through the accumulation of choices, while remaining open to the mystery of all that could have unfolded differently. 
 A seasoned musician, Tysma spent years touring internationally before focusing on his own artistic practice.…

profiles.name: Rhei x Kahateka
profiles.description: Rhei and Kahateka are the driving forces behind the House of Chi Collective. In addition to functional cocktails and adaptogenic drops, these "birds of paradise" also offer mellow downtempo music for a more relaxed atmosphere.

profiles.name: Rodmer
profiles.description:
Rodmer doesn't just play music — he curates feeling. His sets move fluidly between ethereal melodic soundscapes and hypnotic downtempo grooves, weaving a sonic thread that pulls strangers into a shared moment. Whether you're losing yourself on the dancefloor or simply letting the music breathe through you, Rodmer's selections find the space between thought and movement — and live there. Rodmer loves playing in spaces where music and atmosphere become one. (Wildeburg, Into the Woods, Embassy of the Free Mind, Oosterkerk, Mandali, Italy)
 Rodmer runs Studio Rodmer en is part of The Experience Enhancers collective.

profiles.name: JRK x Maud
profiles.description:
JRK.XLSX recently entered the electronic music scene and quickly found itself on festival stages. He's a big fan of his trusted CDJ-3000s & Rane MP2015, creating immersive experiences with no bigger ambition than sharing music that connects people.

Maud Sauer has been performing at Ruigoord for approximately 50 years. Since then, she has worked as an (improvising) oboist, composer, band leader, and advisor to emerging artists. More information: maudsauer.nl.

profiles.name: DeBodhi
profiles.description: Amsterdam based DJ. Expect dirty grooves, funky twists, melodic organic house with a punch of indie dance.

profiles.name: Trippin Jaguar (live)
profiles.description:
Trippin Jaguar is a Dutch organic house and downtempo artist performing both live and DJ sets operating in the space between deep listening and psychedelic club culture. Using synthesizers, live instrumentation and real-time improvisation, he creates slow-burning psychedelic journeys for immersive dance floors and sunset stages.
 Emerging from the European downtempo and psychedelic scene, he has performed worldwide for more than a decade, with highlights including Burning Man, Ozora Festival and Boom Festival, and many international club and festival shows, known for guiding audiences across a wide arc, from deep meditative passages to driving rhythmic flow without breaking the trance.

profiles.name: Bliz Nochi & Marisa Lia
profiles.description:
Bliz Nochi & Marisa Lia are a dynamic DJ duo based in Hamburg/Germany who blend genres with their unique sound, which combines organic house, acid house, and indie dance, drawing strong influences from traditional and ethnic music from around the world. Their high-energy sets, filled with off-beat rhythms and intense grooves, create dancing and intellectual stimulation.

In addition to their joint performances, both Bliz Nochi and Marisa Lia perform solo DJ shows, but when they collaborate as Bliz Nochi & Marisa Lia B2B, it showcases their musical synergy and the limitless possibilities of their collaboration.

With appearances at notable festivals like Fusion Festival (DE), Amsterdam Dance Event (NL), and MS Dockville (DE), and at renowned venues including Golden Pudel Club (DE), Scorpios Mykonos, and Habitas and Nômade Tulum, Bliz Nochi & Marisa Lia have established a reputation for creating diverse, euphoric experiences that challenge and stimulate listeners, igniting the dance floor with every beat.

profiles.name: Laura Sestri x Rhei
profiles.description:
Since 2017, Tim and Laura have been exploring the same magical ecosystems of festivals, art, music, and free play. What began as a love/friendship has grown into a long-term creative partnership, fueled by late-night dance floors, Ruigoord adventures, and a shared passion for authentic human connection.

His sets are warm, adventurous, and connecting – a musical journey where playfulness meets depth.
Tim and Laura have previously performed together at Artlake Festival, the Tropenmuseum, and various cultural and musical venues.
For Landjuweel 2026, they bring this special chemistry to Slooase. A tribute to friendship, freedom, and the art of creating a parallel world together.

profiles.name: Crescone
profiles.description: Crescone creates electronic music that explores the connection between humans and nature. His sound blends world music, downtempo, dub, ambient, house, and techno. By using field recordings, instruments, and synthesizers, he creates soundscapes that feel both earthy and dreamy.

profiles.name: House of Chi Live
profiles.description:
At House of Chi Jam, electronic music and live improvisation flow seamlessly together. DJs provide the musical foundation, while instrumentalists weave melodies, rhythms, and textures into the music, creating a constantly evolving soundscape.

Each session unfolds organically, driven by listening, intuition, and connection. Rooted in musical traditions from around the world, but always focused on the dance floor, House of Chi Jam invites both musicians and audiences to experience an immersive event where no moment is ever the same.

profiles.name: Ed Noodle
profiles.description:
Hypnotizing melodies, bass and percussion blended in a deep tribal mix, that’s what gets Ed Noodle going. From downtempo sets at sunset or sunrise, to an energetic train rolling you into the night: in any case the goal will be to keep you dancing with prehistoric power.
 Ed is at his best when he takes the time to tell a story, relying on the right tracks and buildup for that exact place and moment in time. A craft he honed at gatherings like AfrikaBurn and Midburn, which fit his sound and energetic presentation like a glove. This brought him to places like The Gardens of Babylon Festival, Garbicz, GOAT in Portugal, KitKatClub and Acid Bogen at KATER in Berlin, Secret Brunch in London and Harmonizz Festival in Israel.
 Besides being a DJ, Ed also releases his own music on more and more labels and is co-founder of Wolvenroedel, a party pack that he started with YOИDI to get people dancing. And last but not least, he came up with The Noodle Lounge. It's a beautiful decorated stage at festivals where you can relax and shift back some gears, while you're sitting or lying down and enjoying some soothing downtempo music. And yes, some hot noodles to make the comfort level even bigger.

profiles.name: Rochelle Dietz
profiles.description:
Rochelle Dietz' sets are a reflection of her gentle, yet dynamic spirit — capturing the essence of melodic, introspective moments while unleashing bursts of energy when the time is right. She brings a distinct perspective, blending rich melodies with unpredictable, captivating rhythms which keep the dancefloor alive.

Rochelle has collaborated with events such as Wildeburg, Into the Woods, Gardens of Babylon and Landjuweel, and is occasionally found behind the decks in bars and clubs in Berlin. She is an active member of the Utrecht creative community and her concept, Birdcage Radio, provides a platform for young and established artists to flourish.

profiles.name: Meraque
profiles.description:
Mèraque originates from a passion for Balkan music and curiosity about its fusion with psychedelic sounds and Middle Eastern influences. The musicians Jorick Bronius, Martin Grozdanoski, Onur Kaletas, and Talal Fayad met each other at the Rotterdam Conservatory and share a background in world music and sound design. Their collaboration results in an energetic and refined sonic universe where Eastern melodies and Mediterranean rhythms come together.

Mèraque has performed at venues and festivals such as Wildeburg (NL), Lost Gipsy Land (Bulgaria), 3000grad Festival (DE) and Fusion Festival (DE), and released music through labels like Ohxala Records, Harabe, and Krooks Records.

profiles.name: I Love You, Cacao! – Opening Ceremony
profiles.description:
Temple of Self opens Landjuweel with "I Love You, Cacao!", a warm cacao ceremony for everyone who makes the temple possible. We come together to consciously begin the festival, set intentions, and reflect on all the people who have built, supported, and contributed to the program. Temple of Self is a special stage at Landjuweel. Currently, we are working on a crowdfunding campaign to build a permanent temple. During the opening ceremony, there will be a model of the new temple, which can be viewed in scale 1:10. Visitors are welcome to attend, drink cacao, and contribute financially to the realization of this space through donations.

The ceremony is co-created. Visitors and involved makers who want to showcase themselves, for example by singing a song, are warmly invited to participate. The goal is to open the festival from a place of connection, gratitude, and shared intention.
Limited space, cacao from Spirit of Cacao

profiles.name: Taste of Tantra (Authentic Relating) by Tamara Groen
profiles.description:
Amidst the music, colors, and encounters of the festival, we invite you to slow down. To truly feel who is standing before you… and who you are in this moment. Through playful exercises, presence, and genuine attention, we will discover how connection arises when nothing needs to be proven or achieved. A gentle journey towards greater authenticity, vibrancy, and human connection. Come as you are. Leave with more connection... with yourself, and with the people around you.

Tamara Groen
Tamara Groen has spent over 20 years exploring and sharing what it truly means to be alive — in theory, through the body, breath, and raw human experience.
Her mission is to awaken the quiet power that lives inside each person — to bring people home to themselves, so that connection and peace can ripple outward into the world.
She believes that when people feel connected to themselves and each other, peace becomes natural. Her mission is to help make that happen — one breath, one person, one moment at a time.
After a fast-paced career in international business and a life that looked "successful" from the outside, Tamara hit a breaking point.…

profiles.name: Ecstatic Dance with Dj B.ART
profiles.description:
Hey! I am Bart aka B.Art and I am very excited to bring you an ecstatic dance journey.
 My philosophy around Ecstatic Dance is to craft an intentional journey. Every journey tells a different story, and every journey can be embodied in the dancers own, unique, way. During this journey I invite the dancer to explore their authentic self-expression through movement, sound and to be with whatever arises in the moment. Come explore with me, together as one!
 Expect a musical adventure that carries you through highs and lows, inspired by nature, rooted in a tribal base and infused with sounds and styles that wander across the globe

profiles.name: DJ FeelofSofie
profiles.description:
FeelofSofie combines the warm, earthy sounds of organic house with the rebellious spirit of indie dance and the deep rhythms of progressive house. She carefully selects her intuitive melodies and captivating basslines. Let her take you on a journey without a destination, but one that you are guaranteed to enjoy.

Sofie has a warm, open, and curious personality. Although she is generally calm, you can feel her energy come alive when she's passionate about something. During her DJ sets, there is often a special atmosphere: an exchange of energy with the audience that you must experience yourself. Her sensitivity is one of her greatest strengths; she uses it to connect with the audience, create a sense of community, and invite them on a shared journey. For Sofie, music is one of the most beautiful ways to explore life at a deeper level. Music opens doors for her that would otherwise remain closed. 'Feel-of-Sofie' is a philosophy.

profiles.name: DJ Y∆RL b2b Kahmeya
profiles.description:
DJ YARL
 YARL, also known as JF.HECTOR, is an Amsterdam-based DJ operating in the space between electronic club music and ritual movement. His sets unfold slowly and with intention, blending organic percussion, global influences, and electronic grooves into long-form movement journeys that support release rather than spectacle. Drawing from a background in film music and underground club culture, YARL brings a strong sense of pacing, atmosphere, and narrative into his sets. He plays for dancers, not for attention, with a focus on flow, depth, patience, and shared moments of euphoria. YARL performs at events such as Ambrosia, Free Flow, Maankaravaan, Landjuweel and Ecstatic Dance Festival Holland. Under his alias JF.HECTOR, he explores a deeper club-oriented sound rooted in hypnotic techno and leftfield house, with appearances at Radion, Maanzin Ruigoord, Radio Radio, Sober Clubbing and The Muddshow.
 Kahmeya
 Kahmeya is a Dutch DJ, producer and vocalist with a sound that moves through indie dance, organic house, tribal grooves and live vocals.…

profiles.name: Morning Qi Gong Session by Maarten Collewijn
profiles.description: Maarten Collewijn offers Qi Gong sessions based on an ancient practice that brings body and mind into balance through postures, movements, breathing, and mindful presence. The classes focus on enhancing vitality, health, and inner calm, allowing Qi (life energy) to flow freely through the body once again. A wonderful way to start the day!

profiles.name: Ecstatic Dance with DJ JERA
profiles.description:
As an Ecstatic Dance DJ, Jera aims to cultivate your natural ecstasy and love for life.

At the opening, he will provide you with tools to deepen your dance journey through breath, movement, and connection. With Psychedelic Ambient, Tribal Trance, Bass, and World Music, his set creates a journey that invites surrender, expression, and connection.
Since the age of 19, Jera has been organizing monthly Ecstatic Dances and several festivals annually at his home base, Ottermeer Estate. His passion is to bring people together, create magical experiences, stimulate personal growth, and share the beauty of life.
Driven by his love for dance, he also started DJing, to take people on a journey that can be both profound and playful. For his first time at the Temple of Self, he comes with a suitcase full of love, a generous dose of bass, and the intention to create an unforgettable celebration together.

profiles.name: Reed Jewel for Voice Preservation with Yente & Ocean
profiles.description: We are Yente and Ocean, two passionate creatives and enthusiastic conversationalists. With the Riet(j)uweel for Voice Preservation, we're setting the tone so you can enjoy your voice throughout the entire weekend. We won't reveal too much, but be sure to bring your water bottle! We look forward to welcoming you at The Temple of Self!

profiles.name: DJ Yellow Rose
profiles.description: A techno set featuring high-energy melodic techno, tasty acid sounds, progressive and happy house bangers, indie dance music, and occasional bursts of organic, driving beats. A two-hour long, irresistible, intense, and utterly fantastic experience!

profiles.name: Klam: DJSelene b2b Daktari
profiles.description: Night has fallen, but the coolness is nowhere to be found. It's Klam. Here, your inner animal can roam free. DJ Selene and Daktari move from darkness to light, from water to fire, from comfort to raw energy, and back again. Male strength and female divinity. Be yourself on these beats with benefits.

profiles.name: Sound Medicine Journey with Tessa & Sjoerd
profiles.description: Sjoerd's musical background and Tessa's yoga and meditation experience brought this couple together, introducing them to the magic of healing sounds. They have immersed themselves in a Sound Medicine training on Bali and are eager to share their knowledge and experience with an audience in the Netherlands. Join us on a journey through healing sounds.

profiles.name: Storytelling meditation by Simone Bijlard with live handpan music by Reo Matsumoto.
profiles.description:
Simone Bijlard studied architecture in Delft. While useful, she quickly discovered that it wasn't nearly as enjoyable as writing and drawing. She therefore soon made the (partial) switch to Kinderboekland, where she still feels perfectly at home.

Her book "Dwars door Amsterdam" takes readers on a journey through the city in a way that you would never find with a regular map: using cross-sections.

At Landjuweel, Simone reads children's poems accompanied by the musical accompaniment of musician Reo Matsumoto. In this way, they share their mutual enjoyment of language, rhythm, and imagery with an audience of all ages.

profiles.name: Blindfolded Self Connection with Josefine Adriana
profiles.description:
Eliminate outer judgement and comparison to drop into deeper connection with your true authentic self. A moment to slow down, to make space and to welcome all there is with compassion and love. Exploring your inner world, how does it feel to be 'you' in this moment?
 Josefine Adriana
 Josefine Adriana brings deep presence and awareness into her work, weaving together holistic therapy, trauma-sensitive embodiment, mindfulness and spirituality. She supports others in shedding layers and reconnecting to a deeper sense of inner freedom.
 Her own journey through dark and life changing moments led her to explore a wide range of healing modalities. It became clear that living from the heart, and trusting the innate wisdom of the body, was essential to truly feel alive.
 With a calm and grounded energy, Adriana holds transformative spaces where others can safely explore their inner landscapes. She invites students into deeper connection, while encouraging trust in their unique rhythm and the capacity to meet whatever arises.

profiles.name: Ecstatic Dance & Breathflow with DJ Y∆RL & Eline Bouma
profiles.description:
Temple of Self invites you to Ecstatic Dance & Breathflow with DJ Y∆RL and Eline Bouma. Eline will open the space with breathwork: gentle, clear, and precise enough to ground yourself before the music takes over. Then, Y∆RL will guide the dance in a long journey from earth to sky, incorporating organic percussion, global influences, and electronic grooves.

DJ YARL
Y∆RL is an Amsterdam-based DJ who operates at the intersection of electronic club music and ritual movement. His sets unfold slowly and with intention, blending organic percussion, global influences, and electronic grooves into long journeys that support release rather than spectacle. Drawing from a background in film music and underground club culture, Y∆RL brings a strong sense of pacing, atmosphere, and narrative to his sets. He plays for dancers, not for attention, focusing on flow rather than peak-after-peak drops. The atmosphere is warm, patient, and deep, while leaving room for familiar tracks and shared moments of lightness. Y∆RL regularly performs at Ecstatic Dance events such as Ambrosia, Free Flow, Maankaravaan, and festivals including Landjuweel and Ecstatic Dance Festival Holland.…

profiles.name: Shake Your Booty with DJ Dragonslayer
profiles.description: With "Booty" as its overarching theme, DJ Dragonslayer will be playing music for dancing and shaking your hips. This includes Afrobeat, Favela Funk, R&B bangers, and everything in between.

profiles.name: Live Music Parlour with Lowfire & Liza Weald
profiles.description:
After the sculpture route, take a journey with Liza and Low Fire. They will guide you on an intuitive, live ambient experience. With hushed vocals, subtle soundscapes, and music that emerges in the moment, Liza & Pip Robin (Low Fire) create a soft, connected atmosphere in the Temple of Self. Come and let them carry you away.

LOWFIRE
Music that moves like wind over dunes; scratching guitars and ethereal voices that turn horizons into dreamscapes.
Located in Amsterdam, they get their sound from the clash between city life and cold seas, dunes, and misty meadows. They live like hybrids, looking into another dimension. Lowfire, right on the intersection; the end of a big flame and the start of a new one. You feel the smoothing heat, playing through their chords and melodies.
Location: Amsterdam based
Band setup: Solo, duo, trio to full band
Live setup: Guitars, drum, bass, vocals
Genre: Ambient folk
Style: Slow-burn, cinematic, immersive


Liza Weald (Lisa de Bruijn) is a musical alchemist who combines sounds from folk, pop & new age into captivating songs. Her angelic voice has a huge range and, in combination with the hopeful & catchy lyrics, she creates pure magic…

profiles.name: Temple Night with Ambient Music & Intential Tea Ceremony (Ocean, Yente & Emmy-K)
profiles.description:
After the sculpture route, Temple of Self transitions into a peaceful temple night. Ocean and Yente guide an intentional tea ceremony to gradually approach sunrise.

Sensual, positive, consent-first, with space for tea, cushions, cuddling, cacao, and genuine connection. The temple is Saturday night the place to reconnect with yourself and others if you can no longer see the boogie forest through the willows. The Temple of Self DJs play a set to help you unwind.

profiles.name: Light concert with Liza Weald & Pip Robin
profiles.description: An intimate acoustic concert featuring the clear vocals of Liza Weald, inviting you to slow down and find stillness. Expect warm, heartfelt songs that resonate without many words. At the end of the concert, Liza and Pip Robin (Low Fire) will perform a few collaborative pieces together.

profiles.name: Discover Yourself Through Art with Fleur Cecile Haak
profiles.description:
My name is Fleur Cecile. I am a writer, artist, and guide for authentic self-expression through creativity. I invite you into an intuitive journey of creative expression, a space to soften, connect, and remember your creative potential. Through art, words, color, and presence, we open the door to the essence of our being: the beauty and wisdom already living within us. Out of the mind and into the heart, creative expression reminds us of what words could never explain. It became my way home to myself, and now I love sharing that space with others. Let’s be colourful. Let’s play.
 Links:
 Website 
 Instagram

profiles.name: Spirit Songs door Anouk Anansi
profiles.description:
Anouk Anansi shares gentle, meditative heart songs, guided by piano and voice. More than a performance, this is an invitation for an inner journey and to remember your wholeness.
 Especially for Landjuweel, she will be joined by Rafa Beltrão on guitar.

profiles.name: Ecstatic Dance with Richie Lee
profiles.description: These days, he is mostly known for the Ambrosia sound, which was partly shaped during the early years of his DJ career. He actually started out playing dancehall, reggaeton, bubbling afro, and other urban styles. For this set, he's taking a special trip back in time so we can shake everything loose in the carefree way we did as kids. Wild dancing & steamy grinding sessions. With consent, of course xx

profiles.name: I love you too, Cacao! – Closing Ceremony with live music from Barry Zingt
profiles.description:
I'm Cacao from "Closing Ceremonies"
Limited spaces available, Cacao by Spirit of Cacao 🍫

profiles.name: Tribal Organica with DJ Esthetica
profiles.description: Esthetica's sets feel like a musical journey in a warm bath, where you can experience a wide range of emotions. With tribal sounds, melancholic melodies, and careful arrangement, they build a story that draws you in from beginning to end. Her sound is always warm, yet often has a darker edge; both light and shadow are welcome, and can coexist. Come dance, feel, and experience the organic and progressive house beats of Esthetica at the Temple of Self.

profiles.name: Dancing with DJ Laura Jayne
profiles.description:
DJ Laura Jayne blends her warm and spicy dance floor cocktails, mixing percussive electronic beats with synthy African vibes, Brazilian & UK funk, catchy garage music, and all-time classics with a twist of global bass and tribal dubby electronica. Her sets are an international journey through time and space, and she is sure to surprise you and take you on a trip through her musical selection of gems, getting you into a free flow of embodied explosion and playfulness.

Laura is known for her energizing global groove sets that she plays at festivals with DJ collectives such as the Bankra Bike Soundsystem and Moonshine Sound Collective, where live music is incorporated into the dance sets. As a co-founder of FreeFlow, she loves hosting festival stages with musician and DJ families at venues such as Tivoli, Paradiso, Couleur Cafe (Brussels), and Zwarte Cross.

profiles.name: Foelie & Freude (Temple Closing Set)
profiles.description:
Folie & Freude might seem similar at first glance, but it was the music that truly brought them together. As they each pursued their own musical paths, the friends gradually discovered a shared passion for a distinctive downtempo sound. It was inevitable that these musical brothers would eventually combine their talents.

Prepare yourself for a set filled with raw, rolling basslines and infectious rhythms – somewhere between laid-back and full throttle. This is downtempo with soul, funky grooves, and an energy that will stay with you long after the last beat has faded away.

profiles.name: Temple of Self Art Route
profiles.description: Rolf Huijgen, known as Visual Hacker, brings music to life through light, color, and motion. His visuals move with the rhythm of the dance, translating sound into vivid, evolving worlds that invite people deeper into their journey. During Landjuweel, Rolf tunes into the energy of the space — blending intuition with craft to create visuals that both ground and uplift. His style balances organic flow with digital precision, weaving atmosphere, emotion, and rhythm into a unified experience. With roots in digital art and live performance, Rolf sees visuals as a form of connection — a way to turn sound into sight, and presence into shared movement

profiles.name: Kirtan & Mantra Singing Circle
profiles.description: Kirtan Crew loves sharing mantras and kirtan with everyone. They have rich musical backgrounds: Bastiaan plays guitar and sings; he studied Composition & Sound Design and played worldwide as DJ/producer. Kim plays harmonium and sings; she is part of a choir and also plays ukelele and various shamanic instruments. Aravinda is a rhythm master and plays dholak and other Indian percussion in devotional ensembles. Experience an uplifting kirtan journey: soulful mantra singing, deep driving harmonium and funky Indian percussion. They weave ecstatic chants, joyful melodies and relaxing stillness into a powerful musical journey. Join their gathering to sing, dance, laugh, enjoy and open your heart.

profiles.name: Temple DJ's
profiles.description:
A slight change in our little gem: Unfortunately, DJ Shivanki will not be able to join us on Friday from 02.00 to 05.00.

However, Temple is undeterred. Sometimes the route changes when you're already on your way. Our Temple DJs step up, delve deep into the furthest corners of their USBs, and bring everything that has been waiting for exactly this moment for years.
Prepare yourself for a whirlwind night. A musical relay race without a map, carried by the people, this place, this moment, and everything that happens between 2am and sunrise.
Those who seek the path will miss the turn.

profiles.name: Workshop Resonance Movement Playground
profiles.description:
Resonance Movement is a practice designed to grow in embodying yourself fully through dance and movement. We use somatic exercises to step beyond the patterns that our bodies are familiar with, and bring intention and meaning into our dance. Together, this will form a toolbox for the situations in life to connect to our anger, sadness, joy, or just to feel grounded in the body. Expression is the most powerful tool for processing emotions.
 In the Resonance Movement Playground, we find lightness in everything that we are moving with. We work on our body awareness while connecting to others, expressing and playing. In these exercises, we will flow through limitations, embody strange characters, move through vulnerability, and most of all have fun together. We dance with lightness, presence and intention.

profiles.name: Workshop Playfight
profiles.description: Elena Zanchetta is a certified Tangi Playfight Facilitator, massage therapist, and NVC mediator based in Amsterdam. Rooted in contemporary dance, partner movement, and Non-violent Communication, she brings together strength, softness, and play to create spaces where people can connect beyond words. Together with Joris, she runs Playfight Amsterdam — a growing community of weekly circles and workshops for anyone curious about embodied, playful connection.

profiles.name: Workshop Playful Circus Acrobatics
profiles.description: Climb aboard your partner like a wobbly human ladder, balance with buddy hugs, and lift with loving care for each other's bodies. Unlock wild moves you didn't know existed, build mega-trust through fizzy fun, and co-create a mini circus show that'll have everyone grinning.

profiles.name: Workshop FreePlay Movement
profiles.description:
TEACHER
 For thirty years, former Olympic athlete Virgil Spier* built incredible explosive power. Yet, his elite fitness came with a painful paradox: he could sprint 40km/h and move massive weights, but couldn't sit through a movie or walk for an hour without aching pains. His athletic mastery had trapped him in a shrinking physical cage.
 Determined to reclaim his body, Virgil sought out world-class teachers and discovered a profound truth: true health isn't just about extreme physical capacity, but the continuous cultivation of physical options for everyday life.
 Today, as the co-founder of Movement Based Athletics, Virgil has traded grim, repetitive discipline for joyful exploration and research. His practice bridges the gap between athletic conditioning and play. By replacing traditional exercises with improvisation, communicative partnering, play fighting, and out of the box thinking, Virgil uses movement as the ultimate school of life, helping people build resilience, move with grace, and stay adaptable for the long haul.
 Practice
 Virgil’s Movement practice always revolved around creating options, autonomy and elastic capacity while never compromising on longevity.…

profiles.name: Workshop Playful Partner Yoga
profiles.description: In pairs (and sometimes with the whole group) we explore using our bodies in different shapes to awaken, create space, and activate in a playful way. Expect deep stretches, deep laughs and maybe making some new friends ✨✨

profiles.name: Algarabía - Bardo Teatro Físico
profiles.description:
The company started in 2016, with the objetive of creating a play that combines circus techniques with physical theater.
 The artistic searches of the creators lead to the same path, making the fusion between the theater and the circus, our scenic language. We are interested in moving from a performance show to a theatrical poetics and that circus resources and skills are deeply linked with a dramaturgy. In this dynamic, acrobats are modified by the text and transcend the purely physical.

profiles.name: Rave Baby - Sandra Kaas
profiles.description:
Sandra Kaas is a surrealist costume and prop designer, and she has now created her first theatrical show.

"Rave Baby" is an absurdist puppet theatre performance about a pregnant woman who fears that motherhood will mean the end of her rave life.

profiles.name: I dreamt I had hairy teeth - Humo y Polvo
profiles.description:
In a strange, surreal world, two women confront their inner beast – the one they have learned to keep quiet but which still weighs on them. Between explosion and rebirth, they travel an intimate path of acceptance and rebellion. An acrobatic and visual performance constructed as a journey of thought that moves forward and backward, sharing and transforming. Driven by a shared passion for acro-dance and visual arts, Morgane Maret and Martina Calvo question in their first duo ‘I dreamt I had hairy teeth’ the representations and stereotypes of women in society.

CREDITS
Concept & performers : Morgane Maret, Martina Calvo
Sound design: Gasper Piano
Light design: Valentin Boucq
Technician: Emile Warny
Outside eye: Mayra Bosshard, Maria Clara Villa-Lobos
Design of the hairy thing: Ruben Aranda, Harold Cuellar
Supported by: la maison des cultures et de la cohésion sociale de Molenbeek, le centre culturel Wolubilis, le PALC, Passages Transfestival, la CicaS, Station Circus, le théâtre du Jura, Pianofabriek, la Maison culturelle Ath, ESAC, la Roseraie, Prohelvetia, la ville de Delémont et la compagnie SCOM.

Links:
Website
Youtube
Instagram

profiles.name: Pote CompLote - Las Corpa
profiles.description:
Pote CompLote is a cabaret format that brings together multidisciplinary women circus artists in a collective act of creation. Through their individual artistic research and performance practices, the artists affirm and share their perspectives on emancipation, visibility, and the social role of women in the contemporary circus landscape.
 Guided by the external eye of Las Corpa, this cabaret transforms the performance space into a playground for experimentation, encounter, and expression. Each act reflects a unique artistic voice while contributing to a collective narrative of strength, creativity, and expression.
 This edition features: Rocio Patricio — Fixed Trapeze
 Ana Al Bitar — Aerial Rope
 Rachele Monfort — Cyr Wheel
 Valentina Pace — Hula Hoop Juggling
 A vibrant celebration of contemporary circus, female artistry, and collective creation, Pote CompLote invites audiences into a shared space where personal stories, physical virtuosity, and poetic imagery come together in a unique experience.
 Valentina Pace
 Originally from Chile, Valentina discovered circus arts at the age of 16.…

profiles.name: The Long Circumferential Movement - Ointment
profiles.description: The Dutch indie band Zalf releases narrative lyrics in an unpredictable musical landscape. At Landjuweel, they perform with a small, acoustic ensemble and invite various guest artists for a poetic journey that consists solely of detours – along birthday parties, whispering stones, and enigmatic shadow figures. Their debut album, "De Lange Omtrekkende Beweging" (The Long Winding Movement), is therefore an encouragement to keep searching. After all, there's still plenty of time!

profiles.name: Cronos - Bardo Teatro Físico
profiles.description:
The company started in 2016, with the objetive of creating a play that combines circus techniques with physical theater.
 The artistic searches of the creators lead to the same path, making the fusion between the theater and the circus, our scenic language. We are interested in moving from a performance show to a theatrical poetics and that circus resources and skills are deeply linked with a dramaturgy. In this dynamic, acrobats are modified by the text and transcend the purely physical.

profiles.name: Mantra Kirtan
profiles.description:
Matthieu and Marlies invite you on a chanting journey!

Come join us for the opening evening of our wonderful festival… A good start is half the battle!
Inspiration and fun will go hand-in-hand this evening in the magical tea garden... See you there!

profiles.name: Divine Kirtan
profiles.description:
Growing up in a spiritual family in India, Kirtan has been a natural part of my life since birth. Rooted in the traditions of Bhakti Yoga, I developed a deep connection to devotional music and mantra meditation from an early age.
 In 2024, I began leading Kirtans and founded Divine Kirtan in The Hague. Since then, our community has grown from intimate gatherings in yoga studios to large-scale Kirtan festivals welcoming 150–200 participants. Through these events, we aim to share the authentic spirit and traditional roots of Bhakti Yoga, creating spaces for connection, meditation, joyful devotional singing and community
 Through Divine Kirtan, we regularly organize Kirtan gatherings across the Netherlands. Whether you are new to Kirtan or have been practicing for years, you are warmly invited to join us in chanting, connecting, and experiencing the transformative power of Bhakti.
 Come experience the joy of Kirtan with us at Ruigoord and other inspiring venues throughout the Netherlands.

profiles.name: Sulo
profiles.description: Under the name Sulo, Merel Hegenbart (vocals) and Job van der Linden (guitar) will take you on a journey through a melancholic landscape where intimacy and soul meet.

profiles.name: Hatha Yoga
profiles.description:
gage and relax. Breathe, connect with your body and soul. Feel what is, surrender to being.

Wonderful, relaxing, gentle yoga to recharge your energy 😇

profiles.name: MakaAno – Voice of the Soul
profiles.description:
Music from the heart.

Simple songs can emerge, surprisingly powerful.
With vocals, guitar, and various other instruments, rhythm, and improvisation, Wijnand, Sylvia, and Ali create a warm and intimate atmosphere where music connects people and brings them together.
Let yourself be swept away by the magic of the moment.

profiles.name: Josje & the Catalyst
profiles.description:
A unique dance set where DJ, live guitar and live vocals merge into one flowing performance.
 The Catalyst (Camillo) builds a hypnotic flow of organic, tribal beats, woven together with world fusion and mystical Arabic influences. With his live guitar playing, he adds a unique layer of depth that enriches the set while also lifting it to new heights.
 Josje’s intuitive live vocals bring a mystical and powerful layer into the set, adding an extra dimension to the music. Her voice tunes into the energy of the dance floor and translates it in the moment into sound and expression.
 Together they create a living set: deep, earthy, and immersive. It is inviting you to sink in, open up, and let your body move. From pulsing tribal rhythms to soulful sounds, flowing towards a gentle, musical landing at the end.

profiles.name: Cedric Vermue ft Tjalle Rens
profiles.description: Cedric Vermue creates neo-classical and ambient music, where piano, strings, and electronic elements blend together to create a mesmerizing soundscape. Together with cellist Tjalle Rens, they perform a live ambient set at Landjuweel in Theo's tea garden, featuring piano and cello mixed with electronics and synthesizers.

profiles.name: Simone Alyssa
profiles.description:
Simone Alyssa is the artist who transforms music into medicine.
She effortlessly moves between the ceremonial and the celebratory — between prayer and pure joy. Her performances are not just concerts, but immersive experiences where connection is at the heart. Come listen, sing, or dance along — everyone is welcome.
Through storytelling, poetry, and her original songs in Dutch, English, and Spanish, she brings hope, inspiration, and love to her audience. Her superpower is channeling spontaneous Heart Songs — born in the moment and touching straight into the soul.
Rooted in Aalsmeer and shaped by her creative years on the Caribbean and Balearic islands, she carries a unique blend of humanity and magic. She loves people, and she loves sharing the magic of the present moment through music.
Inspired by artists like Fia, Nessi Gomes, Sam Garrett, J.J. Cale, and Nick Mulvey, she loves sharing her music with those who are ready to discover something new.
With her book, album, and ceremonial concerts, she passionately shares the message of The Soul’s Masterplan, inspiring you to turn your own dreams into reality. Together, we can remember what paradise on Earth feels like.

profiles.name: Flamenco – Everything is Colorful
profiles.description:
Todo es de color consists of:
Nancy Polderman; vocals, dance & flute
Jan Bianchi; guitar and
Samu Ramphal; percussion

We create Flamenco-Fusion music inspired by artists such as Paco Lucia, Vicente Amigo, and Lole & Manuel, with additional Latin influences from Alex Cuba and Silvio Rodriguez.

profiles.name: Florelei
profiles.description:
Florelei is a captivating artist who combines melancholic dark folk with dreamlike atmospheres, woven from the warm textures of voice and guitar. At the heart of it all is a voice of rare and arresting beauty, one that carries equal weight in a barely audible whisper and a soaring, gut-wrenching cry.
The music is steeped in a fascination with life, death, time, nature, and existential questions. Each song feels like a doorway into an inner world, inviting stillness and depth. Performances are more than concerts; they are moments of intimate connection, a shared journey into the depths of emotion and thought, balancing melody and chaos, light and dark.

profiles.name: Fire & Cacao Circle
profiles.description:
Opening Ceremony Thursday
 We gather around the fire to open this celebration of Life ~ sharing the sacred medicine of the heart (Kakaw) and becoming one same song and prayer as we share our offerings and gratitude to the Earth and the land.
 Closing Ceremony Sunday
 We gather around the fire to close this celebration of Life ~ sharing the sacred medicine of the heart (Kakaw) and becoming one same song and prayer as we share our offerings and gratitude to the Earth and the land.
 Bios of all the facilitators:
 Aliix Balam, Human. Earth Guardian. Remembering and bridging ancient practices into this time-space. His path as an ethnobiologist led to scientific work with the FAO/UN on ecosystem approach to fisheries management, which evolved into over a decade serving alongside Mayan communities on permaculture, agroecology, and food sovereignty. Today, Aliix serves as a holistic educator, facilitator, natural builder, weaver, dreamer and visionary at Winaq ~ School of Life and Ancestral Remembrance at Lake Atitlán, Guatemala
 Pim Grinter is a sustainable textile designer whose work is rooted in plant wisdom through an animistic lens.…

profiles.name: Yin Yoga
profiles.description:
Yin Yoga is a calm, meditative form of yoga where all poses are performed sitting or lying down on the floor and held for longer periods (3–5 minutes). This allows you to deeply work with your connective tissues and bring your nervous system to rest.

Let me guide you on a gentle, healing journey inward. During this session, we will combine deep, meditative Yin poses with singing or listening to mantras. This will help you relax, let go, and connect with your heart. A moment of stillness, surrender, and inner harmony.
Meet Tara

profiles.name: Mahfil Duo Ali Seif
profiles.description: Mahfil is a transnational duo exploring the shared roots of Indian and Persian classical music through deep listening and collective memory. While their sound may echo contemporary fusion, Mahfil’s essence lies in returning to origin, where rhythm, melody, and storytelling transcend borders. The duo brings together Ali Seif (Iran/NL), a multidisciplinary artist shaped by Persian folk and cinematic soundscapes, and Dink Rupa, a tabla player of the Lucknow gharana and longtime disciple of Pandit Swapan Chaudhuri. Grounded in classical tradition yet open in form, Mahfil creates immersive performances where ancient modes and intricate rhythms meet in a living, breathing dialogue.

profiles.name: Sumanth Manjunath & Quinten Rutgers
profiles.description: Virtuoso Indian cellist Sumanth Manjunath embarks on a musical journey in Theo's Tea Garden with singer-songwriter and contemporary troubadour, Quinton Rutgers.

profiles.name: Origin Circle
profiles.description: We gather every morning to remember our shared origins on this Earth, connecting with the medicine of the Heart of the Earth (Kakaw) and opening the day for sharing our intentions, songs and prayers.

profiles.name: Wild Grace
profiles.description:
A warm bubble of elements exists between heaven and earth.
 A place where the strength of the ground meets the freedom of the sky.
 Where bare feet touch the earth and roots grow deep into the heart.
 Wild Grace is an invitation to land fully in the present moment. To feel. To listen. To remember the voice of your soul.

 Through movement, sound, nature, and creativity, we reconnect with ourselves and with one another.
 A journey back to yourself.
 A celebration of life.
 A dance between heaven and earth.

 What Can You Expect? 
 At the heart of Wild Grace lies the powerful dialogue between live music, partnerwork, and contemporary dance theatre. The live music breathes with the performers, creating a living landscape of sound that guides every movement and emotion.
 About Ivana
 From a very young age, Ivana knew that dance was her path in life. Movement and creativity have always been her way of expressing emotions, telling stories, and connecting with the world around her.…

profiles.name: DJ PaGLI-Indian Dance Electronica
profiles.description:
PaGLi is Tritha's electronic avatar. Here, she plays her original electronic collaborations and the best tunes picked up from her travels around the world. Using the concept of Indian scales and loops, analog sounds and beats, PaGLi's intention as a DJ is to blend the best organic, pure vibes with a good selection of digital sound frequencies. "PaGLi" means crazy! And her multi-genre DJ set proves it! Her digital releases have been on Ibiza-based Sudam Music in collaboration with French techno producer Hannah Hais and Berlin-based HiFi/LoFi Records with collaborators World Wide Web & London-based Drum & Bass producers Janaka Selekta & Marzman J. She has also been featured on Chinese Man Records based in France for her highly acclaimed song "Balma". Tritha is a vocalist, composer, and music producer based out of India and Europe. She has combined her highly trained Indian classical voice with genres such as Indian dance electronica, acid house, disco, dancehall reggae, drum & bass, and Bollywood retro. Tritha was also featured by "Grimes", an American sensation, on her Instagram list of the best original tracks and artists from around the world.

Links:
Website
Youtube

profiles.name: Healing ragas and mantras
profiles.description:
Tritha is a Sound Yogini, Indian classical & contemporary singer & producer and Naad yoga teacher. Naad Yoga is the discipline and knowledge of healing sound wisdom from ancient India and it's practice of singing, playing instrument or listening helps in aligning and healing the body-mind-heart-soul space. According to Hindu philosophy in Sama Veda and Naad Yoga- The Yoga of Sound, music and sound can affect the human spirit. Some ragas are used in music therapy to treat mental stress, headaches, addiction, insomnia and other health ailments. In this set, Tritha, trained in Indian classical singer for last 3 decades will be sharing a healing concert with the knowledge of mantras, chakras and Indian classical raagas or musical scales- that have certain healing benefits and therapeutic properties. Perfect as a hangover concert!!
 Links:
 Website

profiles.name: Organic Sound Group
profiles.description:
The Organic Sound Group performs an integrated sound performance with poetry for and by the butterfly garden, for the plants, for the animals, and for all the beautiful beings around us. With the special sounds of the ravast, monochord, percussion, flute, kalimbas, bells, and singing bowls, the audience and artists come closer to living nature.

A tribute to everything that grows here. A tribute to all the forest nymphs, elves, and goblins who live here. The group The Organic Sound Group consists of Jill Hesketh (UK), Gunilla Andersson (SE), Valentijn Steenhuis (NL), Frederic Petzoldt (NL) and guest artists.
 Links:
 Youtube

profiles.name: Gurdieff Kwartet
profiles.description:
In 2014, songwriter, singer and visual artist Ibo Bakker decides to take matters into his own hands and start performing the music that has inspired him for years. He collaborates with cellist Rani Kumar, and together they select pieces from the rich repertoire of Gurdjieff and Hartmann. These pieces are then arranged by Rani for a quartet: a string trio combined with electric and acoustic guitar, mandolin or harmonium. This results in unique interpretations of this almost 100-year-old music. While retaining the characteristic melodies, the Gurdjieff Quartet gives these magical compositions new and unheard-of soundscapes. Soon after, performances begin where the music is alternated with stories from the life and times of Gurdjieff. Meanwhile, Ibo and Rani continue to work on expanding their repertoire, paying close attention to what best translates to the quartet's instrumentation. They also seek out new stories that can be well combined with the music. In 2019, the quartet decides to record an album featuring a selection of the collected music, combined with a collection of stories and drawings by Ibo.

The Gurdjieff Quartet consists of:…

profiles.name: Job den Dulk
profiles.description:
Dark and light coming together. Stirring and feeling in a suitcase full of emotional experiences, while with every guitar note the sun rises further. Job den Dulk takes you on a journey with his soft singing and intimate guitar playing, through the flow of love, life processes, and melancholic moments.

Dark & Light is his first EP (2021), an acoustic treasure for the soul that yearns for beauty. Music that is usually small in form and arrangement, as if you find an old photo in the attic and daydream a little while remembering. A glimpse into intimate atmospheres, as if you are secretly peeking through a keyhole. Guest musicians Dick Ronteltap (flute) and Moniek de Leeuw (violin) also played on the album. He also made a live video clip of the song "Signs on the Wall" with Moniek.
In late 2022, the single "Softly Does It" was released, a cover of Ramses Shaffy. The song is enriched with piano playing by Sjaak Douma, who previously made remixes for San Holo and Nils Frahm.
On March 1, 2024, Job celebrated spring with the release of a fresh and uplifting song called "Phoenix in the Air".

profiles.name: Goey & Kirkos
profiles.description: The duo, Goey/Kyrkos, performs a selection of medieval musical pieces using traditional instruments, all in their own unique and improvisational style, complemented by the dancing and singing of Fatima and Sabouschka.

profiles.name: Kagiso Universalis
profiles.description: Kagiso Universalis is a bardic troubadour. A playful and spirited life-artist and a coincidentalist, a running gig on the stage of life. As a childe he played with the English and Dutch language, and developed that into a gift of creating captivating poetry. Kagiso tried studying game design, but realised that the game of life is the most magical of all, so he decided to live fully for his art, starting to paint and write poems about all his mystical and psychedelic revelations. Kagiso honed his performance skills at open stages, and really anywhere people were willing to listen. His poetry isn’t a stage act, it’s a way of life. In 2020 he fell asleep from a treetop and lost his leg as a result, after surviving that accident, he had even more wisdom and love for life to share. To this day, Kagiso exudes that childlike playful spirit, and reminds you gently of your inner child and their long lost dreams. When Kagiso tells you one of his tales of love, adventure or mystical magic, you’ll likely be whisked away by his playful voice, and curiosity inducing cadence.…

profiles.name: Pit Hermans
profiles.description: Cimbalompit is a multi instrumentalist. Her main instruments are cimbalom, bass guitar, violin and cajon. In 1997 she discovered the cimbalom, a beautiful multistringed instrument from the Balkan. She performs solo (world music and impro) and in various formations: Duo Kefi (balkan/Greek/Irish/Breton), Duo Dragoste (Romanian and more), voice trio Jubel & Traan (Dutch&world) and Barrio del Mundo (rumba/flamenco/Cuban). Also she’s organising concert&jam events in Amsterdam west.

profiles.name: Mehmet Polat
profiles.description:
Mehmet Polat is a master ud player, composer, ensemble leader and soloist who tours internationally. From Africa to India, Persia to the Balkans, Contemporary to Jazz, he has been combining various musical genres with his Alevi Spiritual, Anatolian Folk and Ottoman classical music background. Through his improvisations, compositions and his own take on a variety of musical styles, Mehmet is constantly searching for new musical paths and inspiration. After many years of research, he invented his own technique to advance beyond the limitations of his traditional instrument. With this new left hand technique, he can play more advanced and modern pieces. He has also designed an ud with two extra bass strings to broaden the range and function of his instrument.
 Links:
 Website 
 Youtube 
 Facebook 
 Spotify 
 Bandcamp 
 Instagram

profiles.name: Bram & Jara
profiles.description:
Bram van Langen is a songwriter based in Amsterdam who has performed over a thousand shows across Europe, from intimate venues and living rooms to renowned stages like Paradiso and Melkweg in Amsterdam. Trained as a guitarist at the Alkmaar Conservatory, his music balances technical skill with emotional depth.

Live, Bram plays on his almost hundred-year-old Swedish harp-guitar, mostly alone, but this time he will be joined by an orchestra of strings and horns. When on tour, he finds local musicians to play with him.
Inspired by artists like Nick Drake, Paul Simon, James Taylor and Paul McCartney, Bram’s music invites listeners to slow down and drift. His songs feel warm, immersive, and gently mesmerizing – a place to pause, wander, and simply listen.

Late last year, he released his first full-length album, with which he is touring all over Germany, Austria, Italy, Switzerland, Denmark, Norway and the Netherlands and Belgium.

profiles.name: Red Oak Troubadours
profiles.description:
The international trio behind the Red Oak Troubadours formed their band in early 2023, united by their shared love for traditional folk, blues, Americana, and psychedelic rock. Their music is deeply influenced by these genres and reflects a commitment to adapting the tradition of storytelling through music for the modern world.

The Red Oak Troubadours dedicate their work to fostering community and connection. Within their repertoire, they blend their music with cultural influences, spoken word, and sound scaping to create an all-encompassing experience for their listeners. Through their own songs and stories, they explore themes of struggle, hardship, dreams, and hope, aiming to resonate with their listeners on an intimate and authentic level. The Red Oak Troubadours invite you to join them on their musical journey!

profiles.name: The White Peacock Jam
profiles.description: A white peacock has been sighted in the Vlindertuin (Butterfly Garden). It has been heard reciting in French, denouncing the state of society with punk fury, and singing what, according to some, is the most beautiful song ever heard in the Vlindertuin. Rumours have circulated that this animal is attracted to musical instruments. So Arthur, your host for this event, decided to organize a jam session. Open to musicians of all levels, the only condition is no existing songs or covers. Improvised music only! Make those feathers and asses shake!

profiles.name: Roberta Petzoldt
profiles.description: Roberta Petzoldt works as a poet, theater maker, actress, and visual artist. In January 2019, her first poetry collection, *Vruchtwatervuurlinie*, was published by Uitgeverij Van Oorschot. This collection received the ‘Cees Buddingh-Prijs’. In autumn 2025, the collection *Zeebeving* was released. As an actress, she has played three leading roles in feature films. For ‘Meet me in Venice’, she won the ‘Best Actress Award’ at the ‘Woodstock film festival’. She graduated from the Gerrit Rietveld Academie, where she developed both her language and visual work on the 'Beeld en Taal' (Image and Language) department. “Through my indigenous Surinamese ancestors, I also developed a shamanistic awareness alongside my Western perspective. Rituals and myths are a living reality for me. Weaving these realities inspires my work as a visual artist and poet, which explains my need to combine different disciplines. This allows me to approach complex issues such as origin, our relationship with nature, and unity in diversity from multiple perspectives simultaneously.”

profiles.name: The Perculators
profiles.description:
Meet the Perculators, 
 This Rotterdam based 4-piece blends the soul of numerous genres into one instrumental boiling pot of funky grooves. They’ll jump from classic 60’s surf and Peruvian Cumbia to Fat funky Hiphop. Come see them play and you’ll be longing to take something home to keep your record player occupied!

profiles.name: The Underbouncers
profiles.description: The Underbouncers tunes are rooted in exploratory drum and bass, mystical melodies and funky rhythms, and combines them with ethnic drum and spirit-channeling sounds. As the two of us have been co-involved with methods and systems of analysis of the underground as mycologists and biologist, we try to channel our own underground knowledge, taking inspiration from global sounds that do the same. Biology and mycology as professions, and drums and basses as hobby. These common grounds come together and allow for creative experimentation with waves and frequencies and bring esotery through sounds.

profiles.name: Ippy
profiles.description: Ippy is trippy. If you don't know Ippy, you are not a hippie.

profiles.name: Kaleidoscope - hosted by Quinten
profiles.description: A curated musical journey where folk, hip-hop, jazz, funk, and world music come together in unique live performances and surprising musical connections.

profiles.name: Leadbeaters
profiles.description: Amsterdam-based string band that's actually way too rock 'n' roll for a folk outfit. This troupe, while not necessarily sporting beards, certainly possesses a confident and bold attitude, and has been touring the country for many years. Initially as an occasional formation for special events, they quickly transitioned to playing weekly gigs. And now, LEADBEATERS are no longer to be ignored in the local roots, folk, and Americana music scene. With a mixed grill of American traditions, old-time, rhythm & blues, bluegrass, Cajun, and some cumbia, the band offers a very danceable musical offering to the hopeless, the drummers, the poor, the troublemakers, and the outsiders. All in a rowdy fashion. The Leado's initially do this with fiddle, guitar, mandolin, double bass, and somewhat coordinated singing, but the heart and stage are large enough for passionate musical brothers and sisters.

profiles.name: Dizzy Fingers
profiles.description:
Dizzy Fingers play Gypsy Swing, but with a completely unique style. Fans of Django Reinhardt will enjoy it. The rest of the audience will be won over by the energetic, virtuoso, and very danceable sounds that the band produces.

In short: you'll find the hottest and most swinging jazz party in Landjuweel '26 at The Willow Portal. On Friday evening at 7:00 PM, we're getting started!

profiles.name: Kosmies Wilgenportaal
profiles.description: Kosmies: Your Intergalactic Home Away From Home.

profiles.name: Merlijn Nash
profiles.description: Merlijn Nash will perform a show built from the ground up especially made for Landjuweel. Inspired by artists such as James Blake and Radiohead, he’ll perform solo with an array of electronics, beats, and vocals, drawing the listener into a space between tranquil and moodiness.

profiles.name: Metik
profiles.description:
Metik is the Amsterdam–Berlin based duo of guitarist Bram Stadhouders and drummer Luca Marini. Long-time friends and seasoned performers, they bring over two decades of international touring experience into a refined blend of dreamy electronica and melodic IDM, music that engages the body as much as the mind.
 Educated in jazz and contemporary classical composition, they approach electronic music from a deeply instrumental perspective. Acoustic drums, percussion, guitars and a Bolivian charango intertwine in spacious yet grooving, evolving compositions. Balancing club-oriented pulse with concert-hall detail, their music is cinematic and atmospheric yet grounded in groove.
 Their debut album, out on Atomnation Records, was recorded in a home studio overlooking Lake Lugano. The surrounding landscape, its stillness, depth and shifting light, subtly shaped the atmosphere of the record, becoming an integral part of its sonic identity.

profiles.name: Mr Henky
profiles.description: Will dig in to his old collection of obscure minimal techno from the early 2000th. In a 2 hour set he will take you on a rare journey through the beautiful crafted sound of this rare sub genre.

profiles.name: Coffee with Jan
profiles.description:
Wake up in a cozy setting at the Kosmies Wilgenportaal with coffee, poetry, and music. Featuring Amber-Helena Reisig, Jan Modaal, Linde Suzanne, Laurien Schreuder, and Reyer Romkes.

Amber-Helena Reisig is a writer of prose, poetry, and opinion pieces. She is under contract with Uitgeverij Prometheus for both a novel, Het Sterfhuis (The House of Death), and a collection of poems. In addition to her work as a writer, she also works on social issues as a policy maker. In December 2025, she was elected "Officer of the Year 2025".
Jan Modaal writes punk ballads and will perform an acoustic set this morning.
Laurien, founder of Snowapple Collective and many creative projects worldwide, is always up for new adventures and is known for her eccentric ways as a composer, performer, and multi-instrumentalist.
Linde Suzanne brings poetry that is inextricably linked to her voice and body. Her work navigates between abstraction and monologue, between experience and image.
Reyer Romkes works at the Rijksmuseum (National Museum) and will present excerpts from his unpublished manuscript about living with cancer…

profiles.name: Nereo
profiles.description:
Nereo is a living, shifting sound - strings, machines, and motion colliding in real time.
 Working through an expanded vocabulary of cello and string instruments, Nereo shapes sound into movement, the cello's depth and beauty stretched into new forms, the charango's dreamy tone drifting alongside pulsing electronics and transformed fragments of everyday life. Boiling water breathes. Books rustle into rhythm.
 Playful yet precise, unpredictable yet deeply felt, Nereo unfolds as a landscape meant to be felt as much as heard. The music moves with you, around you, through you, inviting listeners, dancers, and dreamers into a shared space where sound becomes physical, and flows like a river.

profiles.name: Estroe
profiles.description: Esther Roozendaal, known as @estroe, is an electronic music artist who explores the boundaries of sound design and atmosphere. While she once shaped dancefloors with deep and refined techno, her focus has now shifted to creating immersive soundscapes and subtle, evolving beats—created using modular synthesizers. Her music moves between texture and rhythm, blending experimental synthesis with a deep emotional core. In her approach, she embraces nuance, space, and imperfection, bringing an organic human touch to electronic sound. In 2025, Estroe is actively performing and releasing modular music, both solo and as part of a duo with Raaf Modular. She is also the driving force behind Rosedale Records, which relaunches with a fresh vision for modular-driven productions. Beyond her own artistry, she is dedicated to coaching and education, mentoring at Music Conservatory Haarlem and integrating Acceptance and Commitment Training (ACT) into her work with students and artists navigating the pressures of the music industry. She also contributes regularly as a columnist for Interface magazine, where she reflects on music, modular performances, and the artistic process.…

profiles.name: Jungle of Wires
profiles.description: Dutch electronic performer Jungle of Wires (Mandy Bakker) always seeking for big sound with an ever changing small setup. There is a story in every patch with lots of rhythmic exploration and improvisations. No live performance is the same so prepare for a sonic adventure!

profiles.name: Koen Boeijinga
profiles.description:
In his solo performance, Koen Boeijinga creates a unique one-man-band experience, inspired by shepherd tales and musical traditions from around the world.
