His wind instruments – saxophone, flute, and bagpipes – are central, while the mesmerizing sounds of harmonium, bass drum, Indonesian gamelan, and countless other instruments fill the space. As a modern nomad, he travels through diverse musical landscapes, where myth, ritual, and improvisation blend together.

Koen Boeijinga: soprano saxophone, wind instruments, harmonium, gamelan, percussion, fx

profiles.name: Oliv Oliv
profiles.description:
Oliv Oliv is the project of composer, producer, and sound artist Olivier Terpstra. Combining synthesizers, instruments, and field recordings, he creates immersive soundscapes that balance electronic experimentation, dance, and storytelling.
 Inspired by cultural exchange, language, and memory, his music is a cinematic journey full of layered textures and evolving rhythms. Following his debut album Tehran Mountains (Uncloud Editions, 2025), he recently released Howl / زوزه showcasing a more expressive and experimental sound.
 For Landjuweel Festival, you can expect an hour-long set that brings an atmospheric and immersive sound, connecting electronic and acoustic elements in a spatial setting.
 Oliv Oliv has performed at events such as Modulation, Uncloud Festival, and Linecheck Festival in Milan, and was part of the Popronde 2025 tour.

profiles.name: JUNO
profiles.description:
JUNO is a live act based in Amsterdam that deconstructs and rebuilds dance music using tuba, saxophone, synthesizers, and drums. What starts as dreamy electronica effortlessly transitions into pumping grooves and raw energy. Is it dreamy electronica or dreamy dancing?

On stage, the clash between live musicianship and electronics is striking. The saxophone and tuba transform into full-fledged synthesizers using pedals. Tight sequences and live music blend together: every show moves and breathes. Throughout the performance, PAUW shines: their self-developed light installation that responds to every beat and gradually takes over the room. An immersive experience in sync with the music.

This year, JUNO played a midnight set at ESNS ‘26 in Groningen. Previously, the band has performed successful shows at venues like Melkweg, TivoliVredenburg, and Doornroosje. The group released their own music with Ruben Hein, played twenty shows across the Netherlands as part of Popronde ‘25, and their live show was broadcast on NPO2.

For fans of Jungle by Night, Eefje de Visser, and Weval.


JUNO is een Amsterdamse live-act die dance uit elkaar trekt en opbouwt met tuba, sax, synths en drums.…

profiles.name: Julien Chaptal
profiles.description:
Julien Chaptal is an electronic assembly - part French electronics, part Detroit synth machine, and part Dutch minimal robot (as part of MFD, Amsterdam 661 and Le Clic).
 Purpose built with a modular brain, Julien's pertormances are switched on, live, utterly groovy, and while pulsating with machine wiring, undeniably human.
 With his modular synth and microscope, Julien explores an audiovisual cosmos of micro- organisms and sound waves. The micro organisms under Julien's magnifying glass are not just any bacilli. With every movement they make, they influence the sound of the music. So they are actually unsuspecting musicians - and Julien their conductor. What emerges is a festive fusion, a real Microbe Rave!

profiles.name: Inphiknight
profiles.description: Inphiknight will take you into the depth of the night with a fine selection of mesmerizing melodies, titillating rhythms and scintillating harmonies that will weave together the pulses of the heart into a mesh of brainwaves that will dance together with the roots and the branches of the willows.

profiles.name: WTF Stage
profiles.description: Lowest stage of the western hemisphere. Share whatever the F you want. Come and join us in/on the theatre of the crazy & openhearted. Only fans surrounding you. PA, piano & mics @ your service. So bring your own instrument if you like! Be on time otherwise you miss the grounding and opening check in..

profiles.name: L’heure Classique
profiles.description:
Classical music is boring, isn’t it? Well think again, it’s not. And to prove this, Arinde links an hour of classical music to sex on this sexy Sunday. Be surprised by the most beautiful music, combined with dirty stories. Curious? Come and listen.
 Arinde Jonker
 Arinde Jonker (1990) hopped to the beats of Bach’s music in her playpen before she could stand. And she never stopped. Since 2020, Arinde has been making (radio)programs in which she presents classical music in an accessible and often alternative way. She makes you listen to classical music in a way you never did before. Classical music is groovy, revolutionary and sexy. Curious? Come and listen.

profiles.name: Cézanne
profiles.description:
Like a sunset, Cézanne opens a portal in time filled with transformation, beauty, and mystery.
 Blending neo-soul, folk, alternative R&B, storytelling, and scent, her work exists at the intersection of ritual and live music. Her songs explore our relationship to ourselves and to the living earth. Moments of melancholy, tenderness, hurt, joy and wonder sit side by side, revealing how each is part of being human.
 Fresh from a three-month artist residency in Japan, where she immersed herself in local mythology, spiritual practices, community, and the landscape while writing her upcoming album, Cézanne brings a special performance to Landjuweel with her live band. The show weaves together songs from her debut album Sunflower Child with new, unreleased material shaped by her time in Japan.

profiles.name: Woodcraft
profiles.description:
Woodcraft is one of the most promising string ensembles in the Netherlands. This innovative ensemble works with jazz, groove, improvisation and vocals, creating colorful and original compositions where forms and styles blend together to create adventurous musical stories.

Driven by curiosity and openness to different genres and playing techniques, Woodcraft constantly seeks ways to reimagine their instruments and thus create a fresh and distinctive sound. They are inspired by musicians such as Brad Mehldau, Herbie Hancock and the Turtle Island Quartet, and composers such as Stravinsky and Shostakovich. This leads to original compositions where forms and styles blend together to create adventurous musical stories.

Born out of a close friendship, the group gives the violin, viola and cello a renewed voice within the contemporary music landscape. Woodcraft won the "Keep an Eye" competition in 2026, and received first prize and the public award at the Dutch Jazz Competition in 2022. They have performed at venues such as North Sea Jazz Festival, Bimhuis and Orange Jazz Days.

profiles.name: Wilgendans
profiles.description:
Wilgendans is an open-groove ensemble featuring saxophonist Koen Boeijinga, guitarist Bram Stadhouders, and drummer Luca Marini. Hypnotic grooves and immersive soundscapes emerge from improvisation, bringing the willows to life. As custodians of the Willow Portal and trusted figures at Landjuweel, they welcome guest musicians for an ecstatic celebration of music, dance, and connection.

Koen Boeijinga: soprano saxophone, wind instruments, harmonium, percussion, fx
Bram Stadhouders: guitar, fx
Luca Marini: drums, percussion

profiles.name: Los Ojos de la Rumba
profiles.description:
Los Ojos de la Rumba is a project based in Amsterdam, inspired by the rich traditions of Afro-Cuban music. Bringing together musicians from different backgrounds, it celebrates the rhythms, energy, and communal spirit at the heart of Cuban musical culture.

Blending rumba, son, Latin Jazz, and other Afro-Cuban influences with a fresh and contemporary approach, Los Ojos de la Rumba creates music that invites people to dance, connect, and share in the joy of live performance. Rooted in tradition while open to new sounds, the project keeps the spirit of the music alive through community, improvisation, and celebration.

profiles.name: Rowel Hazelini
profiles.description: As dawn breaks, we welcome Rowel Hazelini. With the soul of an Italian storyteller and the voice of trombone and piano, he weaves passion, beauty, and longing into every note. Let the rising sun and his music meet as one.

profiles.name: Aja Waalwijk
profiles.description: Aja Waalwijk (1952), a visual artist, writer, performer, and (co-)organizer of cultural events, was involved in the Dichterscircus, the TI-Toy-Orchestra, and the One World Poetry manifestations. She wrote numerous texts with music by her brother Ben Waalwijk for the Paradisoshows of the Amsterdam Balloon Ensemble, and also performed with Ben in bands such as Face Lift and Mokumse Zaken, among others, as a songwriter/singer with Uli Trepte (Germany), Monkey Fantasies (Netherlands), and Tjak (Portugal).

profiles.name: Ellen Deckwitz
profiles.description:
Ellen Deckwitz (born 1982, Deventer) has continued to thrive in the literary world since making her debut: over the past few years, she has released award-winning collections (including *De steen vreest mij*, C. Buddingh Prize 2012), books on writing (*Zo word je een geweldige dichter* – widely praised), and the bestseller *Olijven moet je leren lezen*. In addition, she regularly hosts poetry slams, has won countless awards, reached the finals of *De slimste mens*, travels around the world to discover new poetry, performs theatre, and also writes weekly columns for both NRC Handelsblad and De Morgen. In 2022, her omnibus *Eerste hulp bij poëzie* was released. Ellen won the Johnny in 2021, the prestigious prize for stage poetry. She appeared on episode 5 of *De Poëziepodcast* by SLAA and presented the audio series *Voor de liefste*. She is the City Poet of Amsterdam.

Left:
Website

profiles.name: Menging Chu
profiles.description: Minging Chu will provide the musical introduction for "The Word."

profiles.name: Merlijn Huntjens
profiles.description:
Merlijn Huntjens is a writer and poet, deeply rooted in Heerlen. He reached the finals of the National Poetry Slam in 2016, 2017, and 2018. Currently, Merlijn works as a program maker for and with literary talents at Wintertuin, and he leads the collective PANDA together with Nina Willems in the former Eastern Mining Region in South Limburg. His debut collection, "Under My Feet," was published by De Harmonie in October 2025. The collection has been nominated for the Poetry Debut Prize 2026.

Left:
Website

profiles.name: Robin Block
profiles.description: Robin Block is a poet and musician. In his vibrant performances, he combines language, musicality, and movement. His most recent poetry collection is titled "Handleiding voor Ontheemden" (Atlas Contact). It was awarded the De Herman de Coninck Prize and the J.C. Bloemp Prize, and has been translated into English and Indonesian. Block also works as a columnist, writing instructor, and editor for the poetry magazine Awater.

profiles.name: Tim Lenders
profiles.description:
Tim Lenders has been performing on all the major stages in the Netherlands and Flanders since 2010, with a variety of work. Prose, poetry, spoken word, and music come together in his aim to always present something new during each performance. Over the past fifteen years, he has worked with countless artists in various combinations. The artist has already performed on stage more than one thousand times.

Tim has specifically invited Ruben Adrian Petrus and Humphrey Portier of the Dutch band Stamppot to Landjuweel, with a unique set full of fun, absurdity, and playfulness.
Photo credits: Jan Harryvan

stages.name: Area 46
stages.description:
In the heart of the village of Ruigoord lies a hidden green oasis: AREA 46. Famous and infamous. Just like in recent decades, the "Legal Aliens" will once again be making their presence known.

During the day, the workshop aliens will organize a series of inspiring activities, including "Laughing with Sabine," "Get Happy with Clay with Naomi," "Psychedelic Tea Party with Sami," and "Solar Punk with Priscilla." In addition, Bouke's free clothing shop is open every day for those looking for a surprising new outfit.
And then there's the night...

Expect an adventurous mix of techno, psytech, and psytrance, complemented by live modular synthesizers from TINRS. On Saturday night, Lars Wooferland Johansson will take over.
And finally, as the icing on the cake, coming all the way from Australia: AMYR.

stages.name: Bon Vivant
stages.description:
Behind the church, you'll discover Bon Vivant: a world where DJs, performers, handcrafted chocolate, curious drinks and surreal encounters blur the line between dance floor, theatre and tasting room.

Throughout the weekend, our stage unfolds like a living chocolate ritual. Music, performance and the gentle energy of guayusa (an Amazonian leaf) melt together into one playful, immersive world where each performance reveals a new chapter, drawing you deeper into the story.

For this edition, we've invited every music artist to reveal a different side of themselves. Expect familiar names in unfamiliar territory, surprising musical turns and one-off sets created especially for Bon Bon Vivant.

Come and dance, discover new sounds, taste our handcrafted chocolate, signature guayusa drinks and cacao shots or simply find a place to linger with friends. Stay for a song, a performance – but chances are you'll end up staying much longer.

Just follow the music. We'll take care of the rest.

As Bon Vivants do.

stages.name: Boogie Woods
stages.description: Deep in the village, there's an open space amidst the greenery where a funky dance floor awaits you. An eclectic mix of DJs and live acts have carte blanche here to surprise you and experiment with the many colors that electronic music has to offer. The program was lovingly curated by Ruigoord residents Stefnitz & Mayapapaya.

stages.name: DAF / Fringe
stages.description:
Put on your dancing outfit and your most comfortable shoes, and let loose! Dutch Acid Family is the place for a unique mix of music, creativity, and togetherness. Expect an unforgettable DAF vibe, special encounters, and a dance floor where everyone is welcome. Together, we'll make it another wonderful weekend!

Thursday
Jazzy trip hop

Friday
Sunshine Reggae

Saturday & Sunday
DutchAcidFamily

stages.name: The Playground
stages.description:
At the Play Area, you can let loose and have fun! This is where you can play, explore, meet new people, and enjoy yourself. Try XXL Twister, take a dip in the chat pool for interesting conversations, and indulge in crafting activities. Sing your heart out at karaoke, become a floating poet, jam on some music, challenge someone to a water fight, and meet new people during speed dating.

As evening falls, the Play Area transforms into a vibrant dance paradise where the best DJs will keep you dancing until late.
Whether you come alone or with a group: you'll leave here with new friends and wonderful memories. Come play! Come dance! Come enjoy yourself and capture these moments in the Photobooth. The Play Area is waiting for you.

stages.name: Village Square · Woven Voices Terrace
stages.description:
A place where improvisation, co-creation and connection come together. Where we celebrate life with all generations: community, caring for one another, and our mutual interconnectedness.

Everything here comes to life in the moment.

At the heart of it is "Woven Voices." Driven by a love for meeting people and bringing them together, we create a living artwork where every voice, every hand, and every story is welcome. Each contribution becomes a new thread in a growing community artwork.

During "What's Alive in the Room" and "SoulSong," we deepen this experience. By focusing on what is alive here, we create a song together, a shared voice that makes audible what connects us.

Weaving is an ancient tradition that has connected people for over 30,000 years. Here too, we weave not only with thread, but also with stories, music and encounters.

stages.name: Village Square · Drum Fire
stages.description:
Trommelvuur is a new stage for percussion music in Ruigoord, initiated by Sebastian Skeete McLeed. In collaboration with the founders of the African percussion tradition within Ruigoord and its surroundings, Trommelvuur brings together generations of musicians.

With cornerstones like Momo Touré, Robert Tydrich, Ibou N' Dongue, Jos de Haas, Amara Diabaté, Norbert de Jong, Mame N'Diack, Robert Blankenstein, Roy Groeneveld, Sebastian Skeete Mcleed, and the new generation of drummers such as Amier Sloot, Jasper Hutte, and Jur de Vries, Lamine Dieng, a place is created where knowledge, rhythm, tradition, and new rhythms are formed or passed on.

Trommelvuur offers space for the combination of percussion and wind instruments, workshops in West African and Latin percussion, joint performances, and musical exchange. A meeting place where the history of percussion music in Ruigoord is celebrated and its future is shaped together.

stages.name: Village Square · InstrumenTaal Stage
stages.description:
At InstrumenTaal Stage, you'll find instruments and musicians. A program for all ages, featuring musical travelers, troubadours, and small-scale artists of life.

From children's stories to music tapestries and free-flow rap jam sessions.

stages.name: KALEIDO Faerie Forest
stages.description:
Between the reeds and the trees lies a universe that you can only find if you dare to wander. The Faerie Forest is the enchanted realm of fairies, freaks, and Furby toys, where every encounter is a small ritual.

Get your skin immortalized with a tattoo. Give your most personal space an artistic touch at Intimate Haircuts. Transform yourself into your ultimate version, or discover your future with a tarot reading from an angel or a villain. Dr. Oxytocin offers cozy poetry consultations and hands full of love massage you back to life.

You can't miss the deliciously pink Kalaudo castle. Disney meets Vegas meets the Efteling meets the faded glory of Once Upon a Time. Come for things like a doll show or a serious coaching session with CruLaua. Don't make watchdog BoBo angry, because this playful dog is not for cats.

And then there's the portal. Somewhere between the branches, the gateway to the realm of ÜBERFURBY glows, where dozens of Furby toys await you with an intense message that you won't be able to get out of your head…

The Faerie Forest is for explorers who dare to dream that magic exists. Come and search, come and get lost, and leave as someone who dares to be just a little bit stranger.

stages.name: KALEIDO Queer Stage
stages.description:
Outside, the norm has been reached. But deep within the reeds lies our defensive stronghold, the last queer haven. With steam, slime, bubbles and smoke, we drive away all the forces that try to keep us small. The Queer Stage was built by the KALEIDO Collective, plank by plank, splash by splash. And this year, no one will be going through it!

For four days, we'll play with absurdity, activism, and prime-time entertainment. Expect a lesbian wedding ceremony, a spiritual titan battle, bingo, (baptism) ceremonies, puppies, and drag activists. On Sunday afternoon, "Embrace Your No Talent" bursts open, the day when everything is welcome, including our now legendary children's catwalk.

In everything we do, you'll feel the No Talent philosophy. Alongside seasoned performers, here you'll find stars who are climbing a stage for the very first time. Because creativity belongs to everyone, nothing needs to be perfect, and it's in the messy that the magic lies.

After the shows, our flamboyant DJ crew will take you on with electro bangers, campy fempop, and niche disco gems. We guarantee the vibe: guaranteed soft knees. This is the heart of KALEIDO. Our own universe, and we are incredibly proud of it. Find us in the reeds. But be warned: before you know it, you'll be standing in the spotlight…

stages.name: KALEIDO Workshop Rietreat
stages.description:
In the green field stands a truly unique tent: the Rietreat! For the first time, KALEIDO and Peacock Garden are joining forces. Over four days, KALEIDO will present a workshop program that gently guides you and then takes you just a little further than you expected.

During the day, the Rietreat is a playground for everyone. Transform into a Dragtivist, siren, or your pet. Learn to flirt, get painted, play Consent Bingo, or go on a platonic speed date. Small festival creatures make slime and practice their looks for the children's catwalk. As the evening progresses, the game becomes bolder, and we explore sensual energies, sometimes with blindfolds.

Each workshop is an invitation to touch something within yourself that you didn't know existed. No performance, no norms, no rush. Just the question: what happens if you dare to move with what presents itself?

On Friday from 18:00, our friends at Peacock Garden take over the tent with their own evening. Then, the Rietreat transforms into an intimate escape filled with yoga, art, bubbles, music, and lovely people. A gentle descent into the weekend: pamper yourself and leave our bubble feeling extra energized 🫧

For curious minds, hesitant souls, and those seeking self-discovery. Come to slow down, deepen, or simply disappear in the reeds.

stages.name: Church · Punks of the Parish
stages.description:
On Thursday evening, the church is a punk haven, with activists playing loud music and fiercely advocating for a better world.

A raw tribute to everything that makes Amsterdam a livable city.

Tijdelijke toon invited artists, theater makers, musicians, and activists from Amsterdam's underground scene to take over the legendary Ruigoord church completely.

Bring ear protection, dance through the chains of capitalism, and MAKE the piece.

stages.name: Church · The Art Jewel
stages.description:
New at the Landjuweel Festival! A great selection of Dutch documentaries that capture the essence of artistry, community, identity, and freedom. Step into the church, put on your headphones, open your eyes, and immerse yourself in the stories that breathe the free spirit of Ruigoord. Includes Q&A with the filmmakers: from up-and-coming talent to established names.

Friday 24, Saturday 25, Sunday 26 July

stages.name: Church · KALEIDO Takeover
stages.description:
For the very first time, KALEIDO receives the keys to the Ruigoord church on Friday. We take over from legendary queer collectives like Circus Klauterwerck and Boyscouts, exploring the endless forms of free expression. After all, the most beautiful and sacred place in the village is completely transformed into a queer space for one day and night!

In the afternoon, the church comes alive with dance, workouts, games, and playful fights. Then, a very peculiar cult leader appears, ready to win over hearts and minds. And just before the DJs get going, we unleash all our primal energy in the Church of the Untamed.

In the evening, the KALEIDO DJs take over and test how high the energy can go. The answer: much higher! From five in the morning, they'll take you on a journey from uplifting disco to synthwave and electro, culminating in raw queer rave music, in a paradise of pulsating light and psychedelic visuals. Throughout the night, KALEIDO performers keep things lively. Whether it's kinky, flamboyant, or simply wild, this cacophony is something you won't soon forget.

Churches around the world have been preaching for centuries about who you should be. We preach the opposite: be yourself. Strange, loud, and completely complete. HALLELUJA!

stages.name: Church · All Is One
stages.description:
Everything is inextricably linked together! You, us, everything and everyone.

Unity in diversity, together we are strong. All the colors of the rainbow make up the whole. Don't let black-and-white thinking get you down, but immerse yourself in the infinite source of Love. On Saturday night, after the "Sculpture Route," we will gather at the Church!

stages.name: Church · A’ Ma ‘Na
stages.description:
__*“through unknown cycles, we become
in spaces of curiosity,
it sparkles in the eye.

from the smallest needle point to the darkness that holds all.

in surrender, we unite in each moment, dying.

grieving as a tear from the sky, returning to the sea.

and how light reflects in all reflections:
where we hide, we find ourselves again.

allowing the madness to open and unfold.

beyond the other voices that make you lie.

slowing down to dare, as moss patiently covers stone.

To see who do you obey?

for the fire of the heart to move,
Burning to be all that we are.

We speak the world we believe in.

Remember how earth kisses our feet with each step we take.

let life flow through us as it is.

not to miss, not to force.
As all changes and all remains.

Trusting the innocence of our pulse.
Just as the enthusiasm of fresh greens.
longing to tell a story.

Like seeds that split themselves open, not knowing the forest they will grow in.

the flocks of birds in the sky drawing the unheard song.

the leaves that fall feed the soil, softening our walk.

and so we meet in the dance,

where we allow ourselves, all, to be free.

we play.”*
__
XOXO,

A’MA’NA

stages.name: Frog: Metamorphosis
stages.description:
Maybe you're a frog. Maybe a lizard. Or maybe just someone who doesn't fit anywhere anymore. Here, you don't have to be beautiful, smart, or whole. You just need to break out of your shell. Get lost for a while. Make something. Break something. Start over.

with love by Kikvors Collectief

Special thanks to:
Masha Soetekouw (chief area design)
Lara Wijnker (interactive wire sculptures)
Jasper Veldkamp (light installation “Sól”)
Fabian Dekker, Flynn van Schijndel, Lucas Crietee (soundscape “Sól”)
Kateryna Kukunina (interactive installation “Imag- ining this time as a holy hour”)

stages.name: The Crater
stages.description:
The Birth of The Crater

Come, come, the time that we dream!
The Earth has whispered, Ruigoord has listened.

Beneath the surface of our recently cleared land,
something wonderful, suddenly, has come to be.

Where cosmic dust and earthly clay meet,
a wondrous heart has opened itself to the world.

We have not built, but dug.
We have not stacked, but opened.

In harmony with fire, earth, wind, and water,
we proudly present: The Crater.

Each day, the programming in our natural amphitheater will be inspired by one of the four cardinal directions, protected by our faithful friends who have long watched over our green oasis:

in the North, the clever Fox; in the East, the free Bird; in the West, the ancient Badger; and in the South, the inventive Rat.

We invite you all to dedicate this sacred place with us.

Let us be carried by the winds of earthly rituals and light dreams, from fiery dances to leaps into the depths of still waters.

Come down with us together to The Crater, find your own place in the circle, and become part of the whole.

This is the starting point where we begin again, an timeless foundation, for an endless existence.

Welcome to the new era, where The Crater of Ruigoord will create a hole in time.

stages.name: Natural High
stages.description: We are all connected as one organism. Unseparated from the earth and the cosmos, unseparated from each other. Natural High organizes gatherings based on the concept of non-duality and unity consciousness. This is a state of awareness in which you can increasingly stand in your own light and move with everything that comes your way. If you would like to learn more about this, meet like-minded people, and practice these themes, then let your open heart feel welcome at the Natural High field.

stages.name: Rebel Stage
stages.description: The Rebel Stage was born out of the need to give rebellious sounds free rein at Landjuweel. Here, blues and rock are still very much alive, and pretty much any style can be represented as long as it's rebellious. It is home to the "Ruigoord Rebels," who have been bringing their loud inner rebellions together from outside the village since the beginning of this century!

stages.name: Salon
stages.description: Salon Ruigoord is a magical theater and workshop where angels, elves, dragons, humans, and other creatures are welcome. This beloved space, located in the heart of the iconic artist colony Ruigoord, began 20 years ago as a dilapidated blue square box. Over the years, this space has grown into a place of magic, music, and love, and each year it continues to transform with new instruments, unique components, and, of course, more dragons.

stages.name: Sloooase
stages.description:
SloÔoase is a hidden chill-out space within Ruigoord where music, meeting, and relaxation come together in a warm, intimate atmosphere filled with natural elements.

During the day, the place features live concerts, downtempo grooves, world music, and spontaneous jams, while in the evening it gradually transforms into a hypnotic hangout with deep basslines, psychedelic rhythms, and intimate dance moments around the booth.

Expect soft cushions, botanical drinks, organic decor, special live music, and an environment where you naturally slow down and escape from time.

stages.name: Temple of Self
stages.description: In the wildest corner of Ruigoord, you'll find the Temple of Self. Built as an entrance to your own inner world of wealth. This year also features a program that invites you to learn about yourself in various workshops and discover new ways to connect with your inner self. Of course, there are also beautiful celebrations to mark the occasion. And everything else needed to keep our energy high during Landjuweel – from massages to high-frequency drinks and food.

stages.name: Theatre Field
stages.description:
The Circus of Encounter
Finding Your Space

Amidst the chaos of Landjuweel lies a place to land. A space for circus, street theater, poetry, games, movement, and spontaneous encounters.

Creators from Ruigoord and beyond — from Spain to Argentina — will take you on a journey through the space between dream and reality, between strangers and friends, between identity and collectivity, between being on the move and returning home. The in-between space where we meet each other in the universal language that transcends words and cultural boundaries.

Participate in a workshop. Trust a stranger. Show yourself and meet someone new. And discover that finding your way back can sometimes begin in a place you've never been before.

Come and see. Come and play. Come and get lost.

Workshop

Playful Revolution is a movement to introduce more playfulness into our daily lives. This year, a curated series of workshops will be held, hosted at Theaterveld, on days 14 & 17. The workshops will explore the realms of Play & Movement, delving into partner acrobatics, contact improvisation, and Movement Culture. Why do we ever stop playing?

stages.name: Theo's Tea Garden
stages.description:
A well-known spot in Ruigoord and surrounding areas. It could also have been called "Ruigoord by the Sea," thanks to Theo's tireless efforts of hauling countless buckets of sand with his Piaggio scooter and wheelbarrow.

A unique, tranquil place, a natural green oasis, where you can find shade or bask in the sun while enjoying tea, Indian chai, juices, smoothies, apple pie, raw chocolates, brownies, and, as a tribute to Theo's unforgettable soup.

A wonderful spot for a good conversation or simply relaxing to the sound of birdsong. Theo himself, with his distinctive green mustache, memorable quotes like "Good heavens, what a beautiful life!", infectious laughter, and a deep sense of humor and insight, is sadly no longer around, but his memory and traditions are kept alive by his daughter Tara, her partner Inti, and their grandchildren Sky and Surya, while Yllen keeps the garden in its full glory.

A private beach in Ruigoord's own bay. A stage on the dike where Tara (Esther Kleij) also holds her Sunday yoga classes, with a lunch option available during the summer months in the garden and tea garden. The diverse festivals, such as Vurige Tongen, Lala land, and Landjuweel, will be held here, offering various performances to enjoy.

stages.name: Butterfly Garden
stages.description:
The Butterfly Garden is a magical garden for magical people. During festivals, it's an oasis where people can meet and enjoy poetry and acoustic music. You can also retreat here in the embrace of nature, sitting on a bench or in the treehouse. Since 2015, graphic designer and self-proclaimed "garden pilgrim" Sieger Duinkerken has been working on the garden, inspired by permaculture and environmental art. There are now many unique plants growing, some of which are edible. Discover your inner magic and merge with nature!

The Butterfly Garden is an oasis where humans and nature meet. On special occasions, you can enjoy intimate performances and the now-famous chocolate cake. It's a place where you can marvel at the diversity of natural forms, and feel safe while sitting on a bench amidst the greenery. The garden was discovered in 2015 by graphic designer and self-proclaimed "garden pilgrim" Sieger Duinkerken. Discover your inner magic and merge with nature! See you there...

stages.name: Willow Portal
stages.description:
At the Wilgenportaal, people can indulge in the juices of this cosmic leak: it will make their blood taste sweet like elderflower syrup, and cause it to fizz like a bottle of cider!

This place, built with local wood in harmony with nature, can be visited year-round. During festivals, it offers an eclectic program full of wonderful music, dance, and theater, all taking place around and between the living structures that will continue to grow each year.

entries.title: Welcome to the Free Haven
entries.description: Almost half a century ago, a pioneering group of free-spirited artists launched a kite into the sky against strong winds. The artistic freedom that followed has become an ecstatic foundation for a cultural ecosystem that has proven indispensable to our time, where the blossoms of inspiration, fruits of love, and connection with Mother Earth have formed the "Ruigoord" we know today. Today, Ruigoord Island is much more than just a historical site; it's a living laboratory, a playground for Homo Ludens. Supported by a close-knit community of artists, practitioners, lovers, and admirers – all active guardians of an irreplaceable free space where freedom, safety, and self-expression are elevated to the art form of life itself – the Landjuweel festival is the annual expression of this philosophy. Forget the word "event"; it's a ritual of co-creation, a Hi Ha Happening! More than just an open stage, it's a canvas that is repeatedly repainted each year by a colorful procession of individual eccentrics, independent artist collectives, open poets, dancing dreamers, essential musicians, theatrical performers, and simple all-rounders. The true magic of the Landjuweel lies not in its packed program but in the countless hands and hearts that come together to create it. Here, we don't just fill a dance floor; we enchant the atoms between the blades of grass and the stars above. And why does this matter, especially now? Because today's world constantly encourages us to consume, scroll, and copy…

entries.title: Opening Ceremony
entries.description:
Dear Free Spirits, Dreamers, Paradise Birds, and Wonderful Friends of Joy!

All the winds are slowly returning to Ruigwaarts, and the leaves are already whispering softly above the grounds: The Land Jewel is about to awaken once again!

On the first day of the festival, we will weave together all elements and generations in a ritual that will open all the gates of our magical island. Come with bells and whistles or start practicing playing on your fingers, take your place around the circle, and let's bloom together. Thursday at 4:00 PM in the Crater.

entries.title: Sculpture Route
entries.description:
23:00 - 01:01 - Start at Ruigoord Gate - Route along the Dijk.

When the sun's rays have surrendered to the reflections of the full Moon, the village holds its breath, and we give way to the awakening of the Night. Then this nocturnal pilgrimage begins: The Sculptures Route.

A captivating journey through installations, projections, and performances that take you through the alluring mists of the Ruygen Dijk. And know this: you are not just an observer in this journey; you are one of the many sparks of the fire. Adorn yourself and everyone around you with light, love, color, glitter, and everything that shines. Together, we form a celestial body, a luminous trail of walking stars, visible far into the sky.

Poem by Aja Waalwijk:
Embrace, embrace this night
of a thousand and one nights
the dark splendor that lingers here
is the spark that relieves the darkness
Freed from duality
of light that combats the darkness
and compares it with good and evil
brings the day dream connection
Come home to the dark house
where radiant beings reside
let the glorious splendor
flow in a gentle glow
The illustrious black envelops and conceals
the layers that await you
the shadow mother of the day
wishes you a good night

entries.title: Introduction
entries.description: Step into Ruigoord, our magical village where the grey world fades away and colorful imagination reigns supreme. Here, in this enclave for artists and free thinkers, we celebrate life, love, and endless creativity. Here, you can be who you are, or discover what you've always wanted to be!

entries.title: Respect: A Tribute to Each Other
entries.description: We are all children of the same cosmos, dancing to the rhythm of our own existence. Embrace the unique expressions of your fellow Roughnecks, and weave together a tapestry of love and camaraderie. Keep it loving, keep it light.

entries.title: Pets: Parrots Only
entries.description: Leave your furry, feathered, or scaled four-legged pets at home. On this quirky island, there are already plenty of mythical creatures and strange birds flitting about. This also applies to the construction and demolition phases, when we bring out the motorized dragons and unicorns to enchant the area.

entries.title: Being Silly: A Loving Plea
entries.description: Normalcy is an illusion. Being "weird" isn't an option here, but rather a gentle expectation. Let your inner clown out, dance as if no one is watching, and shout your wildest dreams to the moon. Be absurd, be wonderful, be yourself, be free.

entries.title: Damage and/or loss: Take care of your own cosmos.
entries.description: In this village full of wonders, you are the guardian of your own treasures and your own temple. Dance carefully, cherish your belongings, and look out for each other. The organizers cannot protect your possessions or personal items.

entries.title: Security: Our Friendly Guides
entries.description: Consider our security as the guardians of this dream. They are not controllers, but guides who channel the energy effectively. Listen to their whispers and instructions; they are there to protect the magic.

entries.title: Alcohol and Drugs: Travel with Wisdom
entries.description: A journey inward can take many forms, but be aware of your limits. The trading of earthly goods such as alcohol, drugs, or other mind-altering substances is not permitted in our haven. Anyone who disrupts harmony through excess, intimidation, or trade is kindly asked to leave our dream world. If necessary, we will involve the outside world.

entries.title: Prohibited items: Don't bring anything home from work.
entries.description: Bring only the essentials for this trip. Leave behind anything that makes loud noises, cuts, or unnecessarily dulls your senses. Fireworks, weapons, and hard drugs disrupt the delicate balance of our utopia. Most importantly, bring your open heart and creative mind.

entries.title: Searching: Turning Inside Out
entries.description: To ensure that our shared dream remains pure, it may be necessary for us to ask you to empty your bags. Please don't see this as distrust, but rather as a ritual to protect the gates of Ruigoord.

entries.title: Capacity: Full is Full, The Island Knows Its Limits.
entries.description: Our village and our church are like a beating heart, but even a heart can become overwhelmed. The space is limited. When the boundaries of the island are reached, "full" truly means full. Be on time to secure your place in this dream.

entries.title: PsyCare
entries.description: PsyCare is a safe and peaceful place where trained volunteers are available to support you if you're experiencing a difficult or overwhelming situation – whether you have access to resources or not. You can find them at the Natural High-veld.

Closed: Monday, 2:00 PM

entries.title: Don't leave us hanging: Leave No Trace, Except Love.
entries.description: Ruigoord is one big playground for Homo Ludens, the playing human. And playing works best on a clean slate. Don't let your waste roam around as a solitary soul, but take it to the designated areas. And what about your sacred need? You won't fulfill it in our beloved bushes, but on the thrones we have set up for you.

entries.title: Quench the fire: Nurture the Inner Flame.
entries.description: The fire department experts have spoken: open fires are taboo. Even a single ember from your cigarette can turn our green oasis into a scorched wasteland. Be a guardian of the greenery, and extinguish your flame carefully.

entries.title: Alarming neighbors: Respect the Iron Dragon 🐉
entries.description: Don't touch the fence of our neighbors, EVOS. A fearsome dragon will awaken with a loud roar, and you risk a fine from the outside world. Our freedom ends where their fence begins.

entries.title: Foxglove, aww!: The Kiss of a Wild Flower
entries.description: In our wild gardens, the majestic but poisonous bear's-foot (Lonicera) grows. Touching it can cause painful blisters on your skin. Admire it from a distance and teach your children to appreciate this wild beauty with respect.

entries.title: Cashless: The Magic of the PIN Card
entries.description: Landjuweel is a source of energy, not paper money. Everything you pay for can be done with the magic of your debit card. If you still prefer to use cash and banknotes, you can exchange them at our redemption points into a Landjuweel card.

entries.title: Location
entries.description: Beyond the vibrant energy of the village, this year not around the mystical "Eye of Ruigoord," but a kilometer further into the harbor, you'll find our new campsite. After endless negotiations and discussions with external forces, we are proud to still be able to offer a sacred space for the night, a fully-fledged campsite for a real festival. This year, the campsite is located a bit further than usual. Fortunately, there are shuttle buses available, and we can once again enjoy being surrounded by nature!

entries.title: Opening hours
entries.description: The campsite opens on Thursday at 10:00 and closes again on Monday at 14:00.

entries.title: Facilities
entries.description: At our campsite, you'll find everything you need to take care of yourself: refreshing showers and toilets, a place to wash your pots and pans, delicious food from our food trucks, first aid for minor injuries, and a relaxing area to unwind.

entries.title: Shhh!: The Night Whisperers
entries.description: No matter how loud the call of the festival may be, remember that the campsite is a special place. Keep your primal screams and ecstatic outbursts for the festival grounds. At the campsite, we speak the language of the wind and the stars, far above sea level.

entries.title: Family Camping: A Place for All Generations
entries.description: For the tribes with small warriors, there is a separate family campsite. Here, the night is dedicated to deep rest and the morning to the cheerful chirping of the future.

entries.title: Groenoord, Action and Seed!
entries.description: Ruigoord is a Groenoord. This isn't just a catchy phrase; it's a promise. We are literally planting our love for this place in the ground, with food forests and vegetable gardens. No words, but seeds!

entries.title: Zero-emission: Dancing on the Power of the Sun
entries.description: We aim for a celebration powered by the energy of the future, not by the ghosts of the past. Our power comes from renewable sources, harvested from the sun and stored in batteries. The machines that are still running use HVO (hydrotreated vegetable oil), a blue diesel produced from waste. A generator may sometimes be unavoidable, but even those run on this clean fuel.

entries.title: Flying: Traveling using earthly means.
entries.description: One of the biggest "fingerprints" on Mother Earth's breath is often air travel. Therefore, we invite our artists to travel as pilgrims: by train, boat, or bus. This way, their art arrives in sync with the rhythm of the earth itself.

entries.title: Cashless: No More Plastic Waste
entries.description: By moving away from cash payments, we save a huge amount – around 150,000 plastic coins. A small action with a big impact. No more plastic coins, no more plastic soup. There is one redemption point in the village, located next to the church!

entries.title: Repurposing: The Beauty of a Second Life
entries.description: Three-quarters of the magic you see around you, our decorations, are reborn. They are materials with a past life, or they are waiting for a new future.

entries.title: Recycling: The Cycle of the Cup ☕
entries.description: Your empty glass isn't trash; it's the beginning of something new. Bring it back to the bar, because only clean, collected glasses and bottles can be recycled into the glasses for our next party. A glass on the floor is a lost opportunity.

entries.title: Beakers: The Key to the Circle
entries.description: Upon arrival, you will receive two sacred coins. They have no monetary value, but are the key to obtaining two free cups or bottles. Exchange one coin for a full cup, and your empty cup for a new coin. This way, we all keep the circle going.

entries.title: Food: Eating with Love for the Planet
entries.description: All the food you find here is served on dishes that can be returned to the earth after use. The menu is a tribute to the plant world: completely vegetarian, and most of our kitchens are even 100% vegan. Taste the future!

entries.title: Waste Sorting: Bringing Order to Creative Chaos
entries.description: Behind the scenes and on the field, we separate what we leave behind. By offering our waste in separate streams, we give it a chance to be reborn. A small effort for a world of difference.

entries.title: You: The Hero of this Story
entries.description: We are building a new world together. Systems only change when we ourselves change. Your role is crucial. Come by bike or public transport. Leave no trace, only the footprints of your dance. Help us to separate, put out your cigarette, return your glass. Feel your strength, embrace your responsibility: there is a world to win, and it starts here, with you.

info.title: Everything about the Alles kids and Ruigoord.

entries.title: Introduction: Art for Little Giants
entries.description: Each year, Landjuweel creates a world for the youngest dreamers. Why? Because within their imaginations lie the seeds of the future.

entries.title: Historically Young: A Village That Never Stops Blooming
entries.description: Think of the quiet villages where the children have moved away. A village without children is a village without a heartbeat. Ruigoord has always thrived on the rhythm of playing children. Even when the world said that no one should live here anymore, we remained a home for imagination. The children of that time are now the artists and guardians. They are living proof: the new generation is the engine of the eternal dream.

entries.title: A Multi-Generational Gem: Where Everyone Can Dance
entries.description: Behind the church, on the field of wonders, you'll find a program bursting with magic for young children: theater, puppet shows, circus workshops, and treasure hunts through the unknown. We'll be doing face painting, crafts, and making music until sunset. But take a closer look, and you'll see that this program isn't just for kids. Parents, grandparents – we often see four generations playing together. This shared joy spreads like wildfire across the entire area, creating that unique, loving atmosphere that makes Landjuweel so special. Help us cherish this delicate ecosystem of fun. Give a hand to a child, a smile to an older person, and join us in our multigenerational masterpiece.

entries.title: A Message for Young Dreamers: Passing the Torch
entries.description: As the sun sets, the Landjuweel changes shifts. The playful daytime light gives way to the dazzling lights of the night. This is a signal for all our visitors under 18 years old to leave the premises and allow the party animals to take over at night.
